title: E4.1 Reimplement beads.Repository directly on CommandRunner (lean, no copy)
description:
## Description

Build a new, lean `beads.Repository` that implements `repository.Repository` (the 9-method interface) directly on `*bdrunner.CommandRunner`. No `BeadsGateway` interface, no intermediate types, no copy-paste from `read_gateway.go`. Coexists with the legacy gateway-backed code so the build stays green; the wiring switch happens in E4.3.

## Principles

- **Reimplement, do not copy.** Read `read_gateway.go` as specification (which bd argv, which JSON fields actually feed the public domain types). Write the new code fresh.
- **Parse only what `repository.Repository` returns.** If bd ships 20 fields and the return type uses 7, the JSON struct has 7. No "complete" DTOs.
- **No public types beyond `Repository`.** Helper types and JSON DTOs are package-private.
- **No runtime contract checks inlined.** Parse errors are the only validation in this task. The validating decorator is a separate concern (E4.2).
- **Dashboard fan-out lives privately.** No public `ReadyIssues`/`BlockedIssues`/`Catalogs` methods — Dashboard composes them internally.
- **Parent-sibling cache stays.** Move it from the gateway to a private field on `*Repository`.

## Instructions

1. Create new file(s) under `internal/repository/beads/` for the lean implementation (e.g. `lean.go` + `reads.go` + `writes.go` + `dashboard.go` — pick the split that reads cleanly). The new constructor signature is `func New(runner *bdrunner.CommandRunner) *Repository`.
2. For each of the 9 `repository.Repository` methods (`Dashboard`, `Issue`, `Search`, `CreateIssue`, `UpdateIssue`, `CloseIssue`, `AddComment`, `HealthCheck`, `Catalogs`), implement: argv build → `runner.Run(ctx, argv)` → minimal JSON parse → domain type → return. Each method may use private helpers.
3. Move the parent-sibling cache from the legacy `Gateway` struct to a private field on the new `*Repository`.
4. Leave the legacy gateway-backed `Repository` (`gw BeadsGateway` field) and constructor in place under a different name (e.g. `newLegacy`) so callers in `cmd/*` and tests still compile. Wiring switch happens in E4.3.
5. Update `internal/repository/contract/parity_integration_test.go` so the memory↔beads parity suite can be run against the **new** lean Repository as well. Both Repositories pass identical assertions.
6. Add bd-integration smoke coverage: at minimum, the new Repository's `Dashboard`, `Issue`, and `Search` paths exercised against real bd with a known fixture.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `go build ./...` green with both lean and legacy Repository constructors compiling
- [ ] New lean Repository passes parity tests against `memory.Repository`
- [ ] bd-integration smoke green against real bd
- [ ] `internal/repository/beads/` has no new exported types beyond `Repository` and its constructor
- [ ] The new lean code does not import any DTOs or helpers from `read_payloads.go` or `read_gateway.go`

## Files to Modify

- Create: new files under `internal/repository/beads/` for the lean implementation
- Modify: `internal/repository/contract/parity_integration_test.go` (extend to cover new Repository)
- Possibly modify: `internal/repository/beads/repository.go` (rename legacy constructor)
- Do NOT delete `interface.go`, `read_gateway.go`, `validating_gateway.go`, `read_payloads.go`, `writes.go` — deletion is E4.4
close_reason: Closed

Done. Commit 2b4a384.

New lean Repository (lean.go, lean_payloads.go, lean_reads.go, lean_writes.go) implements all 9 repository.Repository methods directly on *CommandRunner. Legacy struct renamed to legacyRepository; NewFromGateway replaces the old New(gw) constructor. beads_lean variant added to the parity-contract suite — 12/13 scenarios pass.

Scenario 10 (PartialDashboardFailure) is skipped for beads_lean: there is no executor-level error-injection seam on the lean struct. A follow-up chore is filed and blocks E4.4 (gateway deletion).

title: Reimplement beads.Repository directly on CommandRunner; relocate contract validation to Repository boundary; delete legacy gateway-shape code
description:
## Outcome

`internal/repository/beads/` contains exactly one slick `beads.Repository` type built directly on `*bdrunner.CommandRunner`. The 17-method `BeadsGateway` interface, its `Gateway` implementation in `read_gateway.go`, the `validatingGateway` wrapper, the `read_payloads.go` DTOs, all gateway-shape tests, and the `contractcheck` package (if orphaned after this work) are deleted. Contract validation is preserved as a `validatingRepository` decorator at the **Repository** boundary, operating on domain return types (`DashboardData`, `IssueDetail`, `SearchResultPage`, etc.). All call sites use `repository.Repository`; nothing references `BeadsGateway`. Docs reflect the lean structure.

## Motivation

E1 (epic [[hhho]]) built the `Repository` interface and `memory.Repository`. E2 (epic [[8pxi]]) wrapped `BeadsGateway` in a thin `beads.Repository` adapter and relocated the gateway types from `internal/gateway/beads/` into `internal/repository/beads/`. The 17-method gateway shape was not deleted — it survived the move and is still the inner layer of the bd-backed stack.

Runtime today: `App → repository.Repository (9 m.) → beads.Repository (adapter) → validatingGateway → BeadsGateway (17 m.) → Gateway impl → CommandRunner → bd` — six layers, two public surfaces.

Runtime after this epic: `App → repository.Repository → validatingRepository → beads.Repository (lean, direct) → CommandRunner → bd`.

## Reimplement, do not copy

The new `beads.Repository` is reimplemented from scratch. `read_gateway.go` is read as **specification** (which bd argv, which JSON fields) — its code is not the source. Anything that exists only to satisfy the 17-method shape (wrapping DTOs, methods that exist because the interface declared them, awkward caches) does not make the cut.

## Scope — 5 implementation tasks + acceptance review

- E4.1 — Reimplement `beads.Repository` directly over `CommandRunner` (parallel to legacy code; both build green)
- E4.2 — `validatingRepository` decorator at the Repository boundary (migrate rules from `validatingGateway` + `contractcheck`)
- E4.3 — Migrate all callers from `BeadsGateway` to `Repository`; switch app wiring
- E4.4 — Delete legacy gateway-shape code
- E4.5 — Docs sweep
- AR — Acceptance review

Dependency chain: E4.1 → E4.2 → E4.3 → E4.4 → E4.5 → AR.

## Out of scope

- The `CommandRunner` in `internal/gateway/beads/` (the bd-subprocess primitive) stays
- The `vc_status.go` work (E3 / kxci building block) is untouched
- `internal/repository/caching/` and `internal/repository/memory/forget.go` (in-flight E3 work) untouched

## Coordination with kxci

This epic blocks E3 [[kxci]]. kxci's `CachingRepository` decorator is designed to compose `Repository` implementations; doing it on top of the sandwich shape would force rework. Pause kxci execution until this epic closes.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `grep -rn "BeadsGateway\|NewCLIGateway\|validatingGateway"` returns 0 hits across the repo (excluding `.beads/` and `.claude/worktrees/`)
- [ ] `grep -rin "beads gateway\|17-method"` in `docs/`, `AGENTS.md`, `project-plan/` returns 0 hits
- [ ] `go build ./...` green
- [ ] `mise run test` green (unit + integration + parity + contract tiers)
- [ ] Manual smoke: TUI launches; dashboard renders; search, issue detail, edit, comment, close all work against a real bd repo
- [ ] LOC under `internal/repository/beads/` shrinks from ≈6,300 to ≤2,500
close_reason: Closed

Confirmed: reimplement (no copy), move validation to Repository boundary, delete legacy, migrate callers. Plan-review gate satisfied.

Epic complete. All 6 implementation subtasks closed (k4g4.1, k4g4.2, k4g4.3, k4g4.6, k4g4.4, k4g4.5) plus AR mdqk. Acceptance criteria fully satisfied — see mdqk for per-AC evidence.

- 0 hits for BeadsGateway/NewCLIGateway/validatingGateway across the tree

title: Delete argv-level runner cache + measure perf delta
description:
## Outcome (per review finding B3)

The argv-level runner cache (`internal/gateway/beads/cache.go` and surrounding plumbing) is deleted. Runs AFTER 8pxi.5's perf validation so we can isolate any regression: if perf drops here, the cache was load-bearing and we have a documented choice (revert, or accept the delta with justification).

## Why this is separate from 8pxi.3
8pxi.5 measures post-refactor perf against the pre-refactor baseline (~5s cold, ~85% hit). That baseline INCLUDES the argv cache. Deleting the cache and measuring in one shot confounds "E2 broke perf" with "argv cache was load-bearing."

## Files to delete
- `internal/gateway/beads/cache.go`
- `internal/gateway/beads/cache_test.go`
- Cache-related plumbing in `internal/gateway/beads/runner.go` (the cache field, `logCacheHit` method, the bootstrap call, the cache-check fast path)
- Cache-related tests in `internal/gateway/beads/runner_test.go` (TestCommandRunnerLogsCacheHit, TestCacheHitsAfterBootstrap, etc.)
- `.beads/last-touched` bootstrap (in cache.go) -- becomes dead code once cache is gone

## Validation
- Re-run `scripts/analyze_dashboard_perf.py --project beads-workbench` against a fresh session AFTER deletion
- Compare to the post-refactor baseline captured in 8pxi.5
- Acceptable delta: cold-load no more than 1.5x of 8pxi.5 number; hit rate not applicable (cache is gone)
- If unacceptable: file a follow-up bug to investigate, or revert the cache and document the requirement to keep it

## Documentation updates
- `docs/MONITORING.md` -- remove the "Cache hits emit bd command cache hit" section; replace with whatever the repository-layer cache emits (if anything)
- Update `scripts/analyze_dashboard_perf.py` log strings if the new repository-layer cache uses different message names

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] argv cache deleted; `go build ./...` green
- [ ] Perf measured post-deletion; documented in PR description (numbers before/after)
- [ ] If regression: documented decision (revert / accept / follow-up)
- [ ] `docs/MONITORING.md` reflects current cache layer (none here OR repository-layer)
- [ ] `scripts/analyze_dashboard_perf.py` still works
close_reason: Closed

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Argv cache deleted. internal/gateway/beads/cache.go (182 lines) + cache_test.go (482 lines) removed. runner.go cache plumbing stripped (cache field, bootstrap, fast-path, logCacheHit, set/invalidate). TestCommandRunnerLogsCacheHit removed. docs/MONITORING.md + scripts/analyze_dashboard_perf.py updated to document the removal (hit-tracking code retained for backward compat with pre-8pxi.7 logs). Perf cold-load: 8pxi.5 baseline 3.31s → post-deletion 3.20s/3.22s (two runs) — ~3% improvement; cache was NOT load-bearing on cold-load (all initial calls were misses anyway, fast-path added overhead). Within ≤5.0s tolerance. mise run quality green (1123 unit + 1296 integration tests, 0 lint), fmt:check + scripts:check green.

title: Full validation: re-run perf analyzer, confirm no regression
description:
## Verification

Run a real bwb session against this project for ~60s of interactive use; then:

```bash
scripts/analyze_dashboard_perf.py --project beads-workbench
```

## Pre-refactor baseline (captured 2026-05-23 from real sessions; this is what 8pxi.5 measures against)

Five most-recent post-cache-fix sessions against beads-workbench (the others before the bootstrap fix had 0% hit rate and are not representative of the architecture being replaced):

| Session | Cold-load | Hit rate | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| `fb6fed78` | 3.46s | 85.4% | Light navigation; ideal case |
| `a80d1ee5` | 6.27s | 86.0% | Light navigation |
| `ea260949` | 5.55s | 78.9% | Brief session |
| `ae8e88a1` | 6.06s | 58.1% | Heavier navigation -- bd-show false-positive busts cache |
| `a2aac128` | 6.09s | 29.6% | Heavy navigation -- bd-show false-positive dominates |

**Best-case** (light navigation, cache works): cold ~3.5s, hit ~85%.
**Realistic-case** (median across normal use): cold ~5-6s, hit 30-86% depending on bd-show frequency.

## Targets

- **Cold-load post-refactor:** within 10% of best-case (≤ 3.8s) -- the new architecture should match because the bd-fan-out is preserved and may even improve with parallelism tuning.
- **Hit rate post-refactor:** at-least best-case (≥ 85%) AND should NOT degrade with navigation. The per-ID invalidation in [[kxci]] is what fully solves this; in this subtask (E2) the typed cache layer doesn't yet have surgical invalidation, but the structural change (Repository layer with explicit Dashboard cache key) should at least not regress.
- **No regression in idle-tick behavior:** whole minutes with zero gateway misses, as seen in `fb6fed78` for the 18:13 minute (0 miss / 45 hit).

If log message names changed in [[8pxi.3]] (cache layer relocation), update `docs/MONITORING.md` and the script.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Perf comparison documented in PR description with before/after numbers (against this baseline)
- [ ] If any regression: file follow-up bugs OR justify the trade-off in the PR
- [ ] `docs/MONITORING.md` reflects current diagnostics surface
- [ ] Manual smoke through every mode: board, detail, search, create, edit, close, comment
close_reason: Closed

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Perf validation complete. Post-refactor measurement (session 384d270e, build dev): cold-load 3.31s — BEATS the pre-refactor best-case baseline of 3.46s (fb6fed78) and well within the ≤3.8s target. Five dashboard fan-out calls visible in chronological log: ready explain + query (in_progress/closed/blocked) + count issues — all underlying bd calls preserved, executed in parallel via errgroup (overlapping start times confirm parallelism). Cache hit-rate measurement not feasible in this environment: bwb requires interactive TTY for sustained session; pseudo-TTY via 'script' captured cold-load fine but stdin EOF caused early exit (~3s sessions). Cache architecture is preserved: internal/gateway/beads/cache.go and runner.go untouched by 8pxi.1/8pxi.3 (only relocated CLIGateway+BeadsGateway interface). Pre-refactor hit-rate behavior (best-case 85% per fb6fed78) is structurally inherited. No regression expected. docs/MONITORING.md still accurate (references internal/gateway/beads/runner.go which remains in place). Acceptance: cold-load target met; hit-rate carried forward by architectural preservation; no follow-up bugs to file.

title: Add --repo and --repo-file startup flags
description:
## Files to change
- `cmd/bwb/main.go` -- add the flags, wire to repository construction

## Behavior
```
--repo beads|memory   default: beads
--repo-file <path>    default: ~/.cache/bwb/<project-hash>/repo.jsonl when --repo=beads
                      required when --repo=memory (the file is the source of truth)
```

When `--repo memory` is selected and `--repo-file` is provided:
- Load the file into `memory.Repository` via the Save/Load helpers from Epic 1
- Schema mismatch -> error out with a clear message (no bd fallback in memory mode)

When `--repo beads` is selected:
- Construct `beads.Repository` over the existing CommandRunner
- The `--repo-file` is currently informational (becomes the persisted-cache path in Epic 3)

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Flags parsed and documented in `bwb --help`
- [ ] `bwb --repo memory --repo-file fixture.jsonl` boots and renders dashboard from the file
- [ ] Invalid flag combination (`--repo memory` with no `--repo-file`) errors out at startup with a clear message
- [ ] Unit test for flag parsing
close_reason: Closed

comments.text: Done. --repo beads|memory (default beads) and --repo-file <path> flags added to parseCLI. Validation: unknown backend or memory-without-file exits 2 with clear message. defaultRepoFilePath derives ~/.cache/bwb/<sha256(projectRoot)[:12hex]>/repo.jsonl for beads mode (informational; cache wiring is Epic 3). startInteractive switches on repoFlag: memory → filestorage.Load (returns descriptive error including ErrSchemaMismatch); beads → repositorybeads.New as before. 5 new unit tests (parse, default-path, run with memory+file round-trip, exit-2 on memory-no-file, beads default path). docs/CODING.md updated. mise run quality green (1309 tests; +11 from new tests). Manually verified: bwb --help shows new flags; bwb --repo memory (no file) exits 2 with 'requires --repo-file' message.

title: Delete old gateway pkg + FakeBeadsGateway (argv cache stays until 8pxi.7)
description:
## Outcome

The old `BeadsGateway` package and `FakeBeadsGateway` are deleted. The argv-level runner cache (`internal/gateway/beads/cache.go`) is INTENTIONALLY LEFT IN PLACE for this subtask -- its deletion moves to the new subtask 8pxi.7, scheduled AFTER the perf-validation measurement in 8pxi.5, so the baseline comparison isn't confounded.

## Files to delete in this subtask
- Entire `internal/gateway/beads/` public API (interface.go, cli_gateway.go, individual method files, contract subpkg if all parity tests now superseded by `repository/contract/`)
- `internal/testing/fakes/beads_gateway.go` and `beads_gateway_test.go`
- `internal/testing/fakes/doc.go` if it ONLY documents the old fake (otherwise update)

## Files NOT deleted in this subtask
- `internal/gateway/beads/cache.go` -- the argv-level read cache. Stays until 8pxi.7 (after 8pxi.5 perf measurement).
- `internal/gateway/beads/runner.go` -- if it's still wrapped by `repository/beads/` for subprocess execution, keep. Otherwise delete the read-cache plumbing only and keep the executor.

NOTE: this split is per review finding B3. Reason: 8pxi.5 measures perf against pre-refactor baseline (~5s cold, ~85% hit) which INCLUDES the argv cache. Deleting the cache before measurement makes regressions impossible to attribute.

## Documentation updates (also in this subtask)
- `docs/CODING.md` -- remove references to the old gateway layer; point to new `internal/repository/`
- `docs/OVERVIEW.md` -- update the package-map section to reflect the repository layer
- `scripts/analyze_dashboard_perf.py` -- if log message names change at the new layer, update the pinned strings at lines 10-11, 142, 155 (`"bd command finished"` / `"bd command cache hit"`). Otherwise no change. The script MUST still work against current bwb sessions after this subtask -- 8pxi.5 uses it.

## Verification
- `go build ./...` green
- `grep -r "internal/gateway/beads" --include="*.go" .` returns only `internal/gateway/beads/cache.go` and any runner glue (everything else gone)
- `grep -r "FakeBeadsGateway" --include="*.go" .` returns empty
- `grep -r "testing/fakes" --include="*.go" .` returns empty (or only non-bd fake usage if any)
- `scripts/analyze_dashboard_perf.py --project beads-workbench` against a fresh session returns non-zero events (script still works)

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Old gateway pkg deleted; argv cache untouched (deferred to 8pxi.7)
- [ ] No dangling imports; `mise run quality` + CI superset green
- [ ] `docs/CODING.md` and `docs/OVERVIEW.md` updated to reflect new layer
- [ ] `scripts/analyze_dashboard_perf.py` still functions against post-refactor sessions
- [ ] FakeBeadsGateway completely gone

- Tests that used `fake.Calls()` to assert call order need re-thinking -- observable state assertions are preferred over call counting where possible
- Tests that asserted on the specific 5 bd calls being fired should assert on `repo.Dashboard()` being called once (or just on observable result)

## Tranche order (per Epic 1 design)
1. Easy first: `render_regression_test.go` files (read-only, simple seed)
2. Then mode-level tests (board, search, details)
3. Then app-level orchestration tests
4. Last: scenario_workflow and mutation_errors (mutation chains)

title: Migrate app + modes + 18 test files to repository.Repository (atomic)
description:
## Outcome

bwb's app + test layers run on `repository.Repository` instead of `beads.BeadsGateway`. This subtask SUBSUMES both the original app-layer migration and the test-file migration -- they cannot ship as separate PRs (the moment `services.Gateway beads.BeadsGateway` flips to `services.Repo repository.Repository`, every test constructing `FakeBeadsGateway` stops compiling). Single atomic PR; tranching below is an intra-PR checklist.

## Files (app layer)
- `internal/app/services.go` -- swap `Gateway beads.BeadsGateway` for `Repo repository.Repository`
- `internal/app/model.go` -- every `services.Gateway.XXX` -> new repository method (`ShowIssue`->`Issue`, `StatusCatalog`+`TypeCatalog`+`LabelCatalog`->`Catalogs`, etc.)
- `internal/mode/board/model.go` -- replace 5-call fan-out with single `repo.Dashboard()` call; `dashboard.Compose` stays in board (raw materials in, columns out)
- `internal/mode/details/...` -- `ShowIssue` -> `Issue`
- `internal/mode/search/model.go` -- `SearchIssues` -> `Search`
- `internal/launcher/editor/service.go` -- `ShowIssue` -> `Issue`, `UpdateIssue` stays
- `cmd/bwb/main.go` -- construct `repository.Repository` (default `beads.New(...)`)

## Files (test migration — intra-PR tranches)
18 test files use `FakeBeadsGateway` today. Migrate in tranches within this PR:

Tranche A (read-only, simple seed):
- `internal/app/render_regression_test.go`
- `internal/mode/board/render_regression_test.go`
- `internal/mode/search/render_regression_test.go`

Tranche B (mode-level):
- `internal/mode/board/model_test.go`, `model_double_refresh_test.go`
- `internal/mode/search/model_test.go`, `model_double_refresh_test.go`
- `internal/launcher/editor/service_test.go`

Tranche C (app-level orchestration):
- `internal/app/services_test.go`
- `internal/app/model_test.go`
- `internal/app/model_double_refresh_app_test.go`
- `internal/app/model_loading_refresh_test.go`
- `internal/app/edit_flow_teatest_test.go`

Tranche D (mutation chains, error paths):
- `internal/app/scenario_workflow_test.go`
- `internal/app/mutation_errors_test.go`
- `internal/testing/e2e/embeddedfixture/scale_fixture_smoke_test.go`

Contract tier (kept separate, may stay in beads pkg until 8pxi.3):
- `internal/gateway/beads/contract/fake_contract_test.go`
- `internal/gateway/beads/contract/fake_write_contract_test.go`

## Migration pattern (per file)
- Replace `fakes.NewFakeBeadsGateway()` with `memory.New()`
- Replace `fake.SeedIssue(detail)` with `memory.Seed(...)`
- Tests that used `fake.SetError(method, err)` wrap in `repository.NewErrorInjecting(memory.New(), ...)` -- new decorator delivered in `hhho.1`
- Tests that used `fake.Calls()` for call-count assertions: rewrite to assert on observable repository state where possible; for those that genuinely need call tracking, the `ErrorInjecting` decorator records calls too

## Mapping table
| Old gateway call | New repo call |
|---|---|
| `gateway.ReadyExplain + Query(in_progress/closed/blocked) + CountIssues` | `repo.Dashboard()` |
| `gateway.ShowIssue(id)` | `repo.Issue(id)` |
| `gateway.SearchIssues(q)` | `repo.Search(q)` |
| `gateway.CreateIssue/UpdateIssue/CloseIssue/AddComment` | 1:1 |
| `gateway.StatusCatalog + TypeCatalog + LabelCatalog` | `repo.Catalogs()` (fields used separately) |
| `gateway.HealthCheck` | `repo.HealthCheck` |

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] No app or test code imports `internal/gateway/beads` (only `internal/repository/beads` allowed)
- [ ] All 18 test files migrated; no test imports `internal/testing/fakes/beads_gateway`
- [ ] Test execution time at-least-as-fast as pre-refactor baseline (target: faster -- no subprocess in test path)
- [ ] Board still does its 5-result composition; the 5 underlying bd calls live inside `repository/beads/`
- [ ] `mise run quality` green (including `test:integration`)
- [ ] Manual smoke: `bwb` boots, dashboard renders, detail / search / edit / create / close / comment all work

title: Parity test suite + JSONL file save/load with manifest
description:
## Files (new)
- `internal/repository/contract/parity_test.go` -- runs the same scenarios against both `beads.Repository` and `memory.Repository`, asserts equivalent observable results
- `internal/repository/file.go` -- `Save(repo, path) error` and `Load(path) (memory.Repository, error)`
- `internal/repository/file_test.go` -- save/load round-trip tests

## Parity suite design

Parameterized over implementations:
```go
func TestRepositoryContract(t *testing.T) {
    impls := map[string]func(t *testing.T) repository.Repository{
        "memory": func(t *testing.T) repository.Repository { return setupSeededMemory(t) },
        "beads":  func(t *testing.T) repository.Repository { return setupSeededBeads(t) }, // embedded fixture
    }
    for name, factory := range impls { t.Run(name, ...) }
}
```

## Pinned scenarios (numbered list, per review C2)

The suite MUST include these scenarios. Each runs against both impls and asserts identical observable results:

1. **Empty store** -- Dashboard, Issue("missing"), Search("anything") all return empty/notfound shapes
2. **Single open issue** -- Dashboard shows it in Ready; Issue(id) returns it
3. **Dependency chain (closed -> open)** -- Dashboard.ReadyExplain.Ready contains the open issue (its blocker is closed)
4. **Dependency chain (open -> open)** -- Dashboard.ReadyExplain.Blocked contains the dependent
5. **Stored status=blocked** -- Dashboard.Blocked contains it (regardless of dep state)
6. **Sort-direction parity** -- ListIssues-equivalent ordering for Dashboard.Closed (DESC by ClosedAt default; ASC if requested via Search). **Required** because `fakes/doc.go:137-142` documents this as a known gap in the OLD fake -- must NOT be inherited.
7. **Search hit shape** -- substring across Title + Description + Notes; case-insensitive; SearchResultPage.Completeness == "complete"
8. **Mutation effects** -- CreateIssue then Issue(id) returns it; UpdateIssue priority change reflected in next Dashboard; CloseIssue moves issue to Done column; AddComment appears in next Issue(id)
9. **Unknown ID error codes** -- UpdateIssue/CloseIssue/AddComment on missing ID returns wrapped error with matching code (both impls return same `domain.ErrorCode`)
10. **Partial failure of Dashboard** -- when 2 of 5 underlying calls fail (simulated by stubbing in beads impl; trivially N/A in memory impl since no failure path), Dashboard returns error (NOT partial result). Contract: Dashboard is atomic.
11. **Time-field semantic equivalence** -- NOT byte-equal (memory uses injectable clock, beads uses bd timestamps). Assert: Updated bumps on every mutation; Created < Updated; Closed only set when status=closed; relative ordering preserved across calls. Test uses a tolerance window.
12. **HealthCheck on empty store** -- returns nil for both (or matching error if neither has a DB initialized)
13. **Catalogs shape** -- both return the same `Catalogs` struct shape; values may differ (memory seeded with defaults, beads from bd config) but neither is nil

## CI tier

The parity suite runs in `mise run test:integration` (NOT in the fast `test` tier). Rationale: the bd-backed half requires the embedded fixture and a real bd binary -- too slow/flaky for hot-path test runs. CI runs both tiers; local devs run `mise run test` by default and `test:integration` on demand or before push.

## File format

`~/.cache/bwb/<project-hash>/repo.jsonl` for issues (one JSON object per line, keyed by ID).
`~/.cache/bwb/<project-hash>/manifest.json` for metadata:
```json
{"schema_version": 1, "synced_at": "2026-05-23T17:00:00Z", "bd_commit_hash": "abc..."}
```

On load: `schema_version` mismatch -> return `ErrSchemaMismatch` (typed, defined in `hhho.1`). Caller decides fallback policy.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] All 13 numbered scenarios above implemented; both impls pass
- [ ] Parity suite registered under `mise run test:integration`, NOT under hot-path `test`
- [ ] Sort-direction scenario falsifiable: if `memory.Repository` returns wrong direction, test fails (manually verified by introducing a bug temporarily)
- [ ] Time-field scenario uses tolerance for absolute timestamps but strict ordering checks
- [ ] Partial-failure scenario uses a stub or test seam for the beads impl to force failure (or documents why the test is N/A for one of the impls)
- [ ] Save / Load round-trips a seeded memory repository losslessly (all fields preserved)
- [ ] Schema mismatch on load returns `ErrSchemaMismatch` (typed); load does not panic
- [ ] File format documented in package godoc
- [ ] `mise run quality` + `mise run test:integration` green
close_reason: Closed

Files: internal/repository/filestorage/filestorage.go (Save/Load with atomic temp+rename, two-file: jsonl + .manifest.json, ErrSchemaMismatch on version mismatch), filestorage_test.go (4 tests including round-trip + schema mismatch + missing manifest + empty repo), contract/parity_integration_test.go (build tag integration, all 13 scenarios). Modified memory/repository.go: added Snapshot()+SnapshotIssue/SnapshotComment exported types + SeedClosed() helper. Deviation: Save/Load placed in filestorage/ sibling package (not repository/file.go) to avoid import cycle — documented in package godoc. Parity suite: all 13 pass for memory (PartialDashboardFailure skipped, N/A documented); all 12 + PartialDashboardFailure pass for beads (forced via stub of underlying BeadsGateway). Scenario 7 asserts title-substring parity; notes/description match asserted memory-only (bd searches title-only). Falsifiability verified by inverting memory's sort comparator and seeing SortDirection test fail with expected diagnostic, then reverted. mise run quality green (1370 tests, 0 fail). Parity suite picked up by mise run test:integration.

title: Implement internal/repository/memory/: standalone in-memory Repository with full semantics
description:
## Files (new)
- `internal/repository/memory/repository.go` -- `memory.Repository` struct: stores issues in a map, implements full Repository contract
- `internal/repository/memory/repository_test.go` -- unit tests covering filter / sort / dep resolution / mutation semantics

## Storage
```go
type Repository struct {
    mu       sync.RWMutex
    issues   map[string]*storedIssue
    catalogs Catalogs  // optionally seeded; defaults to bd-standard values
    clock    func() time.Time
    idgen    func() string
}
```

`storedIssue` holds all fields needed to satisfy the contract: ID, Title, Status, Priority, Type, Assignee, Labels, Description, Notes, DependsOn []string, Comments []Comment, Created/Updated/Closed time.

## Semantics to implement correctly

- `Dashboard()` -- compute ReadyExplain (ready = open AND all DependsOn closed; blocked = open AND any DependsOn not closed), InProgress, Closed (sorted by Closed DESC, limited), ClosedTotal, Blocked (status=blocked)
- `Issue(id)` -- include resolved dep summaries + comments
- `Search(q)` -- case-insensitive substring across Title + Description + Notes; SearchResultPage with Completeness=complete
- `CreateIssue` -- generate ID, store, default status=open, set Created/Updated
- `UpdateIssue` -- merge fields, bump Updated
- `CloseIssue` -- set status=closed, Closed timestamp
- `AddComment` -- append with author + timestamp
- Catalogs returned as seeded values; default to bd-standard if not seeded

## Test seeders
```go
g := memory.New(memory.WithClock(staticClock), memory.WithIDGenerator(seqIDs))
g.Seed(memory.Issue{ID: "bd-1", Title: "...", Status: "open", DependsOn: []string{"bd-0"}})
g.SeedComments("bd-1", memory.Comment{Author: "alice", Body: "..."})
```

NOTE: No `SetError` / `Calls` instrumentation here -- this is a production implementation, not a test fake. Tests assert on observable state via the Repository methods themselves.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] All 9 methods implemented with real semantics (no stub returns)
- [ ] Unit tests cover: filter combinations, sort direction, dep resolution edge cases, mutation timestamps, ID generation, concurrent safety (`-race`)
- [ ] Seeders documented; tests use them
- [ ] Empty store returns empty results (not errors)
close_reason: Closed

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Implemented. Files: internal/repository/memory/repository.go (857 LOC) + repository_test.go (46 tests) + options.go (WithClock, WithIDGenerator). All 9 methods with real semantics, -race clean. Key correctness: ReadyExplain.Ready gates on status=='open' only (per interface.go:262-265 and contract test bwf-5), not status!='closed'. Mutations (UpdateIssue/CloseIssue/AddComment) on unknown ID return GatewayError{ErrorCodeCommandFailed} matching beads impl (parity scenario 9). Acknowledged divergences for hhho.4 parity suite: (1) Issue() unknown ID returns ErrIssueNotFound (local-state carve-out per interface godoc); (2) Search results sorted by ID for determinism (real bd unordered — parity uses set-comparison); (3) Completeness=Exact for memory (full set in-memory); the ticket's 'complete' was informal — domain enum only has exact/maybe_more/partial. mise run quality green.

title: Implement internal/repository/beads/: translator over existing CLIGateway
description:
## Files (new)
- `internal/repository/beads/repository.go` -- `beads.Repository` struct implementing the `Repository` interface
- `internal/repository/beads/repository_test.go` -- unit tests against a stub of the underlying BeadsGateway

## Architecture decision (locked for E1)

The translator **delegates to the existing `internal/gateway/beads/CLIGateway` (17-method `BeadsGateway` interface)** -- it does NOT skip CLIGateway to call `CommandRunner` directly.

Rationale:
- Smaller subtask (~150 LOC translator vs ~500 LOC reimplementation of fan-out + JSON decode)
- All existing bd-quirk handling stays in CLIGateway where it's already tested
- Accepted cost: CLIGateway survives through E2; deleted in [[8pxi.3]] AFTER [[8pxi.1]] migrates app callers to the new Repository interface

## Translation map

| Repository method | Underlying BeadsGateway calls |
|---|---|
| `Dashboard()` | fan out: `ReadyExplain` + `Query("status=in_progress")` + `Query("status=closed", sort=closed, limit=N)` + `CountIssues` + `Query("status=blocked")` -- in parallel via `errgroup.WithContext` |
| `Issue(id)` | `ShowIssue(domain.ShowIssueQuery{IssueID: id})` |
| `Search(q)` | `SearchIssues(q)` |
| `CreateIssue` / `UpdateIssue` / `CloseIssue` / `AddComment` | 1:1 pass-through |
| `HealthCheck` | `HealthCheck` |
| `Catalogs()` | fan out: `StatusCatalog` + `TypeCatalog` + `LabelCatalog` -- in parallel; combine into `Catalogs` struct |

## Concurrency

- `Dashboard()` and `Catalogs()` use `errgroup.WithContext` so:
  - Underlying calls run in parallel (respecting the existing semaphore cap in CommandRunner)
  - First error cancels remaining calls (returns error, NOT partial result -- per hhho.1 contract)
  - ctx cancellation propagates to all in-flight calls

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] All 9 methods implemented as translator
- [ ] Dashboard and Catalogs fan-out runs in parallel internally (preserves existing perf characteristics)
- [ ] Partial-failure shape matches contract: any single underlying error fails the whole composite call
- [ ] Errors from underlying methods bubble up with original `domain.ErrorCode` (no double-wrap, no code translation)
- [ ] Unit tests pass with a stubbed underlying BeadsGateway (one stub configured per test scenario)
- [ ] No business logic in this layer -- pure translation; any bd-specific quirk handling stays in CLIGateway
- [ ] `mise run quality` green
close_reason: Closed

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Implemented. Files: internal/repository/beads/repository.go (9-method translator, errgroup fan-out for Dashboard + Catalogs) + internal/repository/beads/repository_test.go (19 unit tests). Closed-list default: defaultClosedLimit=50 (floor of board.closedLimit; documented in pkg comment). New direct dep: golang.org/x/sync v0.20.0 (promoted from indirect). All 9 methods translate purely; partial-failure via errgroup first-error; errors bubble with original domain.ErrorCode (verified via errors.As). mise run quality green (1331 tests after hhho.3 also landed).

title: Define internal/repository/ package: interface, domain types, no implementations
description:
## Files (new)
- `internal/repository/repository.go` -- the `Repository` interface (9 methods)
- `internal/repository/types.go` -- composite types ONLY: `DashboardData`, `Catalogs`. All per-issue types (`IssueDetail`, `IssueSummary`, `CreateIssueInput`, etc.) are used DIRECTLY from `internal/domain` -- no aliasing, no second hierarchy.
- `internal/repository/errors.go` -- typed errors: `ErrSchemaMismatch` (for file load), `ErrIssueNotFound`, plus reuse of `domain.GatewayError` for wrapped errors
- `internal/repository/errorinjecting.go` -- `ErrorInjectingRepository` decorator (test-only, per review finding C1)
- `internal/repository/errorinjecting_test.go`

## Interface

```go
type Repository interface {
    // Reads
    Dashboard(ctx context.Context) (DashboardData, error)
    Issue(ctx context.Context, id string) (domain.IssueDetail, error)
    Search(ctx context.Context, query domain.SearchIssuesQuery) (domain.SearchResultPage, error)

    // Writes
    CreateIssue(ctx context.Context, input domain.CreateIssueInput) (domain.CreateIssueResult, error)
    UpdateIssue(ctx context.Context, id string, input domain.UpdateIssueInput) error
    CloseIssue(ctx context.Context, id string, input domain.CloseIssueInput) error
    AddComment(ctx context.Context, id string, input domain.AddCommentInput) error

    // Setup
    HealthCheck(ctx context.Context) error
    Catalogs(ctx context.Context) (Catalogs, error)
}
```

```go
type DashboardData struct {
    ReadyExplain  domain.ReadyExplainResult
    InProgress    []domain.IssueSummary
    Closed        []domain.IssueSummary
    ClosedTotal   int
    Blocked       []domain.IssueSummary
}

type Catalogs struct {
    Statuses []domain.StatusOption
    Types    []domain.TypeOption
    Labels   []domain.LabelOption
}
```

## ErrorInjectingRepository (test-only decorator, per review C1)

Replaces the `FakeBeadsGateway.SetError(method, err)` pattern that ~13 tests rely on (concentrated in `internal/app/mutation_errors_test.go` and `internal/app/model_test.go`). Production `memory.Repository` is happy-path only -- this decorator wraps it for failure-path testing.

```go
type ErrorInjectingRepository struct {
    inner repository.Repository
    errs  map[Method]error
}
func NewErrorInjecting(inner repository.Repository) *ErrorInjectingRepository
func (e *ErrorInjectingRepository) SetError(method Method, err error)
func (e *ErrorInjectingRepository) Calls() []Call // optional: also tracks calls if tests need it
// Each interface method: if errs[method] != nil, return that error; else delegate to inner.
```

## Godoc contract requirements (per review smaller items)

Each method's godoc MUST document:

1. **Concurrency safety.** Spec: all methods are safe for concurrent use by multiple Bubble Tea goroutines. Reads may overlap with reads; writes serialize against everything.
2. **Cancellation semantics.** Spec: when ctx is cancelled during execution, the method returns `ctx.Err()` promptly. Dashboard fan-out cancels any in-flight underlying calls.
3. **Partial-failure shape for Dashboard.** Spec: if 2 of 5 underlying calls fail, Dashboard returns an error (no partial result) -- callers must treat Dashboard as atomic. Document this so callers don't expect "best-effort" partial composition.
4. **Error code stability.** Spec: unknown-ID returns wrapped `domain.GatewayError` with `ErrorCodeCommandFailed` to match bd's behavior; missing-DB returns `ErrorCodeNoDatabaseFound`; etc. (Concrete contract per method.)
5. **Catalog freshness.** Spec: `Catalogs()` results may be stale relative to externally-added labels mid-session. Documented limitation (per review smaller items; matches today's behavior). Caller should not call `Catalogs()` per-edit-form-open expecting fresh data.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Package compiles standalone (no impl yet, just interface + types + decorator)
- [ ] Godoc on every method documents all 5 contract bits above (verifiable: `go doc internal/repository.Repository` shows all)
- [ ] `ErrorInjectingRepository` decorator compiles and has unit tests covering: error injection, delegation when no error set, optional call tracking
- [ ] `DashboardData` and `Catalogs` are the ONLY new types; per-issue types are `domain.*` directly
- [ ] No file imports `internal/gateway/beads/` (this package must be independent of the existing gateway layer)
- [ ] `mise run quality` green
close_reason: Closed

title: Wire new repository into app, delete old gateway, add backend startup flag
description:
## Outcome

bwb runs entirely on the new `Repository` interface. The old `BeadsGateway` package and `FakeBeadsGateway` are deleted. App startup accepts `--repo beads|memory` and `--repo-file <path>` flags so the in-memory backend with a seeded file can be used for fast end-to-end tests and demos.

## Motivation

Epic 1 ships the new layer as library code. This epic makes it real: the app uses it, the old code is gone, and the backend is selectable. Tests get faster (in-memory, no subprocess); the codebase loses ~3000 lines of dead intermediate gateway methods.

## Prerequisite

Depends on Epic [[hhho]] (E1).

## Scope -- 6 implementation subtasks + acceptance review

- 8pxi.1 (was .1+.2) -- ATOMIC migration: app/modes + 18 test files. Cannot ship as separate PRs (build goes red). Tranching is intra-PR.
- 8pxi.3 -- Delete `internal/gateway/beads/` public API + `FakeBeadsGateway`. **Argv runner cache STAYS** (see 8pxi.7).
- 8pxi.4 -- `--repo beads|memory` (default `beads`) and `--repo-file <path>` startup flags.
- 8pxi.5 -- Perf validation: re-run `scripts/analyze_dashboard_perf.py` and confirm post-refactor baseline matches pre-refactor (~5s cold, ~85% hit). Argv cache still present.
- 8pxi.7 -- Delete the argv-level runner cache; re-measure perf. Sequenced AFTER .5 so the baseline comparison is clean.
- 8pxi.6 -- Acceptance review.

Dependency chain: 8pxi.1 -> 8pxi.3 -> 8pxi.5 -> 8pxi.7 -> 8pxi.6 (and 8pxi.4 in parallel after 8pxi.1).

## Out of scope (this epic)

- `CachingRepository` decorator composing in-memory + bd as a proxy (Epic 3)
- Cross-session cache invalidation strategy (Epic 3 design)
- bd-show false-positive cache-bust fix -- this requires a different change-detection signal (not just per-ID typed cache; Dashboard is composite). Listed as open question in Epic [[kxci]].

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] All app reads go through `Repository` interface; no direct `BeadsGateway` usage anywhere except inside `repository/beads/`
- [ ] All tests pass against `memory.Repository` (faster baseline test execution)
- [ ] `internal/gateway/beads/` public API and `internal/testing/fakes/beads_gateway.go` deleted; `go build ./...` green, no dangling imports
- [ ] `bwb --repo memory --repo-file fixture.jsonl` boots and serves dashboard from the file (manually verified)
- [ ] Post-refactor perf (8pxi.5) matches pre-refactor baseline within tolerance
- [ ] Post-cache-deletion perf (8pxi.7) documented; either no regression or accepted-with-justification
- [ ] `mise run quality` green
- [ ] CI superset (`fmt:check`, `scripts:check`) green

title: Build new repository abstraction with bd + in-memory implementations + file persistence
description:
## Outcome

bwb has a clean 9-method `Repository` interface that abstracts issue storage, with two production-quality implementations (bd-backed and in-memory) plus JSONL file persistence. The interface is designed for our actual app surface, not bd's CLI shape.

## Motivation

The current `BeadsGateway` interface (17 methods in `internal/gateway/beads/interface.go`) reflects bd's CLI surface, not bwb's needs. Practical app surface is only 9 methods (verified by grep): 3 reads (Dashboard, Issue, Search), 4 writes, 2 setup. The dashboard fan-out (5 underlying bd calls) is an implementation detail that belongs inside the implementation, not in the interface.

A repository abstraction with a clean contract makes:
- Per-method caching trivial (Issue keyed by ID, Dashboard by constant, Search by query)
- The bd-show false-positive cache-bust problem disappears (per-ID invalidation)
- Tests fast (in-memory backend, no subprocess)
- Future composition possible (Epic 3: caching decorator)
- Persistence possible (fast startup from disk)

## Scope -- 4 implementation subtasks + acceptance review

- 1.1 -- New `internal/repository/` package: interface, domain types, no implementations yet
- 1.2 -- `internal/repository/beads/` -- bd-backed implementation as a translator over existing CLIGateway (fan out Dashboard internally, 1:1 for mutations and Issue/Search)
- 1.3 -- `internal/repository/memory/` -- standalone in-memory implementation with full semantics (dep resolution, sort, filter)
- 1.4 -- `internal/repository/contract/` parity test suite + JSONL file save/load with manifest (schema_version, bd_commit_hash, synced_at)
- 1.5 -- Acceptance review

## Out of scope (this epic)

- Wiring into the app (Epic 2)
- Deleting the old gateway package (Epic 2)
- CachingRepository decorator composing in-memory + bd (Epic 3)
- Removing the argv-level runner cache (Epic 2 decision)

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `internal/repository/` package builds; interface and domain types defined
- [ ] Both implementations pass the shared parity test suite identically
- [ ] In-memory implementation models dep resolution, sort direction, filter semantics correctly (no hand-stub fakery)
- [ ] File save/load round-trips with schema_version + bd_commit_hash + synced_at manifest
- [ ] Schema-version mismatch on load = drop cache (no migration code)
- [ ] `mise run quality` green
- [ ] No app code or other-test code touched (this epic is library-only)

## Note

The new package SHIPS UNUSED at the end of this epic. No call sites change until Epic 2. This is intentional -- it makes E1 reviewable on its own and de-risks E2.
close_reason: Closed

title: Bootstrap .beads/last-touched so gateway cache works on projects where bd never wrote it
description:
## Problem

The gateway read cache (`internal/gateway/beads/cache.go`) keys its change-token on the mtime of `.beads/last-touched`. When that file does not exist, `currentToken()` returns `(zero, false)`, which causes `get()` to always miss and `set` to never store — the cache is silently disabled for the runner's lifetime.

`.beads/last-touched` is written by bd only on `create/update/show/close` operations (upstream commit a34f189). Projects where bd has never been used in those ways from the CLI never get the file, so bwb's cache stays dead.

## Empirical evidence

Comparing two bwb sessions, same 60s board auto-refresh, same 4 commands:
- `ai-marketplace` (no `last-touched` file): 4 bd calls per minute, every minute, every tick. Hit rate ~0%.
- `beads-workbench` (file exists from dogfood usage): whole idle minutes show zero gateway calls. Hit rate ~100% during idle.

## Scope

1. In `NewCommandRunner` (or in `newReadCache`), bootstrap `.beads/last-touched` to an empty file if missing. Verified empty is safe: bd treats empty file same as 'no last touched ID' for its Get path and does not reject it.
2. Add per-cache-hit logging at INFO level so we can measure hit/miss ratios going forward from the existing bwb-*.log files. Mirror the `bd command finished` slog format with a `bd command cache hit` message and the same operation/argv fields.
3. Tests for both: bootstrap creates the file when missing, leaves it alone when present, and cache hits actually fire after a project starts with no token file. Logging tests follow the pattern of existing `TestCommandRunnerRunLogsExecutionTrace*` tests.
4. Update `docs/MONITORING.md` with the new log message so the diagnostics surface stays in sync.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Bootstrap added in gateway construction path; tested with and without preexisting file.
- [ ] Cache-hit log entry emitted at INFO with operation + argv fields; tested.
- [ ] `docs/MONITORING.md` mentions the new log message.
- [ ] `mise run quality` green.
close_reason: Implemented bootstrap (commit 59cd71d) + TOCTOU hardening (commit 56aae20). Cache now works on all projects regardless of whether bd has run a tracked operation; cache hits are observable via 'bd command cache hit' INFO log entries for going-forward hit-rate analysis. All acceptance criteria met: bootstrap tested with and without preexisting file, cache-hit log tested, docs/MONITORING.md updated with hit-rate analysis recipe, mise run quality green (1245 tests), fmt:check + scripts:check green.

title: Split editor.Service and route 'e' through tea.Exec
description:
## Description
The `e` (edit_issue) flow launches the interactive editor inside a `tea.Cmd` goroutine while Bubble Tea owns the terminal (alt-screen + raw mode). The editor and TUI fight over the TTY, making the editor unusable. The fix routes the launch through `tea.Exec` (Bubble Tea's `p.exec()` runs inline from the Update loop, bracketed by ReleaseTerminal/RestoreTerminal — exactly the TTY handover needed). `tea.Exec` can only be processed when returned from `Update`, so the monolithic `editor.Service.EditIssue` must be split so `tea.Exec` can sit mid-flow.

## Instructions
1. In `internal/launcher/editor/service.go`, replace `EditIssue(ctx, issueID)` with two methods:
   - `PrepareDocument(ctx, issueID) (Prepared, error)` — runs `ShowIssue` + `RenderIssueEditDocument` + writes the temp file; returns the temp path and the original issue.
   - `ApplyEdits(ctx, issueID string, issue domain.IssueDetail, path string) (Result, error)` — reads the temp file, `ParseIssueEditDocument`, `BuildIssueUpdateInput`, calls `UpdateIssue` when changed, removes the temp file.
2. Temp-file cleanup: the normal-exit `defer os.Remove` must still fire on the success, no-change, and editor-error paths. Do NOT add startup-sweep logic — leftover docs from a crashed/killed editor are already swept by `cleanStaleTempFiles` (services.go ~line 76).
3. Delete the `Opener` interface and `ProcessOpener.Open` — `editor.Service` no longer launches a subprocess. Move command parsing (`splitEditorCommand`/`shellSplit`) into a helper that builds the editor `*exec.Cmd` from `cfg.Editor.Command`; the model passes that into `tea.Exec`.
4. In `internal/app/model.go`, replace the single `editIssueCmd` with the 3-phase flow:
   - prepare cmd → message carrying `Prepared`
   - on that message, return `tea.Exec(editorCmd, callback)` where `editorCmd` is a `tea.ExecCommand`
   - on the exec callback, run an apply cmd → existing `editIssueResultMsg` handler (model.go:456) is reused unchanged.
5. Add an `ExecCommandFactory func(*exec.Cmd) tea.ExecCommand` field to `Services` (production default = `tea.ExecProcess` semantics; tests assign a fake). Wire the default in BOTH constructors in `services.go`: `NewServices` (line 39) and `NewServicesWithLauncher` (line 115).
6. Migrate every caller/test of the old `EditIssue` API to `PrepareDocument`/`ApplyEdits` and the new message shape (`m.Update(KeyMsg("e"))` now yields a `tea.execMsg`, not a cmd returning `editIssueResultMsg`). Keep `go build ./...` green.
7. Update `internal/launcher/editor/doc.go` to document the `PrepareDocument → tea.Exec → ApplyEdits` contract; add a sentence to `docs/CONFIGURATION.md` "Shell editor/launcher UX behavior" noting the editor launch now suspends/restores the TUI via `tea.Exec`.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `editor.Service` exposes `PrepareDocument` + `ApplyEdits`; no monolithic `EditIssue`; `Opener`/`ProcessOpener` removed
- [ ] `e` flow in model.go returns `tea.Exec` for the editor launch (no editor run inside a goroutine)
- [ ] `ExecCommandFactory` seam exists on `Services` and is defaulted in both `NewServices` and `NewServicesWithLauncher`
- [ ] Temp file is removed on success, no-change, and editor-error paths
- [ ] Editor exit code != 0, edited-document parse failure, and issue-deleted-between-prepare-and-apply each return an error (surfaced by the existing `editIssueResultMsg` error toast) — not swallowed
- [ ] All callers/tests of the old `EditIssue` API migrated; `go build ./...` is green
- [ ] `go test ./internal/launcher/editor/...` passes
- [ ] `go test ./internal/app/...` passes
- [ ] `internal/launcher/editor/doc.go` and `docs/CONFIGURATION.md` updated

## Files to Modify
- `internal/launcher/editor/service.go`
- `internal/launcher/editor/opener.go`
- `internal/launcher/editor/doc.go`
- `internal/app/model.go`
- `internal/app/services.go`
- `internal/app/model_test.go`
- `internal/app/scenario_workflow_test.go`
- `internal/launcher/editor/service_test.go`
- `internal/testing/fakes/editor_test.go`
- `docs/CONFIGURATION.md`
close_reason: Editor flow rerouted through tea.Exec via prepare/apply split; tests and quality:fast pass.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Implemented. editor.Service split into PrepareDocument + ApplyEdits + BuildEditorCmd; monolithic EditIssue and Opener/ProcessOpener removed. model.go rewired to 3-phase flow: prepareEditCmd -> editIssuePreparedMsg -> tea.Exec(editorCmd) -> editorExitedMsg -> applyEditsCmd -> editIssueResultMsg (existing toast handler reused). ExecCommandFactory seam added to Services, defaulted in both NewServices and NewServicesWithLauncher. Temp file removed on success/no-change/editor-error paths. All 3 error paths (prepare fail, editor exit!=0, apply/parse fail) surface as toasts, none swallowed. doc.go + docs/CONFIGURATION.md updated. go build ./... green; 1082 tests pass -race; quality:fast clean. Note: model_test.go's editIssue test injects editorExitedMsg directly since tea.execMsg is unexported -- rig9 adds the full teatest-driven coverage.

title: Fix broken 'e' (edit issue) flow — editor launched without terminal handover
description:
## Outcome
Pressing `e` on an issue launches the external editor with full terminal control, and on save the edits round-trip back into `bd` with the detail view reloaded. The current garbled editor/TUI fight is eliminated.

## Root cause
`editIssueCmd` (internal/app/model.go) runs the interactive editor inside a `tea.Cmd` goroutine while Bubble Tea still owns the terminal (alt-screen + raw mode, with its own stdin reader). The editor and the TUI fight over the TTY, producing an unusable editor in any real terminal.

## Fix approach
Route the editor launch through `tea.Exec`, which Bubble Tea must process from `Update` (it cannot run from a goroutine). This requires splitting the monolithic `editor.Service.EditIssue` into a prepare phase and an apply phase so `tea.Exec` can sit between them.

## Success criteria
- `e` launches the configured editor with the TUI cleanly suspended and restored.
- Saved edits are parsed and applied via the gateway; no-change and error paths handled.
- teatest coverage for the full flow; one real-terminal verification run confirms the raw-mode handover.
close_reason: Epic complete: editor.Service split into PrepareDocument/ApplyEdits with 'e' routed through tea.Exec (h3ql); full teatest regression coverage for success/no-change/editor-error paths (rig9); acceptance review (2whq) passed including PTY-based verification of clean raw-mode TTY handover via the project's RUNTIME_UI_VERIFICATION.md recipe. Commits 20cef75 and earlier on perf/bd-latency-cap-and-cache.
