title: Gateway read cache keyed on argv, invalidated by .beads/last-touched token + singleflight
description:
## Description
99% of logged gateway calls were byte-identical repeats (session 8c657fd1: 1063 calls, 7 distinct argv) -- the board re-runs the same queries every 60s even when the DB is unchanged. Add an in-process read cache so unchanged-DB reads don't spawn bd.

## Instructions
1. Add a read-result cache to the runner (or a thin same-package wrapper) keyed on resolved argv. Guard all cache state with its own mutex -- it is accessed concurrently by parallel RLock-held reads.
2. Change-token: stat mtime of .beads/last-touched (bd maintains this file; verified it advances on writes -- embeddeddolt dir mtime does NOT, so do not use it). Resolve the .beads dir from the runner's bound WorkDir.
3. On a read request: compute the current token; if a cache entry exists with a matching token, return cached stdout without spawning bd; otherwise exec, then store {token, stdout}.
4. Invalidate on writes: any IsWrite=true request clears/stamps the cache so the next read re-execs. External writes are caught by the token advancing.
5. Add per-argv singleflight (golang.org/x/sync/singleflight, already in go.mod) so identical concurrent argv collapse to one exec. The board's 4 refresh commands are distinct argv -- singleflight does not merge them; that burst is bounded by wcgy.1.
6. Only cache successful reads (exit 0). Do not cache failures or write requests.
7. Update docs/MONITORING.md if the diagnostics/contract surface changes.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] A repeat read with an unchanged .beads/last-touched token returns from cache without spawning bd (counting-executor test)
- [ ] A write request, or an advanced token, invalidates the cache so the next read re-execs
- [ ] Identical concurrent argv collapse to a single exec via singleflight (test)
- [ ] Only exit-0 reads are cached; failures and writes are not
- [ ] Cache access is data-race free (go test -race passes)
- [ ] go test ./internal/gateway/beads/... passes; quality:fast gate passes

## Files to Modify
- internal/gateway/beads/runner.go (or a new caching file in the package)
- internal/gateway/beads/runner_test.go
- docs/MONITORING.md (if contract changes)
close_reason: Read cache + singleflight implemented and tested; quality:fast and race tests pass.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Implemented. New cache.go: readCache keyed on resolved argv, token = mtime of .beads/last-touched (resolved from runner WorkDir). Cache hit returns stdout before acquiring runMu/semaphore. Invalidation: external writes caught by token advance; IsWrite=true calls invalidate() under runMu.Lock. Only exit-0 reads cached. Per-argv singleflight via golang.org/x/sync/singleflight (promoted indirect->direct, v0.17->v0.20). 8 new tests in cache_test.go cover all AC; race-clean. docs/MONITORING.md updated: cache hits emit no 'bd command finished' record. 1078 tests pass; quality:fast clean.

title: Cap concurrent bd subprocesses with a bounded semaphore
description:
## Description
Reads through the bd gateway runner execute fully concurrently and uncapped (shared RLock in internal/gateway/beads/runner.go). A board auto-refresh fans out 4 bd subprocesses at once; each cold-opens the embedded Dolt DB and they contend super-linearly -- solo bd measured ~0.28s but contended calls were 1.25s median / 6.1s max across 2578 logged calls.

## Instructions
1. Add a bounded concurrency limiter to CommandRunner -- a buffered-channel semaphore (small constant capacity 2-3; document the choice in a comment).
2. Acquire a slot around the executor.Run call in CommandRunner.Run, released on completion (defer). Applies to all subprocess execs; the existing runMu RWMutex stays as-is.
3. Context cancellation/timeout while waiting for a slot must return promptly without executing -- select on ctx.Done() alongside the semaphore acquire, and return the normalized timeout/cancel error.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Concurrent bd subprocesses are bounded; a burst of N>cap reads never runs more than cap execs at once
- [ ] A runner test asserts the concurrency cap under a parallel burst
- [ ] Context cancellation while waiting for a slot returns promptly without executing
- [ ] go test ./internal/gateway/beads/... passes; quality:fast gate passes

## Files to Modify
- internal/gateway/beads/runner.go
- internal/gateway/beads/runner_test.go
close_reason: Semaphore cap implemented and tested; quality:fast passes.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Implemented. Bounded semaphore cap=2 added to CommandRunner in runner.go; acquire/release wraps executor.Run inside existing RWMutex blocks; context cancellation returns promptly via select. Two new tests: TestConcurrencyCapBurst (asserts peak <= cap under 10-goroutine burst) and TestConcurrencyCapContextCancelWhileWaiting (asserts cancelled waiter returns immediately without executing). TestRWMutexParallelReadOverlap updated to 2xcap goroutines. All 1070 tests pass; quality:fast gate clean.

title: bd subprocess latency: cap concurrency + cache reads on DB change-token
description:
## Outcome
bd CLI calls made by the workbench stop contending under burst and stop re-running when the beads DB is unchanged. The >4s tail latency is eliminated and steady-state board refreshes become near-instant cache hits.

## Root cause (monitoring analysis)
project-analyze-monitoring-data over a ~24h window of ~/.local/state/bwb/bwb-*.log:
- Solo 'bd count' measures ~0.28s (process spawn + cold-open of embedded Dolt).
- Board auto-refresh (internal/app/model.go:32, refreshTickInterval=60s) fans out 4 bd processes per tick in one tea.Batch (internal/mode/board/model.go:357, pendingResults=4).
- The gateway runner runs reads fully concurrently and uncapped (internal/gateway/beads/runner.go:164, shared RLock).
- 4 cold Dolt opens contend super-linearly: median bd call 1.25s, p95 2.2s, max 6.1s across 2578 logged calls.
- 99% waste: session 8c657fd1 made 1063 gateway calls but only 7 distinct argv -- nearly every refresh returned byte-identical data.
- Zero real operational failures in the window; all log ERRORs were test-suite negative cases. Latency is the only genuine finding.

## Scope -- 2 implementation tasks, single PR
- wcgy.1 -- Cap concurrent bd subprocesses with a bounded semaphore in runner.go. Eliminates the 4-6s contention tail.
- wcgy.2 -- In-process gateway read cache keyed on resolved argv, invalidated by a DB change-token (stat mtime of .beads/last-touched), plus per-argv singleflight. Unchanged token -> cached bytes, no bd spawn; covers board/detail/search read surfaces.

The 60s auto-refresh tick is RETAINED -- it becomes the cheap trigger that runs the token check to catch external agent writes.

## Out of scope
Collapsing the 4 board refresh commands to fewer was considered (originally "#3") and DROPPED. 'bd query status=closed --json --limit N' returns a bare JSON array with no total-count field, so the closed-count is not derivable from a capped result set. The separate 'bd count --by-status closed' call is load-bearing -- it is the fix for closed issue beads-workbench-ssom (Done column must show the real DB count, not the capped slice size). The batch stays at 4 commands; the win comes from caching, not from cutting commands.

See NOTES for the empirical-verification audit trail (token confirmation, #3 drop rationale).
acceptance_criteria:
- Concurrent bd subprocesses are bounded by a small semaphore (size 2-3); the >4s tail latency is eliminated under burst.
- Repeat reads with an unchanged .beads/last-touched token return from cache without spawning bd; cache keyed on resolved argv.
- A bd write (IsWrite=true) and any external DB change (last-touched mtime advance) invalidate the cache so stale data is never shown.
- Identical concurrent argv collapse via singleflight to a single bd exec.
- Board still issues 4 bd commands on a real refresh (the separate count call is required by beads-workbench-ssom); the win is from caching, not from cutting commands.
- Auto-refresh still reflects concurrent agent writes within one tick.
- docs/MONITORING.md updated if the diagnostics contract changes.
notes:
monitoring-derived; see analysis
SCOPE CORRECTION after empirical checks:

- #3 DROPPED. 'bd query status=closed --json --limit 56' returns a bare JSON array with no top-level total/count field. The closed-count is NOT derivable in-process from a capped result set. The separate 'bd count --by-status closed' call is load-bearing -- it is the fix for closed issue beads-workbench-ssom (Done column must show real DB count, not capped slice size). Collapsing 4->2 would regress ssom. The 4-call batch stays at 4.

- #2 token CONFIRMED: stat mtime of .beads/last-touched. Verified it advances on bd writes (mtime 20:35:32 -> 20:37:01 after a 'bd update'); .beads/embeddeddolt dir mtime does NOT advance, so it is unusable as a token. Cache token = last-touched mtime. Belt-and-suspenders: bwb-initiated writes (IsWrite=true) also bust the cache directly, so even a write type that somehow skipped last-touched is still covered for our own writes; external writes are caught by the file mtime.

- #2 add per-argv singleflight (golang.org/x/sync/singleflight) so identical concurrent argv (e.g. board + detail both requesting the same 'bd show X') collapse to one exec. Note: the board's 4 refresh commands are 4 DISTINCT argv, so singleflight does not collapse them -- bounding that burst is #1's job (semaphore).

- 60s tick is RETAINED: it becomes the trigger that runs the cheap token stat to catch external agent writes.

REVISED SCOPE = #1 (semaphore cap) + #2 (argv cache keyed on last-touched token, + singleflight). #3 removed.
close_reason: Epic complete: concurrency cap + read cache delivered, acceptance review gate passed.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Acceptance review passed. bd contention cap (semaphore=2) and argv read cache (token = .beads/last-touched mtime, singleflight dedup) implemented and verified. Board retains 4-command refresh; ssom closed-count call intact. docs/MONITORING.md updated. 1078 tests pass; race-clean. x/sync promoted indirect->direct v0.17->v0.20, no API change.

title: Default nvim launcher embeds untrusted issue fields unescaped into a vimscript +call Ex-command (code-injection vector, parallels db0z.1)
description:
What I did: Verified the default launcher config (bwb --print-config) and the launcher interpolation code against the shell-launcher security rule in docs/CODING.md (Core Architectural Rule 6).

Expected (source: docs/CODING.md rule 6): Issue fields (title, assignee, labels) are 'operator-untrusted input'; they must not be embedded where they can be re-parsed as code. The repo already enforces this for the sh launcher (positional args $0-$3, fixed in beads-workbench-db0z.1).

Actual: The DEFAULT 'nvim' launcher definition (internal/config/model.go defaultLauncherDefinitions) ships this arg:
  +call append(0, ["Issue: {{issue.id}}", "Title: {{issue.title}}", "Assignee: {{issue.assignee}}", "Labels: {{issue.labels}}"])
This whole string is passed to nvim as a '+{command}' argument, which nvim executes as an Ex command (vimscript). {{issue.title}} (and assignee/labels) are interpolated UNESCAPED: internal/launcher/service.go:113-125 Interpolate() does strings.ReplaceAll with only stripC0() applied — stripC0 removes C0 control chars but does NOT escape double-quotes, brackets, or pipes.

Consequence: an issue title such as
  x"]) | call writefile(['pwned'],'/tmp/pwned') | "
closes the vimscript string literal and the append() call, then chains an arbitrary Ex command. Pressing 'n' on that issue in detail mode runs attacker-chosen code. This is the same code-execution class as the P0 shell-injection bug db0z.1 — that fix correctly hardened the 'sh -lc' launcher but the nvim '+call' vector was not covered, because rule 6's wording is scoped specifically to 'sh -c'/'sh -lc'.

Threat path: attacker controls an issue title in a beads DB the operator browses (beads trackers are often shared) -> operator opens the issue -> operator presses 'n' (nvim is the default launcher tool). Per the project's own stated threat model ('issue fields ... are operator-untrusted input') this is in scope.

Severity: medium. Arbitrary code execution, but multi-step and dependent on nvim being the configured launcher (it is the default) and the operator pressing 'n'. Not exploited live during exploration — confirmed by code inspection only (unescaped interpolation + default template).

Repro (inspection): bwb --print-config -> see launcher.definitions[action: nvim].args; internal/launcher/service.go:113 Interpolate() -> only stripC0, no metacharacter escaping.

Suggested fix options: (a) generalize rule 6 beyond sh — forbid interpolating untrusted fields into ANY argument that a launched tool re-parses as code (nvim Ex commands, awk, etc.); (b) change the default nvim launcher to write issue context to a temp file and open it, or pass fields via env vars / -c with a non-executing mechanism; (c) add a vimscript-safe escaper for the nvim template. Also update docs/CODING.md rule 6 to state the principle (untrusted fields never embedded in re-parsed code) rather than only the sh-specific instance.
close_reason: Closed

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Priority raised P2->P1. The vulnerability class matches the P0 shell-injection bug db0z.1 (arbitrary code execution); held one notch below P0 only because exploitation is multi-step — it requires nvim to be the configured launcher (it is the default) AND the operator to press 'n' on an attacker-controlled issue. Maintainer should re-triage to P0 if shared/untrusted beads DBs are in the threat model.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Review (2026-05-22): VERIFIED end-to-end. Default nvim launcher (model.go:63) ships '+call append(0,[...{{issue.title}}...])'; Interpolate() (service.go:113-127) substitutes unescaped (stripC0 removes only control chars, not "/]/|). process_runner.go:60 uses exec.Command (no shell) — so this is NOT shell injection, but nvim executes the '+'-arg as a vimscript Ex command, so a crafted title breaks out and chains arbitrary Ex commands. Trigger chain confirmed: n key -> keybindings.go:120 ShellActionLaunchNvim -> app/model.go:771/779 -> launchActionCmd("nvim"). Same vulnerability CLASS as P0 db0z.1, different mechanism; threat model is the project's own (Rule 6). KEEP P1 (P0 only if shared/untrusted beads DBs are explicitly in-scope). RECOMMEND SPLIT: (1) P1 code fix — harden the default nvim template (temp-file context, vimscript-safe escaper, or non-executing arg path); (2) separate doc follow-up — generalize CODING.md Rule 6 from 'sh -c/-lc' to the principle 'untrusted fields never embedded where a launched tool re-parses them as code'. Note: the opencode launcher passes fields as discrete argv/env values and is NOT affected.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Decision (2026-05-22, /beads-work): the code fix AND the doc follow-up are done in this one task — no split. Scope: (1) harden the default nvim launcher so untrusted issue fields cannot be re-parsed as vimscript; (2) generalize docs/CODING.md Rule 6 from the sh-specific wording to the principle 'untrusted fields must never be embedded where a launched tool re-parses them as code'.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Fixed & verified (acceptance review 2026-05-22). Default nvim launcher hardened via fix option (b): the +call append(...) Ex-command vector removed; nvim Args are now static strings only (+normal! gg / +setlocal nomodifiable / +file [Issue]) and issue fields travel as discrete BWB_ISSUE_* env-var values (non-executing path, matches the opencode launcher). Security test uses the exact injection payload and proves the title cannot execute. docs/CODING.md Rule 6 generalized from sh-specific wording to the principle 'untrusted fields must never be embedded where a launched tool re-parses them as code', with sh and nvim as concrete instances. Verifier: PASS. Full quality suite green. UX note recorded by tasker: operator no longer sees issue context inline on nvim open (access via $BWB_ISSUE_* env vars) — file a P3 follow-up only if visible-on-open context is wanted.

title: UpdateIssue ClearLabels workaround emits --remove-labels (plural, invalid bd flag) — silently broken in production
description:
## Summary

Clearing labels via the UI is **silently broken** in production. The ClearLabels workaround at `internal/gateway/beads/writes.go:122` emits `bd update <id> --remove-labels <csv>` (plural), but bd 1.0.4 does **not** have a `--remove-labels` flag. The correct flag is `--remove-label` (singular, takes a comma-separated value).

## Discovered during

`beads-workbench-ibif` Write gateway audit (parent epic: `beads-workbench-yspw`).

## Reproduction

In any bd 1.0.4 workspace:

```bash
TMPDIR=$(mktemp -d) && cd "$TMPDIR" && BD_NON_INTERACTIVE=1 bd init
ID=$(bd create --json --title "x" --labels "a,b,c" | python3 -c 'import json,sys;print(json.load(sys.stdin)["id"])')
bd update "$ID" --remove-labels "a,b,c"   # FAILS: "Error: unknown flag: --remove-labels"
bd update "$ID" --remove-label "a,b,c"    # WORKS: exits 0, labels cleared
```

## Production impact

Any edit-modal action that clears all labels on an issue:
- Sends `UpdateIssueInput{ClearLabels: true}` to the gateway
- Gateway calls ShowIssue to fetch current labels (correct)
- Gateway emits `bd update <id> --remove-labels <csv>` (BROKEN)
- bd 1.0.4 returns exit 1 with `Error: unknown flag: --remove-labels` on stderr
- Gateway surfaces this as `ErrorCodeCommandFailed`
- Labels are NOT cleared
- User sees a generic command-failed error in the UI

## Why this slipped through

The only unit test, `TestGatewayUpdateIssueClearLabelsEmitsRemoveLabels` (`writes_test.go:162,181`), uses the stub `RecordingExecutor` and asserts the **wrong** flag (`--remove-labels`). The stub doesn't know which flags bd accepts — it only records argv — so the test passes against a fiction. There is no integration test that exercises ClearLabels against real bd, so the bug shipped undetected.

## Fix

1. **`internal/gateway/beads/writes.go:122`** — change `"--remove-labels"` → `"--remove-label"`.
2. **`internal/gateway/beads/writes_test.go:162,181`** — update test assertion to the corrected flag.
3. **`internal/gateway/beads/interface.go:82-89` and `:558-562`** — update the documented quirk text to reference the correct flag name.
4. **Add integration test** — see follow-up bug (filed separately) for adding a `RunWriteContract/UpdateIssue/ClearLabels` sub-test that exercises against real bd, so the stub-only test cannot mask this class of bug again.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] writes.go:122 emits `--remove-label` (singular).
- [ ] writes_test.go assertion updated to the corrected flag.
- [ ] interface.go quirk doc updated.
- [ ] Manual real-bd repro confirms labels are now cleared.
- [ ] All existing tests still pass.

## Related

- Parent audit: `beads-workbench-ibif`
- Epic: `beads-workbench-yspw`
- Follow-up: add integration coverage so a stub-only test cannot mask wrong-flag bugs again.
close_reason: Fixed in writes.go:122 (--remove-labels → --remove-label singular). Test updated in writes_test.go. Real-bd verified. Followup integration test 5toe also landed.

title: Acceptance Review: Refresh concurrency safety
description:
## What

Independent acceptance review of the entire 5q6t epic before opening the PR. Verifier spawn (subagent_type: beads-tasks:verifier) inspects the diff, reproduces evidence, validates each child task's acceptance criteria against the actual state of the worktree, and returns recommended tracker actions.

## Why

Per beads-acceptance-review.md, every epic closes through an independent verifier pass. The orchestrator implements; the verifier validates. This prevents an agent from marking its own work done. Pattern matches sibling epics 9x70.5, faif.6, ppja.5.

## Verifier scope

The verifier MUST:

1. **Read the diff** on branch `5q6t-refresh-concurrency` (worktree at `.claude/worktrees/5q6t`) — list every file changed.
2. **For each child task (5q6t.1 through 5q6t.5)**:
   - Read the task's acceptance criteria.
   - Locate the code/test/evidence that satisfies each criterion.
   - Mark each criterion PASS, FAIL, or PARTIAL with file/line evidence.
3. **Run the tests independently**: `cd .claude/worktrees/5q6t && mise run quality` — capture full output.
4. **Confirm runtime evidence**: locate the 5q6t.5 capture artifacts in bd comments; verify they match the acceptance assertions (suppressed-refresh log entries present, no panic, screen still rendering after hammer).
5. **Verify NO unrelated changes** in the diff (no scope creep beyond the epic).
6. **Independently confirm the bug class is fixed** by reasoning about the diff:
   - Is the call-site guard present at internal/mode/board/model.go for BoardActionReload?
   - Is the call-site guard present at internal/mode/search/model.go for SearchActionReload?
   - Is the dedicated `inflight bool` (or equivalent) in place inside startReload?
   - Does the regression test set actually exercise the rapid-double-refresh sequence and assert no underflow?
7. **Cross-reference linked bug ule7**: does the fix address ule7's specific repro (stacked bd subprocesses)?

## Verifier deliverable

A single tracker-ready comment text covering:

- PASS/FAIL/PARTIAL per acceptance criterion across all 5 child tasks
- Output of `mise run quality` (truncated to passes/failures summary)
- A close recommendation: "CLOSE 5q6t and 5q6t.1-.5 and ule7" OR "DO NOT CLOSE — issues: ..."

The verifier MUST NOT write to the tracker itself (per core-rules.md serialized writes). The orchestrator applies the comment + status changes.

## Dependencies

- Blocked by 5q6t.1, 5q6t.2, 5q6t.3, 5q6t.4, 5q6t.5 (all must be implementation-complete with their evidence in bd comments).

## Acceptance

- Verifier comment posted on this task with PASS/FAIL per criterion.
- If all PASS → close 5q6t.1-.5 + this task + epic 5q6t + linked bug ule7.
- If any FAIL → do not close; open follow-up tasks for the gaps (new tasks, not reopened — per core-rules.md "closed work is NOT reopened").

## Quality gates

- This is the final gate before PR open. Nothing closes until verifier returns PASS.
close_reason: Independent acceptance verifier PASS: all criteria across epic + 5 children + linked bug ule7 confirmed. mise run quality (854 tests) and mise run verify:refresh (3 runs) both GREEN.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text:
**Acceptance review PASS** — independent verification by verifier subagent.

**Overall: PASS · Recommendation: CLOSE epic 5q6t + ule7**

Verifier ran mise run quality (854 tests, 0 failures, 0 lint, vet clean, guardrails pass) and mise run verify:refresh independently 3 times — consistent PASS with 19 board suppressed-refresh entries + 1 search suppressed-refresh entry per run, no panic, no negative pendingResults, UI intact across all runs.

**Skeptical findings (all SAFE):**
1. inflight cleared on error paths — every result handler decrements pendingResults and calls maybeCompose unconditionally, even when msg.err != nil. Errors cannot leave inflight=true permanently.
2. maybeCompose guards on pendingResults > 0 — inflight cleared exactly once when composition runs.
3. Init() cold-start — inflight=false at construction, guard passes, refresh proceeds.
4. Search defense-in-depth — confirmed present at search/model.go:318-323 inside triggerSearchWithAnchor.
5. All callers of startReload / triggerSearchWithAnchor enumerated and confirmed covered (6 call sites total across both files; all guarded).
6. Tests genuinely exercise the bug class — verified RED output in 5q6t.4 comment proves the bug was reproducible before the fix; the capture-Cmd-without-draining pattern correctly simulates in-flight state.
7. Slow bd wrapper in scripts/verify_refresh_concurrency.py is honest test infrastructure — 4-line bash script in tempdir, PATH-injected only into bwb child process, cleaned up after run, does not modify product code.

**One pre-existing cosmetic anomaly noted (not blocking):** duplicate 'component' JSON key in suppressed-refresh log entries ('component':'dashboard','component':'board'). Already tracked separately as bug beads-workbench-xm6u.

**ule7 acceptance:**
- 'Ctrl+R stacked → not stacked': PASS (19/20 keypresses suppressed)
- 'pendingResults cannot go negative': PASS (regression test asserts ≥0)
- 'Selection not gratuitously reset': PASS — first manual refresh's selection reset is pre-existing behavior; the bug being fixed (rapid-press storm wiping selection N times) is gone. Auto-refresh anchor reuse for manual reload was explicitly out of scope per epic.
- '10 presses → at most one batch': PASS (20 presses → 1 batch in runtime verification)

Ready to close 5q6t.1-.5 (already closed), 5q6t.6 (this AR task), 5q6t (epic), and ule7.

title: Full-app runtime verification: rapid 'r' hammering in built bwb
description:
## What

Programmatic runtime verification of the refresh-concurrency fix using `scripts/capture_bwb_screen.py` + pyte to drive the built `bwb` binary against the embedded fixture. Per user decision (2026-05-17), do NOT defer to manual operator verification — drive the TUI programmatically and capture evidence.

## Why

Per docs/TESTING.md "Full-App Verification" section, layout/navigation/runtime UI changes require built-binary proof. The bug being fixed manifests as visible UI breakage, so the proof of fix is visible UI stability under load. Programmatic capture via pyte gives reproducible CI-grade evidence; pure manual operator verification leaves no artifact in the PR.

## Approach

1. **Inspect existing harness.** Read `scripts/capture_bwb_screen.py` to learn its PTY/pyte capture API, readiness signals, timeout policy, and cleanup behavior (per docs/TESTING.md process-level capture policy lines 142-159).

2. **Author a new capture scenario** (likely `scripts/verify_refresh_concurrency.py` or extend the existing script with a CLI subcommand) that:
   - Builds `bwb` (or uses pre-built binary path).
   - Spawns it via PTY against an embedded-fixture temp repo, with `--debug` flag and `BD_NON_INTERACTIVE=1`, redirecting stderr/debug log to a known path.
   - Waits for a readiness signal (board rendered, e.g., screen contains "Ready" column header).
   - Sends 20 'r' keypresses rapidly (e.g., 50ms apart).
   - Waits ~500ms for any in-flight refreshes to settle.
   - Captures final pyte screen state.
   - Reads the captured debug log.
   - Sends Ctrl+C / quit to terminate cleanly.

3. **Assertions** (script exits non-zero if any fail):
   - Final screen contains all 4 column headers (UI not corrupted).
   - Final screen contains at least one issue from the fixture (data still rendered).
   - Debug log contains AT LEAST ONE "manual board refresh suppressed" entry (proves the guard fired).
   - Debug log contains NO panic stack traces.
   - Debug log shows pendingResults never went negative (look for any error log mentioning pendingResults).

4. **Repeat for search mode** (Ctrl+Space to switch, type a query, Enter, then hammer 'r' 20×, capture, assert "manual search refresh suppressed" entries present).

5. **Hard timeout**: entire scenario must complete within 10 seconds or fail explicitly with "harness timeout" (per docs/TESTING.md line 154).

6. **Cleanup**: guaranteed child-process termination on success, timeout, AND failure (per docs/TESTING.md line 155).

## Deliverables

- `scripts/verify_refresh_concurrency.py` (or equivalent integration of new logic into existing script — pick whichever fits the existing harness shape better).
- A `mise` task entry: `mise run verify:refresh` (add to .mise.toml or equivalent).
- Evidence captured into bd comments:
  - Final pyte screen capture (board + search).
  - Debug log excerpt showing suppressed-refresh entries.
  - Exit code 0 from the harness.

## Dependencies

- Blocked by 5q6t.1, 5q6t.2, 5q6t.3, 5q6t.4 (all code + test tasks must be merged in the worktree branch first).

## Acceptance

- `scripts/verify_refresh_concurrency.py` exits 0 against the built bwb on branch 5q6t-refresh-concurrency.
- Screen captures + log excerpts attached to bd comments.
- Harness timeout (10s) never triggered in normal run.
- If the script can't be made reliable in <2s per scenario, document why in the task comments and reduce to "best-effort + manual operator runbook fallback" — but try the programmatic path first.

## Quality gates

- Must run AFTER `mise run quality` is green on the worktree branch.
- Output is the runtime-truth artifact — without this passing, the PR is not ready to merge.
close_reason: Runtime verify GREEN at ae906b3; both guards fire under PTY load (19 board + 1 search); UI intact

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text:
PASS. Runtime verification complete at commit ae906b3 on branch 5q6t-refresh-concurrency.

'mise run verify:refresh' exits 0. Scenario time: ~6.5-7.5s (under 15s hard timeout).

Evidence:
- Board guard fired 19 times: 'manual board refresh suppressed; refresh already in flight' (slog.Debug entries from 5q6t.1)
- Search guard fired 1 time: 'manual search refresh suppressed; refresh already in flight' (5q6t.2)
- UI intact after rapid 'r' storms: all 4 column headers visible, fixture issues present (bwf-* IDs), no panic on screen
- No panic/fatal log entries; pendingResults never went negative

Harness implementation: scripts/verify_refresh_concurrency.py drives bwb via PTY using pyte, mirrors the patterns in capture_bwb_screen.py and verify_bwb_state_flow.py. mise task: 'mise run verify:refresh'.

Harness note (documented for future maintainers): 'bd search' against the embedded fixture completes in ~188ms — fast enough that BubbleTea's event loop processes searchLoadedMsg between 'r' keypresses at 50ms delay, resetting m.loading=false before the next 'r'. A test-only 'bd' wrapper with 'sleep 0.04' (4-line bash script created per-run in tmpdir, PATH-injected only into the bwb child process) broadens the in-flight window to ~280ms so the Reload() guard fires reliably. This is test infrastructure inside the verification script — no product code was changed to accommodate the harness.

Sample log entries:
  {"level":"DEBUG","message":"manual board refresh suppressed; refresh already in flight","component":"board","trigger":"board-manual","session_id":"ec2985bf","build_version":"v0.4.0-51-g13916cc"}
  {"level":"DEBUG","message":"manual search refresh suppressed; refresh already in flight","component":"search","trigger":"search-manual","session_id":"ec2985bf","build_version":"v0.4.0-51-g13916cc"}

Unblocks 5q6t.6 (acceptance review).

title: Regression tests for rapid double-refresh in board and search modes
description:
## What

Add model-level regression tests that drive the Update loop with two reload key events in sequence while the first refresh's gateway Cmds are still pending. Tests must be written FIRST (TDD), MUST FAIL against current unfixed code, and MUST PASS after the sibling fix tasks land.

## Why

The bug is silent in normal smoke tests because the gateway in tests returns "fast enough" that double-refresh races don't surface in a single Update pass. Without a regression test driving the message order explicitly — by capturing the in-flight Cmd without draining it before firing the second keypress — this class of bug will return.

## Test approach (simplified — no fake changes needed)

The existing infrastructure is sufficient:

- `internal/app/model_test.go:3028` defines `runBatch(cmd tea.Cmd) []tea.Msg` — recursively expands tea.BatchMsg into individual msgs.
- `internal/app/model_test.go:3054` defines `applyMessages(t, model, msgs)` — runs Update against each msg, recursively expanding returned Cmds.
- The pattern at `internal/app/model_loading_refresh_test.go:117` captures a returned Cmd from `m.Update(msg)` WITHOUT calling `applyMessages(runBatch(cmd))` — leaving the gateway Cmds "in flight" from the model's perspective.

For the board/search package tests, lift `runBatch` / `applyMessages` into a shared test-helpers location (or duplicate locally — pick whichever fits the package's existing conventions; check internal/mode/board/model_test.go patterns).

## Required tests

### Board (internal/mode/board/model_double_refresh_test.go)

1. **`TestBoardManualReloadIgnoredWhileInFlight`**
   - Setup: NewModel with fake gateway; call `m.Init()` and `applyMessages(runBatch(cmd))` to settle cold-start.
   - Snapshot: `selectedRow`, `focusedColumn`, `pendingResults` (should be 0 after settle).
   - Send first reload key event: capture returned Cmd, do NOT drain it.
   - Assert: `m.pendingResults == 4`, all columns `loading == true`.
   - Send second reload key event WITHOUT draining the first Cmd.
   - **Assert (currently FAILS):** `m.pendingResults` is still 4 (not reset).
   - **Assert (currently FAILS):** `selectedRow` and `focusedColumn` from snapshot are preserved (the second handler must NOT have wiped them via the manual-refresh reset at startReload lines 358-362).
   - **Assert (currently FAILS):** The second Update returned `nil` Cmd (no new gateway batch).
   - Drain the first Cmd, verify counter goes to 0 cleanly and never negative.

2. **`TestBoardPendingResultsNeverGoesNegativeUnderKeySpam`**
   - Setup as above.
   - Fire reload key event, capture Cmd, do NOT drain.
   - Fire reload key event 10 more times without draining.
   - Drain the first Cmd's messages individually.
   - **Assert (currently FAILS):** at every step `m.pendingResults >= 0`.
   - **Assert:** after all messages drain, `m.pendingResults == 0` and no `maybeCompose` call ran with partial data (verified via a deterministic gateway response — assert the composed columns match the gateway's single response, not a mix).

3. **`TestBoardInternalStartReloadGuardedByInflightFlag`** (gated on 5q6t.3 landing)
   - Setup as above.
   - Call `m.startReload(refreshModeManual)` directly, capture Cmd, do NOT drain.
   - Call `m.startReload(refreshModeManual)` a second time directly.
   - **Assert:** returned Cmd is nil; `m.pendingResults` is still 4; partial buffers untouched.

### Search (internal/mode/search/model_double_refresh_test.go)

1. **`TestSearchManualReloadIgnoredWhileInFlight`**
   - Setup with fake gateway returning a deterministic SearchResultPage.
   - Settle initial state (typing into query, applying search).
   - Send first reload key event, capture Cmd, do NOT drain.
   - Assert: `m.loading == true`, gateway has 1 query call recorded.
   - Send second reload key event WITHOUT draining the first Cmd.
   - **Assert (currently FAILS):** gateway still has only 1 query call (not 2).
   - **Assert (currently FAILS):** `m.pendingSelectionAnchor` matches the value set by the FIRST keypress (not overwritten).
   - **Assert (currently FAILS):** second Update returned nil Cmd.

### App-level (internal/app/model_double_refresh_app_test.go)

1. **`TestAppRapidMutationsDoNotEnqueueConcurrentRefreshes`** — lock down the existing guard at app/model.go:1076 against silent regression.
   - Setup: app with board active, fake gateway.
   - Fire mutation messages (e.g., issueClosedMsg) in rapid succession, each triggering `maybeAutoRefreshActiveSurfaceCmd()`.
   - Capture the Cmds at each step.
   - **Assert:** at most one refresh batch is ever in flight; `boardIsLoading()` guard fires when expected.
   - This test should PASS on current code — its purpose is to prevent future regression of the existing guard, not to expose a current bug.

## TDD ordering (REQUIRED)

For each FAIL-then-PASS test above:

1. Write the test against current code.
2. Run `mise run test -- ./internal/mode/board/...` (or search) — confirm test FAILS.
3. Record the failing output in the task's bd comments (paste the assertion failure message verbatim) so future readers can verify the test was actually red.
4. Apply the sibling fix (5q6t.1 or 5q6t.2 or 5q6t.3).
5. Re-run the test — confirm it now PASSES.
6. Run the full package test suite to confirm no regression in other tests.

## Files

- internal/mode/board/model_double_refresh_test.go (new)
- internal/mode/search/model_double_refresh_test.go (new)
- internal/app/model_double_refresh_app_test.go (new)

## Acceptance

- Each FAIL-then-PASS test has a bd comment with the verbatim RED output captured against unfixed code.
- All new tests pass after sibling fix tasks land.
- No existing tests broken (`mise run test` and `mise run test:integration` both green).
- Test runtime overhead < 100ms total across all new tests (per docs/TESTING.md tier 1 budget).

## Quality gates

- `mise run quality:fast` green before commit.
- `mise run quality` green before close.

title: Guard search manual reload against in-flight refresh
description:
## What

In internal/mode/search/model.go at the SearchActionReload keyboard handler (currently line 243-244), guard against re-entering triggerSearchPreservingSelection while a search is already in flight, and emit a debug log when the guard fires.

## Why

Search mode has the same bug as board mode but with a different failure mode. The keyboard handler calls m.triggerSearchPreservingSelection() unconditionally, while Reload() (line 380-385) and AutoRefresh() (line 388-396) both already check `if m.loading`.

**Search-specific failure mode:** unlike board (where pendingResults underflows), search has no counter to corrupt. Instead, when 'r' is pressed twice rapidly:
- Both keypresses invoke triggerSearchWithAnchor.
- Both set m.loading=true (idempotent).
- Both overwrite m.pendingSelectionAnchor and m.appliedQuery (the SECOND call wins).
- Both dispatch loadSearchCmd → two concurrent gateway queries.
- Their loadSearchResultMsg responses land in unknown order; the LAST one to arrive replaces page state.
- User sees results from the older/stale query, or results that don't match the displayed appliedQuery.

The debug log makes the suppression visible in `bwb --debug` traces per docs/MONITORING.md.

## Change

Route the keyboard handler through Reload() (which already has the guard) and add the debug log inside Reload (or inline both at the call site — equivalent):

```go
case m.keys.Match(config.SearchContext, config.SearchActionReload, msg):
    return m.Reload()

// in Reload():
func (m *Model) Reload() tea.Cmd {
    if m.loading {
        m.logger.Debug("manual search refresh suppressed; refresh already in flight",
            "component", "search",
            "trigger", "search-manual")
        return nil
    }
    return m.triggerSearchPreservingSelection()
}
```

Note: confirm `m.logger` exists on the search model; if not, plumb a logger through or use slog.Default() consistent with the board model pattern.

## Dependencies

- Depends on 5q6t.4 having landed first (TDD: failing search regression test must exist first).

## Acceptance

- Two consecutive SearchActionReload key events while the first search is in flight produce only one gateway call (assert via fake gateway's Calls slice).
- m.loading, m.reloading, m.pendingSelectionAnchor, and m.appliedQuery are not overwritten by the second keypress.
- The regression test from 5q6t.4 (search portion) transitions from FAIL to PASS after this change.
- A `bwb --debug` run hammering 'r' mid-search shows the "manual search refresh suppressed" log entries (verified manually as part of 5q6t.5).
- Existing AutoRefresh() and Reload() paths are unchanged — all existing search tests in internal/mode/search/model_test.go and model_integration_test.go must still pass.

## Quality gates

- `mise run quality:fast` green before commit.
close_reason: Guard routed through Reload() at fbb4baa; search RED test now GREEN; quality:fast all green

title: Guard board manual reload against in-flight refresh
description:
## What

In internal/mode/board/model.go at the BoardActionReload keyboard handler (currently line 233-234), guard against re-entering startReload while a refresh is already in flight, and emit a debug log when the guard fires.

## Why

This is the primary trigger for the refresh-corruption bug described in the parent epic. The handler currently calls m.startReload(refreshModeManual) unconditionally, while AutoRefresh() at line 323-328 already does the right thing with `if m.IsLoading() { return nil }`.

The debug log makes the suppression visible in `bwb --debug` traces and the persistent JSON Lines log per docs/MONITORING.md — silent no-ops make this class of bug invisible.

## Change

```go
case m.keys.Match(config.BoardContext, config.BoardActionReload, msg):
    if m.IsLoading() {
        m.logger.Debug("manual board refresh suppressed; refresh already in flight",
            "component", "board",
            "trigger", "board-manual")
        return nil
    }
    return m.startReload(refreshModeManual)
```

## Dependencies

- Depends on 5q6t.4 having landed first (TDD: the failing regression test for double-refresh must exist and FAIL on current code before this fix is applied).

## Acceptance

- Two consecutive BoardActionReload key events while the first refresh is in flight produce only one startReload invocation.
- pendingResults is not reset by the second keypress.
- The regression test from 5q6t.4 transitions from FAIL to PASS after this change.
- A `bwb --debug` run hammering 'r' mid-refresh shows the "manual board refresh suppressed" log entries (verified manually as part of 5q6t.5).
- Existing AutoRefresh path is unchanged (assert by running existing tests in internal/mode/board/model_test.go and internal/app/model_loading_refresh_test.go — all must still pass).

## Quality gates

- `mise run quality:fast` green before commit.
- Existing test suite unchanged in behavior (no flakes, no new skips).

title: Refresh concurrency safety — only one refresh in flight at a time
description:
## Problem

Manual refresh ('r' key) in both board and search modes bypasses the in-flight guard that AutoRefresh respects. Pressing 'r' while a refresh is already running enqueues a second concurrent refresh, which corrupts the board's pendingResults counter and partial-result buffers and leaves the UI in a broken state.

## Observed symptoms

- After two rapid 'r' presses the board can render stale or incomplete columns.
- Selection / focus is wiped twice and restored against partial data, producing out-of-bounds rows.
- Subsequent refreshes inherit a poisoned (negative) pendingResults counter — they can fire maybeCompose early with only a fraction of the columns updated.
- Each extra refresh spawns 4× additional bd subprocesses.

## Root cause

`internal/mode/board/model.go:233-234` — the BoardActionReload keyboard handler calls `m.startReload(refreshModeManual)` with no guard. Compare `AutoRefresh()` at line 323-328 which guards with `if m.IsLoading() { return nil }`.

`internal/mode/search/model.go:243-244` — the SearchActionReload keyboard handler calls `m.triggerSearchPreservingSelection()` with no guard. Compare `Reload()` (line 380-385) and `AutoRefresh()` (line 388-396) which both check `if m.loading`.

`startReload()` itself (line 333-372) is destructive — it resets pendingResults=4 and clobbers the partial buffers unconditionally. There is no defensive guard inside the function, so any future caller can re-trigger the bug.

`maybeCompose()` (line 376-439) only guards with `if m.pendingResults > 0`, so negative counter values trigger compose on every result arrival.

## Goal

Refresh must run one-at-a-time per surface. A manual refresh request while a refresh is in flight must be a no-op (logged at debug level so the suppression is visible in `--debug` traces).

## Acceptance

- Pressing 'r' rapidly in board mode never causes pendingResults to be reset while >0.
- Pressing 'r' rapidly in search mode never spawns concurrent gateway queries.
- pendingResults never goes negative under any keypress sequence (regression test).
- Visible user behavior: the refresh that is already running completes normally; the user's extra keypresses are no-ops (no flicker, no selection reset, no compose with partial data).
- Suppressed refreshes are logged at debug level with the trigger source (board-manual, search-manual) so the suppression is visible in `bwb --debug` traces and the persistent JSON Lines log.
- Auto-refresh paths in app/model.go continue to work unchanged.

## Out of scope

- Cancelling an in-flight refresh.
- Queueing multiple pending refreshes (one-pending-after-current is a possible follow-up if UX demands it).
- Surfacing a "refresh ignored" toast — defer unless explicitly requested.

## Notes / design

- Simplest fix is to add `if m.IsLoading() { return nil }` at the keyboard-handler call site for board, and `if m.loading { return nil }` for search.
- Defense-in-depth (task .3): introduce a dedicated `inflight bool` set by `startReload` and cleared by `maybeCompose`. Use it inside `startReload`'s own guard so future callers cannot bypass it. The dedicated bool removes the Init/cold-start ambiguity that `IsLoading()` (column-loading driven) would create.
- Bubble Tea's single-threaded Update means a mutex is not needed — boolean state is sufficient.
- Tests are model-level: drive the Update loop with two BoardActionReload key events back-to-back and assert no negative counter, no double column-reset. The existing `applyMessages` / `runBatch` pattern in `internal/app/model_test.go` plus capturing returned Cmds without invoking them (pattern used at `internal/app/model_loading_refresh_test.go:117`) is sufficient — no fake-gateway changes needed.

## Quality gates (MUST pass before any task in this epic closes)

- `mise run quality:fast` (~15s, runs build + vet + unit tests) — green before commit.
- `mise run quality` (~2min, runs scripts:check, lint, guardrails, build, vet, unit + integration tests) — green before final close.
- Full-app verification per `docs/RUNTIME_UI_VERIFICATION.md` — tracked as task 5q6t.5.

## Execution order

1. 5q6t.4 — write failing regression tests first (TDD; tests must FAIL with documented error on current code).
2. 5q6t.1 and 5q6t.2 — apply call-site guards in parallel (independent files).
3. 5q6t.3 — defense-in-depth `inflight bool` inside `startReload` / `triggerSearchWithAnchor`.
4. 5q6t.5 — full-app runtime verification against embedded fixture with rapid 'r' hammering.
5. Acceptance review task (verifier) — reproduces by hand using 5q6t.5 instructions and recommends close.
close_reason: All 6 child tasks closed (regression tests RED-then-GREEN, board guard, search guard, defense-in-depth inflight bool, runtime verification PASS, acceptance review PASS). Refresh-concurrency fix verified at code, test, and runtime levels. Branch 5q6t-refresh-concurrency ready for PR.

title: Migrate embedded-fixture setup to support both minimal anchor and scale fixtures
description:
## Description

Make the embedded fixture system support both:
- **Minimal anchor** (existing 3-issue seed.json) — for tests that need exact ID assertions (`bwf-1`, `bwf-2`, `bwf-3` etc.).
- **Scale** (new ~600-issue scale-seed.json from [[faif.2]]) — default for parity/integration tests that need realistic edge cases.

Audit existing consumers and route them appropriately.

## Instructions

1. Read `internal/testing/e2e/embeddedfixture/fixture.go` (and siblings) to understand the current API. Currently `SharedFixtureRepoPath(t)` returns a single fixture path.
2. Add a parallel `SharedScaleFixtureRepoPath(t)` that returns a path to the scale fixture (using a separate sync.Once / temp dir from the minimal one).
3. In `internal/testing/datasets/datasets.go`:
   - Keep `Fixture(t)` returning the minimal anchor (backward compat for existing tests).
   - Add `ScaleFixture(t)` returning the scale fixture as a Dataset.
   - Update `External(t)` doc to mention `ScaleFixture` for tests wanting realism without an external DB.
4. Audit every consumer of `Fixture(t)` and `SharedFixtureRepoPath(t)`:
   - For tests that assert specific IDs (`bwf-1` etc.) → keep on minimal anchor.
   - For tests that iterate "any issue" (parity tests, contract invariants, smoke checks) → consider migrating to ScaleFixture or running against both.
   - For tests that benefit from realistic data shapes (search relevance, sort tie-breaks) → migrate to scale.
5. Update the parity tests (`internal/dashboard/parity/`, `internal/mode/search/parity/`) to iterate over `Fixture`, `ScaleFixture`, `ThisRepo`, `External` — runs against all four when env-gates are set.
6. Update `bwb-smoke` to run against scale fixture as part of its check matrix (in addition to whatever `--dir` is passed).
7. Document the dual-fixture policy in `internal/testing/datasets/datasets.go` package doc:
   - "Use Fixture for tests that need exact ID assertions; use ScaleFixture for tests that need realistic edge cases."

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `Fixture(t)` returns the minimal anchor; existing tests pass unchanged
- [ ] `ScaleFixture(t)` returns the scale fixture; new tests can opt in
- [ ] Parity tests iterate over both fixtures (where applicable)
- [ ] Smoke binary's check matrix includes scale fixture
- [ ] Dual-fixture policy documented in datasets package doc
- [ ] `mise run test:integration` passes — every existing test still works
- [ ] At least one new test in the parity suite exercises an edge case only present in the scale fixture (e.g., cap-engagement on Done column)

## Files to Modify

- `internal/testing/e2e/embeddedfixture/fixture.go` (add ScaleFixture helpers)
- `internal/testing/datasets/datasets.go` (add ScaleFixture constructor)
- `internal/dashboard/parity/*.go` (extend matrix)
- `internal/mode/search/parity/*.go` (extend matrix)
- `cmd/bwb-smoke/main.go` (extend check matrix)
- possibly individual test files migrating to ScaleFixture

title: Run parity + smoke + contract suites against scale fixture; document discovered bugs per discipline
description:
## Description

The scale fixture is built for the express purpose of exercising edge cases that the 3-issue minimal fixture cannot. Once it lands (via [[faif.4]]), the full test suite must run against it — and any newly-surfaced bugs must be filed per the established discipline.

## Instructions

1. Run `mise run test:integration` with the scale fixture as default for parity and contract tests. Triage every failure:
   - **Real bug surfaced by scale data**: file via discipline (`bd create --type=bug --title="Discovered by scale fixture: ..."`), link back to this task, include verification requirement.
   - **Fake fidelity gap surfaced**: fix the fake per existing fake-update discipline (`internal/testing/fakes/doc.go`); add drift log entry.
   - **Test that incorrectly assumed 3-issue scale**: fix the test (probably needs minimal-fixture-only annotation, or rewrite assertions to be cap-aware).
   - **bd quirk only visible at scale**: update interface.go contract; add a new "bd quirks observed at scale" appendix if needed.
2. Run `mise run smoke -- --dir <scale-fixture-path>` if the smoke binary supports it (per [[faif.4]] step 6). Triage failures the same way.
3. Run the full `RunReadContract` (and `RunWriteContract` from 9x70 if landed) against the scale fixture. The fixture-agnostic invariants from 8qw9 should pass cleanly; if any don't, the invariant logic itself may have implicit small-N assumptions to fix.
4. Document material findings:
   - Bugs filed: list each with ID and one-line description.
   - Fake updates: append to drift log.
   - Test changes: note which tests now opt-in to minimal-only.
5. Goal: by task close, every existing test passes against the scale fixture (or is explicitly annotated as minimal-only with rationale).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `mise run test:integration` passes with scale fixture as the parity-test default
- [ ] `mise run smoke` against scale fixture passes
- [ ] `RunReadContract` (+ `RunWriteContract` if available) passes against scale fixture for both fake AND real implementations
- [ ] Every newly-discovered bug filed per discipline and linked back to this task
- [ ] Fake updates (if any) reflected in drift log
- [ ] Tests opting for minimal-fixture-only have an inline comment explaining why

## Files to Modify

- depends on findings — most likely: individual test files, fakes, possibly composer/gateway code
close_reason: Closed

title: Define + verify invariant manifest for scale fixture; wire fixture:verify into CI
description:
## Description

Machine-readable invariants that the scale fixture MUST satisfy. The verifier runs against the generated fixture; if any invariant fails, CI fails — preventing silent drift as the fixture is edited.

## Instructions

1. Create `internal/testing/e2e/embeddedfixture/scale/invariants.yaml` declaring the assertions that hold for any well-formed scale fixture. Each invariant has a name, predicate description, and threshold. Examples:
   ```yaml
   invariants:
     - name: closed_count_min
       description: Done column cap engagement requires >=75 closed issues
       predicate: bd count --status closed >= 75
       threshold: 75
     - name: cardinality_warning_threshold
       description: One active group must exceed 500 to engage composer cardinality warning
       predicate: max(bd count --status open, in_progress, blocked) > 500
       threshold: 501
     - name: bulk_closed_at_cluster
       description: At least 10 closed issues share one closed_at timestamp (kh54 regression guard)
       predicate: groupby(bd query 'status=closed', closed_at).max_size >= 10
       threshold: 10
     # ...one per recipe section
   ```
2. Build the verifier program (`internal/testing/e2e/embeddedfixture/scale/verifier.go` or similar). It:
   - Sets up the scale fixture in a temp DB (via setup.sh + scale-seed.json).
   - Runs each invariant predicate against the live DB.
   - Outputs per-invariant PASS/FAIL with the observed value.
   - Exit 0 if all pass, 1 if any fail.
3. Add `mise run fixture:verify`:
   ```toml
   [tasks."fixture:verify"]
   description = "Verify the scale fixture honors all declared invariants"
   dir = "{{config_root}}"
   run = "go run ./internal/testing/e2e/embeddedfixture/scale/cmd/verifier"
   ```
4. Wire `fixture:verify` into `mise run quality`'s dependency chain (or at least `quality:fast`) so CI catches drift.
5. Document the manifest as the "fixture contract" — analog of the gateway contract from 8qw9. Any change to recipe.yaml or generator.go must keep the manifest green.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `invariants.yaml` covers every recipe threshold and every bug class the recipe was designed to exercise
- [ ] Verifier runs against a freshly-generated scale fixture and reports per-invariant status
- [ ] `mise run fixture:verify` passes on the generated fixture
- [ ] CI quality chain includes `fixture:verify` (gate it as `quality` dependency)
- [ ] Deliberate-break: edit recipe.yaml to remove the bulk-close cluster; regenerate; verifier fails with a clear message; restore. Document the test in your closure comment.

## Files to Modify

- create `internal/testing/e2e/embeddedfixture/scale/invariants.yaml`
- create `internal/testing/e2e/embeddedfixture/scale/verifier.go` (or `cmd/fixture-verify/main.go`)
- update `.mise.toml` (`fixture:verify` task + add to quality dependencies)
close_reason: Merged into beads-workbench-faif.1 per epic-review 2026-05-16. Invariants are now product-behavior assertions in scale_fixture_smoke_test.go (Go), not a YAML manifest + verifier program. Example invariants in this task were tautological (asserting the generator produced what the recipe declared) — replaced with assertions on observable bwb behavior.

title: Author scale-seed.json + product-behavior smoke invariants
description:
## Description

**Merged scope (epic-review 2026-05-16):** this task absorbs the original faif.2 (generator) and faif.3 (invariant manifest + verifier). The 3-layer abstraction was over-built for a stable fixture. This task delivers the scale fixture as a single committed JSON file plus a Go smoke test enforcing **product-behavior** invariants — not generator counts.

## Deliverables

1. **`internal/testing/e2e/embeddedfixture/scale-seed.json`** — committed artifact, ~550-700 issues. Author by hand or via a one-shot script (`scripts/gen-scale-seed.sh` or similar) — the script does NOT need to live in production code; the seed is the deliverable.

2. **`internal/testing/e2e/embeddedfixture/scale_fixture_smoke_test.go`** — Go test that loads the scale fixture and asserts product-behavior invariants (NOT generator counts).

## Scale fixture must contain

- ≥ 75 closed (engages Done column 50-cap; produces "+" badge — ssom regression guard)
- ≥ 510 issues in one active group (engages 500 cardinality threshold in composer)
- A block of 10+ closed issues sharing one `updated_at` (kh54 regression trigger)
- A block of 10+ closed issues sharing one `closed_at` (sort tie-break)
- A block of P1 issues sharing one `created_at` (active-column tie-break)
- At least one 5-deep parent chain; at least one parent with 3+ children
- At least 5 dependency-blocked issues; one block-cycle if bd supports it
- Edge text: one emoji title; one shell-metacharacter title (`;`, `'`, `"`, backtick); one max-length title; one multi-line description; one null/missing description
- A label shared across 20+ issues (for filter testing); plus rare labels
- ≥ 3 distinct keywords each appearing in 20+ issue titles/descriptions (search corpus)
- A few assignees with many issues; a few with one

## Product-behavior invariants (smoke test asserts these)

These are the **correct** invariants — they assert observable bwb behavior, not generator output:

- `composer.Compose(scaleFixture).Closed.Total >= bd count --status closed` (ssom regression guard)
- Done column row count ≤ 50 when total > 50 AND `+badge` is rendered
- Composer emits cardinality warning when one active group ≥ 500 (load it; assert log line)
- Sort order is stable when N closed issues share one `closed_at` (no kh54 false-positive warning fires; emit deterministic ordering)
- `dashboard.View()` against the scale fixture renders without doubled headers (o7tk regression guard)
- `gateway.SearchIssues("<shared-keyword>")` returns ≥ 20 results in deterministic order
- `gateway.ShowIssue` succeeds against the emoji-title, shell-metachar, and max-length issues
- `gateway.ShowIssue` succeeds against the null-description issue (781a regression guard)

## Instructions

1. Author the seed: decide between hand-edited JSON (~600 issues is tedious but auditable) vs. a one-shot generator script. Either works — the seed is the artifact.
2. If using a script, commit it to `scripts/` (not `internal/`) so it's clearly a build tool, not production code. The script need not be deterministic; the seed it produces does (commit the output).
3. Run `setup.sh` against the new seed and confirm it loads into a real bd DB without errors. If `setup.sh` needs extensions to support parents/dependencies/etc., extend it minimally.
4. Author `scale_fixture_smoke_test.go` with the product-behavior assertions above. Use `t.Run` sub-tests per invariant so failures are isolated.
5. Each invariant gets a one-line comment citing the regression class it guards (ssom / kh54 / o7tk / 781a / etc.).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `scale-seed.json` committed, ~550-700 issues, hits every edge case listed above
- [ ] `setup.sh` loads `scale-seed.json` into a real bd DB without errors (manual verification noted in closure comment)
- [ ] `scale_fixture_smoke_test.go` has ≥ 8 product-behavior invariant assertions, each tagged with the regression class it guards
- [ ] **Deliberate-break**: pick one product-behavior invariant; mutate `scale-seed.json` to break it (e.g., remove all but 50 closed issues); confirm smoke test fails with a clear message; restore. Document in closure comment.
- [ ] `mise run quality` passes
- [ ] No YAML recipe, no separate generator binary, no YAML invariants manifest — keep it flat

## Files to Modify

- create `internal/testing/e2e/embeddedfixture/scale-seed.json`
- create `internal/testing/e2e/embeddedfixture/scale_fixture_smoke_test.go`
- possibly extend `internal/testing/e2e/embeddedfixture/setup.sh`
- possibly create `scripts/gen-scale-seed.sh` (one-shot author tool, NOT production code)
close_reason: Closed

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Done. scale-seed.json (590 issues, 75 closed, 515 active, 13 deps, prefix bws) + 6 pure unit smoke tests + 2 opt-in integration tests + Python generator. Quality green (874 tests, 11 expected skips including the new opt-in scale fixture smoke). Deliberate-break verified: removing closed issues fires TestScaleFixtureInvariant_DoneColumnBadgeWhenCapped.

title: Build deterministic scale fixture generator at internal/testing/e2e/embeddedfixture/scale/generator.go
description:
## Description

Go program that consumes the recipe from [[faif.1]] and produces a deterministic `bd init` + create-issues sequence (or a single seed JSON file) representing the scale fixture. Fixed RNG seed for reproducibility. Add a `mise run fixture:generate` task.

## Instructions

1. Read [[faif.1]] recipe schema before designing.
2. Create `internal/testing/e2e/embeddedfixture/scale/generator.go` (or `cmd/fixture-gen/main.go` if a standalone binary reads better — pick after weighing repo conventions).
3. The generator:
   - Loads `recipe.yaml`.
   - Uses a fixed-seed RNG (e.g., `rand.New(rand.NewSource(42))` or seed from recipe).
   - Produces output in one of two forms — pick the simpler:
     - **Form A**: a single `scale-seed.json` file matching the format of the existing `seed.json` (which can be replayed by `setup.sh`). Pro: same setup pipeline; con: needs the setup.sh to support all the things in the recipe (parents, dependencies, etc.).
     - **Form B**: a shell-script of `bd create/update/close/dep add` commands that build the fixture. Pro: most direct; con: slower per-test setup.
   - I lean **Form A** — extend setup.sh capability as needed (smaller change than building a new pipeline).
4. The generator must be idempotent — running it twice produces byte-identical output.
5. Add `mise run fixture:generate`:
   ```toml
   [tasks."fixture:generate"]
   description = "Generate the scale fixture seed from the recipe"
   dir = "{{config_root}}"
   run = "go run ./internal/testing/e2e/embeddedfixture/scale/cmd/generator > internal/testing/e2e/embeddedfixture/scale-seed.json"
   ```
6. Document the generated `scale-seed.json` as a build artifact (commit it OR re-generate on every test run — pick after measuring generation time; if < 1s, regenerate; if longer, commit).
7. Add a unit test that runs the generator and asserts the output is byte-identical to a committed reference (catches accidental nondeterminism).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Generator compiles and runs from `mise run fixture:generate`
- [ ] Output is byte-identical across runs (determinism test passes)
- [ ] Generated fixture honors every count/threshold from the recipe
- [ ] Generator completes in < 5 seconds (target < 1s; budget for soft cap)
- [ ] If output is a JSON seed: setup.sh works against it unchanged or with documented extensions
- [ ] `mise run quality` passes

## Files to Modify

- create `internal/testing/e2e/embeddedfixture/scale/generator.go` (or `cmd/fixture-gen/main.go`)
- possibly extend `internal/testing/e2e/embeddedfixture/setup.sh`
- update `.mise.toml` (`fixture:generate` task)
- possibly commit `internal/testing/e2e/embeddedfixture/scale-seed.json` (decision per step 6)
close_reason: Merged into beads-workbench-faif.1 per epic-review 2026-05-16. Recipe→generator→verifier 3-layer abstraction descoped; scale-seed.json delivered as a committed JSON artifact instead.

title: Acceptance Review: Harden gateway write contract
description:
## Description

Gate task for epic [[9x70]]. Verifies the write contract is documented, machine-checked, and that the "Discovered-during-audit bug discipline" was followed.

**Note (epic-review 2026-05-16):** validatingGateway write extension (was 9x70.4) was descoped. Write-side runtime validation deferred until a real consumer divergence justifies it. This acceptance review covers only the doc + RunWriteContract + fake-fidelity work.

title: Audit FakeBeadsGateway write methods against RunWriteContract; close fidelity gaps
description:
## Description

Run the new `RunWriteContract` from [[9x70.2]] against `FakeBeadsGateway` write methods. **Conditional task (re-shaped per epic-review 2026-05-16):** the original "audit & close gaps" framing pre-allocated closure work. Re-shape: file each surfaced gap as its own bug per discipline. If 9x70.2 surfaces zero fake-write divergences, this task closes as a no-op with that rationale.

## Instructions

1. Run `go test -tags=integration ./internal/gateway/beads/contract/... -run TestFakeGatewayWriteContract` and triage failures.
2. For each failure, decide:
   - **Fake gap**: real bd does X (per audit), fake does Y. File a bug per the parent epic's "Discovered-during-audit bug discipline": `bd create --type=bug --title="Fake write fidelity gap: <method> <X-vs-Y>"`, link back via `bd dep add <bug-id> beads-workbench-9x70.3`.
   - **Contract gap**: assertion is too strict. Soften or document as a carve-out in [[9x70.1]]'s contract — file as a bug against 9x70.1 instead.
   - **Real bd quirk**: assertion expected something bd doesn't honor. Fix the assertion; document in interface.go.
3. The fake currently treats writes as opaque (the `FakeBeadsGateway` likely just stores inputs and the test reads them back). For RunWriteContract to pass, the fake needs to make writes OBSERVABLE through subsequent reads — Create returns an issue that ShowIssue can find; Update mutates state ShowIssue reflects; etc. This may require extending the fake's internal state model — but only do this work HERE if no consumer bug is surfaced; otherwise file a focused bug.
4. Update the drift log in `internal/testing/fakes/doc.go` with entries for each material fake-write update.
5. If no failures surface, close this task with: "RunWriteContract passes against fake on first run; no fidelity gaps detected. No bugs filed."

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Either: every fake-write fidelity gap from 9x70.2 is filed as its own bug AND fixed (with drift-log entry), OR: no gaps surfaced and the closure comment documents that
- [ ] `mise run test:integration` passes end-to-end (no consumer regressions)
- [ ] Newly-discovered consumer bugs filed per discipline, linked back to this task

## Files to Modify

- `internal/testing/fakes/beads_gateway.go` (only if gaps surface)
- `internal/testing/fakes/doc.go` (drift log — only if updates made)
- possibly `internal/gateway/beads/contract/contract.go` (if assertions need softening)
