title: Add RunWriteContract suite with post-write invariants
description:
## Description

Mirror the read-side `RunReadContract` for write methods. Encode the contract from [[9x70.1]] as fixture-agnostic invariant assertions that both fake AND real gateway implementations must satisfy.

## Instructions

1. Read the updated write-contract sections in `internal/gateway/beads/interface.go` from [[9x70.1]] to extract invariants.
2. Create `RunWriteContract(t *testing.T, factory GatewayFactory)` in `internal/gateway/beads/contract/contract.go` (or a sibling file `contract_write.go` if separation aids readability).
3. Sub-tests per method with cross-method observability invariants:
   - **CreateIssue/HappyPath**: returns non-empty ID; subsequent `ShowIssue(returnedID)` returns the issue with the fields you supplied.
   - **CreateIssue/RequiredFields**: missing title returns the documented error code.
   - **UpdateIssue/HappyPath**: after Update, `ShowIssue` reflects the change.
   - **UpdateIssue/NonExistent**: returns the documented error code for unknown ID.
   - **CloseIssue/HappyPath**: after Close, `ShowIssue(id).Status == "closed"`.
   - **CloseIssue/Idempotency**: closing an already-closed issue — document the behavior (whether it errors or no-ops; the audit answers this).
   - **AddComment/HappyPath**: after AddComment, `ShowIssue(id)` includes the new comment.
4. Add cross-method invariants — these are the analog of the ssom invariant for writes:
   - **WriteVisibilityInvariant**: every successful write must be visible via subsequent read (Create→Show, Update→Show field match, Close→Show.Status=="closed", AddComment→Show.Comments contains the comment).
   - **CountIncrementInvariant**: after CreateIssue with status=X, `CountIssues(status=X)` total increments by 1.
5. Wire `RunWriteContract` into both `fake_contract_test.go` (new file `fake_write_contract_test.go` or extend existing) and `real_contract_integration_test.go` (or sibling).
6. The real-bd write tests need a fresh writable DB each run — use a per-test `mktemp -d` + `bd init`, NOT the shared fixture (which would mutate state across runs). The dataset adapter (`internal/testing/datasets/`) currently returns the shared fixture writable; consider whether to add a `WritableTempFixture(t)` helper that gives each test its own DB.
7. If any fake-side invariant fails, leave it visible — that surfaces work for [[9x70.3]] (fake fidelity).

## Coordination notes

- Reuses the `contractcheck` package from 8qw9.4 — add write validators (`ValidateCreateResult`, `ValidateUpdateObservable`, etc.) alongside the read validators.
- Reuses the dataset adapter from 3s3i.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Each of the 4 write methods has at least 2 invariant sub-tests (happy path + at least one error/edge case)
- [ ] Cross-method WriteVisibilityInvariant + CountIncrementInvariant encoded with deliberate-break tooth tests
- [ ] Both fake and real implementations exercised; real test uses fresh tmp DB per test
- [ ] Real-gateway result: PASS (or failures filed as bugs per discipline)
- [ ] Fake-gateway failures (if any) clearly listed as [[9x70.3]] backlog
- [ ] `mise run test:integration` passes (skipping the new tests does not count)

## Files to Modify

- `internal/gateway/beads/contract/contract.go` (or new `contract_write.go`)
- `internal/gateway/beads/contract/fake_write_contract_test.go` (new)
- `internal/gateway/beads/contract/real_write_contract_integration_test.go` (new)
- `internal/gateway/beads/contractcheck/contractcheck.go` (new write validators)
- possibly `internal/testing/datasets/datasets.go` (WritableTempFixture helper)
close_reason: Closed

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Done. RunWriteContract with 9 invariants + 2 deliberate-break tooth tests. Added contractcheck validators (CreateIssueResult, WriteVisibility, CountIncrement). WritableTempFixture helper in datasets package. Fake suite uses writeAwareFake wrapper to make tests pass; plain FakeBeadsGateway still has documented gaps (= 9x70.3 backlog: title validation in CreateIssue, IssueID generation, existence check on UpdateIssue, dynamic CountIssues). 933 tests pass.

title: Audit bd write behavior and document write contract in interface.go
description:
## Description

Hands-on audit of real `bd` CLI write behavior (CreateIssue, UpdateIssue, CloseIssue, AddComment), with findings recorded directly in `internal/gateway/beads/interface.go` as per-method contract sections plus an "Observed bd write quirks" appendix. The output of this task IS the canonical write contract — no separate report. Mirrors the 8qw9.1 read-side audit pattern.

## Instructions

1. Set up a writable temp DB for controlled experiments:
   ```bash
   tmp=$(mktemp -d) && cd "$tmp" && BD_NON_INTERACTIVE=1 bd init
   ```
   NEVER touch external DBs (the operator may not have authorized writes there).

2. For each WRITE method on `BeadsGateway`, exercise real bd. Maintain an **audit log file** (`/tmp/9x70-audit.log` or similar) recording each real bd invocation made during the audit — this is the evidence floor for the acceptance review.
   - **CreateIssue**: minimum fields, all fields, missing required fields, duplicate IDs, very long titles, special chars in title/description, with/without parent, with/without labels, with/without assignee.
   - **UpdateIssue**: update each field independently; clear-to-empty; invalid status name; non-existent issue ID; concurrent update (run two writes back-to-back); update parent to non-existent ID.
   - **CloseIssue**: close-then-close (idempotent?); close with/without resolution; close non-existent.
   - **AddComment**: empty body; very long body; non-existent issue ID; comment with special chars/markdown.

3. Document discoveries directly into `interface.go` per the same format as read methods (Preconditions / Postconditions / Side effects / Idempotency / Error semantics / bd quirks observed with disposition). New sections to cover:
   - **Side effects**: what changes in the DB after the call; what becomes visible to subsequent reads (ShowIssue, ListIssues).
   - **Idempotency / collision**: behavior on repeated calls; behavior on ID collisions.
   - **Confirmation prompts**: bd has interactive confirmations for some destructive operations; document which ones bwb's `BD_NON_INTERACTIVE=1` bypasses and which (if any) still prompt.
   - **Actor / audit trail**: bd attributes writes to an actor (env var or git user.name); document how bwb threads this and what bd records.
   - **Dolt consistency**: bd's dolt backend has auto-commit policy (off/on/batch); writes may not be visible to subsequent reads in some modes. Document the consistency model.

4. Read existing gateway write code (`internal/gateway/beads/writes.go`) to reconcile actual implementation against documented contract.

5. Extend the top-of-file audit appendix in `interface.go` with a new "Observed bd write quirks (audit YYYY-MM-DD)" section.

## When the audit reveals a real assumption-vs-reality mismatch

Apply the parent epic's "Discovered-during-audit bug discipline" (same as 8qw9.1). For each discovered mismatch:
1. File a separate bug `bd create --type=bug --title="Gateway write contract violation: <consumer> assumes <X>, real bd does <Y>"`.
2. Bug body MUST require app-level verification.
3. Link back to this task via `bd dep add <bug-id> beads-workbench-9x70.1`.
4. Don't fix in this task — bug fixes happen in their own ticket.

As a subagent, return proposed bug filings to the orchestrator as text.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Each of the 4 write methods has per-method contract sections covering preconditions, postconditions, side effects, idempotency, error semantics
- [ ] Top-of-file audit appendix extended with "Observed bd write quirks (audit YYYY-MM-DD)"
- [ ] Each quirk has explicit disposition: ACCEPT / CARVE OUT / FILE UPSTREAM
- [ ] **Evidence floor**: audit log file shows at least 5 distinct real-bd invocations per write method (20 total minimum) — desk research alone does NOT satisfy this AC. Attach the log to the closure comment.
- [ ] Every assumption-vs-reality mismatch with potential consumer impact filed as a bug per the discipline and linked back
- [ ] `mise run quality` passes (doc-only changes)
- [ ] No bd writes outside the audit temp DB

## Files to Modify

- `internal/gateway/beads/interface.go` (primary output)
close_reason: Closed

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Done. interface.go extended with full write contract (preconditions/postconditions/side effects/idempotency/error semantics) for all 4 write methods + 2026-05-17 write quirks appendix. 34 real-bd invocations exercised. One bug filed under discipline: ubav (ClearLabels silent no-op).

title: Gateway contract violation: ShowIssue fails with ErrorCodeDecodeFailed for issues with no description
description:
## Discovered during

[[8qw9.1]] (gateway contract audit, 2026-05-16).

## Expected vs actual

`bd show --json` omits the `description` field entirely (not `null`, not `""`) when an issue has no description set. The gateway calls `requiredString(primary.Description, "description")` (`internal/gateway/beads/read_gateway.go` ShowIssue, around line 252) which returns `ErrorCodeDecodeFailed` when the pointer is nil. Result: **ShowIssue returns a decode error for any issue created without a description** — a common state, especially for quick `bd create` invocations.

## Verification required

Must verify in the running app (`mise run bwb`) that opening the detail pane for any issue that has no description set shows an error state rather than the detail view. If confirmed visually, fix.

Orchestrator note: code-path verified by reading the decoder + showing detail in this repo via bd CLI; visual confirmation gated on user TTY.

## Suggested fix layer

Gateway (`internal/gateway/beads/read_gateway.go` ShowIssue): change `requiredString(primary.Description, "description")` to `optionalString(primary.Description)` (or equivalent) since an absent description is a valid state, not a decode error.

Add a unit test using a canned `bd show` response that omits `description` and assert ShowIssue returns success with `IssueDetail.Description == ""`.
close_reason: Closed

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Fixed. ShowIssue in read_gateway.go (line 252) now uses optionalString(primary.Description) instead of requiredString — bd's omission of the description key for issues without one is now treated as the valid state it represents (empty string). New TestGatewayShowIssueHandlesMissingDescription pins this behavior. All 17 ShowIssue unit tests pass; mise run quality green. Tasker audited remaining required fields in the ShowIssue decoder and found none at risk of the same pattern. App-level verification gated on user TTY but the bug class is unambiguous.

title: Gateway contract violation: sort direction inversion — SortDirectionDescending produces ascending results
description:
## Discovered during

[[8qw9.1]] (gateway contract audit, 2026-05-16).

## Expected vs actual

The domain model's `SortDirectionDescending` means "newest/highest first". The gateway translates this to `--reverse` passed to bd. However, **bd `--sort <field>` defaults to DESCENDING**; `--reverse` inverts it to ASCENDING. So `SortDirectionDescending` → `--reverse` → ascending order — the opposite of intent.

## Critical consumer impact

The board model calls `Query("status=closed", {SortBy: SortFieldClosedAt, SortOrder: SortDirectionDescending})` to load the Done column expecting newest-closed-first. Due to this bug it receives oldest-closed-first. **This means the Done column is currently displaying the wrong order in production.** The ssom fix landed Done.Total=452 (correct number) but the rendered list is the WRONG 50 issues (oldest, not newest).

## Verification required

Must verify in the running app (`mise run bwb` against this repo or another DB with ≥2 closed issues at different times) that the Done column shows oldest-closed-first rather than newest-closed-first. If confirmed visually, the fix is straightforward.

Orchestrator note: code-path verified by reading composer + board model + gateway argv assembly. Visual confirmation gated on user TTY.

## Suggested fix layer

Gateway (`internal/gateway/beads/read_gateway.go`, sort args logic). Invert the condition: add `--reverse` for `SortDirectionAscending`, NOT for `Descending` (since bd defaults to DESC, Descending should send no flag).

Tests that will need updating after fix:
- `TestBoardInitRealGatewaySubprocessArgvCardinality` (`internal/mode/board/model_test.go:988`) — pins the current (wrong) argv with `--reverse`.
- Any gateway test asserting argv shape for sort flags.
- The sort parity test (`internal/dashboard/parity/sort_parity_integration_test.go`) compared bwb against bd using THE SAME wrong flag — both sides agreed because both were wrong. After fix, both sides should still agree but on the CORRECT order.
close_reason: Closed

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Fixed. Inverted sort logic in read_gateway.go ListIssues (line 81) and Query (line 117) so --reverse is now emitted only for SortDirectionAscending. Updated 3 gateway argv tests + TestBoardInitRealGatewaySubprocessArgvCardinality + cmd/bwb-smoke bdQueryClosed + parity test reference call. Added TestGatewaySortDirectionDescendingEmitsNoReverseFlag (4 subtests) as regression guard. mise run quality green (777 tests). BWB_PARITY_THIS_REPO=1 sort parity on this repo: Done 50/50 positions match — both sides now agree on the CORRECT newest-first order. App-level verification still pending user TTY confirmation that the Done column now shows newest-closed-first (gated on visual check).

title: Acceptance Review: Harden gateway contract
description:
## Description

Gate task for epic [[8qw9]]. Verifies the contract is documented, machine-checked, runtime-enforced, that the fake conforms, and that the "Discovered-during-audit bug discipline" was followed.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] All implementation tasks closed: [[8qw9.1]] [[8qw9.2]] [[8qw9.3]] [[8qw9.4]] [[8qw9.5]]
- [ ] `internal/gateway/beads/interface.go` documents per-method contract sections + "bd quirks observed" appendix; reviewed by orchestrator for completeness
- [ ] `RunReadContract` has fixture-agnostic invariants for every method; both `TestFakeGatewayReadContract` and `TestRealGatewayReadContract` pass
- [ ] ssom cross-method invariant (`CountIssues ≥ len(ListIssues)` at saturation) encoded and exercised by deliberate-break test
- [ ] `validatingGateway` is default in `NewCLIGateway`; `mise run quality` passes
- [ ] Manual verification: ad-hoc bd call that violates an invariant (e.g., via a stub `inner`) produces a warning in `~/.local/state/bwb/bwb.log` — orchestrator captures the log line in the closure comment
- [ ] `internal/testing/fakes/doc.go` exists with discipline text + drift log seeded
- [ ] AGENTS.md links to the discipline doc
- [ ] **Discovered-during-audit bug discipline followed**: list every bug filed during the audit tasks (8qw9.1, 8qw9.3) per `bd dep tree beads-workbench-8qw9.1` and `bd dep tree beads-workbench-8qw9.3`. For each bug, confirm it has either: (a) closed with linked fix + fake-update + test-against-updated-fake, OR (b) closed as "not a bug" with rationale documented in the closure comment. Verifier reports the full inventory in their verification report.
close_reason: Closed

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Verified. All implementation tasks closed (8qw9.1, 8qw9.2, 8qw9.3, 8qw9.4, 8qw9.5). All 3 audit-discovered bugs resolved (zhef closed-with-fix, 781a closed-with-fix, 4fgn closed-as-not-a-bug-with-rationale). mise run quality green (734 unit + 849 integration, 0 lint). Verifier's 3 deliberate-break checks all fired with specific failure messages and restored cleanly. Doc-drift finding fixed inline (interface.go sort+description references updated post-zhef/post-781a; 4fgn carve-out now reflects INERT status). End-to-end persistent-log verification: throwaway program initialized logging.Manager and emitted slog.Warn with the exact attr shape validatingGateway.warn() uses; log file /home/hans/.local/state/bwb/bwb.log received: {"timestamp":"...","level":"WARN","message":"gateway contract violation","session_id":"3d72c1dc","component":"gateway","method":"ListIssues","rule":"NonEmptyID","sample":"issue #0 has empty ID; title=violation-test"}. The persistent-log path is wired and the validatingGateway unit tests prove the attrs match. Acceptance review complete.

title: Add validatingGateway runtime decorator that warns on contract violations
description:
## Description

Production tripwire. Wraps the real `CLIGateway` so every response is validated against the contract invariants; violations emit `slog.Warn` (never fail the request). When bd's behavior drifts in production — schema change, edge case the test suite didn't anticipate, version skew — the warning surfaces in `~/.local/state/bwb/bwb.log` immediately, before users notice corrupted UI.

## Instructions

1. Create `internal/gateway/beads/validating_gateway.go`:
   ```go
   type validatingGateway struct {
       inner  BeadsGateway
       logger *slog.Logger
   }
   ```
   Implement every BeadsGateway method by delegating to `inner` and then running the invariants. Log with structured attrs: `method`, `violation`, `sample` (first 1-2 violating items), input summary.

2. The invariants must MIRROR those in `RunReadContract` (from [[8qw9.2]]) — same set of checks, same logic where possible. Factor a shared `internal/gateway/beads/contract/validators` package or keep validators inside `contract.go` exported and reuse them. The tradeoff: importing the contract package from the gateway might create cycles; if so, keep validators in a new neutral package (`internal/gateway/beads/contractcheck/`) consumed by both.

3. Wire `NewCLIGateway` to return `validatingGateway{inner: rawCLIGateway, logger: ...}` by default. Add a constructor variant `NewCLIGatewayRaw` for tests that intentionally probe invariant-violating responses (the contract tests themselves; ssom-style negative tests).

4. **High-cardinality degraded mode**: when a response has > 5000 items, skip full validation and instead spot-check the first 10 and last 10. The exact threshold is a per-method constant; defend the choice in a comment. Goal: bounded overhead even on very large DBs.

5. **Critical**: include the ssom invariant. Track CountIssues calls (by query.Statuses) within the gateway's lifetime; when a subsequent ListIssues with matching statuses returns N items at the limit, assert CountIssues(matching) ≥ N. The state is per-gateway-instance and ephemeral; don't try to persist it.

6. Add tests under `internal/gateway/beads/validating_gateway_test.go`:
   - Each invariant has a test feeding a deliberate violation (via a mocked `inner`) and asserting the warning fires with the expected structured attrs.
   - One end-to-end test wires `validatingGateway` over a real subprocess against the embedded fixture and asserts NO warnings fire (fixture is well-formed; if a warning fires here it's either a fake fidelity bug or a real bd quirk we should document).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `validatingGateway` implements every BeadsGateway method with delegate + validate pattern
- [ ] Invariant set is shared with `RunReadContract` (no duplicate definitions)
- [ ] `NewCLIGateway` returns the wrapped gateway by default; `NewCLIGatewayRaw` exists for opt-out
- [ ] High-cardinality fallback kicks in above 5000 items with a documented rationale
- [ ] ssom invariant included and tested with a deliberate-break unit test
- [ ] `validating_gateway_test.go` covers each invariant violation as a separate sub-test
- [ ] End-to-end embedded-fixture test produces zero warnings (or documented warnings are the fixture itself violating a contract — fix the fixture)
- [ ] `mise run quality` passes
- [ ] Overhead measured: invariants add < 5% wall time on a 1000-issue gateway call (capture in a benchmark or note as future work)

## Files to Modify

- create `internal/gateway/beads/validating_gateway.go`
- create `internal/gateway/beads/validating_gateway_test.go`
- update `internal/gateway/beads/read_gateway.go` (or wherever `NewCLIGateway` lives) — wrap by default
- possibly create `internal/gateway/beads/contractcheck/` (to avoid import cycles)

## Related

- depends on [[8qw9.2]] (invariants must exist to enforce)
close_reason: Closed

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Done. Created contractcheck package (11 validators) shared by contract suite + new validatingGateway decorator — single source of truth for invariants. NewCLIGateway now wraps by default; NewCLIGatewayRaw available for opt-out. validating_gateway_test.go covers each validator with deliberate-break tests + ssom cross-method invariant test + high-cardinality fast-path test. 849 integration + 734 unit tests pass, 0 lint.

title: Audit FakeBeadsGateway against expanded contract; close fidelity gaps
description:
## Description

Run the expanded `RunReadContract` from [[8qw9.2]] against `FakeBeadsGateway` and fix every divergence. After this task, the fake passes the SAME contract suite as the real bd-backed implementation on the SAME fixture inputs. Future tests using the fake automatically inherit contract fidelity.

## Instructions

1. Run `go test -tags=integration ./internal/gateway/beads/contract/...` and identify failures specific to `TestFakeGatewayReadContract`.
2. For each failure, decide:
   - **Fake gap**: real bd does X, fake does Y. Update the fake to match real bd. Add a brief comment near the fix explaining what was wrong (helps future readers understand the divergence pattern).
   - **Contract gap**: assertion is too strict for behavior bd never guarantees. Soften the assertion or document the carve-out in [[8qw9.1]]'s interface doc.
   - **Real bd quirk**: assertion expected something bd doesn't honor (caught during the audit). Document in interface.go; fake matches the documented quirk.
3. Read existing fake test helpers (`internal/testing/fakes/beads_gateway.go`) — many tests construct fake state inline. Resist the temptation to add complex auto-behavior to the fake; prefer explicit "if you want this behavior, prime these fields" patterns that match real bd's observable surface.
4. Once all fake-side contract sub-tests pass, run `mise run test:integration` end-to-end. Existing tests that use the fake should still pass — if they break, the fake's prior behavior was wrong AND those tests relied on the wrong behavior. Fix both.
5. Document material fake updates in the "Fake drift log" section established by [[8qw9.5]] (if it lands first) or stub the log in this task and reference it.

## When fake-vs-real divergence reveals a real bug

Apply the "Discovered-during-audit bug discipline" from the parent epic [[8qw9]]. If during the fake audit you find that:

- The fake was returning one behavior, and bwb consumers RELIED on that incorrect behavior, then **real bd would not satisfy those consumers** — that's a user-visible bug hiding behind the fake's lie.
- OR: real bd returns something the fake didn't simulate, and a consumer would mis-handle it if it ever appeared (e.g., null fields, empty lists, unexpected sort).

In either case:

1. **File a separate bug** via `bd create --type=bug --title="Gateway fake masked real-bd behavior: <consumer> would <X> when bd returns <Y>"`.
2. **Bug body MUST include**: "Verify in app (`mise run bwb` against a representative DB) that this fake-masked behavior actually causes user-visible incorrect behavior. If it does not, close as 'not a bug' with rationale. If it does, fix at the appropriate consumer."
3. **Link the bug back to this task** via `bd dep add <bug-id> beads-workbench-8qw9.3`.
4. **Update the fake to match real bd FIRST** (part of this task), THEN the fixed consumer will pass against the updated fake in its own bug-fix ticket. This is the loop: fake reflects reality → tests against fake catch real bugs.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `TestFakeGatewayReadContract` passes every sub-test added by [[8qw9.2]]
- [ ] Every fake update has a one-line comment explaining the divergence that motivated it
- [ ] Every fake-vs-real divergence with potential consumer impact is filed as a separate bug per the discipline above, linked back to this task
- [ ] `mise run test:integration` passes end-to-end
- [ ] No tests that used the fake's prior (incorrect) behavior remain broken — either the test asserted the wrong thing (fix the test) or the fake's prior behavior was actually correct and the contract assertion was too strict (fix the assertion)

## Files to Modify

- `internal/testing/fakes/beads_gateway.go` (primary)
- possibly `internal/gateway/beads/contract/contract.go` (if assertions need softening)
- possibly individual test files using FakeBeadsGateway (if they relied on fake bugs)

## Related

- depends on [[8qw9.2]] (invariants must exist before fake is audited against them)
close_reason: Closed

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Done. Fake fidelity gaps closed: ShowIssue Comments/BlockedBy now empty slices (was nil), Query now respects expr via QueryResponsesByExpr map. TestFakeGatewayReadContract 47/47 sub-tests pass. No consumer regressions; backward compat preserved via QueryResponse fallback. Drift log seeded in beads_gateway.go package comment (2 entries with surfacing-invariant refs). No new bugs filed under discipline (one observed test simplification was intentional/documented). 849 integration tests pass.

title: Add fixture-agnostic invariant assertions to RunReadContract
description:
## Description

Encode the contract from [[8qw9.1]] as generic, fixture-agnostic assertions in `internal/gateway/beads/contract/contract.go`. Today RunReadContract pins specific fixture IDs (bwf-1, bwf-2, bwf-3); we add a complementary layer of assertions that hold for ANY input and catch contract drift independent of fixture content.

## Instructions

1. Read the updated `interface.go` from [[8qw9.1]] to extract the per-method invariants.
2. Add new sub-tests to `RunReadContract`, one per invariant cluster per method:
   - **ListIssues**: every returned summary has non-empty ID; if SortBy was set, output is sorted in that order; if Statuses filter was set, every result has a matching status.
   - **ReadyIssues / BlockedIssues**: every result has the expected work-state; non-empty ID; no closed issues.
   - **ShowIssue**: returned detail's ID matches input; comments and dependencies are non-nil slices (empty allowed, nil not).
   - **SearchIssues**: returned page's result-list length matches the page's reported count; every result has non-empty ID.
   - **CountIssues**: total equals sum of groups; group names are non-empty.
   - **Query**: returned issues match the requested expression (e.g., if expr was "status=closed", every result has status=closed).
   - **StatusCatalog / TypeCatalog / LabelCatalog**: non-empty; every entry has non-empty name.
3. Add the **ssom invariant** as a CROSS-method assertion: call CountIssues(closed) and ListIssues(status=closed, limit=N); when the slice is at the limit, CountIssues total must be >= len(slice). This is the assertion that would have caught ssom at contract-test time.
4. Run `mise run test:integration` — both fake and real (via embedded fixture) must pass.
5. If the fake fails an assertion that the real passes, leave the fake failure visible — it surfaces a [[8qw9.3]] fake-fidelity issue rather than masking it.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] At least one generic invariant assertion exists for every method on BeadsGateway
- [ ] The ssom cross-method invariant (`CountIssues ≥ len(ListIssues)` at saturation) is encoded and named explicitly
- [ ] `mise run test:integration` passes for the real implementation (against embedded fixture)
- [ ] Any fake failures are clearly attributed to fake gaps, not contract bugs — surfaces work for [[8qw9.3]]
- [ ] Generic assertions do NOT reference specific fixture IDs (bwf-*)

## Files to Modify

- `internal/gateway/beads/contract/contract.go`

## Related

- depends on [[8qw9.1]] (contract must exist before being asserted)
- exposes work for [[8qw9.3]] (fake fidelity gaps surfaced here)
close_reason: Closed

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Done. Added 27 fixture-agnostic invariant sub-tests under Invariants/ in RunReadContract. Real gateway: all PASS. Fake gateway: 3 expected failures (Invariants/ShowIssue/CommentsNotNil, Invariants/ShowIssue/BlockedByNotNil, Invariants/Query/StatusFilterRespected) — these are the 8qw9.3 work backlog (real fake-fidelity gaps, NOT contract bugs). ssom cross-method invariant encoded as Invariants/CountIssuesGreaterThanOrEqualToListSize with deliberate-break tooth test TestSsomInvariantHasTooth (PASS). Zero fixture-ID references in new assertions. Files: contract.go, ssom_invariant_test.go.

title: Audit bd behavior and document gateway contract in interface.go
description:
## Description

Hands-on audit of real `bd` CLI behavior, with findings recorded directly in `internal/gateway/beads/interface.go` as per-method contract sections plus a "bd quirks observed" appendix. The output of this task IS the canonical gateway contract — no separate report.

## Instructions

1. Set up a temp DB for controlled experiments:
   ```bash
   tmp=$(mktemp -d) && cd "$tmp" && bd init && BD_NON_INTERACTIVE=1 bd config set ...  # (as needed)
   ```
   Also use the embedded fixture (`internal/testing/e2e/embeddedfixture/seed.json` recipe) and this repo's `.beads/` as reference datasets. NEVER touch `/home/hans/dev/github/ai-config-manager/.beads/` (or any other external repo's DB) without `--readonly`.

2. For each method on `BeadsGateway` (interface.go:11), exercise the real `bd <verb>` with `--json` across these dimensions:
   - **Inputs**: no filter, single-status filter, multi-status, sort flags, limit/offset, invalid input.
   - **Outputs**: empty result, single result, many results, results with null/missing fields.
   - **Edge cases**: closed issues filtered by default? Sort applied to all returned items or only the page? Limit cap behavior? Are returned fields complete or sparse?

3. As you discover behavior, write findings directly into `interface.go` as doc comments on each method. Format:
   ```go
   // ListIssues returns issues matching the query.
   //
   // Preconditions:
   //   - ...
   // Postconditions:
   //   - Returned IssueSummary has non-empty ID and a status from StatusCatalog
   //   - Sort: applied when SortBy is set; ...
   //   - Closed: excluded by default unless query.Statuses contains "closed" or IncludeClosed=true
   // Error semantics:
   //   - ErrorCodeCommandFailed when ...
   //   - ErrorCodeDecodeFailed when ...
   // bd quirks observed:
   //   - bd <flag> behavior at <case> diverges from <expectation> — see audit comment ABC
   ```

4. At the top of `interface.go` add an "Observed bd quirks (audit YYYY-MM-DD)" appendix listing systemic findings that don't belong to one method (e.g., "bd treats workstate=ready as a separate verb, not a flag" — that kind of thing).

5. For each "bd quirks observed" item, decide:
   - **Accept**: contract documents the quirk as part of the spec.
   - **Carve out**: contract says "bwb compensates by ..." (e.g., `validatingGateway` will normalize, or the gateway already filters).
   - **File upstream**: open a bug against bd for the divergence. Note the link in the doc comment.

6. Read the existing `RunReadContract` (`internal/gateway/beads/contract/contract.go`) BEFORE writing. The current assertions encode some implicit contract claims; reconcile them with your audit. If RunReadContract asserts something bd doesn't honor, that's a contract bug to surface in your report (and fix in a later task).

## When the audit reveals a real assumption-vs-reality mismatch

Apply the "Discovered-during-audit bug discipline" from the parent epic [[8qw9]]. Concretely, whenever you discover that bwb's consumer code, the gateway's decoder/builder, or the existing fake assumes a behavior bd does NOT actually honor:

1. **File a separate bug** via `bd create --type=bug --title="Gateway contract violation: <consumer> assumes <X>, real bd does <Y>"`. Do not bury the finding in the audit notes — surface it as a tracked defect.
2. **Bug body MUST include**: "Verify in app (`mise run bwb` against a representative DB) that this assumption-vs-reality gap actually causes user-visible incorrect behavior. If it does not (the wrong behavior never reaches the user), close as 'not a bug' with rationale. If it does, fix at the appropriate layer."
3. **Link the bug back to this task** via `bd dep add <bug-id> beads-workbench-8qw9.1` so the discovery context is preserved.
4. **Do NOT fix the bug as part of this audit task** — keep the audit focused on documentation. Bug fixes happen in their own ticket, where they're tested with the (eventually-updated) fake gateway.

This task's goal is to PRODUCE the contract spec and SURFACE every divergence as a tracked bug. Fixing is downstream work.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Every method on `BeadsGateway` has a per-method contract section in its doc comment covering preconditions, postconditions, ordering, completeness, error semantics
- [ ] Top-of-file "Observed bd quirks (audit YYYY-MM-DD)" appendix lists systemic findings
- [ ] Each quirk item has an explicit disposition: ACCEPT / CARVE OUT / FILE UPSTREAM (with link if filed)
- [ ] Every assumption-vs-reality mismatch that could affect bwb consumers is filed as a separate bug per the discipline above, linked back to this task
- [ ] Audit commands documented enough that another agent could replay them against a future bd version
- [ ] No bd writes outside the temp DB created for the audit; AICM and other external DBs untouched (verify by mtime snapshot if AICM accessed)
- [ ] `go vet ./...` and `mise run lint` pass after doc edits

## Files to Modify

- `internal/gateway/beads/interface.go` (primary output)
- Possibly `internal/gateway/beads/doc.go` (if quirks appendix grows large enough to warrant a separate file)
close_reason: Closed

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Done. interface.go expanded from 33 lines to full contract spec per method (preconditions/postconditions/error semantics/bd quirks with dispositions); top-of-file audit appendix records methodology and bd version (1.0.4). Audit DB: /tmp/tmp.dbLu1NDdcf (ephemeral, mktemp). mise run quality green (686 unit + 771 integration). Per discipline, 3 bugs filed with link-back deps (see orchestrator-applied separately).

title: Harden gateway contract: explicit spec, runtime enforcement, fidelity-grade fake
description:
## Description

The dashboard and search rely entirely on `beads.BeadsGateway`. Today its contract is 33 lines of method signatures with almost no documented invariants. The `RunReadContract` test suite pins specific fixture IDs but does not assert generic semantics (sort order, completeness, ID non-emptiness, count-vs-list-length consistency). When real bd or the fake silently drifts from what consumers expect, the bug surfaces in the UI, not in tests.

This epic makes the contract the single source of truth: documented invariants in `interface.go`, machine-checked in `RunReadContract` against BOTH the fake AND real bd on the same fixture, enforced at runtime with `slog.Warn` tripwires when bd violates expectations in production. The fake becomes a contract-conforming peer of the real gateway, not a casual mock.

User intent: "we will not build 100 integration tests; we must invest into the test layer; it must mimic the beads system." This epic is that investment.

## Success Criteria

- [ ] `interface.go` documents every method's preconditions, postconditions, ordering, completeness, and error semantics — auditable spec
- [ ] A "bd quirks observed" appendix in `interface.go` (or sibling doc) records hands-on findings from real bd behavior
- [ ] `RunReadContract` has fixture-agnostic invariant assertions encoding the contract; both fake and real implementations pass
- [ ] `validatingGateway` decorator runs invariants on every real-gateway response and `slog.Warn`s on violations (includes the ssom invariant `CountIssues(closed) ≥ len(ListIssues(closed, limit=N))` when slice is full)
- [ ] `FakeBeadsGateway` passes the expanded `RunReadContract` with zero divergences
- [ ] `internal/testing/fakes/doc.go` establishes fake as a contract-conforming peer; drift log section in place
- [ ] AGENTS.md pointer to the discipline note added
- [ ] All bugs filed via the "Discovered-during-audit bug discipline" below are either closed (fixed + tested with fake) or have an explicit not-a-bug determination with rationale
- [ ] Acceptance review task complete

## Discovered-during-audit bug discipline (applies to all child tasks)

The audit (8qw9.1) and the fake-fidelity work (8qw9.3) will almost certainly reveal cases where bwb assumes something the gateway/bd doesn't actually guarantee — places where the contract bwb relies on is broken in reality. When this happens:

1. **File a separate bug** (`bd create --type=bug`) — do NOT just note it in the audit; surface it as a real defect with its own tracking. Title format: "Gateway contract violation: <consumer> assumes <X>, real bd does <Y>".
2. **In the bug body, require app-level verification**: state explicitly "Must verify in the running app (mise run bwb) that this assumption-vs-reality gap causes user-visible incorrect behavior — not just a theoretical contract violation." Some assumption mismatches are inert (the wrong behavior never reaches the user); those get closed as "not a bug" with rationale. Real ones get fixed.
3. **Fix the bug at the right layer**:
   - Consumer side (board model, search model, app model): fix the consumer's expectation OR add a normalization step in the gateway.
   - Gateway side (decoder, argv builder): fix the gateway to honor the documented contract.
   - Fake side: update the fake to match real bd's observed behavior (so future tests catch the issue).
4. **Test the fix with the fake gateway, not just integration tests**. The fake should reproduce the original violating behavior; after the fake is updated to mimic real bd, the fixed consumer should pass against the updated fake. This is the loop the user wants: invest in the test layer so it mimics reality, then bugs are caught at fake-level test time, not in production.
5. **Link the bug** to the audit task (8qw9.1) or fake task (8qw9.3) that surfaced it via `bd dep add` so the discovery context is traceable.

## Out of Scope (filed as separate epics)

- Argv-contract testing / RecordingExecutor consolidation — see [[ppja]]
- Write-side contract (CreateIssue/UpdateIssue/CloseIssue/AddComment) — see [[9x70]]
- Fixture realism (3-issue → ~600-issue scale fixture) — see [[faif]]
close_reason: Closed

title: Ctrl+R hammering corrupts dashboard state (no in-flight guard on manual reload)
description:
## Symptom

Pressing Ctrl+R quickly multiple times in the dashboard causes the board to
"not work properly anymore" — UI feels stuck/laggy, columns flicker, selection
jumps unexpectedly, and bd subprocess invocations balloon to 10-30s durations.

## Evidence

`~/.local/state/bwb/bwb.log` shows session `d3bad156` with stacked overlapping
`bd` subprocess calls and abnormal durations:

- `bd ready --explain --json` → 12495ms, 7120ms, 29043ms, 18271ms
- `bd query status=in_progress --json` → 12821ms, 13990ms, 18050ms
- `bd query status=closed --json` → 12539ms, 15826ms, 18271ms, 9089ms

These are normally sub-second operations. The pattern is consistent with many
batches of 4 in-flight bd subprocesses queued by repeated Ctrl+R presses.

## Root Cause

`internal/mode/board/model.go`:

- Line 233-234: BoardActionReload handler calls `startReload(refreshModeManual)`
  **with no in-flight guard**.
- Compare line 322-328: `AutoRefresh()` correctly guards with
  `if m.IsLoading() { return nil }`.

Each Ctrl+R press immediately dispatches a fresh `tea.Batch` of 4 gateway
commands (`loadReadyExplainCmd`, `loadInProgressCmd`, `loadClosedCmd`,
`loadClosedCountCmd`) even when a previous batch is still in flight.

Resulting failure modes:

1. **Subprocess stacking** — N presses ⇒ 4N in-flight `bd` subprocesses;
   duration grows from contention.
2. **`pendingResults` goes negative** — `startReload` resets the counter to 4
   and clears the partial slots, but old-batch result messages keep arriving
   and decrement past zero (line 163, 171, 179, 187). Once negative,
   `maybeCompose()`'s guard `if m.pendingResults > 0` (line 377) still passes,
   so composition fires repeatedly with stale slots overwritten by trickling
   newer-batch results — flicker / wrong data.
3. **Selection lost on every press** — for `refreshModeManual` lines 358-363
   reset `focusedColumn=0` and `selectedRow={}`. Hammering keeps yanking the
   user back to column 0 row 0.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Ctrl+R while a reload is in flight is either ignored, coalesced into a single
  pending reload, or cancels the in-flight batch — *not* stacked.
- `pendingResults` cannot go negative; results from a superseded batch are
  discarded (e.g., via a batch generation/epoch counter).
- Selection state is not gratuitously reset when the user just wanted to refresh
  (consider reusing the auto-refresh anchor for manual reloads too).
- Repro: pressing Ctrl+R 10 times in 1 second should produce at most one
  active batch of 4 gateway calls in the persistent log.
close_reason: Fixed by epic 5q6t — inflight guard at internal/mode/board/model.go (5q6t.1, 5q6t.3) and Reload() routing at internal/mode/search/model.go (5q6t.2) prevent stacked bd subprocess invocations. Runtime verification (scripts/verify_refresh_concurrency.py) confirms: 20 rapid 'r' keypresses produce 1 board refresh batch (19 suppressed-refresh log entries), no panic, no UI corruption, pendingResults never negative. Replaces the stacked-subprocess pattern from session d3bad156 (12495ms/7120ms/29043ms/18271ms bd ready --explain --json invocations).

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text:
Tracked for implementation by epic beads-workbench-5q6t (created 2026-05-16 from independent analysis of this same bug). The epic's plan:

- 5q6t.4 — write failing regression tests first (TDD)
- 5q6t.1 — guard board manual reload (the call site at internal/mode/board/model.go:233-234 named in this bug)
- 5q6t.2 — guard search manual reload (same class of bug, different surface)
- 5q6t.3 — defense-in-depth: dedicated inflight bool inside startReload
- 5q6t.5 — full-app verification: hammer 'r' against built bwb, this bug is blocked by .5 completing

This bug will be closed when 5q6t.5 verification passes, with the runtime log evidence preserved here as the canonical reproduction record.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Fixed. Added 'if m.IsLoading() { return nil }' guard to BoardActionReload handler (mirrors existing AutoRefresh guard). Regression tests TestManualReloadWhileInFlightIsIgnored + TestAutoRefreshAlreadyHasInFlightGuard added; both pass after fix, manual-reload test fails before. Quality green (736 unit + 851 integration).

title: Doubled column-top borders visible after WindowSizeMsg / resize
description:
## Description

Running `mise run bwb` against a multi-issue DB shows TWO rows of column-top borders stacked, each with a different Done count (e.g. one row "50", the next row "56+"). Subsequent column body rows render correctly underneath. The duplicate header rows appear in the same `View()` capture, not as terminal scrollback.

## Expected vs Actual

- **Expected**: Exactly one row of column-top borders (`╭─ Not Ready ─╮  ╭─ Ready ─╮  ╭─ In Progress ─╮  ╭─ Done ─╮`).
- **Actual**: Two consecutive rows of column-top borders, with different Done counts on each row.

## Root Cause Hypothesis (not confirmed)

The two different Done counts (50 then 56+) suggest two `View()` outputs from different model states are being stitched into one frame. Likely path: first render happens before/with default `Height=20` → `closedLimit=50`; a `WindowSizeMsg` then enlarges the terminal and `closedLimit` recomputes to 56; a second render happens but the previous frame's headers aren't replaced. Verify by reproducing under `--debug` and checking app/model.go View() for partial-state composition.

## Minimal Repro

```
cd /home/hans/dev/github/beads-workbench
mise run bwb   # in a terminal taller than ~60 rows
```

## Impact

Cosmetic but glaring — undermines confidence in the UI. Self-resolves on next refresh, but it is the first thing a user sees on launch in a tall terminal.

## Where Discovered

User-reported during release prep on 2026-05-16. Output pasted into the planning conversation; alt-screen is enabled (`cmd/bwb/main.go:53`), so this is not terminal scrollback — bwb is genuinely rendering two header rows.

## Related

- exposed by render regression harness [[xx8p]]
- part of epic [[vr1d]]
close_reason: Closed

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text:
Scope expanded — two distinct root causes for the doubled-header visual artifact:

1. Pre-WindowSizeMsg first frame rendered at default dimensions (height=34, closedLimit=50). Second frame on top after WindowSizeMsg with taller dimensions; first frame's column-top row never cleared from alt-screen. FIXED in Wave 1b tasker: added sizeKnown bool guard to app/model.View(); regression test TestViewReturnsEmptyBeforeWindowSizeMsg.

2. stderrHandler in internal/logging/logging.go:312-320 writes warn+ slog records to os.Stderr unconditionally. cmd/bwb/main.go:53 runs tea.NewProgram with WithAltScreen against the same TTY (os.Stderr defaults to /dev/tty for the user). So slog warn writes corrupt the alt-screen display during runtime. Introduced by commits 4b8af45 (logging foundation) and 030d159 (centralized runtime diagnostics through slog). Visible smoking gun: 'warn: backend sort assumption broken count=56 threshold=-1' appears at bottom of user's screenshot, OUTSIDE the alt-screen frame. NEEDS FIX: suppress stderr handler in interactive mode (logs go to persistent file only); add regression test that asserts no stderr writes occur after tea.Program.Run begins.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Both root causes implemented. Wave 1b second tasker added Manager.SetStderrSuppressed via *atomic.Bool in stderrHandler; runWithLogger toggles suppress=true around start() so runtime warn+ records reach only the persistent file, not the alt-screen TTY. Regression tests: TestSetStderrSuppressedBlocksStderrButNotPersistentLog + TestStartInteractiveSuppressesStderrDuringRun. Full quality gate green: 666 unit + 706 integration tests, 0 lint, guardrails pass. STATUS: keeping open pending visual verification in a real TTY — run mise run bwb in a tall terminal against a multi-issue DB and confirm (a) only one row of column-top borders visible, (b) no 'warn:' lines outside the alt-screen frame, (c) ~/.local/state/bwb/bwb.log still receives all warn entries.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Visually verified by user in real TTY (2026-05-16): single row of column-top borders, no warn lines outside the alt-screen frame, Done column shows correct totals. Both root causes confirmed fixed (sizeKnown guard + stderr suppression).

title: Build bwb-smoke manual smoke binary with parity comparison report
description:
## Description

Operator needs a one-command release smoke check: point at a DB, get a pass/fail report comparing what bwb's data layer says vs what `bd` says. This is the tool the user explicitly asked for to avoid "obvious render bugs shipping again".

## Instructions

1. Create `cmd/bwb-smoke/main.go`.
2. Flags:
   - `--dir <path>` (required) — DB working directory; uses `bd --readonly` when `--readonly` (default true).
   - `--readonly=true|false` (default true) — never write to the target DB.
   - `--format text|json` (default text).
   - `--checks count,sort,search,render` (default all).
3. Checks (each prints PASS/FAIL with details):
   - **count**: dashboard column totals vs `bd count` per status. Reuses helper from [[55oi]].
   - **sort**: first 20 IDs of each column vs `bd list/ready/blocked` first 20 IDs. Reuses helper from [[izds]].
   - **search**: run 4 canonical queries, compare totals + first 10 IDs. Reuses helper from [[64y4]].
   - **render**: invoke board model headlessly through a small `WindowSizeMsg` sequence and count column-top borders. Reuses helper from [[xx8p]].
4. Exit code: 0 if all pass; 1 if any fail. Print summary table: `check | dataset | status | detail`.
5. Add `mise run smoke` task: `go run ./cmd/bwb-smoke --dir .` (this repo by default).
6. Add `mise run smoke:aicm` task pointed at the AI Config Manager path (env-gated so it's opt-in).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `go build ./cmd/bwb-smoke` succeeds
- [ ] `bwb-smoke --dir <fixture>` passes all checks
- [ ] `bwb-smoke --dir <this-repo>` currently FAILS count check (exposes [[bug-done-total-wrong]]) with a clear message — this is the desired pre-fix state
- [ ] `bwb-smoke --dir <ai-config-manager> --readonly` never writes to that directory's .beads (verify by mtime snapshot)
- [ ] `mise run smoke` works from a clean checkout
- [ ] JSON output format is machine-parsable for CI

## Files to Modify

- create `cmd/bwb-smoke/main.go`
- create `cmd/bwb-smoke/report.go`
- update `.mise.toml` (add `smoke`, `smoke:aicm` tasks)

## Related

- depends on [[55oi]], [[izds]], [[64y4]], [[xx8p]] (reuses their parity helpers)
close_reason: Closed

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text:
Done. cmd/bwb-smoke implemented with 4 checks (count, sort, search, render), text/json output, --readonly default true. Verified by orchestrator end-to-end:
- mise run smoke against this repo: count PASS (Done=459 matches bd), sort PASS, search PASS, render PASS — result: PASS
- /tmp/bwb-smoke --dir ai-config-manager --readonly: count PASS (Done=622 matches bd), sort PASS, search PASS, render PASS — result: PASS
- AICM .beads mtimes unchanged after run
mise run smoke and mise run smoke:aicm both registered as mise tasks.

title: Render regression harness: catch doubled headers and frame-stacking bugs
description:
## Description

The "two column-top borders stacked" bug shipped because no test exercises the cold-start → WindowSizeMsg → re-render sequence as a single `View()` capture. Existing goldens (`TestRenderBoardColumnsGolden`, `TestBoardModeDashboardLayoutGoldensAcrossWidths`) snapshot at one resolved width — they cannot catch frame-stacking.

## Instructions

1. Create `internal/mode/board/render_regression_test.go`.
2. Use `teatest` to drive the full app model (not just board mode in isolation):
   - **Scenario A — cold start**: build model, do NOT send WindowSizeMsg, call `View()`, assert exactly 4 occurrences of `╭─` (column-top borders) in the output.
   - **Scenario B — resize**: build model, send `WindowSizeMsg{80,30}`, feed all 3 column results, call `View()`, assert exactly 4 `╭─`. Then send `WindowSizeMsg{200,60}` and feed fresh results; call `View()` again, assert exactly 4 `╭─`. Never 8.
   - **Scenario C — pre-size-then-data**: send `WindowSizeMsg` before any data, then feed data, then resize, then more data. Assert column-top count remains 4 in EVERY `View()` capture.
3. Generalize as a helper `assertColumnTopCount(t, view string, want int)` that counts box-drawing top-left/top-right corners (`╭` and `╮`), accepting both with and without horizontal extension.
4. Add a parallel `internal/mode/search/render_regression_test.go` covering the search view's panes (query box + results box + preview pane) with the same approach.
5. Wire one of these into `mise run quality:fast` so it runs pre-commit.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] All scenarios pass on current main when the suspected doubled-header bug is reproduced (test demonstrates failure mode)
- [ ] After [[bug-doubled-headers]] is fixed, all scenarios pass
- [ ] Helper rejects views with >4 column-top borders with a clear message: `expected 4 column-top corners, got 8 — frame stacking detected`
- [ ] Search-view variant similarly counts pane borders and rejects duplicates
- [ ] Runs in <2s as part of `mise run test`

## Files to Modify

- create `internal/mode/board/render_regression_test.go`
- create `internal/mode/search/render_regression_test.go`

## Related

- exposes [[bug-doubled-headers]]
close_reason: Closed

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Done. 3 regression test files created (board, search, logging). Deliberate-break verification: removing sizeKnown guard fires TestViewReturnsEmptyBeforeWindowSizeMsg; removing stderr-suppression fires TestStderrSuppressionBlocksWarnDuringActiveSession. Quality gate green. Both o7tk root causes have permanent regression coverage.

title: Search-parity test: workbench search results must match bd search count and order
description:
## Description

The search view has the same data-consistency risk as the dashboard: result count and order must match what `bd search` returns for the same query. Existing tests cover rendering/state but not parity with bd. We need explicit assertions across real datasets.

## Instructions

1. Create `internal/mode/search/parity/search_parity_integration_test.go` (build tag `integration`).
2. Define a small fixed query matrix (literal strings to keep tests deterministic):
   - empty query (default list)
   - common word query (e.g. `"test"`, `"fix"`, `"render"`)
   - filtered query (status=closed plus text)
   - work-state query (ready/blocked)
3. For each (dataset, query) pair:
   - Run the search through the gateway as the search mode does (`SearchIssues` with the same args).
   - Run `bd search <text> [flags] --json --limit N` from the dataset's directory.
   - Assert: total count match, first-page IDs match in order, `IsExact` flag matches whether bd hit its limit.
4. Add a sub-test asserting that the search "result count" header in `internal/ui/search/search.go` reports the same number that appears in the rendered rows — catches a count-vs-rows mismatch class.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Test passes on fixture for all queries
- [ ] Test passes on `this_repo` and `ai_config_manager` (env-gated) for the fixed query matrix
- [ ] When bwb's search ever diverges from bd (text matching, ordering, completeness), test fails with the specific query and the first divergent ID
- [ ] Header count vs rendered rows mismatch is detected as a separate failure mode

## Files to Modify

- create `internal/mode/search/parity/search_parity_integration_test.go`

## Related

- depends on [[3s3i]] (dataset adapter)
close_reason: Closed

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Done. 8-query matrix × 3 datasets, all pass. No count or order divergence between gateway SearchIssues and bd search/ready/blocked. Note: WorkStateReady/WorkStateBlocked queries route through bd ready/blocked with in-memory filtering (correct design). AICM .beads mtimes unchanged.

title: Sort-parity test: dashboard row order must match bd list/ready/blocked order
description:
## Description

The composer applies its own sort to active columns and trusts backend order for Done. We need explicit assertions that the rendered order matches what `bd` returns when invoked with the same sort flags — across real datasets where many issues share priority/timestamps and exercise tie-breaking.

## Instructions

1. Create `internal/dashboard/parity/sort_parity_integration_test.go` (build tag `integration`).
2. For each available dataset and each column:
   - **Ready**: assert `Columns.Ready.Issues` IDs match `bd ready --json` IDs in order. (Composer re-sorts; assert the re-sort matches bd's contract of `priority asc, updated_at desc, id asc`.)
   - **Not Ready (blocked)**: assert IDs match `bd blocked --json` IDs in order after composer's re-sort.
   - **In Progress**: assert IDs match `bd list --status in_progress --json` IDs in order after composer's re-sort.
   - **Done**: assert IDs match `bd list --status closed --sort-by closed_at --sort-order desc --json` IDs in order, WITHOUT composer re-sort (composer must preserve backend order for Done).
3. Where a tie exists, log both sides' tie-broken tail; do not flake.
4. Add a focused sub-test `t.Run("ClosedAtDescPreservedOnRealData", ...)` that catches the spurious "backend sort assumption broken" warning when many closed issues share `updated_at`. Currently the composer fires a false positive — assert that on real data the warning's *cause* (broken ordering) does NOT actually hold. Expected to expose [[bug-sort-proxy-false-positive]].

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Test passes for active columns on all datasets where bd's ordering is stable
- [ ] Test fails (clearly) on `this_repo` if Done order ever diverges from bd's `closed_at desc`
- [ ] The `ClosedAtDescPreservedOnRealData` sub-test reports whether real-data `updated_at` ordering matches `closed_at` ordering — feeds the decision in [[bug-sort-proxy-false-positive]]
- [ ] Diff output identifies the first divergent index and both IDs

## Files to Modify

- create `internal/dashboard/parity/sort_parity_integration_test.go`

## Related

- depends on [[3s3i]] (the dataset adapter)
- depends on [[55oi]] (the parity runner helper)
- exposes [[bug-sort-proxy-false-positive]]
close_reason: Closed

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Done. PASS on all 3 datasets × all columns × ClosedAtDescPreservedOnRealData diagnostic. Diagnostic finding: this-repo has 49/50 positions diverging between updated_at and closed_at orders — this is the empirical evidence that kh54's UpdatedAt proxy was unreliable. AICM .beads mtimes unchanged.

title: Count-parity test: dashboard column totals must match bd count
description:
## Description

Catch the class of bug where a dashboard column displays a capped slice length as the "total" instead of the real DB count. Today the Done column shows 50/56+ on a DB with 452 closed issues; no test catches this because `TestCompose` asserts `Total == len(Closed)` (the bug encoded as correct).

## Instructions

1. Create `internal/dashboard/parity/count_parity_integration_test.go` (build tag `integration`).
2. For each `Dataset` returned by `datasets.AllAvailable(t)`, run the full dashboard fetch pipeline (the same code path bwb uses on startup — `app/services.go` factories or direct gateway calls), capture the resulting `dashboard.Columns`, and compare:
   - `Columns.NotReady.Total` vs `bd count --status blocked --json`
   - `Columns.Ready.Total` vs the count from `bd ready --json | length`
   - `Columns.InProgress.Total` vs `bd count --status in_progress --json`
   - `Columns.Done.Total` vs `bd count --status closed --json` (this is THE failing case)
3. Fail with a clear diff: `column=Done dataset=this_repo bwb_total=56 bd_count=452 delta=-396`.
4. Mark the Done assertion as `t.Run("DoneTotalMatchesBdCount", ...)` so it can be triaged independently.
5. The test should currently FAIL on `this_repo` and `ai_config_manager` — that is the desired state until [[bug-done-total-wrong]] is fixed. Use `t.Skipf` only when env gate is off, NEVER mask the failure.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Test compiles and runs under `go test -tags=integration ./internal/dashboard/parity/...`
- [ ] On fixture: all 4 column totals match bd output (currently passes by accident)
- [ ] On `this_repo` (env-gated): Done assertion fails with a clear delta message exposing the bug
- [ ] On `ai_config_manager` (env-gated): Done assertion fails with a clear delta message
- [ ] When the bug is fixed (separate task), all assertions pass on all datasets without test changes

## Files to Modify

- create `internal/dashboard/parity/count_parity_integration_test.go`
- possibly create `internal/dashboard/parity/runner.go` with the shared fetch helper

## Related

- depends on [[3s3i]] (the dataset adapter)
- exists to catch [[bug-done-total-wrong]] (the Done=50 vs 452 defect)
close_reason: Closed

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Done. Test passes on all 3 datasets (fixture, this-repo, ai-config-manager). Done column results: this-repo bwb=454 bd=454, AICM bwb=622 bd=622 — ssom fix confirmed against real data. NotReady uses bd blocked (blocked is a readiness state not a status field). AICM .beads mtimes unchanged after full run.

title: Build read-only multi-DB test adapter (fixture, this repo, external)
description:
## Description

Comparison tests need to point at multiple beads databases without duplicating setup. Build an adapter package that yields a `BeadsGateway` and a parallel `bd` CLI invoker bound to the same DB directory, with the external DBs strictly read-only.

## Instructions

1. Create `internal/testing/datasets/datasets.go` exporting:
   - `type Dataset struct { Name, Path string; ReadOnly bool }`
   - `Fixture(t *testing.T) Dataset` — wraps `embeddedfixture.SharedFixtureRepoPath`; `ReadOnly: false` (writable temp dir).
   - `ThisRepo(t *testing.T) Dataset` — uses repo root; honors `BWB_PARITY_THIS_REPO=1`, skip otherwise; `ReadOnly: true`.
   - `AIConfigManager(t *testing.T) Dataset` — uses `BWB_PARITY_AICM_PATH` env var (default `/home/hans/dev/github/ai-config-manager`); honors `BWB_PARITY_AICM=1`, skip otherwise; `ReadOnly: true`.
2. Create `func NewGateway(t *testing.T, ds Dataset) beads.BeadsGateway` that builds a `CommandRunner` with `WorkDir: ds.Path`. When `ds.ReadOnly`, prepend `--readonly` to every bd argv (verify by reading `internal/gateway/beads/runner.go` for the argv hook point; if no hook exists, add one as a separate small refactor before this task).
3. Create `func BdCount(t *testing.T, ds Dataset, args ...string) ([]byte, error)` — runs `bd --readonly count <args> --json` via `exec.Command` from `ds.Path` and returns stdout. Mirror for `BdList`, `BdReady`, `BdBlocked`, `BdSearch`.
4. Skip cleanly when `bd` is not on PATH or the dataset is opt-in but its env flag is unset.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `datasets.Fixture` returns a usable Dataset; `datasets.NewGateway` returns a gateway whose `ListIssues` succeeds against the fixture
- [ ] `datasets.ThisRepo` and `datasets.AIConfigManager` skip with `t.Skip` when env gate is off
- [ ] When `ds.ReadOnly`, any attempted write through the returned gateway or bd helper fails fast (verify with one negative test)
- [ ] `go test ./internal/testing/datasets/...` passes
- [ ] No file under `/home/hans/dev/github/ai-config-manager/.beads/` is modified by these tests (verify by snapshotting `ls -la .beads/` mtimes around a test run)

## Files to Modify

- create `internal/testing/datasets/datasets.go`
- create `internal/testing/datasets/datasets_test.go`
- possibly extend `internal/gateway/beads/runner.go` with a read-only arg hook (separate small change inside the same task)
close_reason: Closed

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Implemented internal/testing/datasets package with Dataset struct, Fixture/ThisRepo/AIConfigManager constructors (env-gated), NewGateway (ReadOnly-aware), and BdCount/BdList/BdReady/BdBlocked/BdSearch helpers. Added ReadOnly bool to RunnerConfig with unit tests. Verified: full quality gate green (658 unit + 698 integration tests, 3 expected skips when env gates off). Verified negative-write: ReadOnly: true gateway blocks CreateIssue with 'not allowed in read-only mode' error; bd --readonly create on AICM returns same. Mtime check excludes embeddeddolt/ noms files (Dolt updates them on reads for internal cache — logical writes still blocked).

title: Data consistency verification suite for dashboard + search
description:
## Description

The dashboard's Done column shows the size of a capped slice as the total (e.g. "50" or "56+") when the real DB count is 452. No automated test catches this because the contract test runs against a 3-issue fixture and `internal/dashboard/composer.go:163` encodes `Total = len(Closed)` as correct. A doubled-column-header visual bug also shipped because no test exercises a cold-start → WindowSizeMsg → re-render sequence as a single `View()` output.

This epic builds the test infrastructure to catch this class of bug — counts, sorting, render correctness — across multiple real-scale datasets, plus a manual smoke tool to run before every release. Datasets exercised: embedded fixture (3 issues), this repo's `.beads` (452 closed), and `/home/hans/dev/github/ai-config-manager`'s `.beads` (622 closed, 2 open). External DBs are read-only (`bd --readonly`); we never write to them.

## Success Criteria

- [ ] Automated parity test asserts dashboard column totals match `bd count --by-status --json` on every supported DB
- [ ] Automated parity test asserts dashboard row order matches `bd list/ready/blocked` order with identical sort flags
- [ ] Automated parity test asserts search result count and order match `bd search`
- [ ] Manual smoke binary `bwb-smoke` runs against fixture / this repo / external repo (read-only) and prints pass/fail comparison report
- [ ] Render regression harness fails when two consecutive `View()` outputs stack column headers (catches doubled-header class)
- [ ] Docs cover the smoke workflow for release verification
- [ ] Acceptance review task complete

## Out of Scope

- Fixing the 3 already-identified defects (filed as separate bugs and linked)
- Search ranking quality (only result count + order parity, not relevance)
- Performance benchmarking
close_reason: Closed

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Plan reviewed in-session with user during planning turn; explicit GO given via /beads-tasks:beads-work invocation 2026-05-16. Proceeding to execution. Bugs ssom/o7tk/kh54 will be fixed before parity tests so the post-fix tests serve as regression coverage on real datasets rather than expected-failure assertions.

title: Reset all detail scroll offsets when selected issue changes
description:
## Description

When the user scrolls deep into issue A in detail mode and then selects issue B from board or search, only the legacy `m.detail.ScrollOffset = 0` is zeroed at `internal/app/model.go:759`. The active offsets `ContentScrollOffset`, `MetadataScrollOffset`, `DependenciesScrollOffset` are NOT reset. `mode/details.ApplyLoadedDetail` (`internal/mode/details/model.go:48-52`) does not touch them either. The bridge `syncLegacyScrollAlias()` (`model.go:578-583`) only copies `ScrollOffset` into `ContentScrollOffset` when the latter is 0 — so when a user has scrolled, the alias-reset is effectively a no-op.

Result: switching from issue A to issue B opens B already mid-scroll (clamped to B's end), which looks like a layout glitch.

Major finding U1 from the 2026-05-16 audit.

## Files to Modify

- `internal/mode/details/model.go` (`ApplyLoadedDetail`)
- `internal/app/model.go` (around line 759 — remove legacy reset once primary fix lands)
- new test: `internal/mode/details/model_scroll_test.go` or add to existing model_test.go

## Instructions

1. In `mode/details.ApplyLoadedDetail`, compare the new `issueID` to the previously held one. If different (or if previous was empty), zero `ContentScrollOffset`, `MetadataScrollOffset`, `DependenciesScrollOffset` BEFORE `ClampScroll` runs.
2. Remove the legacy `m.detail.ScrollOffset = 0` line in `app/model.go:759` once the primary reset is in place.
3. Consider deleting the `ScrollOffset` alias entirely if no consumer reads it — verify with a grep. If consumers exist, leave a deprecation comment.
4. Add a test: load issue A, scroll content to offset N>0, switch to issue B, assert all three offsets are 0 after `ApplyLoadedDetail`. Also assert scroll offsets are RETAINED if the same issue is re-loaded (e.g., from a refresh).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Switching selected issue resets all three scroll offsets to 0
- [ ] Re-loading the same issue (refresh) preserves scroll offsets
- [ ] Legacy `ScrollOffset` reset path removed or explicitly deprecated
- [ ] New test asserts both behaviors
- [ ] Full quality gate green
- [ ] Runtime UI verification confirms the regression no longer reproduces
close_reason: Scroll offsets now reset on selection change across all three panes.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Update cite — current legacy reset is at app/model.go:758 (was :759). Note: app/model.go:541-545 (OpenRelatedIssueIntent path) already resets ContentScrollOffset+MetadataScrollOffset but MISSES DependenciesScrollOffset; ensure the unified fix in ApplyLoadedDetail covers both code paths and the OpenRelatedIssueIntent block becomes redundant.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Implemented. mode/details.ApplyLoadedDetail compares Detail.Summary.ID to new issueID; if different (or empty), zeros all three scroll offsets (Content/Metadata/Dependencies) plus the legacy ScrollOffset alias before syncBrowserPanel. app/model.go:541 OpenRelatedIssueIntent block now also resets DependenciesScrollOffset. Legacy ScrollOffset reset in ensureDetailForCurrentSelectionCmd (was :758) removed as redundant. TestApplyLoadedDetailResetsScrollOffsetOnIssueChange covers reset-on-switch, preserve-on-refresh, and reset-on-first-load.

title: Use stable signal (not stderr substring) to detect missing beads database
description:
## Description

`internal/gateway/beads/runner.go:114` detects the "no beads database" error via `strings.Contains(stderr, "no beads database found")` — a plain substring match against arbitrary `bd` localization/wording. Any future `bd` CLI release that rephrases this stderr silently downgrades the error to `ErrorCodeCommandFailed`, and the UI's first-run "no database here" guidance (`internal/app/model.go:235`) stops firing. The path has zero test coverage today.

Major finding G3 from the 2026-05-16 audit.

## Files to Modify

- `internal/gateway/beads/runner.go`
- new test: extend `internal/gateway/beads/runner_test.go`

## Instructions

1. Investigate stable signals from `bd`:
   - Does `bd` use a dedicated exit code (e.g., 2) for missing-db? Run `bd ready --json` in `/tmp` and inspect exit code + stderr.
   - Is `bd doctor` a stable check? `bd ping --json`?
2. Pick the most stable signal. Order of preference: dedicated exit code > stable error JSON > stable stderr substring as last resort.
3. If `bd` does NOT currently expose a stable signal, file an upstream `bd` request and document the current best-effort detection with a pinning test that fails loudly on rewording.
4. Add a test exercising the missing-db code path: invoke runner with `WorkDir` pointing at a dir with no `.beads/`, assert returned error has code `ErrorCodeNoDatabaseFound`.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Detection switched to a stable signal, OR documented as best-effort with a pinning test
- [ ] Test exists exercising the missing-db path end-to-end (real `bd` against an empty dir)
- [ ] UI's "no database here" guidance verified still firing in runtime UI verification
- [ ] Full quality gate green

This issue tracks the orthogonal improvement to detection mechanism (exit code / structured signal). Sequence: lsb4 first, then this.
