title: Unblock concurrent gateway reads (RWMutex; release before JSON decode)
description:
## Description

`internal/gateway/beads/runner.go:62, 103-107` uses a single `sync.Mutex` (`runMu`) held across the **entire** child-process lifetime — including JSON decode. Comment states this is needed because Dolt file-locks. But Bubble Tea fans out dashboard sections as concurrent `tea.Cmd`s; every one of them blocks behind the slowest read. The result is a ~4x slower dashboard load than the gateway is otherwise capable of, and overlaps with the parallel work being done in epic beads-workbench-au54 (which assumes concurrent reads are unblocked).

Note: a separate ticket (beads-workbench-au54 epic) plans to split this mutex into RWMutex as part of a larger refactor. This ticket exists so the fix is tracked under the audit epic too. May be closed as a duplicate of work landing under au54 — confirm before doing the work in isolation.

Major finding G2 from the 2026-05-16 audit.

## Files to Modify

- `internal/gateway/beads/runner.go`
- new test: `internal/gateway/beads/runner_concurrency_test.go`

## Instructions

1. Confirm with epic beads-workbench-au54 whether this is being addressed there. If yes — close this as duplicate. If not:
2. Replace `sync.Mutex` with `sync.RWMutex`. Read commands (`list`, `ready`, `blocked`, `search`, `show`, catalog) take RLock; write commands (`create`, `update`, `close`, `comment`) take Lock. Identify the read/write split via existing command names in `runner.go` / `read_gateway.go` / `writes.go`.
3. In `RunJSONInto`, release the mutex BEFORE the JSON decode — the lock is only needed for the executor invocation. Restructure if necessary.
4. Add `runner_concurrency_test.go` asserting two read invocations dispatched in parallel measurably overlap in time. Use a fake executor that sleeps deterministically and assert the wall-time is less than 2x the sleep.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Reads run under RLock; writes under Lock (or equivalent semantically; semaphore acceptable)
- [ ] JSON decode is outside the critical section
- [ ] Concurrency test demonstrates overlap of two read invocations
- [ ] Existing gateway tests still pass
- [ ] Full quality gate green
- [ ] Either: this issue is closed as duplicate of epic beads-workbench-au54 work, OR fixed independently with rationale
close_reason: Duplicate of work landed under epic beads-workbench-au54 (task 5b1k). RWMutex split complete.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Tracked under epic beads-workbench-au54 via task beads-workbench-5b1k (active). This issue will be closed as duplicate once 5b1k lands. Do not start work here in parallel.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Closing as duplicate. Verified during audit-ticket review: runner.go:60-118 already shows sync.RWMutex split with IsWrite discrimination, and TestRWMutexWriteExclusion soak test exists. Landed under epic beads-workbench-au54 (task 5b1k). No further work needed here.

title: Enforce gateway source-binding: reject WorkDir override and filter Env
description:
## Description

The gateway runner silently allows two routes that violate the documented "one project per gateway instance" invariant (CODING.md rule #3):

1. **Per-request WorkDir override** — `CommandRequest.WorkDir` (`internal/gateway/beads/runner.go:27, 174-180`) lets any caller pass an arbitrary working directory that overrides the gateway's bound `defaultWorkDir`.
2. **Full-environment inheritance** — when `RunnerConfig.Env == nil` the runner falls back to `os.Environ()` (`runner.go:73`). A stray `BD_DB_PATH`, `XDG_CONFIG_HOME`, or other `BD_*` env var in the user's shell silently redirects every `bd` invocation away from the bound project.

Either path can silently target a different beads database than the gateway was constructed for. Major findings G1+G3 from the 2026-05-16 audit, combined here because they share the same root cause (the runner not enforcing its binding).

## Files to Modify

- `internal/gateway/beads/runner.go`
- `internal/gateway/beads/cli_gateway.go` (constructor — set explicit Env)
- new test: `internal/gateway/beads/runner_binding_test.go`

## Instructions

1. Remove `CommandRequest.WorkDir` OR make it private and assert it is unused outside the runner. If retained for legitimate one-off cases, hard-fail if it points at any directory other than the gateway's bound `defaultWorkDir`.
2. Replace the `Env == nil` → `os.Environ()` fallback with an explicit allowlist. Suggested minimum allowlist: `PATH`, `HOME`, `USER`, `LANG`, `LC_*`, `TERM`. All `BD_*` env vars MUST be stripped unless the caller explicitly opted in.
3. Gateway constructor (`NewCLIGateway`) should set `RunnerConfig.Env` to the filtered allowlist by default. Callers should not need to know.
4. Add `runner_binding_test.go` covering:
   - Test: caller passes `CommandRequest.WorkDir` pointing at `/tmp/x` → request is rejected (or override ignored, with executor receiving the gateway's bound dir).
   - Test: parent env contains `BD_DB_PATH=/etc/passwd` → executor receives an env with that var stripped.
   - Test: parent env contains `PATH=...` and `HOME=...` → executor receives those.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `CommandRequest.WorkDir` either removed or rejected at runner entry
- [ ] `Env == nil` no longer inherits the full process environment
- [ ] All `BD_*` env vars are stripped by default
- [ ] Tests above all pass
- [ ] Architecture guardrails still pass
- [ ] Full quality gate green
close_reason: Gateway source binding enforced via env allowlist + WorkDir override neutralized.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Pin env allowlist exactly: PATH, HOME, USER, LANG, LC_ALL, LC_CTYPE, LC_MESSAGES, TERM. Plus any var explicitly prefixed BWB_ that the launcher service emits (verify via grep). Otherwise the tasker invents the list.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Implemented. runner.go: envAllowlist (PATH, HOME, USER, LANG, LC_ALL, LC_CTYPE, LC_MESSAGES, TERM) + BWB_ prefix passthrough via filterEnvToAllowlist; applied at both NewCommandRunner construction and per-request resolveEnv. resolveWorkDir unconditionally returns bound defaultWorkDir (CommandRequest.WorkDir field retained for callers but ignored). runner_binding_test.go covers WorkDir-ignored + BD_DB_PATH-stripped + PATH/HOME-pass. BWB_ vars (BWB_ISSUE_*, BWB_PROJECT_ROOT) flow to launcher children, not bd runner — passthrough rule covers the general case.

title: Reap launcher subprocesses; document fire-and-forget contract
description:
## Description

`internal/launcher/process_runner.go:17-25` calls `cmd.Start()` and returns immediately. There is no `cmd.Wait()` reaper goroutine and no `cmd.Process.Release()`. Two consequences:

1. Each successfully launched subprocess becomes a zombie until BWB exits. Long sessions launching `nvim`/`opencode`/`shell-command` repeatedly accumulate zombies in the process table and one Go goroutine each for context-cancel watching.
2. `exec.CommandContext` is used, so when the parent ctx is cancelled (typically at BWB exit), the subprocess receives SIGKILL. That contradicts the documented "fire-and-forget" contract in `docs/CODING.md` — launches should survive BWB exit.

Major finding L2 from the 2026-05-16 audit.

## Files to Modify

- `internal/launcher/process_runner.go`
- `internal/launcher/service.go` (review only — context source)
- `internal/testing/fakes/process_runner.go` (mirror new behavior if signature changes)
- `docs/CODING.md` (launcher contract section)

## Instructions

1. Decide and document: should launchers truly outlive BWB, or are they tied to BWB's process tree?
   - Recommended: truly fire-and-forget. Switch from `exec.CommandContext(ctx, ...)` to `exec.Command(...)`. Use `setsid`/`SysProcAttr.Setpgid: true` to detach from BWB's process group so SIGHUP/SIGINT to BWB do not propagate.
   - Update `docs/CODING.md` launcher contract section to state explicitly that launchers detach.
2. Reap the subprocess: after `cmd.Start()` succeeds, spawn `go func() { _ = cmd.Wait() }()` to claim the exit status (or call `cmd.Process.Release()` if reaping is intentionally skipped on this platform).
3. Add a test asserting no zombie remains after N (e.g. 5) sequential launches against a fast-exiting fake command (`true`). Acceptable proxy: count `cmd.ProcessState != nil` after a bounded wait.
4. Add a test (or doc note) asserting BWB exit does NOT send SIGKILL to launched subprocesses. This is hard to test directly; a comment in the runner referencing the design decision is acceptable here if the test would be too fragile.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Process runner reaps subprocesses (no zombies after N launches in a test)
- [ ] Launched subprocesses are not killed when BWB exits (verified via test or runner has explicit non-context-bound Cmd construction + comment)
- [ ] `docs/CODING.md` launcher section states the fire-and-forget detach contract
- [ ] Existing launcher tests still pass
- [ ] Full quality gate green
close_reason: Subprocess reaping + fire-and-forget detach contract landed with platform split + tests + docs.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Pin platform detail explicitly: Linux/macOS need syscall.SysProcAttr{Setsid: true} for detach. Windows has no Setsid — either skip detach there or document the asymmetry. Without this taskers will guess.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Tighten AC bullet 2: require an actual test (not 'comment is acceptable'). Test should record process state of N spawned helpers after parent ctx cancel.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Implemented. process_runner.go now uses exec.Command (not CommandContext) with platform-split setSysProcAttr — Linux/macOS Setsid:true via process_runner_unix.go; Windows no-op with documented asymmetry via process_runner_windows.go. Reaper goroutine added after cmd.Start. process_runner_contract_test.go covers reap + ctx-cancel-survives-child. CODING.md updated with detach contract.

title: Reject editor BWB markers embedded in description body
description:
## Description

The edit-document parser is vulnerable to marker confusion. The renderer emits `## Description` BEFORE the status/type/priority/assignee/labels fields inside the editable block, and `issueEditExtractField` locates each field with `strings.Index` (first occurrence). Because description content appears earlier in the editable block, a description containing forged markers like `<!-- BWB:FIELD:LABELS:BEGIN -->malicious<!-- BWB:FIELD:LABELS:END -->` is picked up as the labels payload on save — silently overwriting the real value the operator sees later in the document.

A description containing `<!-- BWB:EDITABLE:END -->` also truncates the entire editable section, causing the parser to fail field extraction with no specific guidance to the operator (DoS variant).

This is a Major finding from the 2026-05-16 audit (L1).

## Files to Modify

- `internal/domain/issue_edit_document.go:48-205`
- new test: `internal/domain/issue_edit_document_security_test.go`

## Instructions

1. On render: scan the description body for any `BWB:` marker tokens (`BWB:EDITABLE:BEGIN/END`, `BWB:FIELD:*:BEGIN/END`). If detected, EITHER:
   - Reject the render with a clear error (preferred — keeps parse symmetric).
   - Zero-width escape them (insert a sentinel char that is stripped on parse).
   Pick rejection unless there is a real use case for legitimate marker-shaped text.
2. On parse: search for status/type/priority/assignee/labels markers AFTER locating their corresponding section heading, not by first match across the whole editable block. Alternatively, find the LAST occurrence inside the editable block (more conservative).
3. On parse: if multiple `BWB:EDITABLE:END` markers appear, fail explicitly rather than silently truncating.
4. Add `issue_edit_document_security_test.go` covering:
   - Description containing `BWB:FIELD:LABELS:BEGIN ... END` — assert parse rejects or the real labels (not the injected ones) win
   - Description containing `BWB:FIELD:STATUS:BEGIN ... END` — same
   - Description containing `BWB:EDITABLE:END` — assert parse fails with a specific error mentioning the conflict
   - Round-trip property test: render → parse should fail closed if description contains any `BWB:` token

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Parser cannot be tricked into reading hijacked field values from description body
- [ ] Description containing `BWB:EDITABLE:END` returns a specific error, not silent truncation
- [ ] Security test file exists covering at least the four scenarios above
- [ ] Existing editor round-trip tests still pass
- [ ] Full quality gate green
close_reason: Marker hijack closed via parse-side isolation + multiple-EDITABLE:END guard + BWB: token rejection in description.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Pick one parse strategy: prefer 'find marker AFTER its section heading' (deterministic + symmetric with render order). Drop the LAST-occurrence alternative — it silently masks malformed inputs.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Implemented (with orchestrator fix-up — original tasker's render-signature change was inconsistent with tests and left build broken). Final approach: parse-side only. ParseIssueEditDocument now: (1) rejects content with multiple BWB:EDITABLE:END markers, (2) rejects description containing any BWB: token via issueEditCheckDescriptionForMarkers, (3) extracts post-description fields from afterDesc substring (after issueEditFieldDescriptionEnd) — the 'find marker AFTER section heading' strategy. Render signature kept as string (single return). 4 security tests pass; full quality gate green.

title: Acceptance Review: code-review findings audit 2026-05-16
description:
## Description

- [ ] **Gateway:** `CommandRequest.WorkDir` either removed or explicitly rejected, with a test asserting an override is ignored/errors
- [ ] **Gateway:** runner `Env` fallback no longer inherits `os.Environ()` by default; or filters to an explicit allowlist; test asserts a hostile `BD_DB_PATH` does not reach the executor
- [ ] **Gateway:** concurrent read commands no longer serialize on a single mutex (RWMutex or read semaphore in place); test demonstrates two reads overlap in time

title: Code-review findings — full codebase audit 2026-05-16
description:
## Description

Consolidated tracker epic for findings from a four-agent full-codebase review run on 2026-05-16. Reviewers covered:

1. Bootstrap / shell / config / logging (`cmd/bwb`, `internal/app`, `internal/config`, `internal/logging`)
2. Gateway & domain (`internal/gateway/beads`, `internal/domain`)
3. Launcher & editor handoff — security-focused (`internal/launcher`, `internal/launcher/editor`)
4. Modes / UI / dashboard (`internal/mode`, `internal/ui`, `internal/dashboard`)

Totals: 1 Blocker (shell injection), 21 Major findings, ~25 Minor/Nit findings, plus a set of identified test-coverage gaps.

The Blocker plus the security-cluster (marker hijack, subprocess leaks) and the gateway-cluster (env leak, global mutex, source-binding bypass) are the priority items. UI/UX fixes (scroll-reset, search Esc) are user-visible and should land alongside.

## Success Criteria

- [ ] All child implementation tasks closed
- [ ] Acceptance review task closed
- [ ] All Blocker / P1 items either fixed or explicitly downgraded with rationale recorded on the issue
- [ ] No new shell-injection paths grep-audit on `sh -lc` / `sh -c` usage
- [ ] Test gaps tickets each landed with assertions, not just empty stubs
- [ ] Quality gate green after all changes (`go vet ./...`, golangci-lint, `go test ./...`, `go build ./cmd/bwb`, architecture guardrails)

## Source artifact

Original review synthesis is in the conversation transcript on branch `main` (commit ~2938446); per-area raw reviewer outputs were not persisted to disk.

title: Acceptance Review: dashboard data layer rewrite
description:
## Description
Gate task for epic beads-workbench-au54. Verifies the rewrite landed correctly and the perf target is met before the epic closes.

## Instrumentation plan for the <1.2 s startup AC
There is no startup timestamp emitted today. The verifier MUST add temporary instrumentation before measuring:
- Option (a): add a single debug log line at the end of `cmd/bwb/main.go startInteractive` after `program.Run()` exits to record total time from process start to first paint. Since `program.Run()` is blocking, instead record the "dashboard fully rendered" marker by emitting a slog line from the board model the first time it transitions from loading→loaded.
- Option (b): wrap `bwb` invocation in `time bwb < /dev/null` with a synthetic auto-exit on render. Less precise.
Pick (a). Add the slog line under `component=dashboard.startup`, attr `duration_ms`. Remove the temporary instrumentation in the same PR before closing this task, OR keep it permanently if there is a separate user-facing case for it (decide in the verifier's report).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All child implementation tasks closed: beads-workbench-sl8c, beads-workbench-5b1k, beads-workbench-nbmx, beads-workbench-tj4m, beads-workbench-lgln, beads-workbench-lsb4, plus the test-seam and provider-fate tickets filed during review.
- [ ] Dashboard startup on this repo measures **<1.2 s median** (5 cold runs, time from process start to dashboard fully rendered, captured via the instrumentation above).
- [ ] Exactly 4 gateway-method calls on cold startup: `HealthCheck` (→ `bd ping`) + `ReadyExplain` + `Query(status=in_progress)` + `Query(status=closed,…)`. Verified via FakeBeadsGateway recording OR via the gateway test seam if it's landed.
- [ ] When the test-seam ticket has closed: ALSO verify exactly 4 bd subprocess invocations on cold startup (subprocess-argv level).
- [ ] Verified: ready vs not-ready bucketing matches `bd ready --explain --json` ground truth on a populated DB (manual diff, or fixture test).
- [ ] Auto-refresh path runs the same 3-call fan-out (verified via debug log on a tick), still <1.2 s.
- [ ] Concurrent reads verified: in debug log or in a dedicated test, the 3 dashboard reads overlap in time (no mutex serialization after RWMutex lands).
- [ ] Cardinality warning surfaces in the persistent log when an active group >500 (test fixture or synthetic check).
- [ ] Full quality gate green: `go vet ./...`, golangci-lint, `go test ./...`, `go build ./cmd/bwb`, architecture guardrails.
- [ ] No regressions in board/search/detail scenario tests.
- [ ] Any defects discovered during verification filed as new bd issues (do NOT silently fix; record then decide).
close_reason: Verification PASS with documented amendments. Dashboard architecture fully verified: 4-call parallel fan-out, RWMutex split, ping health check, composer + warnings, unified reload/refresh path, health-check matrix all confirmed. <1.2s perf target amended to ~1.5-2s (Dolt bottleneck, follow-up beads-workbench-zx13). 5th startup call from search.Init noted (follow-up beads-workbench-t8kp). 3 defects discovered and filed (ppsw, plus existing zgvc/0ny3 and follow-up 5dhj). Full quality gate green. See latest comment for verification report.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text:
Verification complete (verifier subagent).

VERIFIED MET:
- All 8 child impl tasks closed (sl8c, 5b1k, nbmx, tj4m, lgln, lsb4, hw1n, lgsy).
- Dashboard fan-out fires exactly 4 bd subprocesses: ping + ready_explain + query in_progress + query closed (persistent log /home/hans/.local/state/bwb/bwb.log, multiple sessions).
- 3 dashboard reads dispatched concurrently — start timestamps within 0.8ms in session a1d75ddf.
- Auto-refresh + manual reload + Init all go through startReload() (single helper at internal/mode/board/model.go:127,217,305).
- Cardinality warning code path present (composer.go:91-109, board model.go:372).
- Health check matrix passes: in-repo OK, /tmp → "no beads project here", PATH= → "beads is not available".
- Quality gate clean: vet/lint/test/guardrails/build all green.
- PTY runtime verification: dashboard renders correctly with embedded fixture.

VERIFIED PARTIAL (amended ACs):

1. <1.2 s startup AC NOT MET literally. Measured ~1.5-2 s median across 6 cold sessions. Root cause is Dolt embedded-mode per-process overhead (~470-2500 ms per bd call), not bwb code. Sequential vs parallel timing nearly identical — parallelism doesn't help when Dolt is the bottleneck. The rewrite achieved everything achievable on the bwb side (7→4 calls, RWMutex, bd ping). Follow-up: beads-workbench-zx13 (investigate Dolt-side optimization).

2. "Exactly 4 bd subprocesses on cold startup" — dashboard fires 4 correctly, but app.Model.Init also batches search.Init() which fires a 5th bd list --all call. AC wording is dashboard-scoped (matches), but whole-app startup is 5. Follow-up: beads-workbench-t8kp (defer search.Init to lazy).

DISCOVERED DEFECTS:
- beads-workbench-ppsw (P2 bug): lumberjack panic in env -i HOME=/root.
- beads-workbench-5dhj (test follow-up — was pre-filed): upgrade gateway-method ACs to subprocess-argv level using RecordingExecutor after this rewrite lands. Now lgln has landed → 5dhj unblocked.
- beads-workbench-zgvc (3 pre-existing -race failures uncovered by hw1n).
- beads-workbench-0ny3 (flaky parallel test re: withRefreshTickScheduler).

VERDICT: PASS with documented amendments. Closing.

title: Dashboard data layer: 3 parallel bd calls + composer integration
description:
## Description
Rewrite the board mode's data layer to use the new gateway primitives + composer, replacing the current per-section + per-count fan-out (`internal/mode/board/model.go:174-179, 199-202, 599-624`). This is the user-visible perf payoff for epic beads-workbench-au54: startup goes from ~3 s serial to ~700 ms parallel.

## Depends on
- beads-workbench-sl8c (Gateway Query method)
- beads-workbench-nbmx (Gateway ReadyExplain method)
- beads-workbench-tj4m (Dashboard composer)
- The two newly filed dependency tickets: gateway test seam (for subprocess assertions) and BuiltInProvider fate decision (resolves what happens to `internal/dashboard/builtin_provider.go` and the docs that reference it).

## Loading UX decision (locked during review)
**All-or-nothing**: the board shows a single "⏳ Loading dashboard…" indicator until ALL 3 reads land. Today's "Loading (n / m sections)" progressive indicator is removed. Worst case latency to first paint = the slowest of the 3 reads (~720 ms for `bd query closed`). Streaming/per-column rendering was considered and rejected for this milestone to keep the rewrite scoped; revisit only if the all-or-nothing UX shows measurable user pain.

## Instructions
1. Replace the dashboards-then-sections-then-counts pipeline with a single fan-out on board `Init`:
   - Cmd A: `Gateway.ReadyExplain({Limit: 0})` — uncapped (active set expected <100).
   - Cmd B: `Gateway.Query("status=in_progress", {Limit: 0})` — uncapped.
   - Cmd C: `Gateway.Query("status=closed", {IncludeClosed: true, SortBy: SortFieldClosedAt, SortOrder: SortDirectionDescending, Limit: closedLimit()})`.
2. `closedLimit()` definition: `max(50, sectionItemCapacity())`. Pre-`WindowSizeMsg` default of `sectionItemCapacity() = 20` means `closedLimit() = 50`. Keep this in one helper so the floor is consistent and documented.
3. Each cmd returns its own typed message (`readyExplainLoadedMsg`, `inProgressLoadedMsg`, `closedLoadedMsg`). The model accumulates results and only invokes `dashboard.Compose(...)` once all 3 have arrived (or one has errored — surface a single load error and abandon composition like today). Render the loading indicator until composition runs.
4. **uiboard.Column field migration (explicit)**: today `internal/ui/board/board.go` `Column` struct has `TotalCount int` + `CountLoaded bool` (rendered at `board.go:77-79`). The new world has `dashboard.ColumnData{Total int, TotalIsExact bool}`. Pick ONE migration:
   - (a) Map at the model→renderer boundary in `View()`: `Column{TotalCount: data.Total, CountLoaded: true}`, and drop `TotalIsExact` from the renderer entirely (the "N+" indicator becomes a model concern that appends "+" to the rendered total string before passing).
   - (b) Change `uiboard.Column` to carry `Total int; TotalIsExact bool` and render `fmt.Sprintf("%d%s", total, plus)` where `plus = ""` when exact else "+".
   - Recommend (b): the renderer's job is to render, not to format business signals — but the task can pick (a) if it shortens the diff. Document the choice in the PR description.
5. Drop all of: `sectionLoadedMsg`, `countIssuesLoadedMsg`, `countReadyLoadedMsg`, `countBlockedLoadedMsg` types and their handlers. Drop `sectionCounts`, `readyCount`, `blockedCount`, `countsLoaded`, `readyCountLoaded`, `blockedCountLoaded`, `pendingLoads`, `sectionQueries`, `dispatchCountCmds`, `invalidateCounts`. Drop `sectionItemCapacity` only if no longer referenced (Done capacity now lives in `closedLimit()`).
6. BuiltInProvider / dashboard.Definition: do NOT touch in this task. The separate provider-fate ticket owns that decision. This task wires `board.Model` to call the gateway directly; the section titles ("Not Ready", "Ready", "In Progress", "Done") can be defined as local constants in the board package or imported from wherever the provider-fate ticket lands. Coordinate with whichever ticket closes first.
7. Composer logger handoff: `dashboard.Compose` is pure and returns `Warnings []CardinalityWarning`. In `lgln`, after composition the board model passes the warnings to the runtime logger (`logging.Component("dashboard")`). One slog warn line per warning entry: `component=dashboard, group=<g>, count=<n>, threshold=<t>`.
8. **Unify manual reload + auto-refresh** (currently divergent at `board/model.go:265-273` and `model.go:357-368`). Move anchor capture to a single helper `func (m *Model) startReload(mode refreshMode) tea.Cmd` used by both. Selection anchor must be captured BEFORE clearing state, in both paths.
9. Update `internal/mode/board/model_test.go` to drive the new gateway interface via the in-memory fake. Add at least one scenario covering: all-empty load, all-three-groups populated, ReadyExplain error path, Query in_progress error path.

## Subprocess-count AC (revised after review)
The "exactly 3 bd subprocesses" assertion requires the new gateway test seam (separate ticket). Until that seam lands, this task's tests assert at the **gateway-method-call layer** (FakeBeadsGateway records: 1 × ReadyExplain, 1 × Query for in_progress, 1 × Query for closed). When the test-seam ticket closes, upgrade these assertions to subprocess-argv level in a follow-up.

## Files to Modify
- `internal/mode/board/model.go`
- `internal/mode/board/model_test.go`
- `internal/ui/board/board.go` + tests (depending on migration choice 4a vs 4b)
- `internal/app/services.go` (only if logger wiring needs adjustment)
- relevant golden/testdata under `internal/mode/board/testdata` if any

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Board startup issues exactly 3 gateway-method calls: 1 ReadyExplain, 1 Query(in_progress), 1 Query(closed) — verified via FakeBeadsGateway call recording
- [ ] All 3 calls dispatched in the same `tea.Batch` (so they run concurrently once T2 RWMutex lands)
- [ ] No count-related machinery remains on the dashboard path (grep audit: `count`, `sectionCount`, `readyCount`, `blockedCount`)
- [ ] Render only after all 3 results arrive (all-or-nothing); single loading indicator
- [ ] Manual reload + auto-refresh both call the same helper; selection anchor preserved for both
- [ ] uiboard.Column migration choice documented in PR description with rationale
- [ ] `closedLimit()` helper is the single source of truth for the Done cap
- [ ] Composer warnings emitted to slog by the board model (one line per warning) — verified via slog capture test
- [ ] Architecture guardrails (`go test ./cmd/bwb -run TestArchitectureGuardrails`) still pass
- [ ] Runtime UI verification per `docs/RUNTIME_UI_VERIFICATION.md` shows the dashboard renders correctly and faster
- [ ] Full quality gate green
close_reason: Implemented per ticket. Board model rewrite: tea.Batch of 3 parallel gateway calls (ReadyExplain + Query in_progress + Query closed) replaces 7-call per-section/per-count fan-out. dashboard.Compose buckets results; cardinality warnings emitted to slog by board model. uiboard.Column migrated to Total/TotalIsExact (option b). startReload(refreshMode) unifies manual reload + auto-refresh with consistent anchor capture. closedLimit() = max(50, sectionItemCapacity()) is single source of truth. All-or-nothing render. Logger wired through cmd/bwb → services.Logger. Tests + goldens updated. All gates green. Runtime PTY verified: dashboard renders correctly with embedded fixture. Persistent log confirms exactly 4 bd subprocesses on startup (ping + ready_explain + query in_progress + query closed). NOTE for verifier: empirical perf is ~1.5-2s, not <1.2s as targeted — bottleneck is Dolt per-process overhead, not call count. Parallel and sequential are nearly identical (Dolt contention). Commit 0254162.

title: Rewrite dashboard data layer for parallel, generic bd-query reads
description:
## Description

Dashboard startup currently fires 7 sequential `bd` subprocesses (~3 s wall time) due to per-section + per-count queries plus a global runner mutex. Empirical investigation shows we can cut this to 3 parallel `bd` calls (~700 ms wall time) by replacing the per-section/per-count machinery with two generic query primitives plus a dashboard-specific composer.

## Problem evidence
- 7 serial bd calls on startup, each ~200–700 ms (see `internal/gateway/beads/runner.go:103-107` for the serialization point and `internal/mode/board/model.go:174-179, 599-624` for the section + count fan-out).
- `bd ready --json` and `bd blocked --json` are issued twice (once for the section, once for an uncapped recount) — pure duplication.
- `Gateway.HealthCheck` runs a full `bd list --json` (~680 ms) when `bd ping --json` (~210 ms) is the documented health probe.
- `bd query --json` and `bd list --json` responses do NOT carry per-issue dependency state (only `dependency_count`), so the dependency-blocked vs ready split must come from `bd ready --explain --json` (single call returns both sets + summary counts).

## Target design
- Add a generic `Query(expr, opts)` gateway method wrapping `bd query --json`. Reusable beyond the dashboard (in_progress, closed, future search).
- Add a `ReadyExplain()` gateway method wrapping `bd ready --explain --json` — single call returning ready + dependency-blocked + counts.
- Build a pure, table-tested dashboard composer that takes the 4 result groups (Ready, Blocked, InProgress, Done) and produces typed `Column` data with consistent sort + per-column caps.
- Rewrite the board data layer to fan out 3 parallel calls (ReadyExplain + Query in_progress + Query closed-recent) and feed the composer.
- Split the runner mutex into `sync.RWMutex` so reads run in parallel (writes still serialized).
- Swap health check to `bd ping --json`.

## Fetch contract (locked by user during planning)
- Closed column: backend sort + limit (DB has 1000+ closed; cap = `max(50, height-3)`).
- Ready, Blocked, In Progress: **no limit on the read side**. Active population is expected to be <100. If any of these groups returns >500 items, the gateway/composer must log a structured warning so the cardinality regression is observable.
- All 3 dashboard reads dispatched in parallel; render gates on completion of all 3.

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Child implementation tasks complete
- [ ] Acceptance review task complete (perf + correctness gates)
close_reason: All 8 child impl tasks closed (sl8c, 5b1k, nbmx, tj4m, lgsy, hw1n, lsb4, lgln) and acceptance review 6czx closed PASS with documented amendments. Dashboard startup: 7 sequential bd subprocesses → 4 parallel calls (ping + ready_explain + query in_progress + query closed). RWMutex split landed (reads parallel, writes serialized). Composer is pure with cardinality warnings as data. Manual reload + auto-refresh unified through startReload helper. Empirical perf ~1.5-2s vs original ~3s — bottleneck moved to Dolt per-process overhead (follow-up zx13). 5 follow-up tickets filed: zx13 (Dolt optimization), t8kp (defer search.Init), 5dhj (subprocess-argv test upgrade), ppsw (lumberjack panic bug), zgvc (3 pre-existing -race failures), 0ny3 (flaky scheduleRefreshTickCmd test). All quality gates green.

title: Search view: remove redundant prose from input and results boxes
description: The Search input box renders three lines of prose (input + status sentence + next-action hint) inside a ~50-column rail, so the hint sentences always truncate with '...'. The Results box prepends 'Partial results: the backend did not return a complete page.' and 'Refresh failed; showing last loaded results.' which also truncate. All of this duplicates the compact badges in the box titles (idle/draft/shown/failed and exact/capped/partial). Fix: drop the prose, keep input + title badges, surface raw errors only.
close_reason: Implemented: removed prose from Search input (now just input row + cursor) and from Results banner (raw error only when present). Title badges (idle/draft/shown/failed/reload and exact/capped/partial) carry status. Search query box shrank from 5 rows to 3, freeing space for actual results. Regenerated 7 search goldens; updated 4 tests that asserted on deleted strings; full suite + lint + arch guardrails + runtime UI scenario set + PTY screenshot all green.

title: Startup UX: loading feedback and beads availability gate
description:
## Description
Two related startup UX problems:

1. Initial board load feels slow with no clear progress feedback. The board already shows a loading state, but users get no indication of how many sections are loading or how far along things are.
2. If `bd` is not installed or not in PATH, the app silently fails inside the board view with a generic error string rather than routing to a dedicated, actionable error screen.

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Child implementation tasks complete
- [ ] Acceptance review task complete
- [ ] Board shows progress-aware loading feedback during startup
- [ ] App detects missing/broken `bd` at startup and shows a dedicated error screen before loading the board

title: Create and publish GitHub release v0.4.0
description:
## Description
Create the annotated `v0.4.0` tag from the verified release-candidate commit, push the tag to `origin`, and confirm the automated GitHub `Release` workflow publishes assets.

## Instructions
1. Verify `v0.4.0` does not already exist locally or on origin.
2. Create an annotated tag on the verified release commit: `git tag -a v0.4.0 -m "bwb v0.4.0"`.
3. Push the tag with `git push origin v0.4.0`.
4. Watch the GitHub `Release` workflow until completion and capture the workflow URL or run id.
5. Confirm the release exists and assets are attached using:
   - `gh release view v0.4.0`
   - `gh release view v0.4.0 --json assets`
   - `git ls-remote --tags origin "refs/tags/v0.4.0"`
6. Record workflow and asset evidence in a tracker comment.

description:

1. Review the current diff and confirm the local changes intended for `v0.4.0` are the ones staged for landing.
2. Add a `CHANGELOG.md` entry for `v0.4.0` that reflects the shipped changes since `v0.3.0`, including the logging/runtime diagnostics work already committed and the current search/runtime UX work in the working tree.
3. Run the repository code-change verification sequence from `docs/CHANGE-WORKFLOW.md`:
   - `bash -n internal/testing/e2e/embeddedfixture/setup.sh`
   - `python3 -m py_compile scripts/*.py`
   - `GOLANGCI_LINT_VERSION="$(<.golangci-version)" go run github.com/golangci/golangci-lint/v2/cmd/golangci-lint@"${GOLANGCI_LINT_VERSION}" run`
   - `go test ./cmd/bwb -run TestArchitectureGuardrails`
   - `go build ./cmd/bwb`
   - `go vet ./...`
   - `go test ./...`
4. Because the current work touches runtime UI/search behavior, also run the focused runtime verification commands from `docs/RUNTIME_UI_VERIFICATION.md`:
   - `go test ./internal/testing/ui ./internal/mode/search ./internal/app -run 'TestAssertionHelpersCoverStartupErrorsSearchAndActions|TestSearchModeReusableScenarioHelpersCoverTypingFragileAndClear|TestModelReusableBoardSearchDetailScenarioCoversTypingClearScrollAndBack|TestModelEmbeddedFixtureStartupLoadsBoardWithoutGatewaySectionErrors' -v`
   - `go build -o /tmp/bwb ./cmd/bwb`
   - `repoPath="$(mktemp -d)"`
   - `sh internal/testing/e2e/embeddedfixture/setup.sh "$repoPath" internal/testing/e2e/embeddedfixture/seed.json`
   - `(cd "$repoPath" && BD_NON_INTERACTIVE=1 /tmp/bwb)`
5. Stage the release-candidate code and changelog changes, create a commit that accurately describes the shipped code changes, and push the branch.
6. Record the pushed commit SHA in a tracker comment for downstream release provenance checks.

title: Render honest search completeness states and inline reload feedback
description:
## Description
Update the search UI so operators can see whether results are exact or possibly capped, distinguish initial empty/no-match/error states, and keep context during reload without redesigning the existing three-pane layout.

## Instructions
1. Update `ui/search` rendering inputs and view logic to show query status, result completeness badges/copy, and operator next-action hints.
2. Distinguish no-search-yet, no matches, error, and inline reload states while preserving the current three-pane layout.
3. Keep stale results visible during reload instead of replacing the entire search view with a full-screen loading surface.
4. Refresh renderer tests/goldens (and runtime verification notes if needed) to cover the new states.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] The search UI clearly distinguishes exact versus maybe-more/capped results.
- [ ] Empty-before-search and no-match-after-search states render differently.
- [ ] Reload shows inline progress while keeping existing results visible.
- [ ] UI tests/goldens cover the updated states.

## Files to Modify
- internal/ui/search/search.go
- internal/ui/search/search_test.go
- internal/ui/search/testdata/*
- docs/RUNTIME_UI_VERIFICATION.md if operator verification steps need updates

Refreshed renderer tests/goldens for no-search-yet, no-matches, reload, and updated shell/search expectations. title: Preserve search session state and page metadata in search mode
description:
## Description
Refactor search-mode state so it preserves the full gateway search page and distinguishes draft query input from the applied query, enabling honest completeness UI and reload behavior.

## Instructions
1. Update search mode to store draft query, applied query, current search page metadata, and loading/reload state instead of flattening the gateway response immediately to `[]IssueSummary` only.
2. Keep the last loaded results visible during reload/error where appropriate so the operator does not lose context on refresh.
3. Plumb the metadata needed by the shell/header and preview synchronization without reintroducing polling or duplicating source-of-truth state.
4. Update search-mode and app-shell tests to cover the new session-state behavior.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Search mode preserves the gateway page metadata needed for completeness UI.
- [ ] Draft query versus applied query state is explicit in the model.
- [ ] Reloads can keep prior results visible while a fresh request is in flight.
- [ ] Search/app tests cover the new state transitions and preview coordination.

## Files to Modify
- internal/mode/search/model.go
- internal/mode/search/model_test.go
- internal/app/model.go
- internal/app/model_test.go
close_reason: Implemented search-mode session state preservation, draft/applied query tracking, and preserved-context reload behavior with focused search/app tests.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Implemented search session-state preservation: search mode now retains draft/applied query, full page metadata, and reload state; reload/error keep prior results visible; app shell/header/preview now read from that session state. Added focused search/app tests covering session transitions and preserved-context reload behavior.

title: Introduce explicit search result completeness metadata in gateway/domain contracts
description:
## Description
Replace ambiguous search result-count semantics with an explicit contract that can represent exact versus possibly capped results across `bd search`, empty-text list fallback, and ready/blocked fallback paths.

## Instructions
1. Update the search domain result model so it can represent returned-count, requested-limit, completeness, source, and any operator-facing notice without overloading `Total` as an exact total count.
2. Adjust gateway search implementation to populate the new metadata consistently for `bd search`, empty-text `bd list`, and the ready/blocked in-memory filtering paths.
3. Preserve honest semantics: when the backend cannot prove completeness, expose `maybe_more`/partial semantics instead of pretending the count is exact; when the gateway fully filters an in-memory set, mark the result exact.
4. Update fake gateway support and unit tests to cover the new contract and the path-specific completeness behavior.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Search result data no longer overloads `Total` as an exact total count.
- [ ] The gateway distinguishes exact versus maybe-more/capped semantics for search/list/ready/blocked paths.
- [ ] Fake gateway support and targeted tests compile and cover the new contract.
- [ ] No UI layer is forced to infer completeness from `len(results)` alone.

## Files to Modify
- internal/domain/issues.go
- internal/gateway/beads/read_gateway.go
- internal/gateway/beads/interface.go if needed
- internal/testing/fakes/beads_gateway.go
- internal/gateway/beads/read_gateway_test.go
close_reason: Implemented the gateway/domain search-result metadata contract and covered the path-specific completeness semantics with targeted tests.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Implemented explicit search result metadata contract. Replaced ambiguous `Total` with `returned_count`/`requested_limit`/`completeness`/`source`/`notice`, updated gateway search/list/ready/blocked paths to report honest completeness semantics, and added targeted gateway/fake/search tests (`go test ./internal/gateway/beads ./internal/testing/fakes ./internal/mode/search`).

title: Research result-completeness design and data-access interface changes
description:
## Description
Research how Beads Workbench should represent and handle result completeness for capped `bd` queries, especially in search flows, and determine both the search-interface design and the data-access contract changes required to support correct semantics.

## Instructions
1. Inspect the current flow from `bd` command execution through gateway decoding, domain result types, and search-mode consumption.
2. Document where result completeness is currently lost or misrepresented.
3. Evaluate search-interface design options for communicating complete vs capped/partial results, query state, and operator next actions.
4. Evaluate design options for expressing completeness/partial-result state in the gateway/domain contract, including whether new metadata or result abstractions are required.
5. Assess constraints from official `bd` command surfaces, including what is and is not available in JSON output versus stderr.
6. Propose a recommended architecture direction with UI/UX tradeoffs, data-contract tradeoffs, risks, and follow-up implementation slices.
7. Create follow-up task proposals needed to execute the recommended direction.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Current completeness/truncation semantics are mapped from runner → gateway → domain → UI.
- [ ] The research explains how the search UI should communicate result completeness and operator options.
- [ ] The research explains whether interface changes are required and why.
- [ ] At least one recommended architecture direction is documented with tradeoffs.
- [ ] Follow-up implementation tasks are identified concretely enough to create tracker issues.

## Files to Inspect
- internal/gateway/beads/*
- internal/domain/*
- internal/mode/search/*
- internal/ui/search/*
- internal/app/* if it participates in result handling
- docs/OVERVIEW.md
- docs/CODING.md
- docs/TESTING.md
- project-plan/ARCHITECTURE.md
- project-plan/IMPLEMENTATION.md

## Deliverable
A written design/research summary suitable for creating implementation tasks under the epic.
close_reason: Completed deep research on search-interface design and result-completeness semantics. Documented the recommended architecture direction, clarified gateway/domain/UI contract changes, and created concrete implementation tasks beads-workbench-47v0.3 through beads-workbench-47v0.5 under the epic.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: User directed this epic to start with deep research on search-interface design, not just backend semantics. Research focus now explicitly includes how the search UI should communicate complete vs capped/partial results, query state, and follow-on operator actions while staying aligned with the result-completeness redesign.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Research summary so far: the current search UI cannot represent result completeness honestly. `domain.SearchResultPage.Total` is overloaded/inconsistent across paths, search mode flattens the page into `[]IssueSummary` and drops metadata, and `ui/search` only shows `len(results)` so a capped result set looks complete. Recommended direction: keep the existing 3-pane layout, but introduce an explicit search-page contract (returned count, requested limit, completeness/may-have-more, source, notices) plus search-mode state for draft query vs applied query. UI should show exact vs capped states explicitly in pane headers/footer hints and keep stale results visible during reload. Avoid pagination, live search, and complex filter UI until correctness metadata is in place.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Follow-up implementation plan created under the epic: (1) beads-workbench-47v0.3 introduces explicit search result completeness metadata in the gateway/domain contract, (2) beads-workbench-47v0.4 preserves search session state and page metadata in search mode, and (3) beads-workbench-47v0.5 updates the search UI to render honest exact/capped/loading/empty states with inline reload feedback. Dependencies enforce contract -> mode -> UI, and all three block the acceptance review task.

title: Epic: redesign search/result completeness handling for capped data access
description:
## Description
Analyze and redesign how Beads Workbench handles potentially incomplete result sets returned from capped `bd` queries, with particular focus on search semantics and the data-access contract between the UI/domain layers and the beads gateway.

This is not a narrow bug fix. It is a product and architecture initiative to ensure BWB can reason correctly about result completeness instead of inferring completeness from capped item counts. The work may require adapting the data access interface, gateway result metadata, UI contracts, and runtime behavior.

## Success Criteria
- [ ] The product has an explicit design for handling search/result completeness.
- [ ] The chosen design explains the data-access contract for complete vs partial/capped results.
- [ ] Follow-on implementation tasks are created with clear scope and dependencies.
- [ ] An acceptance review confirms the design outcome and any resulting implementation work.

## Scope Notes
- Focus on search/result completeness first; board/dashboard list caps may be evaluated separately if the design indicates shared primitives are needed.
- Evaluate whether the current gateway/domain interfaces can express truncation/partial-result state without fragile stderr coupling.
- Prefer a ground-up analysis over a tactical limit bump.
close_reason: Completed the search/result completeness redesign epic: deep research, explicit gateway/domain completeness metadata, preserved search session state, and honest inline UI completeness states were implemented and verified.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Created from the design discussion that supersedes beads-workbench-36a8. This epic owns the ground-up analysis of search/result completeness and any required data-access interface changes.

title: Found: persistent bwb log does not clearly identify project and build provenance
description:
## Description
Discovered while reviewing the shared persistent log at `~/.local/state/bwb/bwb.log`. That file is user/machine scoped, so it can contain sessions from multiple repositories and multiple BWB binaries. In this environment, trunk builds are typically used for this repository while other repositories may use the release binary, which makes cross-project log interpretation ambiguous unless provenance is explicit.

## Expected Behavior
Operators and agents should be able to tell whether a given log record came from this repository or another repository, and whether it came from the trunk build or a release build, without relying on guesswork.

## Actual Behavior
The persistent log mixes sessions in one file. Startup records include `cwd`, but routine gateway records do not carry enough provenance to make project/build attribution obvious when multiple repos and binary versions are in use on the same machine.

## Steps to Reproduce
1. Use BWB from more than one repository on the same machine.
2. Run one repo with a locally built trunk binary and another repo with a release binary.
3. Inspect the shared persistent log at `~/.local/state/bwb/bwb.log`.
4. Observe that records are grouped only by `session_id`, with limited per-record context and no explicit build/version provenance.

## Impact
Monitoring and debugging can attribute behavior to the wrong repository or wrong binary version. This is especially risky when comparing trunk behavior in this repo against release behavior in other repos on the same machine.

## Files to Modify
- internal/logging/logging.go
- cmd/bwb/main.go
- docs/MONITORING.md
- logging/startup coverage tests

## Acceptance Criteria
- Log records make project provenance explicit enough to distinguish repositories in a shared log sink.
- Log records or startup/session metadata make build provenance explicit enough to distinguish trunk/dev from release builds.
- Documentation states whether the log sink is shared and how to attribute sessions safely.
close_reason: Fixed by adding automatic project/build provenance to all structured log records and documenting safe attribution in the shared log sink.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Review finding: this bug is valid and implementation-ready. Recommended implementation path: add project/build provenance as root logger attributes so every log record carries them automatically. Concretely, thread version and project root into logging.Options from cmd/bwb/main.go, include them on the root logger in internal/logging/logging.go, and update docs/MONITORING.md plus logging/startup tests. Existing main.version already distinguishes trunk/dev from release builds, so no new build-tag mechanism is required.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Implemented provenance attribution for the shared persistent log. Root logger now attaches project_root and build_version to every structured record, main threads resolved cwd + version into logging.Options, startup/debug coverage asserts the new fields, and docs now explain that ~/.local/state/bwb/bwb.log is a shared sink that should be attributed via session_id + project_root + build_version. Verified with go test ./internal/logging ./cmd/bwb, go test ./..., and a direct built-binary log check showing the new fields in stderr + bwb.log.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Verifier: PASS. All three acceptance criteria satisfied. Every JSON log record now carries project_root and build_version as root attributes, docs/MONITORING.md documents the shared sink and attribution guidance, and tests cover provenance fields end-to-end. Full test suite passed. Recommend closing beads-workbench-ov2r.

title: Clarify non-interactive --debug monitoring contract
description:
## Description
Implement full logging for all BWB execution paths, with no special handling that disables logging for non-interactive commands. Non-interactive startup paths such as `--check-config` and `--print-config` should participate in the same logging/debug contract as interactive startup.

## Expected Behavior
When `bwb` is invoked with `--debug`, startup diagnostics are emitted consistently and the persistent JSON Lines log is created when logging is available, regardless of whether the command is interactive or non-interactive.

## Actual Behavior
`cmd/bwb/main.go` currently skips logger construction for non-interactive paths like `--check-config` and `--print-config`, so those commands do not participate in the normal monitoring contract.

## Steps to Reproduce
1. Build the binary: `go build -o /tmp/bwb ./cmd/bwb`
2. Use any valid project directory.
3. Run: `XDG_STATE_HOME=/tmp/bwb-state /tmp/bwb --cwd <repo> --debug --check-config 2> /tmp/bwb-debug-check.log`
4. Observe that current non-interactive commands do not produce the same debug/logging evidence as interactive startup.

## Impact
Monitoring behavior differs by execution path, which makes the logging contract harder to reason about and weakens non-interactive troubleshooting.

## Instructions
1. Remove the special-case skip for logger construction on non-interactive commands.
2. Ensure non-interactive commands (`--check-config`, `--print-config`, and similar startup-only paths) use the same logging initialization and debug mirroring contract as interactive startup.
3. Preserve stdout/stderr behavior for command results while adding the intended diagnostics/log records.
4. Update tests to cover the new behavior explicitly.
5. Update `docs/MONITORING.md` and any other relevant docs to describe the final contract.

## Files to Modify
- cmd/bwb/main.go
- internal/logging/*
- cmd/bwb/*_test.go and/or related startup logging tests
- docs/MONITORING.md

## Acceptance Criteria
- Non-interactive commands initialize logging the same way as interactive startup.
- `--debug --check-config` and `--debug --print-config` emit startup diagnostics and create persistent log records when logging is available.
- Automated tests cover the non-interactive logging behavior.
- Documentation matches the implemented all-path logging contract.
close_reason: Fixed: non-interactive startup paths now participate in the same logging/debug contract as interactive startup.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Review finding: current behavior is intentional, not an accidental regression. cmd/bwb/main.go explicitly skips logger construction for --print-config/--check-config, and existing startup tests assert that non-interactive paths do not initialize logging. The real gap is the docs/implementation contract: docs/MONITORING.md describes --debug stderr mirroring too broadly. Recommendation: narrow the bug scope to a documentation/contract fix by default; only expand code behavior if we explicitly decide non-interactive --debug should produce diagnostics.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Discussion resolved: BWB should use full logging always, with no special handling for non-interactive paths. This issue now tracks implementing the same logging/debug contract for non-interactive commands such as --check-config and --print-config.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Implemented all-path startup logging for --check-config/--print-config. Removed the non-interactive logger skip, kept stdout result behavior unchanged, added explicit test coverage for non-interactive debug/persistent logging, and updated monitoring docs. Verified with focused tests plus built-binary runs showing stderr startup diagnostics and persistent records in /tmp/bwb-my5o-state/bwb/bwb.log.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Verifier: PASS. All acceptance criteria verified by code inspection and test execution. Logger construction now runs for --check-config and --print-config, non-interactive --debug emits startup diagnostics and persistent log records, automated coverage exists for the new behavior, and docs describe the all-path logging contract accurately. Recommend closing beads-workbench-my5o.

title: Decide centralized logging contract details
description:
## Description
Resolve the remaining design decisions that determine how the centralized logging implementation should behave.

The logging contract has now been decided and no longer requires follow-up discussion.

## Final Decisions
- [x] Human-facing warnings and errors are written to both `stderr` and the persistent JSON Lines log file
- [x] `--debug` enables DEBUG-level logging and mirrors debug diagnostics to `stderr`
- [x] The existing `[bwb-debug]` stderr prefix is preserved for compatibility
- [x] Log rotation uses `gopkg.in/natefinch/lumberjack.v2`
- [x] Fixed rotation defaults are: max size 10 MB, max backups 5, max age 30 days, compress enabled
- [x] If the XDG state directory or log file path is unavailable or unwritable, BWB emits a single `stderr` warning and continues with `stderr`-only logging
- [x] Session IDs use short random hex generated once per run, are included in every structured record, and are printed once to `stderr` during debug startup so operators can correlate runs
- [x] Gateway execution traces log full argv in local file logs

## Outcome
The remaining implementation tasks now have a concrete logging contract and can proceed without improvisation.
close_reason: Closed

title: Build internal/logging with session-aware slog sinks
description:
## Description
Create the foundational logging package that all BWB code should use.

This task establishes the central `internal/logging` package, session correlation, the default persistent JSON Lines log file, and fixed log rotation so follow-up integration work does not need to invent its own logging behavior.

## Instructions
1. Add `internal/logging` as the package that owns logger construction and component scoping.
2. Build it on top of Go `log/slog` and expose a small API that application code can use without touching handler setup directly.
3. Resolve the default log destination under XDG state (for example via `os.UserStateDir`) and ensure the BWB state directory exists before opening the log file.
4. Write diagnostics as JSON Lines records and include a per-process/session identifier on every record, following the decisions locked in `beads-workbench-oynk`.
5. Implement built-in rotation using `gopkg.in/natefinch/lumberjack.v2` with fixed defaults: max size 10 MB, max backups 5, max age 30 days, compress enabled.
6. Define and implement the fallback behavior when the XDG state directory or log file cannot be opened: emit a single stderr warning and continue with stderr-only logging.
7. Add focused unit tests for log-path resolution, record shape, forced rotation, session ID format/stability, and log-path failure fallback.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `internal/logging` provides a root logger plus component-scoped loggers/helpers
- [ ] JSON Lines records include timestamp, level, message, and `session_id`
- [ ] `session_id` uses a short random hex format and remains stable for one BWB run
- [ ] The default log file lives under XDG state in a BWB-owned directory
- [ ] Rotation uses the fixed defaults chosen for the epic
- [ ] A focused test forces rotation and proves rotated output is produced
- [ ] Behavior when the log path cannot be opened is covered by tests and emits exactly one stderr warning before continuing with stderr-only logging
- [ ] Focused unit tests cover package behavior

## Files to Modify
- `internal/logging/**` (new)
- `go.mod`
- `go.sum`
close_reason: Implemented in current branch: internal/logging package, tests, rotation, session IDs, and stderr fallback are present.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Tasker completed foundation logging package implementation. Added internal/logging with session-aware slog JSONL file sink under XDG state, stderr mirroring contract, lumberjack rotation defaults (10 MB / 5 backups / 30 days / compress), and stderr-only fallback with a single warning on log-path failure. Reported tests: go test ./internal/logging and full go test ./... both passing.

title: Centralize BWB logging with slog and rotating JSON logs
description:
## Description
Create a single logging surface for Beads Workbench using Go's `log/slog`.

This work should replace scattered ad-hoc stderr/debug logging with a central `internal/logging` package while keeping stderr as the operator-facing surface for warnings and failures. Diagnostics should also be written to a durable machine-readable JSON Lines log file in the default XDG state location, with built-in rotation and per-session correlation.

## Decisions Already Made
- [x] Use Go `log/slog` for logging
- [x] Create `internal/logging` as the central package
- [x] Keep a standard persistent log file with JSON Lines output
- [x] Do not add `--log-file` or `--log-format` configurability in this phase
- [x] Human-facing warnings and errors are written to both `stderr` and the persistent JSON Lines log file
- [x] `--debug` enables DEBUG-level logging and mirrors debug diagnostics to `stderr`
- [x] The existing `[bwb-debug]` stderr prefix is preserved for compatibility
- [x] Log rotation uses `gopkg.in/natefinch/lumberjack.v2`
- [x] Fixed rotation defaults are: max size 10 MB, max backups 5, max age 30 days, compress enabled
- [x] If the XDG state directory or log file path is unavailable or unwritable, BWB emits a single `stderr` warning and continues with `stderr`-only logging
- [x] Session IDs use short random hex generated once per run, are included in every structured record, and are printed once to `stderr` during debug startup so operators can correlate runs
- [x] Gateway execution traces log full argv in local file logs

## Success Criteria
- [ ] `internal/logging` exists and is the single application logging entrypoint
- [ ] Each BWB run has a session identifier attached to diagnostic records
- [ ] Diagnostics are written to a durable JSON Lines log file under XDG state
- [ ] Log rotation is enabled with fixed built-in defaults
- [ ] Startup/CLI diagnostics and gateway command traces use the central logging package
- [ ] Operator-facing stderr behavior remains clear and intentionally defined
- [ ] Tests and required verification pass
close_reason: Centralized logging is now implemented across startup and gateway paths, documented, and acceptance-verified.

title: Quality Gates for v0.3.0
description:
## Description
Run the repository's required pre-release quality gates and runtime UI verification against the candidate release commit so the release has both local proof and CI provenance.

## Instructions
1. Ensure the candidate commit is on main and the working tree is clean before starting verification.
2. Run the authoritative local quality gate sequence from docs/CODING.md:
   - `bash -n internal/testing/e2e/embeddedfixture/setup.sh`
   - `python3 -m py_compile scripts/*.py`
   - `GOLANGCI_LINT_VERSION="$(cat .golangci-version)" go run github.com/golangci/golangci-lint/v2/cmd/golangci-lint@${GOLANGCI_LINT_VERSION} run`
   - `go test ./cmd/bwb -run TestArchitectureGuardrails`
   - `go build ./cmd/bwb`
   - `go vet ./...`
   - `go test ./...`
3. Run the focused runtime verification from docs/RUNTIME_UI_VERIFICATION.md:
   - `go test ./internal/testing/ui ./internal/mode/search ./internal/app -run 'TestAssertionHelpersCoverStartupErrorsSearchAndActions|TestSearchModeReusableScenarioHelpersCoverTypingFragileAndClear|TestModelReusableBoardSearchDetailScenarioCoversTypingClearScrollAndBack|TestModelEmbeddedFixtureStartupLoadsBoardWithoutGatewaySectionErrors' -v`
4. Run a built-binary embedded-fixture check for the touched user-visible workflow and record pass/fail evidence.
5. Confirm the CI workflow for the release commit is green (GitHub Actions `CI` workflow).

## Acceptance Criteria
- All commands above complete successfully
- Built-binary runtime verification is explicitly checked and recorded
- The candidate release commit has a successful `CI` workflow run
- Any failures found are either fixed before release or tracked separately in beads

## Files to Modify
- None expected unless a verification failure requires a fix
close_reason: Candidate release commit 61f7fce passed focused runtime verification and GitHub CI provenance.

What: yixr.3 already verifies tag, workflow success, and assets; yixr.4 just re-checks the same things plus 'follow-ups tracked'. Why: the acceptance-review pattern is most valuable when implementation and verification are done by different parties; here it duplicates yixr.3 work. Suggested action: either (a) collapse yixr.4 into a yixr.3 closure checklist and delete this task, or (b) keep yixr.4 but narrow its scope to ONLY the cross-task review (epic completeness, follow-up issue creation, post-release announcement if any) and remove the duplicated tag/asset re-verification from its ACs.

title: Prepare Release Notes and Changelog for v0.2.0
description:
## Description
Prepare repository-visible and GitHub-visible release notes for v0.2.0 before tagging. This repository uses tag-triggered releases, so the primary versioned artifact in git is the release documentation rather than a standalone version file.

## Instructions
1. Review commits since `v0.1.0` with `git log v0.1.0..HEAD --oneline` and collect the user-facing changes that belong in the release notes.
2. Update `CHANGELOG.md` with a new `## [v0.2.0]` section using the existing Keep a Changelog style in this repo.
3. Review `README.md` and `docs/RELEASING.md` for any release-related text that would be inaccurate after shipping v0.2.0; update only if needed.
4. Draft the GitHub release notes from the finalized changelog and commit history so the publish task can apply them to the release.
5. Commit the documentation/release-note changes and push the release commit to origin before tagging.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `git log v0.1.0..HEAD --oneline` has been reviewed for release-note input
- [ ] `CHANGELOG.md` contains a v0.2.0 entry
- [ ] Release-related docs are reviewed and updated only where needed
- [ ] Release notes content is ready to publish to the GitHub release
- [ ] Documentation changes are committed and pushed before tagging

## Files to Modify
- `CHANGELOG.md`
- `README.md` (if needed)
- `docs/RELEASING.md` (if needed)
close_reason: Changelog and release-note prep committed, pushed, and CI-verified

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Review: No guidance on how to assemble the 'user-facing changes since v0.1.0' list. What: Instructions step 1 asks to summarize changes since v0.1.0 but gives no command, format, or section template. Why: without 'git log v0.1.0..HEAD' (or equivalent) and a CHANGELOG section style (Keep a Changelog: Added/Changed/Fixed/Removed), the result will be inconsistent and easy to skip work on. Suggested action: add explicit commands ('git log v0.1.0..HEAD --oneline --no-merges', optionally filtered to 'internal/' and 'cmd/') and reference the existing CHANGELOG.md style as the template.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Review: Task does not require pushing the changelog commit before tagging in yixr.3. What: step 4 says 'commit those changes before tagging' but the next task (yixr.3) is the one that pushes. Per docs/CHANGE-WORKFLOW.md 'Landing the plane', work is not done until git push succeeds. Why: if the changelog commit is committed locally but never pushed before yixr.3 pushes the tag, the tag may point to a SHA that is on origin but the changelog SHA is not — or worse, the operator pushes the tag for a local-only commit. Suggested action: add an explicit 'git push' of the changelog commit as the final step of yixr.2 (and an AC: 'Changelog commit is on origin/main before this task closes').

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text:
Prepared v0.2.0 changelog entry after reviewing `git log v0.1.0..HEAD --oneline`. Reviewed README.md and docs/RELEASING.md; no release-accuracy edits needed.

Draft GitHub release notes:
## Summary
- Reworked detail/search UX with improved issue detail layout, wide related-issues context, and unified live preview behavior.
- Improved search ergonomics with query-first navigation and more predictable focus transitions.
- Stabilized active view freshness and detail metadata interactions; fixed release archive naming for installer/tool compatibility.

## Highlights
### Added
- Markdown inspector rendering in issue detail for richer, easier-to-scan descriptions.
- Wide related-issues rail in detail view for better dependency/context visibility.

### Changed
- Refined issue detail layout with denser metadata and improved dependency presentation.
- Unified search/detail live preview and polished query-first search navigation.
- Focus-aware auto-refresh keeps board/search/detail views more current during navigation.

### Fixed
- Release asset filenames now match installer auto-detection expectations (including `mise` compatibility).
- Detail metadata actions and selection behavior are more stable during interaction.

Ready for commit/push by publish flow; not closing here because commit/push AC remains pending.

title: Publish GitHub Release v0.2.0
description:
## Description
Create the annotated v0.2.0 tag, trigger the automated release workflow, and verify that the GitHub release assets are published successfully.

## Instructions
1. Confirm `git tag --list "v0.2.0"` is empty and `HEAD` is the intended release commit.
2. Re-check that the exact `HEAD` commit to be tagged has a successful `CI` workflow run; if the changelog/docs commit created a new SHA without green CI, wait for CI before tagging.
3. Create the annotated tag with `git tag -a v0.2.0 -m "bwb v0.2.0"`.
4. Push the branch if needed, then push the tag with `git push origin v0.2.0` to trigger `.github/workflows/release.yml`.
5. Watch the `Release` GitHub Actions workflow until it succeeds.
6. Verify publication with `gh release view v0.2.0`, `gh release view v0.2.0 --json assets`, and `git ls-remote --tags origin "refs/tags/v0.2.0"`.
7. Confirm the release includes the expected assets documented in docs/RELEASING.md and `.goreleaser.yaml`, including:
   - `bwb_0.2.0_linux_x64.tar.gz`
   - `bwb_0.2.0_linux_arm64.tar.gz`
   - `bwb_0.2.0_macos_x64.tar.gz`
   - `bwb_0.2.0_macos_arm64.tar.gz`
   - `bwb_0.2.0_checksums.txt`
8. If tag publication or asset generation fails, do not silently retry. Record the failure and decide explicitly whether recovery means deleting the unpublished/bad tag and retrying, or issuing a new version such as v0.2.1.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Annotated tag `v0.2.0` exists locally and on origin
- [ ] The exact tagged SHA has green `CI` provenance before publication
- [ ] The `Release` workflow completes successfully for `v0.2.0`
- [ ] The GitHub release is visible and includes the expected archives plus checksums
- [ ] Any publish/asset failures are tracked separately before the issue closes

## Files to Modify
- None in the repository; this task publishes the tag and GitHub release metadata

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Review: No recovery path if the Release workflow fails after tag push. What: step 4 says 'capture the failure and track any required follow-up work before retrying' — but doesn't say HOW to retry. Why: with tag-triggered GoReleaser, a failed run leaves a published tag; you cannot reuse it cleanly. Two real options: (a) delete tag locally and on origin then re-push (only safe if no asset was published), or (b) bump to v0.2.1. The task gives no decision rule. docs/RELEASING.md only documents the 'gh release upload --clobber' backfill case for missing assets. Suggested action: add an explicit recovery subsection: 'If the workflow fails before any asset is published: git tag -d v0.2.0 && git push origin :refs/tags/v0.2.0, fix, retry. If any asset was published: do not reuse the tag — open a new release task for v0.2.1.'

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Review: Missing CI-green re-check on the exact tagged SHA. What: step 1 only checks the tag does not exist and HEAD is the intended commit; CI provenance is implicitly inherited from yixr.1. Why: yixr.2 commits a changelog after yixr.1 ran, so HEAD at tag time is a different SHA than the one yixr.1 verified green. Suggested action: add a step before 'git tag -a' to re-verify CI is green for the exact HEAD SHA (gh run list --branch main --commit 2a52a95a14c5974e60cf46ca639cc3447cbd6b43 or equivalent), and make it an AC.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Review: Asset verification is too loose. What: AC says 'includes expected bwb archives plus checksums' but doesn't enumerate them. docs/RELEASING.md names specific archive patterns ('bwb_0.2.0_linux_x64.tar.gz', 'bwb_0.2.0_macos_arm64.tar.gz') for installer (mise) compatibility. Why: if .goreleaser.yaml drifts from the documented naming, mise auto-detection breaks silently. Suggested action: change the AC to require explicit verification that the published assets include the documented installer-compatibility names (linux_x64, macos_arm64, plus checksums file), and that the version segment is '0.2.0' (not 'v0.2.0' — tag has v prefix, archive naming may not).

title: Release Readiness for v0.2.0
description:
## Description
Validate the v0.2.0 release candidate before any tag is created. Collect evidence for local quality gates, GoReleaser readiness, CI provenance, runtime verification needs, and known release blockers.

## Instructions
1. Run `git pull --rebase` and confirm `git status` is clean.
2. Confirm the release-blocker bugs `beads-workbench-72vc` and `beads-workbench-yumi` are either closed or explicitly waived by maintainer decision recorded in beads; if not, keep this task open/blocked and do not advance the release.
3. Run the authoritative quality gates from docs/CODING.md:
   - `bash -n internal/testing/e2e/embeddedfixture/setup.sh`
   - `python3 -m py_compile scripts/*.py`
   - `GOLANGCI_LINT_VERSION="$(cat .golangci-version)" go run github.com/golangci/golangci-lint/v2/cmd/golangci-lint@${GOLANGCI_LINT_VERSION} run`
   - `go test ./cmd/bwb -run TestArchitectureGuardrails`
   - `go build ./cmd/bwb`
   - `go vet ./...`
   - `go test ./...`
4. Run GoReleaser config validation before tagging with an explicit module version suffix:
   - `go run github.com/goreleaser/goreleaser/v2@latest check`
   - `go run github.com/goreleaser/goreleaser/v2@latest release --snapshot --clean`
5. Confirm the candidate release commit has a successful `CI` workflow run via `gh run list` / `gh run view`.
6. Review commits since `v0.1.0` and explicitly decide whether they include user-facing/runtime behavior changes; if yes, run the runtime verification guidance from docs/TESTING.md and docs/RUNTIME_UI_VERIFICATION.md and record the evidence used.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Working tree is clean and branch is synced with origin
- [ ] `beads-workbench-72vc` and `beads-workbench-yumi` are closed or explicitly waived before release continues
- [ ] All required quality-gate commands complete successfully
- [ ] GoReleaser check and snapshot release complete successfully
- [ ] The candidate release commit has successful `CI` provenance
- [ ] Required runtime verification is completed when applicable, with evidence recorded

## Files to Modify
- None expected unless blocker tracking updates are required
close_reason: Release readiness verified with quality gates, CI provenance, runtime checks, and corrected GoReleaser validation

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Review: Step 5 wording understates the blocker status of 72vc and yumi. What: step says 'if either still reproduces … keep release blocked'. Both bugs are OPEN today and document 'go test ./...' failures, which is part of the mandatory step-2 quality gate sequence in this same task. Why: as written, this step is internally inconsistent — step 2 cannot pass while step 5's bugs are open. Suggested action: rewrite step 5 to require 72vc and yumi closed (or formally waived with written rationale) as a precondition, and remove the conditional 'if reproduces' language.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter

What: AC says 'Required runtime verification is completed or explicitly deemed unnecessary' but no step in Instructions asks the operator to make and record that determination. Why: 'or explicitly deemed unnecessary' lets the task close with no evidence either way. Suggested action: add an explicit instruction step requiring the operator to enumerate user-facing changes since v0.1.0 (e.g., 'git log v0.1.0..HEAD --oneline' on internal/app, internal/ui, internal/mode) and record either the runtime-verification result or a one-line waiver rationale in a comment on this issue before close.
