title: Release v0.2.0
description:
## Description
Plan and execute the internal v0.2.0 release for Beads Workbench using the tag-triggered GitHub Actions + GoReleaser flow documented in docs/RELEASING.md.

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Release readiness confirms a clean tree, synced branch, local quality gates, local GoReleaser validation, CI provenance, and any required runtime verification for the chosen release commit.
- [ ] Release documentation is updated for v0.2.0, with CHANGELOG content and release-note source prepared and pushed before tagging.
- [ ] Open test-failure bugs `beads-workbench-72vc` and `beads-workbench-yumi` are resolved or explicitly waived before release publication.
- [ ] Annotated tag v0.2.0 is pushed and the GitHub Release workflow publishes the expected archives and checksums.
- [ ] Acceptance Review: Release v0.2.0 closes with any discovered gaps tracked separately.

## Known Risks
- [ ] The release cannot proceed to publication while `go test ./...` is known-broken for open blockers.
- [ ] The release tag must point at the exact commit with green CI provenance; post-changelog commits require re-checking CI before tagging.
close_reason: all steps complete

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Review: Known-risks framing is too soft. What: epic lists 72vc and yumi under 'Known Risks' with 'check during readiness; if reproduces, treat as blocker'. Both bugs are currently OPEN and document concrete failures in 'go test ./...' which IS the authoritative quality gate per docs/CODING.md. Why: the readiness gate cannot pass while go test ./... fails, so these are not conditional risks — they are hard blockers today. Suggested action: either (a) add explicit blocks-by dependencies from yixr.1 to 72vc and yumi, or (b) require a written waiver decision recorded on the epic before yixr.1 can close.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Review: No release-commit SHA pinning across tasks. What: yixr.1 validates 'the candidate release commit', yixr.2 commits changelog, yixr.3 tags HEAD — but nothing requires the SHA validated in yixr.1 to equal the SHA tagged in yixr.3. Why: changelog commit in yixr.2 changes HEAD after CI was verified green in yixr.1, so the tagged commit is NOT the commit whose CI provenance was checked. Suggested action: restructure so CI provenance is re-verified after the changelog commit lands on origin (yixr.3 should re-check 'CI green for the exact tagged SHA' before tag push, not rely on yixr.1's earlier check).

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Review: No version-source / goreleaser dry-run step. What: plan never checks whether bwb embeds a version string (e.g., ldflags in .goreleaser.yaml or a version package) that needs updating, and never runs 'goreleaser check' or a snapshot build before tagging. Why: tag-triggered releases are unforgiving — a malformed .goreleaser.yaml or stale embedded version is only discovered after the tag is pushed, and tags cannot be safely reused. Suggested action: add a step in yixr.1 to (a) confirm version source-of-truth (ldflags vs embedded constant) and (b) run 'goreleaser check' and a local 'goreleaser release --snapshot --clean --skip=publish' to validate the config and archive naming end-to-end.

title: Finalize metadata pane alignment and shared selection gutter consistency
description:
## Description
Finish the last metadata-pane polish work for the issue detail screen. The current metadata rail still does not reserve a uniform selection gutter across all line types, so titles, editable fields, read-only fields, divider rows, and label rows do not share one clean left edge. This follow-up must align the metadata pane with the app-wide `› ` selection convention while keeping all rows horizontally stable.

## User Decision
- Keep the global app-wide blue `› ` selection marker.
- Do not invent a metadata-specific selection pattern.
- Reserve a permanent 2-character gutter for **all** metadata lines.
- Selected line uses `› `.
- Non-selected lines use `  `.
- The marker glyph/styling should be shared from one place so global selection-style changes propagate consistently.

## Target Mockup
Status selected:
```text
╭─ Metadata ───────────────────────╮
│  Core                            │
│  Type    : task                  │
│› Status  : open                  │
│  Priority: P1                    │
│  --------------------------------│
│  Ownership                       │
│  Assignee: ...                   │
╰──────────────────────────────────╯
```

Priority selected:
```text
╭─ Metadata ───────────────────────╮
│  Core                            │
│  Type    : task                  │
│  Status  : open                  │
│› Priority: P1                    │
│  --------------------------------│
│  Ownership                       │
│  Assignee: ...                   │
╰──────────────────────────────────╯
```

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Metadata line alignment is uniform across titles, editable rows, read-only rows, label rows, and dividers.
- [ ] Gutter is reserved regardless of metadata focus state.
- [ ] Selection marker glyph/styling comes from one shared helper used across surfaces.
- [ ] Automated tests cover the focused/unfocused alignment cases.
- [ ] Real-app review confirms the metadata pane has one clean left edge.
close_reason: Delivered by beads-workbench-3lhw; remaining search golden fallout tracked separately in beads-workbench-fbu2.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Closing epic: metadata-pane alignment and shared selection gutter work shipped in beads-workbench-3lhw. Remaining fallout is tracked separately in beads-workbench-fbu2 for stale search preview goldens after the gutter change.

title: Metadata pane should reserve a full selection gutter so all rows stay aligned
description:
Discovered during live review of the detail metadata pane after prior selection styling fixes. Expected: the metadata pane should use the app-wide blue `› ` selection pattern, but the entire pane should reserve that two-character selection gutter so **all** lines remain aligned. Actual: selected/editable rows still use a different indentation pattern than other metadata lines, so titles, fields, divider rows, and labels do not share one clean left edge.

## Required UX Rule
- Keep the application-wide selection marker style: `› ` (blue indicator + trailing space).
- Do **not** invent a metadata-specific selection pattern.
- Reserve the same 2-character gutter for every metadata line so all text starts in the same column.
- Selected row uses `› `.
- Non-selected rows use `  `.
- The selection marker glyph and its styling must be sourced from a single shared helper in `internal/ui/styles` (already reused by issue-row rendering); do **not** introduce a new package, struct, or renderer abstraction.

## Target Mockup
Status selected:
```text
╭─ Metadata ───────────────────────╮
│  Core                            │
│  Type    : task                  │
│› Status  : open                  │
│  Priority: P1                    │
│  --------------------------------│
│  Ownership                       │
│  Assignee: ...                   │
╰──────────────────────────────────╯
```

Priority selected:
```text
╭─ Metadata ───────────────────────╮
│  Core                            │
│  Type    : task                  │
│  Status  : open                  │
│› Priority: P1                    │
│  --------------------------------│
│  Ownership                       │
│  Assignee: ...                   │
╰──────────────────────────────────╯
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] The metadata pane reserves a permanent 2-character selection gutter for all rendered lines.
- [ ] Group titles, editable rows, read-only rows, divider rows, and label-group bullet lines all align to the same content column.
- [ ] The selected metadata row uses the standard app-wide `› ` selection marker.
- [ ] Non-selected metadata lines use `  ` in that gutter so alignment stays stable.
- [ ] The 2-character gutter is reserved regardless of focus state; switching focus into/out of Metadata does not shift any line horizontally.
- [ ] The selection marker glyph and styling are sourced from a single shared helper in `internal/ui/styles`; no new package, struct, or renderer abstraction is introduced.
- [ ] Truncation behaves correctly with the styled marker and preserves display-width alignment.
- [ ] Very narrow widths degrade safely instead of producing broken output.
- [ ] Automated tests cover: (a) unfocused gutter reservation, (b) focused selected-row marker only on the selected row, (c) label-row alignment, and (d) divider alignment.
- [ ] Real-app review confirms the metadata pane has one clean left edge and no indentation mismatch.
close_reason: Implemented permanent 2-char metadata gutter via shared styles.SelectionPrefix helper, updated metadata/issuerow render paths, added focused coverage for gutter alignment and narrow-width behavior, and refreshed detail goldens.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Review verdict: Implementation-ready, with small wording tightening recommended below. Diagnosis is accurate — current renderMetadataRail only prefixes editable+interactive rows (metadata.go L83-90), so group titles, non-editable fields (Type, Assignee, Created, Counts), labels and the divider currently render with no gutter at all. Mockup target is therefore not just about selected-row indentation, it requires uniformly prefixing every line emitted by renderMetadataRail.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Likely clean implementation: (1) compute prefix once per rendered line in renderMetadataRail — selected row gets selectedPrefix, every other line (titles, non-editable rows, labels, divider, even when nothing is selected/non-interactive) gets idlePrefix; (2) apply prefix BEFORE truncation and pass width-2 to inner truncation so the gutter never gets eaten on narrow widths; (3) reuse the existing styled-marker helper. The shared-source requirement is already partly met: issuerow/row.go has selectedPrefixText/idlePrefixText + styles.SelectionIndicatorStyle.Render("›"). Easiest path: extract a tiny helper styles.SelectionPrefix(selected, styled) returning (plain, styled) and call it from issuerow.RenderCompact, RenderReferenceCompact, and metadata.go. No new component/package needed.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Scoping concern on "shared selection component/helper": Why: phrased as written, a reader could build a new package or RowSelectionRenderer abstraction. That would be over-engineering — there are only three call sites (issuerow row, issuerow reference row, metadata) and they all want the same 2-char gutter with one styled glyph. Suggested action: reword the AC to require a shared constant+styling helper (e.g. a single function in internal/ui/styles) rather than a shared component, and explicitly forbid introducing a new package or renderer struct.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Edge case: non-interactive metadata pane (selectedField == MetadataFieldNone, e.g. unfocused detail). What: today renderMetadataFieldLine returns the bare line with no gutter when interactive=false, so the pane shifts left by 2 cols when focus leaves Metadata. Why: violates the "all lines reserve the gutter" rule and causes layout jitter on focus changes. Suggested action: gutter must be reserved unconditionally — idle prefix is applied even when no row is selected and even for non-editable rows; only the styled '›' glyph is conditional on selection.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Edge case: divider line. What: divider is currently strings.Repeat("-", width) (metadata.go L52). If a 2-char gutter is reserved, the divider must render as '  ' + strings.Repeat('-', width-2) (or similar) so its left edge matches the content column instead of starting at col 0. Why: mockup shows the divider visually offset to match content; without explicit handling the divider will break the single clean left edge. Suggested action: include divider in AC and specify it uses idle gutter + (width-2) dashes.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Edge case: labels group lines. What: renderLabelLines emits '• label' strings rendered via styles.TruncateString("• "+label, width) at L75. With the new rule these must also be prefixed with the idle gutter so the bullet aligns with field labels. Why: mockup only shows Core/Ownership; labels group is a real surface that will silently regress. Suggested action: explicitly include label-group lines in AC #2 ("group titles, editable rows, read-only rows, dividers, AND label-group bullet lines all align").

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Edge case: truncation + ANSI width. What: prefix must be added BEFORE TruncateString, and TruncateString must see styled '›' (which has ANSI escapes so byte-len ≠ display-width). Existing TruncateString in styles needs to be lipgloss.Width-aware for the styled glyph; current code at L92-97 truncates the plain prefix+line then re-styles only the leading '›' which is fragile if truncation shaves the trailing space. Why: previous misalignment bug (3lhw's predecessor s4f7) was rooted exactly in this plain-vs-styled width mismatch. Suggested action: add an explicit AC item that truncation must operate on display-width, and add a unit test asserting display-width of every metadata line equals min(width, naturalWidth).

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Edge case: very narrow width (width <= 2). What: if width is 2 or less, reserving a gutter leaves zero columns for content. Current code has no explicit floor for the metadata pane. Why: edge case that can produce empty/garbled output rather than graceful degradation. Suggested action: define behavior — either (a) skip rendering the pane below a minimum width, or (b) drop the gutter and render content-only below threshold. Either is fine, but pick one and add it to AC.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Wording change: AC #5 says "sourced from a shared component/helper". What: the word "component" invites a new package/struct. Why: see scoping comment — only a constant+style helper is needed. Suggested action: rewrite to: "The selection marker glyph and its styling are sourced from a single shared helper in internal/ui/styles (already used by issuerow); no new package, struct, or renderer abstraction is introduced."

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Wording change: AC #2 is incomplete. What: lists "group titles, editable rows, read-only rows, and dividers" but omits label-group bullet lines (• label). Why: silent regression risk on issues with labels. Suggested action: add "label-group bullet lines" to AC #2.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Wording change: add explicit AC for unfocused state. What: nothing in current ACs forces gutter to be reserved when the pane is unfocused (selectedField=MetadataFieldNone). Why: easy for an implementer to keep the existing 'interactive==false → no prefix' branch and still satisfy the literal mockups. Suggested action: add AC: "The 2-char gutter is reserved regardless of focus state; switching focus into/out of the Metadata pane does not shift any line horizontally."

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Wording change: add testing AC. What: AC #6 only requires "Real-app review". Why: per project testing policy this kind of layout change should also have an automated golden/unit guard. Predecessor bug s4f7 already added a focused selection-movement alignment test — the same harness should cover the unfocused/focused × selected/non-selected × labels-present matrix. Suggested action: add AC: "Unit/golden tests cover (a) gutter reserved on every line type when unfocused, (b) selected '›' appears only on the selected row when focused, (c) label-group rows aligned with field rows, (d) divider aligned with content column."

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Note on existing related-issues rail consistency. What: renderRelatedIssuesRail (metadata.go L220) currently renders group titles like "Blocked by (3)" and reference rows via issuerow.RenderReferenceCompact, which already has its own '› '/'  ' gutter. Group title "Blocked by (3)" however is emitted with no gutter, so reference rail has the same misalignment class as metadata. Why: out of strict scope of 3lhw (Metadata pane), but if shared selection helper is introduced cleanly it should be reused here too in a follow-up to avoid the same bug class. Suggested action: leave 3lhw scoped to Metadata, but file a follow-up task once 3lhw lands to align the related-issues rail group titles with their reference rows using the same helper.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text:
Verifier: PASS with follow-up.

All metadata rail AC verified:
- styles.SelectionPrefix helper exists and tested (TestSelectionPrefix: pass)
- All 10 TestRenderMetadataRail* tests pass: unfocused gutter, selected marker, label alignment, divider alignment, narrow widths
- Runtime capture confirms: all metadata lines show `  ` idle gutter (group titles, fields, dividers, label bullets) when metadata is unfocused
- Build: clean

Unrelated failure found → filed beads-workbench-fbu2: search preview metadata goldens are stale (3 golden files still expect old no-gutter format; the new gutter output is correct).

Task criteria fully met. Follow-up: beads-workbench-fbu2.

title: Dependencies pane may still skip visible issues during deeper traversal
description:
Discovered from ongoing live user review after group-boundary traversal was fixed. Expected: moving up/down in Dependencies should visit every visible issue row exactly once in render order. Actual: user still reports that sometimes selection appears to jump over an issue. Reviewer analysis indicates this is a confirmed real bug: the renderer can show the same issue ID in multiple visible groups, while the selectable dependency list de-duplicates by ID, making one visible row effectively unreachable/skipped.

## Root Cause
Selection is keyed by flattened unique issue IDs, but rendering still shows duplicate visible rows when the same issue appears in multiple groups. That makes some visible rows impossible to land on during one-row-at-a-time traversal.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] One-row-at-a-time traversal can land on every visible dependency issue row in render order.
- [ ] If the same issue appears in multiple visible groups, either each visible row is separately selectable or duplicate visible rows are suppressed/de-emphasized so traversal matches what the user sees.
- [ ] Real-app verification confirms no visible issue row is skipped during stepwise traversal.
close_reason: Aligned dependencies rendering with traversal order by suppressing duplicate visible rows across groups; added focused model/UI tests and verified with real-app capture.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Confirmed view/model mismatch causing the skip. What: browserItemsFromDependencies() (model.go:427-459) deduplicates IDs across all groups. But renderDependenciesPaneLines() (details.go:269-271) renders the original detail.BlockedBy/Blocks/Related refs which may contain the same ID in multiple groups. The render uses selectedMatched=first-match-only (line 300), so if issue X appears in both 'Blocked by' and 'Related' visually, BrowserItems has it once, selection highlights only the first visual occurrence, and the second visual row is never selectable — appearing as a 'skip'. Why: this is a real bug, not just a user perception issue. The render list and the selectable list are built from different sources. Suggested action: either (a) render only what's in BrowserItems per group (filter each group's refs to the deduped set), or (b) render duplicates but make each occurrence independently selectable. Option (a) is simpler — show each issue exactly once in its first-appearing group.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Investigation-first framing is unnecessary given the code analysis. What: the issue is written as 'investigate whether this is real' but the code clearly shows the view/model mismatch. The acceptance criteria are investigation-oriented rather than fix-oriented. Why: this adds an unnecessary investigation phase before implementation. Suggested action: update acceptance criteria to: (1) dependency pane renders each issue ID exactly once in its first-appearing group, (2) every visible issue row is reachable by one-at-a-time traversal, (3) test covers an issue appearing in multiple dependency groups.

title: Metadata actionable-field order and Enter edit flow do not match expected UX
description:
Discovered from live user review after metadata field navigation/editing shipped. Expected: metadata actionable-field traversal follows a sensible order, and Enter on an actionable field opens a dialog to change that field. User decision: actionable order must be `Status` first, then `Priority`; Enter on both `Status` and `Priority` should open a dialog; within that dialog the user changes the value and presses Enter to save, or Escape to close without saving. Actual: user reports the metadata selection order feels wrong under up/down traversal, and the current `Priority` behavior does not match the expected dialog-driven change flow.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Metadata actionable-field order is `Status` first, `Priority` second.
- [ ] Up/Down in Metadata traverses actionable fields in that order only.
- [ ] Enter on `Status` opens the status-change dialog.
- [ ] Enter on `Priority` opens a priority-change dialog (not instant cycle).
- [ ] In both dialogs, Enter saves the changed value.
- [ ] In both dialogs, Escape closes without saving.
- [ ] Real-app verification confirms the full UX flow: select field -> Enter opens dialog -> choose value -> Enter saves / Escape cancels.
close_reason: Implemented metadata actionable order (Status then Priority), switched Priority Enter to modal edit flow, added focused tests, and verified runtime dialog open/cancel flow.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Issue conflates two distinct problems. What: (1) field traversal order and (2) Priority edit UX are bundled in one bug. These have different resolution paths — order is a straightforward code fix, Priority UX needs a product decision. Why: mixing investigation-needed work with clear-fix work blocks the clear fix on the unclear one. Suggested action: split into two issues — one for traversal order (implementable now: verify and fix order in metadataGroups to Status-first-Priority-second or vice versa), one for the Priority edit UX product decision (dialog vs cycle). Alternatively, if you want to keep it as one issue, at minimum the acceptance criteria should mark criteria #3 (product decision) as a blocker for criteria #4 (Enter behavior consistency).

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Current traversal order is determined by metadataGroups() field order in metadata.go:105-108: Type, Priority, Status. Only Priority and Status are editable, so actionable traversal is Priority→Status (top to bottom). What: if user expects Status first, the order is wrong. But the issue doesn't state what the expected order IS. Why: without specifying the target order, the implementer has to guess. Suggested action: state the intended order explicitly in acceptance criteria before implementation.

title: Metadata edit dialogs should support standard Enter-to-confirm and Escape-to-cancel behavior
description:
Discovered from live user review of detail metadata editing. Expected: once a metadata edit dialog is open, the user should be able to change the value and press Enter again to save it; pressing Escape should close the dialog without saving. Actual: user reports the dialog change flow feels broken and not like standard confirm/cancel behavior. Existing tests confirm status editing opens a modal, but live UX needs verification for keyboard submit/cancel behavior.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] In a live-app metadata edit dialog, Enter submits the selected/edited value and saves it.
- [ ] Escape closes the dialog without saving.
- [ ] Verification covers both save and cancel paths.
- [ ] Enter on an unchanged value follows a defined no-op/close behavior without causing a broken save flow.
- [ ] If current modal behavior differs, the fix aligns metadata edit dialogs with standard confirm/cancel expectations.
close_reason: Implemented Enter submit + Escape cancel for metadata status dialog with save/cancel/no-op tests.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Issue is well-scoped and ready to implement. What: acceptance criteria are clear and testable — Enter saves, Escape cancels. One gap: criteria don't specify behavior when Enter is pressed with no change (same value re-selected). Why: edge case matters because it determines whether a no-op save triggers a bd update call or silently closes. Suggested action: add criterion for no-change case (recommend: close dialog silently without issuing update).

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Overlap with 8haw: 8haw criterion #4 ('Enter behavior for actionable metadata fields is consistent') is essentially a subset of this issue. What: if vvoe is implemented fully, 8haw's Enter-behavior criterion is satisfied. Why: implementing both independently risks conflicting fixes. Suggested action: implement vvoe first, then update 8haw to remove the Enter-behavior criterion and focus it purely on traversal order + Priority UX decision.

title: Metadata selected-row prefix still causes value-column misalignment
description:
Discovered from live user review after the metadata selection styling polish shipped. Expected: metadata rows should keep their value columns aligned whether `Priority` or `Status` is selected. Actual: on closed issue detail views (example: `beads-workbench-omd.1`), the selected-row prefix still causes the selected metadata row to indent differently from sibling rows, e.g. `Priority: P1` vs `› Status  : closed`, so the value column does not line up cleanly. Repro: open `beads-workbench-omd.1` in detail mode, focus Metadata, compare alignment of `Priority` and `Status` rows as selection moves. Impact: metadata pane feels visually sloppy even though shared selection styling is mostly in place.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Selected and non-selected editable metadata rows preserve the same label/value column alignment.
- [ ] Moving selection between `Priority` and `Status` does not visually shift indentation or the value column.
- [ ] Real-app review on a closed issue detail confirms clean alignment.
close_reason: Fixed metadata selected-row prefix rendering to preserve stable label/value alignment; added focused selection-movement alignment test and verified metadata rail tests pass.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Alignment bug root cause is clear in metadata.go:78-89. What: when interactive=true, selected rows get '› ' (2 chars) prefix and non-selected get '  ' (2 chars), but the '›' is styled via SelectionIndicatorStyle which likely has different rendered width than a plain space. The line content itself (label:value) stays the same, so the alignment issue is purely in the prefix rendering width. Why: lipgloss styled characters can have different terminal widths than their plain equivalents depending on style attributes. Suggested action: verify lipgloss.Width of the styled prefix matches the plain prefix width; if not, pad to equal rendered width. This is a straightforward fix — ready to implement without product decisions.

title: Focus-regain auto-refresh waits for stale interval
description: Discovered during acceptance review of beads-workbench-t7o.4. Expected: after a real blur->focus cycle, the active view refreshes immediately so off-terminal tracker changes are visible without waiting for the ~60s stale interval. Actual: quick focus regain does not refresh the active surface unless it is already dirty/stale. Reproduced on board and detail. Board repro: build /tmp/bwb, seed embedded fixture repo, start bwb, run bd update bwf-1 --title 'Immediate focus refresh ready', then send focus-reporting blur/focus within a few seconds; board still shows the old title. Detail repro: open detail for bwf-2, run bd update bwf-2 --title 'Detail focus refresh blocked', then blur/focus within a few seconds; detail still shows the old title. The refresh only happens once the stale interval elapses. Likely cause: focus-regain path in internal/app/model.go still gates refresh through shouldRefreshSurface().
acceptance_criteria:
- Blur->focus triggers an immediate refresh of the active surface without requiring stale/dirty state
- Repro above shows updated board data immediately after focus regain
- Repro above shows updated detail data immediately after focus regain
- Add regression coverage for focus-regain refresh across active surfaces
close_reason: Implemented immediate active-surface refresh on real focus regain by bypassing stale/dirty gating while preserving modal/loading guards, active-surface scope, scheduled tick behavior, and adding regression tests for board/detail/search focus-regain refresh safety.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Execution started from acceptance-review follow-up. Goal: blur->focus should bypass stale/dirty gating and force immediate refresh of the active surface with regression coverage for board/detail/search behavior.

title: Auto-refresh in search mode types reload key into query
description: Discovered during verification of beads-workbench-t7o.1. Expected: focus/tick refresh in search mode reloads current results without changing the query or depending on a typed rune. Actual: internal/app/model.go refreshActiveSurfaceCmd sends tea.KeyMsg{Type: tea.KeyRunes, Runes: []rune("r")}; internal/mode/search/model.go handles rune input first while query focus is active, so auto-refresh appends "r" to the query instead of issuing a pure reload. Repro: build /tmp/bwb, seed the embedded fixture repo, switch to Search, leave query focus active, then send blur/focus or wait for the refresh tick; the query becomes "r" and results change unexpectedly. Impact: auto-refresh corrupts active search state, and non-rune reload bindings are not honored by this path.
close_reason: Fixed search auto-refresh to call explicit Reload API, preserving query/selection and covering with focused app/search tests.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Root cause confirmed: model.go:768-773 synthesizes a tea.KeyMsg rune and calls m.search.Update() directly. search/model.go:123 unconditionally appends any KeyRunes to query when FocusQuery is active — this fires BEFORE the reload keybind match at line 161 because the FocusQuery branch is a separate earlier case. The synthetic rune is indistinguishable from user typing. Fix: search mode should expose an explicit exported method (e.g. Reload() tea.Cmd) or accept a dedicated RefreshMsg type that bypasses the Update key-dispatch entirely. The shell calls search.Reload() instead of injecting synthetic keys. This is architecturally correct: shell owns orchestration timing, mode owns its own reload logic without coupling to keybind config. Edge cases: (1) typing state — if m.typing is true during reload, debounce timer may conflict; Reload() should not set m.typing. (2) selection preservation — triggerSearch() resets results; Reload() should preserve selectedIdx or restore it post-fetch. (3) shell key capture — CapturesShellKey at line 334 is irrelevant here since shell bypasses Update entirely. (4) loading guard — shell already checks searchIsLoading() so double-fire is safe but Reload() should still be idempotent. Required tests: (a) unit test calling Reload() with FocusQuery active confirms query unchanged, (b) unit test confirming selection index preserved across reload, (c) integration test that focus-regain in search mode does not mutate query. Verdict: straightforward — the fix is a 10-line method + 3-line call-site change.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Reviewer analysis: root cause is synthetic KeyMsg rune injection from internal/app/model.go into search.Update(), which is interpreted as user typing before reload key matching when query focus is active. Recommended fix: expose an explicit search reload API (for example Reload() tea.Cmd calling triggerSearch()) and have shell orchestration call that instead of synthesizing a key event.

title: Dependencies pane navigation is asymmetric across group boundaries
description:
Discovered from live user review of the current detail UI on `beads-workbench-t7o.2`. Expected: dependency selection should traverse grouped rows in a predictable linear order, so moving down from the last row of one non-empty group should enter the first row of the next non-empty group, and moving up should behave symmetrically in reverse. Actual: in the Dependencies pane for `beads-workbench-t7o.2`, when selection is in `Blocks (2)`, pressing Down on the last Blocks row does not advance into the `Structure (5)` list below it. However, pressing Up from the top of the Blocks list can move into the Structure list, so traversal is asymmetric and confusing. Repro: 1) `go build -o /tmp/bwb ./cmd/bwb` 2) run `BD_NON_INTERACTIVE=1 /tmp/bwb` from the repo root 3) navigate to `beads-workbench-t7o.2` and open detail mode 4) focus Dependencies with `LEFT` 5) move selection within `Blocks (2)` to the second row 6) press Down and observe selection does not enter `Structure (5)` 7) from the top Blocks row, press Up and observe selection can move into Structure. Impact: dependency browsing feels broken because list traversal order is not intuitive or consistent across group boundaries.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Dependency selection traverses non-empty grouped dependency rows in a consistent linear order across `Blocked by`, `Blocks`, `Related`, and `Structure`.
- [ ] Pressing Down on the last row of a group moves to the first selectable row of the next non-empty group.
- [ ] Pressing Up on the first row of a group moves to the last selectable row of the previous non-empty group.
- [ ] Real-app verification on `beads-workbench-t7o.2` confirms symmetric traversal between `Blocks` and `Structure`.
close_reason: Fixed dependency navigation list to match rendered group row order (Blocked by -> Blocks -> Related -> Structure), added focused traversal tests, and verified symmetric Blocks/Structure movement on beads-workbench-t7o.2 in runtime UI capture.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Root cause confirmed: moveRelatedSelection (model.go:309-324) navigates a flat BrowserItems list, but the rendering in dependencyGroups (details.go:273-283) includes Structure items via browserItems only when ParentGroupBrowser is present. The flat list is built by browserItemsFromDependencies (model.go:436-465) which concatenates BlockedBy+Blocks+Related but does NOT include Structure/browserItems. So when ParentGroupBrowser exists, browserItemsFromParentGroup builds a completely different list (parent+children), and BrowserItems is that structure list — meaning dependency-group rows (BlockedBy/Blocks/Related) shown in the rendering are NOT in the navigation list at all. The asymmetric behavior comes from the mismatch between what renderRelationshipGroups renders vs what BrowserItems contains. Fix: BrowserItems must be rebuilt as a flat ordered list matching the exact rendered row order across ALL visible non-empty groups (BlockedBy → Blocks → Related → Structure), so moveRelatedSelection traverses them linearly. syncBrowserPanel needs to unify both paths.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Coupling note: 4k8p and 3ymu are independent. 4k8p is purely rendering (metadata.go + details.go renderMetadataPaneLines). 3ymu is purely navigation model (model.go syncBrowserPanel/browserItemsFromDependencies). No shared code paths. Recommended order: fix 3ymu first — it's a functional navigation bug (users cannot reach items). Then 4k8p — it's cosmetic polish. Both are small (< 30 lines changed each).

title: Metadata pane uses redundant editable banner and non-shared selection styling
description:
Discovered from live user review of the current detail UI after the earlier metadata-interaction follow-up was shipped. Expected: when the Metadata pane is focused, the pane border already communicates focus, so metadata field selection should use the same shared selected-row/cursor treatment as Dependencies/dashboard (same alignment and same blue cursor/selection style) without adding a redundant chevron banner. Actual: in detail mode on `beads-workbench-t7o.2`, focusing Metadata shows a top line `› Editable: Status, Priority`, which duplicates the pane-focus signal and introduces an extra chevron that does not match the rest of the UI. The selected metadata field is also rendered with an inline `›` prefix (`› Priority: P1` / `› Status ...`) rather than with the same shared selected-row treatment used in Dependencies/dashboard, so text alignment shifts and the selection affordance feels inconsistent. Repro: 1) `go build -o /tmp/bwb ./cmd/bwb` 2) run `BD_NON_INTERACTIVE=1 /tmp/bwb` from the repo root 3) open `beads-workbench-t7o.2` in detail mode (`3`) 4) focus Metadata with `RIGHT` 5) observe the `› Editable: Status, Priority` banner and inline-chevron selection styling while moving between Status/Priority. Impact: metadata interaction works functionally but feels visually inconsistent and less polished than the rest of the selection system.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] The redundant `› Editable: Status, Priority` banner is removed or replaced with a non-chevron affordance that does not compete with pane focus.
- [ ] Metadata field selection uses the same shared selection/cursor component style as Dependencies/dashboard, including consistent color treatment and stable text alignment.
- [ ] Moving between Status and Priority does not shift row alignment with ad-hoc inline prefixes.
- [ ] Real-app review confirms Metadata selection feels visually consistent with the rest of the app.
close_reason: Removed metadata editable banner and switched metadata selection to shared styled row indicator with stable alignment for editable fields; verified via focused tests and runtime UI capture.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Root cause confirmed: renderMetadataPaneLines (details.go:354-357) unconditionally prepends '› Editable: Status, Priority' when focused=true, and renderMetadataRail (metadata.go:63-64) prefixes selected fields with '› ' inline. Both are ad-hoc — no shared selection component is used. Fix: (1) Delete lines 354-357 (the banner). (2) Replace the inline '› ' prefix in metadata.go:63-64 with the same issuerow.RenderReferenceCompact or shared selected-row style used in the dependencies pane — this means adding a padding/indent to ALL metadata rows and using a highlight color on the selected one, so alignment stays stable. Implementation is self-contained in these two files.

title: relates-to dependents still render under Blocks in detail view
description:
Discovered during verification of beads-workbench-x2u in the built binary against the embedded fixture repo. Expected: after running `bd dep relate <issue-a> <issue-b>`, the linked issue appears only under Related in detail view. Actual: `bd show --json` includes the relates-to issue in both dependencies and dependents with `dependency_type=relates-to`; the gateway normalization was fixed on the dependency side but the dependents path still maps entries straight into Blocks, so the live app shows the same issue under Blocks and Related.

## Root Cause
Gateway handling for dependents still treats all dependent refs as Blocks. A `relates-to` dependent must be classified as Related, not Blocks.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Gateway/detail normalization classifies `relates-to` dependents as Related instead of Blocks.
- [ ] Unit tests cover the dependent-side `relates-to` case.
- [ ] In the real app, an issue whose dependents include a `relates-to` entry shows that issue only under Related, not under Blocks.
close_reason: Fixed dependent-side normalization: relates-to dependents now map to Related (not Blocks); added gateway + embedded fixture coverage and verified targeted tests.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Root cause confirmed at read_gateway.go:152. What: referencesFromPayload(primary.Dependents) dumps all dependents into Blocks without checking dependency_type. The x2u fix added relates-to filtering only on the dependencies side (line 543), not the dependents side. A bd dep relate A B produces relates-to entries in both A.dependencies and B.dependents — the dependencies side now correctly routes to Related, but the dependents side still falls through to Blocks. Why: this means every relates-to link appears under both Related AND Blocks, which is the exact symptom described. Suggested action: replace referencesFromPayload for dependents with a type-aware function (like dependencyReferencesFromPayload but for the dependent direction) that routes relates-to dependents to Related and parent-child dependents to nowhere (they are handled by parent-group). This is the mirror of the x2u fix.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Missing acceptance criterion. What: no criterion explicitly states that dependents with dependency_type relates-to must NOT appear in Blocks. Why: a tasker could fix the Related rendering without removing the duplicate from Blocks, leaving the bug half-fixed. Suggested action: add criterion: 'An issue whose dependents include a relates-to entry must show that entry only under Related, not under Blocks.'

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Ordering relative to 74r/6eq. What: 6sk is a functional correctness bug in the gateway layer (read_gateway.go). 74r/6eq are cosmetic layout bugs in details.go. Why: 6sk changes what data flows into Blocks vs Related, which affects row counts in the Dependencies pane. If 74r width tuning is done first, the visible result will shift when 6sk removes phantom Blocks entries. No code conflict — 6sk touches read_gateway.go, 74r/6eq touch details.go — but visual verification would need redoing. Suggested action: fix 6sk before 74r/6eq. 6sk and yxjx are independent (gateway vs UI) and can run in parallel.

title: Dependencies pane selection ignores blocked/related rows
description:
Discovered during verification of beads-workbench-59z. Expected: in detail-mode Dependencies, Blocked by / Blocks / Related rows visibly use the shared selected-row treatment, up/down moves that visible selection, and Enter opens the selected dependency issue. Actual: the live app only derives dependency-pane selection from BrowserItems/Structure rows. On an issue with normal dependency refs but no parent-group structure (for example the embedded fixture issue bwf-2), rows render compact metadata but no dependency row is visibly selected, down does not move a visible selection, and Enter does not open a dependency.

## Root Cause
`BrowserItems` is only populated from `ParentGroupBrowser`. When an issue has BlockedBy/Blocks/Related refs but no parent-group structure, the model leaves the selectable dependency list empty, so the render fix from beads-workbench-59z has nothing meaningful to select.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] When an issue has BlockedBy, Blocks, or Related refs but no parent-group structure, the detail-mode selectable dependency list is populated from those refs.
- [ ] The default dependency selection is initialized predictably for those refs.
- [ ] Up/down in Dependencies visibly moves selection across blocked/blocks/related rows in the real app.
- [ ] Enter on the selected dependency opens the selected issue in the real app.
- [ ] The fix preserves existing parent-group/structure browsing behavior when that context is present.
close_reason: Fixed detail-mode dependency browser population for non-parent-group issues; added model/app tests for selection defaults and Enter-open behavior; focused detail/app test suites pass.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Review: Root cause confirmed and bug is well-scoped. What: BrowserItems is only populated from ParentGroupBrowser (model.go:312), never from BlockedBy/Blocks/Related refs. When an issue has blockers but no parent-group structure, BrowserItems stays empty, so moveRelatedSelection has nothing to move and BrowserSelectedIndex stays -1. The render side (59z fix) correctly passes selection state now, but there are zero items to select. Why: this is the actual functional gap — 59z fixed render wiring but the model never populates BrowserItems for non-structure dependency rows. Suggested action: populateBrowserFromIssue (or equivalent) must flatten BlockedBy + Blocks + Related into BrowserItems when no parent-group is active, so selection/navigation/enter all work on ordinary dependency refs.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Review: Missing acceptance criterion. What: no criterion says 'BrowserItems must be populated from blocked-by/blocks/related refs when parent-group is absent.' The existing criteria inherited from 59z assume render-only fixes. Why: without this criterion, a tasker could fix render wiring again and miss the model population gap — exactly what happened with 59z. Suggested action: add criterion: 'When an issue has BlockedBy, Blocks, or Related refs but no parent-group structure, BrowserItems must contain those refs and BrowserSelectedIndex must default to 0.'

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Review: Ordering relative to 74r/6eq. What: 2l4 is a functional bug (dependency browsing is broken); 74r/6eq are cosmetic layout bugs. Why: 2l4 blocks the core use case of navigating dependencies. Layout polish on a non-functional pane is wasted effort if 2l4 changes how BrowserItems are populated (which may affect row count and scroll behavior). Suggested action: implement 2l4 before 74r/6eq. x2u is independent (gateway-only, no UI coupling) and can proceed in parallel with anything.

title: Dependency issue rows do not show ANSI color/styled dashboard rendering in detail pane
description: Discovered from user review after epic beads-workbench-90w was closed. Expected: dependency issue rows in the Dependencies pane should use the same styled/colored rendering as dashboard rows. Actual: dependency rendering currently calls `issuerow.RenderReferenceCompact(... Styled: false)` so ANSI/styled dashboard-like color treatment is absent. Repro: compare dashboard issue rows vs Dependencies pane rows in the running app. Impact: the promised renderer reuse is incomplete visually and dependency rows feel flatter than dashboard rows.
close_reason: Closed

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Review: Actionable and trivial — flip Styled:false to Styled:true in renderReferenceRow (details.go:531). However, this is the same code site as beads-workbench-59z (Selected:false at line 529). What: these two bugs touch the same function call with the same root cause — renderReferenceRow doesn't pass through real state. Why: implementing them separately risks merge conflicts and duplicated test updates for no benefit. Suggested action: merge beads-workbench-2a6 into beads-workbench-59z as a single fix that wires both Selected and Styled through renderReferenceRow.

title: Dependencies pane selection and keyboard navigation should match dashboard issue selection behavior
description:
Discovered from user review after epic beads-workbench-90w was closed. Expected: in the Dependencies pane, up/down keys should provide the same visible issue selection behavior as the dashboard, including shared row styling/color treatment for the selected dependency and non-selected dependency rows. Actual: detail-mode state tracks a related selection index, but dependency rendering hardcodes `renderReferenceRow(... Selected:false, Styled:false)`, so the pane does not show dashboard-like selection or ANSI/styled row rendering. Repro: focus Dependencies in detail mode and use up/down keys on a detail item with multiple dependency/structure rows. Impact: dependency browsing is confusing and the pane does not behave like a proper issue list.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Dependency rows render through the shared compact issue-row path with styled output enabled.
- [ ] The currently selected dependency row uses the same visible selected treatment as dashboard rows.
- [ ] Up/down in Dependencies moves the visible selected row predictably.
- [ ] Enter still opens the selected dependency issue.
- [ ] The fix applies to dependency/structure rows rendered in this pane without introducing a separate one-off row renderer.
close_reason: Fixed dependencies-pane row rendering to use shared styled compact rows with selected issue highlighting wired to BrowserSelectedIssueID; verified detail UI and detail mode tests pass.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Review: This is partially already implemented. Code at model.go:178-186 shows moveRelatedSelection is called on scroll-up/down when FocusPaneDependencies is active, and BrowserSelectedIndex is tracked. The real bug is in the render side: renderReferenceRow (details.go:526-532) hardcodes Selected:false and Styled:false, so the selection state is never visually reflected. What: the model-side selection logic exists; only the render path is broken. Why: this means the fix is simpler than the description implies — it's a render wiring bug, not a missing navigation model. Suggested action: narrow the description to 'pass BrowserSelectedIndex to renderReferenceRow so Selected reflects actual state'. This overlaps heavily with beads-workbench-2a6 (Styled:false). Consider merging them into one fix.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Set review — this is solid and ready. What: the prior reviewer correctly identified the fix is render-only (renderReferenceRow hardcodes Selected:false, Styled:false at line 526-532). The model-side selection logic already exists. Acceptance criteria are clear and testable. Why: this is the highest-value fix in the set — it makes the dependency pane actually interactive. One coupling note: if 74r (width change) lands first, the styled rows will render with the new width, which is fine. If 6eq (framing) also lands, the styled rows need to fit inside frame borders. Suggested action: implement this first or second. The fix is: pass selection index and Styled:true through renderReferenceRow. Ensure renderRelationshipGroups receives the selected index to compare against flat row position.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Verifier rerun: FAIL. Shared compact rows render, but live Dependencies selection still only follows BrowserItems/Structure context. On fixture issue bwf-2, blocker rows show no selected-row treatment, DOWN leaves no visible selection change, and ENTER does not open a dependency. Follow-up: beads-workbench-2l4.

title: Metadata rail should use a framed panel instead of an unboxed text rail
description:
Discovered from user review after epic beads-workbench-90w was closed. Expected: the metadata side panel should use a frame/border similar to Dependencies and Content. User decision: frame the Metadata pane only; Dependencies and Content are already correctly framed. Actual: metadata is rendered as an unboxed text rail with a vertical separator only, which looks mechanically weaker and less polished than the other panes. Repro: open the detail screen in the real app and compare visual treatment of Dependencies/Content vs Metadata. Impact: detail layout feels visually inconsistent and less polished than intended.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Metadata pane is rendered in a framed/bordered panel.
- [ ] Dependencies and Content keep their existing framed treatment; this follow-up only adds a frame to Metadata.
- [ ] Real-app review confirms improved visual consistency across the three panes.
close_reason: Rendered metadata as framed FormSection pane while preserving existing dependencies/content pane framing and interaction behavior.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Review: Well-scoped, straightforward visual change. No hidden complexity. One observation: the current rendering in renderThreePane (details.go:195-198) uses a '│' separator for metadata but no box for any pane — Dependencies and Content are also unboxed columns. What: verify that Dependencies and Content actually use frames/borders today. If they don't, this bug may be requesting framing for metadata alone, which would create visual inconsistency in the opposite direction. Why: the description says 'match the other major panes' but if those panes aren't framed either, the fix scope changes. Suggested action: confirm current frame state of all three panes before implementing; if none are framed, consider whether all three should be, or just metadata.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Set review — frame scope is ambiguous and prior reviewer's concern is unresolved. What: the prior comment correctly identified that Dependencies and Content are ALSO unboxed (renderThreePane joins columns with padToWidth + gap, no borders). The description says 'match the other major panes' but those panes have no frames either. Why: if you frame only metadata, you create a different inconsistency. If you frame all three, scope triples. The owner hasn't responded to clarify intent. Suggested action: owner must decide: (a) frame all three panes consistently, or (b) frame metadata only and accept the asymmetry. Option (a) is the right call but needs to be stated explicitly. This issue is blocked on that decision.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Verifier rerun: PASS. Built /tmp/bwb and reviewed real detail mode against embedded fixture at 120x34 and 160x34. Metadata now renders as a framed panel, three-pane composition looks coherent, and metadata paging/scroll behavior remained intact in runtime review.

title: Dependencies pane is too narrow relative to content and should use a larger share of width
description:
Discovered from user review after epic beads-workbench-90w was closed. Expected: the Dependencies pane should use a somewhat larger share of width so dependency rows and structure data are comfortably readable beside Content, but not as large as one-third. User decision: target roughly 25% of available width (subject to sensible min/max bounds) instead of the current narrow near-fixed 22..28 column pane. Actual: detail layout currently uses a narrow left pane derived from `splitThreePaneWidths` with `left = clamp(available/5, 22..28)`, which makes Dependencies feel too cramped. Repro: open the real app detail view at 120x34 or 160x34 and compare Dependencies width vs Content width; dependency titles truncate aggressively. Impact: relationship browsing/readability is weaker than intended and panel balance feels off.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Dependencies width uses an updated formula targeting roughly 25% of the available pane width rather than the current near-fixed narrow rail.
- [ ] The pane remains bounded with sensible min/max values so narrow terminals still render acceptably.
- [ ] Real-app review at 120x34 and 160x34 shows materially better dependency readability without starving Content.
close_reason: Updated detail pane width split to target ~25% dependencies rail with bounded min/max and verified at 120x34 and 160x34.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Review: Well-scoped and actionable. The code path is clear (splitThreePaneWidths, line 212). One concern: the description says 'roughly around double current width' but doesn't specify a target ratio or formula. What: acceptance criteria should specify the desired allocation (e.g., 'left pane gets 1/3 of available minus metadata' or a specific min/max range). Why: without a target, the implementer will guess and may need rework. Suggested action: add a concrete width target or ratio to the description, e.g., 'left = clamp(available/3, 30..50)' or similar.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Set review — width target still missing. What: prior reviewer correctly flagged that no concrete ratio/formula is specified. The description still says 'roughly around double' which is not implementable. Why: the implementer will pick an arbitrary number, get feedback, and rework. Suggested action: owner should specify a target formula before this is ready for implementation, e.g., 'left = clamp(available/3, 30..50)' with metadata staying at 34. This is a 1-line constant change once the target is decided — do not overcomplicate.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Execution ordering note for full set (74r, 6eq, 59z, axe, pxs). Recommended order: (1) 59z — render wiring fix, highest value, no dependencies. (2) 74r — width change, requires owner to specify target first. (3) 6eq — framing, requires owner decision on scope, touches same renderThreePane code as 74r so do after. (4) axe — new feature, lowest risk of rework if done after layout stabilizes. (5) pxs — investigation only, may close as non-bug. Coupling risk: 74r and 6eq both modify renderThreePane/splitThreePaneWidths and the column assembly code. If done in parallel they will conflict. Do them sequentially.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Verifier rerun: PASS. Built /tmp/bwb and reviewed real detail mode against embedded fixture at 120x34 and 160x34. Dependencies rail is materially wider (~29 cols at 120, ~39 at 160), titles are more readable, content remains usable, and no dependency-pane regressions were observed.

title: Detail content pane leaves lower half blank and does not anchor comments low
description:
## Description
Discovered during verification of beads-workbench-90w.2. Expected: the redesigned 3-pane detail screen should use available vertical space well, with comments rendered as a visually secondary lower subsection inside the dominant Content pane. Actual: built-binary runtime captures at 120x34, 140x34, and 160x34 against the embedded fixture show description/notes/comments clustered in the upper half of Content with a large blank region below, so comments are not positioned as a lower subsection and the layout still feels vertically under-filled.

## Likely Root Cause
The Content pane currently renders sections top-down and then pads remaining height with blank lines, so short/medium content stays top-aligned and comments are never intentionally pushed lower inside the pane.

## Fix Direction
Preserve the accepted single Content-pane scroll model. Rebalance vertical allocation inside the Content pane by inserting adaptive spacer lines between upper content (details/notes) and the comments subsection when total content height is shorter than the available inner pane height. When content already fills/exceeds available height, insert no spacer and preserve current scroll behavior.

## Constraints
- Do not split Content into multiple focus panes or multiple independent scroll regions.
- Do not hardcode for a single terminal size such as 120x34.
- Keep comments visually secondary and lower in the Content pane when there is spare height.
- Preserve shared rendering/component reuse already introduced by beads-workbench-90w.1.

## Repro
1. `go build -o /tmp/bwb ./cmd/bwb`
2. `repoPath="$(mktemp -d)" && sh internal/testing/e2e/embeddedfixture/setup.sh "$repoPath" internal/testing/e2e/embeddedfixture/seed.json`
3. `python3 scripts/capture_bwb_screen.py --cwd "$repoPath" --width 120 --height 34 --startup-wait 1.2 --steps '0.2:3,0.2:CTRL+Q' -- -- env BD_NON_INTERACTIVE=1 /tmp/bwb`
4. Repeat at 140x34 and 160x34.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] When total center-pane content is shorter than the available inner height, the comments subsection is pushed lower instead of remaining immediately under notes/body.
- [ ] When total center-pane content fills or exceeds available inner height, no spacer is inserted and scrolling behavior remains unchanged.
- [ ] Spacer calculation is adaptive to available pane height rather than hardcoded to one terminal size.
- [ ] Runtime captures at 120x34 and 160x34 show materially stronger vertical usage in the Content pane.
- [ ] Comments remain visually secondary and read as a lower subsection within Content.
close_reason: Implemented adaptive Content-pane spacer insertion so comments shift lower when content is shorter than pane height while preserving existing single-scroll behavior when content fills/exceeds available height; updated detail renderer goldens and verified via focused tests and 120x34/160x34 runtime captures.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Root cause is clear from code: renderContentPaneLines() builds content top-down (title, description, notes, comments) and returns a flat line slice. sliceWithOffset() then pads remaining lines with empty strings to fill innerHeight (line 371-373 of details.go). The content is top-aligned by construction — there is no mechanism to push comments toward the bottom or distribute vertical space between sections. The bug description correctly identifies the symptom but does not name this root cause. Suggested action: add a one-line root cause note to the bug description referencing the top-aligned padding in sliceWithOffset and the lack of vertical space distribution in renderContentPaneLines.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Fix direction: renderContentPaneLines should insert spacer lines between the notes section and the comments section so comments are pushed toward the bottom of the available height. Specifically: compute the total lines used by title+description+notes and by comments, then fill the gap with empty lines so comments anchor to the lower portion of innerHeight. This keeps rendering as a flat []string, preserves sliceWithOffset scroll semantics unchanged, and requires no new abstractions. Why this over alternatives: (1) bottom-aligning ALL content would look wrong when content is short, (2) a split-viewport approach would add complexity and break the single-scroll model, (3) spacer insertion is ~10 lines of code in one function.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Risk: spacer insertion must be height-aware, meaning renderContentPaneLines needs the available innerHeight as a parameter (it currently only takes detail and width). If the spacer is computed naively without clamping, short-content issues with many comments could produce zero or negative spacer — handle with max(0, ...). Also: when total content exceeds innerHeight (long description + many comments), spacer should be 0 and scroll should work normally. Test both cases.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Bug acceptance criteria are underspecified. The bug says 'distributes content more intentionally' which is not testable. Suggested acceptance language: (1) When total content lines < innerHeight, comments section starts no higher than 60% of innerHeight (i.e. lower portion). (2) When total content lines >= innerHeight, no spacer is inserted and scroll works as before. (3) Runtime captures at 120x34 and 160x34 show no blank region larger than 2 lines between the last notes line and the comments header. These three criteria let a tasker verify mechanically.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Anti-pattern warning: do NOT solve this by adding a second scrollable viewport or splitting Content into two Bubble Tea models. That would break the single-scroll-offset model already working in sliceWithOffset and add disproportionate complexity for a layout spacing fix. The fix belongs entirely in renderContentPaneLines (plus passing innerHeight into it).

title: Preserve board and search context during auto-refresh
description:
## Description
Make board and search safe for automatic reload so freshness checks do not yank the user away from current context.

## Instructions
1. Introduce a non-destructive board auto-refresh path separate from the existing manual `r` full reload behavior.
2. Update `internal/mode/board/model.go` so board auto-refresh can preserve the focused column and previously selected issue when that issue still exists after reload.
3. Define predictable fallback behavior when the prior board selection no longer exists.
4. Update `internal/mode/search/model.go` and shell orchestration so search auto-refresh reruns the current query only when safe and does not interrupt active typing.
5. Preserve existing selection/query state as much as possible across auto-refresh, and do not force mode switches or clear current detail state as a side effect of board/search refresh.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Board auto-refresh restores prior selection and focus when the issue still exists
- [ ] Board uses a deterministic fallback when the prior selection disappears
- [ ] Board auto-refresh is non-destructive relative to the existing manual full reload behavior
- [ ] Search auto-refresh does not fire while the user is actively typing in the query field
- [ ] Search refresh preserves the current query and current result selection when possible
- [ ] Board/search refresh does not force active-mode changes or clear current detail state as a side effect

## Files to Modify
- `internal/mode/board/model.go`
- `internal/mode/board/model_test.go`
- `internal/mode/search/model.go`
- `internal/mode/search/model_test.go`
- `internal/app/model.go`
close_reason: Implemented non-destructive board/search auto-refresh with context preservation and typing-safe search suppression; added focused board/search/app tests for selection/focus/detail-state stability.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Hidden complexity: board reload currently resets all state — loading=true, pendingLoads=0, selectedRow reset to 0 for all columns (line 114-118 of board/model.go). What: preserving selection requires either (a) a new reload path that diffs old/new data and restores cursors, or (b) splitting load into fetch + merge phases. Why: this is the hardest part of the entire epic and the instructions understate it with 'reload can preserve the focused column.' Suggested action: add an instruction step that explicitly says 'introduce a non-destructive reload path separate from the existing full-reset reload triggered by the R key.'

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Missing AC: what happens to detail view during auto-refresh? What: the task says 'board and search' but detail is listed in the epic's success criteria and in t7o.1's routing. Detail view is not mentioned in t7o.2's instructions or AC at all. Why: if board auto-refresh triggers while detail pane is open (split view), the detail pane's issue might disappear from the board. Suggested action: either explicitly scope detail out of t7o.2 with a note, or add an AC covering detail stability during board refresh.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Execution sequencing note: although this task is ready, it also touches internal/app/model.go. Running t7o.1 first to avoid overlapping edits in shared shell orchestration code; t7o.2 remains next once t7o.1 is landed/verified.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Execution note: verifier found search auto-refresh bug beads-workbench-87mc in the shell orchestration foundation. This task's search-safe auto-refresh work must align with that fix (avoid synthetic key injection; use an explicit reload path that preserves query state).

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Execution started. This task is now unblocked after t7o.1 and follow-up bug 87mc landed. Focus areas: non-destructive board auto-refresh, safe search refresh behavior, and preserving current context without mode/detail disruption.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Board auto-refresh selectedRow map reset bug. What: In dashboardsLoadedMsg handler (line 135), selectedRow is unconditionally recreated as a new map with all values 0, even during refreshModeAuto. The anchor captures the old row, but restoreFromAnchor writes into the NEW map only for focusedColumn — other columns lose their selectedRow state. Why: If a user navigates to column 1 row 2, then triggers auto-refresh, after restore the other columns (0, 2, 3) will have selectedRow=0 which is correct (new map), BUT if the issue is found in a DIFFERENT column than the anchor's focusedColumn via findIssue, it writes selectedRow[col]=row for that column only — the original anchor.focusedColumn gets no explicit row write and defaults to 0. This is benign since focus moves to the found column, but it's worth noting the row memory for non-focused columns is always lost on auto-refresh. This is acceptable but differs from the AC wording 'preserve focused column AND selected issue' — it only preserves one column's row. Suggested action: Document this as intentional (preserving only focused column is fine for v1), or save per-column rows in anchor if you want stronger preservation later.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Board loading=true during auto-refresh blocks View rendering. What: AutoRefresh sets m.loading=true (line 281), which causes View() to render the loading spinner until all sections complete. Why: During auto-refresh, the user sees a flash of loading state instead of stale-but-usable content. This is non-destructive in that it doesn't lose state, but it IS visually disruptive — the user's board disappears momentarily during every tick/focus-regain refresh. A truly non-destructive refresh would keep rendering old data until new data arrives. Suggested action: Consider a 'refreshing' flag separate from 'loading' that still shows old content with a subtle indicator, or accept this flash as v1 behavior and document it.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Search typing suppression is correct but has a subtle edge. What: The typing flag is set true on keypress and cleared on searchLoadedMsg receipt (line 83). AutoRefresh checks m.typing AND m.focus==FocusQuery. Why: If the user types a character, the search fires, and the response comes back (clearing typing=false) BEFORE the next tick, the tick will auto-refresh even though the user may still be mid-thought. The window between keystrokes where typing=false is a valid auto-refresh window. This is actually fine behavior — it means 'suppress only while a typed query is in-flight' not 'suppress while user is composing.' Suggested action: None needed — this is reasonable semantics. Just noting the behavior is 'in-flight suppression' not 'debounced typing window suppression.'

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: App-level auto-refresh does not force mode switches or clear detail — verified correct. What: refreshActiveSurfaceCmd (line 759) dispatches to board.AutoRefresh() or search.AutoRefresh() based on active mode. The resulting SelectionChangedMsg flows through the normal path, which calls ensureDetailForCurrentSelectionCmd — but that method short-circuits if detail already has the same issue loaded (line 622). Why: This satisfies AC6 — no forced mode changes, no detail clearing. The tests at lines 423-509 of model_test.go directly verify this. Suggested action: None — this is solid.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Missing test: board auto-refresh while loading is a no-op. What: AutoRefresh() (line 278) returns nil if IsLoading() is true, but there's no unit test verifying this guard. Why: If this guard regresses, concurrent refreshes could corrupt anchor state or fire duplicate gateway calls. Suggested action: Add a one-liner test that calls AutoRefresh() while board is mid-load and asserts nil return and no state mutation.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Review verdict: PASS. Implementation is correct and well-tested for the stated AC. The board anchor/restore logic, search typing suppression, and app-level side-effect avoidance all work as specified. Two minor concerns noted (loading flash during auto-refresh, missing guard test for concurrent auto-refresh) — neither are blockers. Architecture fit is good: clean separation between manual reload and auto-refresh paths, no leaky abstractions across mode boundaries.

title: Apply dirty and stale refresh policy across active views
description:
## Description
Combine event-driven invalidation with time-based staleness so the active view stays fresh without turning every surface into a polling loop.

## Instructions
1. Track per-surface dirty/stale metadata and last-refresh timestamps in `internal/app/model.go`.
2. Keep immediate detail reload after successful edit/update/close/comment/status flows, and mark board/search dirty when those writes can invalidate browse data.
3. Refresh only the active surface on eligible focus regain, timer ticks, or view activation when that surface is dirty or stale.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Write flows still reload detail immediately after success
- [ ] Board/search can be marked dirty by writes and refresh on the next eligible active-view refresh
- [ ] Auto-refresh is limited to the active surface rather than hidden background polling of all views
- [ ] Default refresh cadence is low-frequency (about 60 seconds) and test-covered

## Files to Modify
- `internal/app/model.go`
- `internal/app/model_test.go`
close_reason: Implemented per-surface dirty/stale refresh policy in app shell with active-surface-only refresh and added coverage for dirty marking, stale cadence, and write-triggered behavior.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Scope concern: instruction 4 says 'update user-facing guidance or docs if auto-refresh changes reload expectations' and files-to-modify lists docs/CODING.md and key-bindings.md. What: docs updates are filler in a technical task and will either be skipped or done perfunctorily. Why: docs changes should follow implementation, not be bundled into the integration task. Suggested action: drop docs from t7o.3's files-to-modify and AC, and handle docs as part of the acceptance review (t7o.4) or a separate micro-task if needed.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Good: AC bullet 3 correctly limits refresh to the active surface only. This prevents the background-polling-all-views antipattern.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Execution started. Foundation tasks t7o.1 and t7o.2 are verified. This integration step will add dirty/stale tracking on top of the new safe active-surface refresh APIs without expanding scope into unrelated views.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Review verdict: PASS. Implementation is correct, minimal, and well-tested. The dirty/stale/lastRefresh tracking in surfaceRefreshState is clean. Active-surface-only refresh is properly enforced via maybeAutoRefreshActiveSurfaceCmd checking only m.active. Immediate detail reload after writes is preserved (loadDetailCmd in all mutationResultMsg cases). markBrowseSurfacesDirty correctly marks both board+search without touching detail. The 60s stale cadence is test-covered with time mocking via modelNow. No hidden bugs found; the guard conditions (modal open, terminal blurred, already loading) are all correctly gated.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Minor observation: shouldRefreshSurface uses >= for the stale comparison (line 861), meaning at exactly 60s the surface is considered stale. This is fine but means the effective cadence can be slightly less than 60s if the tick fires at exactly the threshold. Not a bug — just noting the boundary is inclusive.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Test coverage is thorough: dirty marking after writes (TestModelMutationResultMarksBrowseDirtyAndRefreshesOnlyActiveSurface), stale cadence (TestModelRefreshTickHonorsStaleCadenceForActiveSurface), active-surface-only (TestModelRefreshInDetailDoesNotBackgroundPollInactiveBrowseSurfaces), modal suppression (TestModelRefreshTickSkipsWhileModalsOpenAndDetailLoading), focus regain (TestModelFocusRegainRefreshesOnceAndSkipsRepeatedFocus). No test gaps identified for the stated acceptance criteria.

title: Acceptance Review: focus-aware and scheduled auto-refresh
description:
## Description
Verify focus-aware and scheduled auto-refresh works end-to-end before closing the epic.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All implementation tasks for the epic are closed
- [ ] Manual verification confirms active-view refresh on focus regain
- [ ] Manual or automated verification confirms low-frequency scheduled refresh behavior
- [ ] Board/search/detail keep user context stable during refresh, or any gaps are tracked as follow-up issues
- [ ] Any user-facing reload/help text changes are documented when implementation changes behavior expectations
- [ ] Required tests run and pass
- [ ] Any discovered defects are captured as new beads issues
close_reason: Acceptance review passed: focus-regain refresh verified on board/detail/search, low-frequency scheduled refresh verified, context remained stable, docs updated, and full quality gates passed.
