- [x] Logs: no new error/warning patterns introduced.

### Commit trail

- `f503aad` test(scripts): add end-to-end verification harness
- `7666e32` test(scripts): fix F14 to exercise parentSiblingCache.Invalidate via child fetch (initial F14 called Issue(epicID) which doesn't populate parentSiblingCache; rewritten to call Issue(childID) which triggers parentChildSiblings and exercises the invalidation hook)

### Defects discovered

None. Every kb8e.1–15 fix is observed correct in an end-to-end run.

title: beads.Repository.parentSiblingCache: add invalidation hook + drain on CachingRepository Reset
description:
## Description

`internal/repository/beads/lean.go:132` shows `r.parentSiblingCache[parentID] = out` populated under Lock — but no write path or external invalidation ever clears the cache. Per-instance, once a parent's children are cached, that snapshot lives for the lifetime of the `*beads.Repository`. CachingRepository's hash-change `Reset` only clears its own `memory.Repository`; it cannot reach into the backing `*beads.Repository`'s `parentSiblingCache`.

User impact: external developer adds child c3 to parent P. bwb detects hash change and Resets its caching layer. User opens c1 again — backing reads stale `parentSiblingCache[P] = [c1, c2]` — ParentGroupBrowser never shows c3 until bwb restarts.

## Instructions

1. Add an `Invalidate()` method on `*beads.Repository` (or a more specific `InvalidateParentSiblingCache()`) that clears `parentSiblingCache` under its lock.
2. Wire `CachingRepository.RefreshIfChanged` to call this invalidation in the same place it calls `c.memory.Reset()`. The CachingRepository holds the backing as a `repository.Repository` interface — extend the interface narrowly with an optional `Resetter` capability OR type-assert in CachingRepository for backing implementations that support invalidation.
   - Type-assert is the simpler / less-intrusive choice: `if r, ok := c.backing.(interface{ Invalidate() }); ok { r.Invalidate() }`.

## Files to Modify

- `internal/repository/beads/lean.go` (add Invalidate method)
- `internal/repository/caching/caching.go` (RefreshIfChanged calls backing.Invalidate via type assertion)
- `internal/repository/caching/caching_test.go` (new test)
- `internal/repository/beads/lean_test.go` (new — first test file for this package)

## Test

Two tests:

A. `TestBeadsRepository_Invalidate_ClearsParentSiblingCache` in `internal/repository/beads/`:
   - Skip if no bd binary (integration tier) OR use a `CommandRunner` mock to construct a beads.Repository with deterministic output.
   - Seed parentSiblingCache by calling Issue on a child.
   - Call Invalidate.
   - Assert next Issue call re-runs the bd subprocess (visible via the mock runner's call log).

B. `TestCachingRepository_RefreshIfChanged_InvalidatesBacking` in caching:
   - Build CachingRepository over a fake backing that implements Invalidate (tracked via a counter).
   - Trigger RefreshIfChanged with a hash change.
   - Assert backing.Invalidate counter incremented.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] beads.Repository exposes Invalidate
- [ ] CachingRepository.RefreshIfChanged calls backing.Invalidate when present
- [ ] Both tests pass
- [ ] `go build ./...`, `go vet ./...`, `go test ./internal/repository/...` pass
close_reason: completed; tests pass, no regression

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: beads.Repository.Invalidate() added; CachingRepository.RefreshIfChanged calls it via narrow type assertion (interface{Invalidate()}) immediately after c.memory.Reset() on hash change. White-box test in package beads + caching-side test using invalidatingStub. quality:fast green (992 tests). Commit cd1b3bb.

title: beads.leanDependentsFromPayload: filter parent-child out of the Blocks bucket
description:
## Description

`internal/repository/beads/lean_payloads.go:447` (`leanDependentsFromPayload`) classifies every non-`related` dependent into the Blocks bucket. The forward-direction helper `leanDepsFromPayload` (~line 422-432) explicitly handles `parent-child` by routing it to `hasParent`/parent-id resolution; the reverse-direction `leanDependentsFromPayload` has no such case. Result: when opening an epic P that has children c1, c2 (bd dependents with `dependency_type=parent-child`), the children appear in `Issue.Blocks` and the UI's "Blocked by" / "Blocks" panel shows the children as if the epic blocked them.

## Instructions

1. In `leanDependentsFromPayload`, add a case for `dependency_type == "parent-child"`. Children of the current issue should populate a separate Children list (parallel to how leanDepsFromPayload handles parent). Or, if the dependents endpoint can also report parent-of-X (X being depended-on by parent), handle both directions.
2. Verify against actual bd output for an epic with children — inspect a real `bd dependents <epic-id>` payload during development to confirm the `dependency_type` and direction semantics.
3. Make sure callers of leanDependentsFromPayload populate `Issue.ChildrenIDs` (or whatever the projected list is) instead of `Issue.Blocks`.

## Files to Modify

- `internal/repository/beads/lean_payloads.go` (~line 447)
- `internal/repository/beads/lean_reads.go` (call sites that consume the helper's output)
- new test in `internal/repository/beads/` (a payload-helper test file; previously this package had no unit tests — adding one establishes the pattern)

## Test

Add `TestLeanDependentsFromPayload_ParentChildNotInBlocks`:
1. Construct a synthetic bd dependents payload (JSON) with two entries: one `dependency_type="blocks"`, one `dependency_type="parent-child"`.
2. Call `leanDependentsFromPayload(payload, ...)`.
3. Assert the parent-child entry does NOT appear in the returned Blocks bucket.
4. Assert it appears in the Children bucket (or wherever the fix routes it).

This is a pure helper — no bd subprocess needed.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] parent-child entries no longer appear in Blocks
- [ ] Children bucket (or equivalent) populated
- [ ] New unit test passes
- [ ] `go build ./...`, `go vet ./...`, `go test ./internal/repository/beads/...` pass
close_reason: completed; tests pass, no regression

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter

First unit test in internal/repository/beads/. quality:fast green (978 tests). Commit 89e24e9.

title: memory.SeedDetail/toDetailLocked: project Creator field
description:
## Description

`internal/repository/memory/forget.go:33` (SeedDetail) and `memory/repository.go` (toDetailLocked) do not store or project the `Creator` field of `domain.IssueDetail`. CachingRepository serves cache hits via `memory.Issue`, so every Issue cache hit returns `Creator=""` — even though the backing `bd` Repository populates it on the first call. The Creator line in the metadata pane disappears on every second view of any issue.

## Instructions

1. Add a `creator string` field to the in-memory `storedIssue` struct.
2. Populate it in SeedDetail from `detail.Creator`.
3. Project it back in toDetailLocked by setting `Creator: si.creator` on the returned IssueDetail.

## Files to Modify

- `internal/repository/memory/forget.go` (SeedDetail and storedIssue if defined here)
- `internal/repository/memory/repository.go` (storedIssue, toDetailLocked)
- `internal/repository/memory/repository_test.go` (new test)

## Test

Add `TestSeedDetail_PreservesCreator`:
1. Seed memory with an IssueDetail having Creator="alice".
2. Call Issue(id).
3. Assert Creator=="alice".

Or, more robustly, extend an existing round-trip detail test to assert Creator equality.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Creator preserved on SeedDetail → Issue
- [ ] New / extended test passes
- [ ] `go build ./...`, `go vet ./...`, `go test ./internal/repository/memory/...` pass
close_reason: completed; tests pass, no regression

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: creator field added to storedIssue + projected via toDetailLocked + round-tripped via SnapshotIssue/SeedFromSnapshot. Tests: TestSeedDetail_PreservesCreator, TestSnapshotRoundTrip_PreservesCreator. quality:fast green (968 tests). Commit 9e0b823.

title: caching.AddComment: mark dashboardDirty (UpdatedAt advances on comment)
description:
## Description

`internal/repository/caching/caching.go:559` (`AddComment`) Forgets the per-ID cache but does NOT set `dashboardDirty`. The current comment says "Dashboard is NOT marked dirty because comments do not affect the Dashboard projection" — but that is wrong: Dashboard slot summaries (InProgress, Blocked, Ready, Closed) carry `UpdatedAt`, and bd advances UpdatedAt when a comment is added. Sort orders by UpdatedAt and any "recently active" affordance render with the pre-comment timestamp until 60s tick or another write.

User-visible effect: user posts a comment, dashboard list-row position does not update; user re-comments thinking the first post failed.

## Instructions

1. In `AddComment` after `c.memory.Forget(id)`, set `c.dashboardDirty = true` (under c.mu.Lock).
2. Update the godoc comment at the AddComment site to reflect the new behavior.

## Files to Modify

- `internal/repository/caching/caching.go` (AddComment ~line 559)
- `internal/repository/caching/caching_test.go` (new test)

## Test

Add `TestAddComment_MarksDashboardDirty`:
1. Build CachingRepository over a fake backing whose Dashboard returns InProgress with issue I at UpdatedAt=T0, then after AddComment returns InProgress with I at UpdatedAt=T1.
2. Call Dashboard() — assert UpdatedAt==T0; cache is warm.
3. Call AddComment(ctx, I, "comment body").
4. Call Dashboard() — assert UpdatedAt==T1 (cache refetched).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] AddComment marks dashboardDirty
- [ ] godoc updated to match
- [ ] New test passes
- [ ] `go build ./...`, `go vet ./...`, `go test ./internal/repository/caching/...` pass
close_reason: completed; tests pass, no regression

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: AddComment now sets dashboardDirty=true; godoc updated. Pre-existing TestAddComment_Success_IssueDropped_DashboardUntouched encoded the bug (asserted no re-fetch) — renamed to _DashboardDirty and inverted. New TestAddComment_MarksDashboardDirty asserts re-fetch via UpdatedAt timestamp change. quality:fast green (977 tests). Commit bfd7848.

title: caching: invalidate catalogsCache when writes introduce new labels/types
description:
## Description

`internal/repository/caching/caching.go:437` (`Catalogs`) is TTL-only (default 5 min). `CreateIssue`/`UpdateIssue`/`CloseIssue`/`AddComment` never invalidate `catalogsCache`. When a user creates an issue with a new label `urgent-2026`, the next create-form opening within 5 minutes does not list it — the user re-types, risking typos that fork labels across the project.

The deleted argv-level cache invalidated every entry (including catalogs) on every `IsWrite=true` call, so this regression was introduced by the new CachingRepository.

## Instructions

1. On any successful CreateIssue / UpdateIssue / CloseIssue / AddComment that could add a new label or type — i.e. when `input.Labels` is non-empty in Create/Update — invalidate `catalogsCache` so the next Catalogs() refetches.
2. The cheapest correct invalidation: clear the entire catalogsCache on any successful write. This is what the old argv-level cache did. Document that catalogs are cheap and infrequent enough to justify full invalidation.
3. Keep the TTL as a safety net for EXTERNAL catalog changes.

## Files to Modify

- `internal/repository/caching/caching.go` (CreateIssue, UpdateIssue, CloseIssue, AddComment, Catalogs methods)
- `internal/repository/caching/caching_test.go` (new test)

## Test

Add `TestCreateIssue_InvalidatesCatalogs`:
1. Build CachingRepository over a fake backing whose Catalogs returns `{Labels: ["a","b"]}` initially and `{Labels: ["a","b","new"]}` after a write.
2. Call Catalogs() — expect ["a","b"].
3. Call CreateIssue(ctx, input{Labels:["new"]}).
4. Call Catalogs() — expect ["a","b","new"].

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] catalogsCache invalidated on successful writes
- [ ] New test passes
- [ ] `go build ./...`, `go vet ./...`, `go test ./internal/repository/caching/...` pass
close_reason: completed; tests pass, no regression

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: invalidateCatalogsLocked() called from all four write methods (CreateIssue/UpdateIssue/CloseIssue/AddComment). Sets catalogsCache=nil; Catalogs() fast path then refetches. TestCreateIssue_InvalidatesCatalogs passes. quality:fast green (976 tests). Commit 260e65f.

title: caching.Issue cache-hit: preserve Title/Status/Type/Priority for cross-referenced IssueReferences
description:
## Description

`internal/repository/caching/caching.go:391` (Issue cache-hit path) routes through `memory.Issue` which calls `toDetailLocked`. For Related/Blocks/ParentGroupBrowser cross-references, `toDetailLocked` looks up referenced issues in `r.issues`. If a referenced issue was never seeded (e.g. the user opened A which references B, but B was never opened), the resulting `IssueReference` carries only `{ID: depID}` with empty Title/Status/Type/Priority. The detail view "Blocked by" row then shows a bare ID instead of the human-readable title — appears on every cache hit after the first.

## Instructions

Two acceptable approaches:

A. **Store the projected references at SeedDetail time** instead of reconstructing them from the memory map. SeedDetail receives the full IssueReference list from backing; persist that list verbatim on the storedIssue and return it from toDetailLocked without re-resolving. Cost: extra memory per issue.

B. **Fall back to backing for unresolved cross-refs**: when toDetailLocked finds an unseeded cross-ref ID, return the bare ID but mark the result as partial; caching layer detects partial and refetches from backing. Cost: extra round-trip.

Pick (A) — simpler, lower latency, and matches what bd already returns. The slight extra memory cost is bounded by the number of cross-refs.

## Files to Modify

- `internal/repository/memory/repository.go` (storedIssue struct, SeedDetail, toDetailLocked)
- `internal/repository/memory/forget.go` (if SeedDetail/storedIssue live here)
- `internal/repository/memory/repository_test.go` (new test)

## Test

Add `TestSeedDetail_PreservesCrossRefMetadata`:
1. Seed memory with issue A whose IssueDetail has BlockedBy=[{ID:"B", Title:"Real title", Status:"open", Type:"task", Priority:1}]. Do NOT seed B separately.
2. Call Issue("A").
3. Assert BlockedBy[0].Title=="Real title", Status=="open", Type=="task", Priority==1.

Add the same coverage for Related and ParentGroupBrowser.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Cross-referenced IssueReferences retain Title/Status/Type/Priority across SeedDetail → Issue
- [ ] New test passes
- [ ] `go build ./...`, `go vet ./...`, `go test ./internal/repository/...` pass
close_reason: completed; in-memory + persistence both covered, no regression

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Fixed in two commits: a65546b (in-memory cross-ref preservation via storedIssue.blockedByRefs/relatedRefs/parentRef/childrenRefs with nil-sentinel semantics) and 03851b1 (persistence gap closed — SnapshotIssue carries the refs, LoadWithManifest routes through new SeedFromSnapshot). 8ad17ae documented the gap before the follow-up landed. Tests: TestSeedDetail_PreservesCrossRefMetadata, TestSnapshotRoundTrip_PreservesCrossRefMetadata, TestSnapshotRoundTrip_NilRefFields_FallsBackToReResolution. quality:fast green (966 tests).

title: caching.CreateIssue: stop fabricating seed values (Status/Priority/Type/CreatedAt) — refetch or skip seed
description:
## Description

`internal/repository/caching/caching.go:482` (`CreateIssue`) eagerly seeds `c.memory` from input-derived guesses: Status="open", Type default, Priority default, zero CreatedAt, no Description hydration in Summary. The backing `bd` may assign different defaults (project policy priority, server-stamped CreatedAt, normalized title). Subsequent `Issue(newID)` cache-hit returns the fabricated detail rather than backing truth — until the user edits the issue (which triggers Forget) the read-only view shows wrong values.

## Instructions

Two acceptable approaches:

A. **Skip the seed entirely**: after `backing.CreateIssue` returns, don't seed memory; let the first `Issue(newID)` call miss the cache and fetch real backing detail. Simplest, no fabrication, but pays one backing round-trip on first detail view.

B. **Refetch and seed authoritatively**: after `backing.CreateIssue`, call `backing.Issue(newID)` and `SeedDetail` with the real result. Adds latency to CreateIssue but eliminates the fabrication.

Pick (A) unless there's evidence that the post-create detail view is frequently fetched and the round-trip is costly. Document the decision in the Create method's godoc.

Also: mark `dashboardDirty=true` (this already happens — verify it).

## Files to Modify

- `internal/repository/caching/caching.go` (CreateIssue ~line 482)
- `internal/repository/caching/caching_test.go` (new test)

## Test

Add `TestCreateIssue_DoesNotFabricateSeedValues`:
1. Build CachingRepository with a fake backing whose CreateIssue returns `{ID: "wb-42"}` and whose Issue("wb-42") returns Status="todo", Priority=2, CreatedAt=`time.Date(...)`, Type="bug".
2. Call CreateIssue(ctx, input{Title:"foo"}) — note no Status/Priority/Type/CreatedAt in input.
3. Call Issue(ctx, "wb-42").
4. Assert Status="todo", Priority=2, CreatedAt=expected, Type="bug" — NOT input-defaults.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Cache no longer fabricates Status/Priority/Type/CreatedAt for new issues
- [ ] Approach (A or B) documented in CreateIssue godoc
- [ ] New test passes
- [ ] `go build ./...`, `go vet ./...`, `go test ./internal/repository/caching/...` pass
close_reason: completed; tests pass, no regression

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Option A: SeedDetail call removed from CreateIssue; next Issue(newID) fetches real backing detail. dashboardDirty still set. Existing TestCreateIssue_Success_DashboardDirty rewritten (it had encoded the bug by asserting cache hit); new TestCreateIssue_DoesNotFabricateSeedValues asserts backing Status/Priority/Type/CreatedAt are returned. quality:fast green (975 tests). Commit 0c332cf.

title: caching.Hydrate: surface loaded.Dashboard error instead of discarding with _
description:
## Description

`internal/repository/caching/caching.go:282` computes `precomputedDashboard, _ = loaded.Dashboard(context.Background())` — the error is silently discarded. If `memory.Repository.Dashboard` ever errors (corrupted snapshot, future invariant check, etc.), a zero-valued `DashboardData` is stored with `dashboardDirty=false` and served as fresh. The user sees a blank board with no warning.

Currently `memory.Dashboard` only errors on `ctx.Err()` so this is defensive — but the `context.Background()` use is also wrong (no cancellation), and the silent-discard masks any future error path.

## Instructions

1. Capture the Dashboard error: `precomputedDashboard, err := loaded.Dashboard(ctx)` (use Hydrate's incoming ctx, not Background()).
2. On error: do NOT precompute the dashboard cache. Instead leave `c.dashboardDirty=true` and let the first user-facing Dashboard() call fetch fresh from backing. Optionally log via the cacheLogger.
3. Keep the rest of the Hydrate logic.

## Files to Modify

- `internal/repository/caching/caching.go` (Hydrate ~line 282)
- `internal/repository/caching/caching_test.go` (new test)

## Test

Add `TestHydrate_DashboardError_FallsBackToBackingFetch`:
1. Inject a fake `memory.Repository` whose Dashboard returns a stub error (use the existing memory.Repository or wrap with an error-injecting variant).
2. Call Hydrate.
3. Assert no precomputed empty cache: subsequent Dashboard() call routes to backing.

Use the existing `errorinjecting` package if it supports Dashboard.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] loaded.Dashboard error no longer discarded
- [ ] On error, cache is marked dirty (not pre-stored empty)
- [ ] New test passes
- [ ] `go build ./...`, `go vet ./...`, `go test ./internal/repository/caching/...` pass
close_reason: completed; tests pass, no regression

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Hydrate now takes ctx; vcStatusFunc and loaded.Dashboard both receive it. On Dashboard error: dashboardCache assignment is skipped so dashboardDirty stays true and next Dashboard() routes to backing. Hydrate still returns nil — graceful degradation. Test TestHydrate_DashboardError_FallsBackToBackingFetch uses a vcStatusFunc that cancels ctx after returning the matching hash. 29 call sites updated (1 prod + 28 test). quality:fast green (974 tests). Commit 832fc86.

title: caching.Hydrate: stage loaded state so concurrent writes are not overwritten when lock is dropped
description:
## Description

`internal/repository/caching/caching.go:263` (`Hydrate`) releases `c.mu.RLock` before the multi-second `vcStatusFunc` call and `loaded.Dashboard` precompute, then reacquires `c.mu.Lock` to swap state. Any `CreateIssue`/`UpdateIssue`/`CloseIssue`/`AddComment` mutation that happens during this window is overwritten when `c.memory = loaded` executes — the freshly-created issue's cache entry vanishes; next `Issue(newID)` misses and pays a backing round-trip.

## Instructions

Pick one of:

A. **Forbid concurrent writes during Hydrate**: add a `hydrating` bool flag set under c.mu.Lock at Hydrate entry. Write methods check the flag and either wait via a `*sync.Cond` or fail-fast. Document this in the Repository contract for callers.

B. **Merge loaded into existing memory rather than swapping**: only seed entries that don't already exist in `c.memory`; preserve any in-flight mutations. This requires a `memory.MergeFromSeed` helper.

C. **Document a hard precondition**: "Hydrate MUST be called before Start and before any user-facing write." Add a runtime guard: if Hydrate is called after Start (started=true), return an error.

Option C is the simplest correct fix because there's no legitimate use case for late Hydrate. The current code already states this informally in a comment; just enforce it.

## Files to Modify

- `internal/repository/caching/caching.go` (Hydrate)
- `internal/repository/caching/caching_test.go` (new test)

## Test

If option C: add `TestHydrate_AfterStart_ReturnsError`:
1. Construct CachingRepository, call Start.
2. Call Hydrate; assert error mentions "after Start" or similar.

If option A/B: add a test that races a write against Hydrate using a controlled sync point and asserts the write survives.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Lost-write window in Hydrate eliminated (one of A/B/C applied)
- [ ] Test covers the chosen approach
- [ ] `go build ./...`, `go vet ./...`, `go test ./internal/repository/caching/...` pass
- [ ] `go test -race ./internal/repository/caching/...` passes
close_reason: completed; tests pass, no regression

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Option C implemented: Hydrate fails fast when c.started==true. Guard at function entry under c.mu.RLock; godoc updated to state the precondition. Test TestHydrate_AfterStart_ReturnsError. quality:fast green (973 tests). Commit 04c47aa.

title: Research: read docs and history
description:
## Description

Read the docs and recent git history to establish a working understanding of `bwb` before starting the exploration loop. Produces an inline mental model — no committed artifact required.

## What was covered

- `AGENTS.md`, `docs/OVERVIEW.md`, `docs/CODING.md`, `docs/TESTING.md`, `docs/CONFIGURATION.md`, `docs/CHANGE-WORKFLOW.md`
- `docs/user-guide/key-bindings.md` (treated as the documented contract for runtime behavior)
- `git log` for the last ~30 commits — the BeadsGateway → Repository refactor (epics `hhho`, `kxci`, `8pxi`, `k4g4`) was the most recent significant change at session start

## Acceptance Criteria

- [x] Repo layout, package boundaries, and the runtime flow (cmd/bwb → app → mode → ui) understood well enough to reason about TUI behavior
- [x] Documented keybindings catalogued so exploration could spot divergences

## Resolution

Closed as `done` — the understanding fed into the 20-iteration exploration that produced the bug findings in this epic.

title: Explore beads-workbench — 2026-05-24
description:
## Outcome

A focused exploratory session run on 2026-05-24 against commit `ff591f4` to surface latent runtime bugs in `bwb`. The session produced a research note, observed the running TUI for 20 iterations against the embedded fixture via `scripts/capture_bwb_screen.py`, filed every divergence between observed and documented behavior, and either pinned the real ones with tests or closed harness-artifact false-positives.

## Outputs of the session

- 1 research task (znri.1) — read docs/code/history baseline
- 4 closed-as-not-reproducible bug records (znri.2, znri.3, znri.4, znri.6) — kept for traceability so the same false-positive doesn't get refiled
- 2 confirmed real bugs (znri.5, znri.7) — both have failing pinning tests in tree
- 1 testing task (znri.8) — added the pinning tests + fixed the PTY harness's silent fall-through

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] znri.5 closed (help modal bottom border rendered)
- [ ] znri.7 closed (`ctrl+q` honored on the fatal-error screen)
- [ ] Both pinning tests (`TestHelpModal_RenderedFrameHasClosingBottomBorder`, `TestFatalErrorScreen_CtrlQQuits`) are no longer marked as `t.Skip` in the source — they pass instead

## Notes for future readers

This epic is intentionally **finding-only**, not a refactor or feature. Each child issue is self-contained; closing the two open bugs closes the epic.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text:
## Wrap-up — 20-iteration TUI runtime exploration

### Actions tried (20 iterations)

Built bwb (commit ff591f4) and exercised the running TUI against the embedded fixture (`internal/testing/e2e/embeddedfixture/setup.sh` with seed.json, 5 issues bwf-1..bwf-5) via `scripts/capture_bwb_screen.py`:

1. Cold-start board capture at 120x34 with default --repo caching + 2.5s wait → empty board (suspicious).
2. Re-run at 120x34 with --repo caching + 5s wait → still empty (rules out timing).
3. Re-run at 120x34 with --repo beads → board correctly shows 2/2/0/1.
4. Second --repo caching run (after prior session JSONL exists) → still empty.
5. --repo caching + manual 'r' reload → still empty.
6. Inspected ~/.local/state/bwb/bwb-<session>.log → caching session log shows ONLY `vc status` and `ping`, no `bd query`/`ready`/`count` calls fire.
7. Filed F1 (znri.2).
8. Board → detail navigation (Enter) at 160x40 with --repo beads → works.
9. Esc from detail → does NOT return to board (header still 'Detail:'). Confirmed with two Esc presses.
10. F13 (documented switch-to-board) from detail → also no-op.
11. Pressed '?' to open help, then '?' again to close → modal does not close.
12. Tested all dismissal keys for help: q, n, Esc do nothing; Space and Enter dismiss.
13. Filed F2 (znri.3), F3 (znri.4), F4 (znri.5).
14. Search mode: ctrl+space → 'bwf' → Enter, 3s wait → results still loading.
15. Same with 8s wait → 5 results loaded, but UI still labels 'draft'/'5 partial'/'stale'.
16. Filed F5 (znri.6).
17. CLI exit codes: --version=0, unknown flag=2, positional=2, --check-config=0, --print-config=0. All correct.
18. Non-beads cwd: fatal-error screen 'no beads project here' renders with 'Press q or ctrl+c to quit.'
19. Pressed 'q' on fatal screen → process does not exit. Pressed ctrl+q → also does not exit. Only ctrl+c (SIGINT) terminates.
20. Filed F6 (znri.7).

### Tasks filed (6 findings; 0 questions)

- **znri.2** (F1, P0/P1) — Caching repository backend never fires board reads on cold-start project. Empty session JSONL + 'fresh' manifest = permanent empty board on the default backend. First-run UX is broken on the documented default path.
- **znri.3** (F2, P1) — Esc does not return from detail to board, contradicting docs/user-guide/key-bindings.md.
- **znri.4** (F3, P2) — '?' does not toggle help closed; only Enter or Space dismiss the help modal.
- **znri.5** (F4, P2) — Help modal renders only top portion; bottom border missing; content truncates with no overflow indicator; board cells render through the unrendered modal area.
- **znri.6** (F5, P2) — Search 'draft'/'partial'/'stale' markers persist after Enter submits the query; the message asks the user to press Enter again while showing fresh results.
- **znri.7** (F6, P1) — Fatal-error 'no beads project' screen ignores q and ctrl+q; only ctrl+c (SIGINT) quits. Discoverability trap for misdirected users.

### Coverage and limits

Focus per Phase 0: **TUI runtime**. Tested against `--repo beads` for most iterations because F1 blocks meaningful exercise of the default `--repo caching` backend.

**Tested**: cold-start board render (caching and beads backends), board → detail navigation, Esc/F13 from detail, '?' help open/close, all common help-dismissal keys, search toggle/type/submit/results, search stale-marker handling, CLI flag exit codes (--version, --check-config, --print-config, unknown flag, unexpected positional), fatal-error screen on non-beads cwd, fatal-screen quit affordances.

**NOT explored** (out of session scope or blocked):
- External launcher flows: 'n' nvim, 'p' opencode, 'l' shell-command (would require launcher subprocess inspection).
- Editor handoff 'e' rich marker-based document round-trip.
- Mutation modals: 'c' create, 'u' update, 'a' add comment, 'x' close (the documented per-mutation flows).
- search → detail → search round-trip.
- Scroll/page keys in detail (pgup/pgdown/home/end).
- Mode cycle keys (F15/F16) — F-keys above F12 require terminal capability; not exercised.
- Narrow-width layouts (<120 cols) for layout integrity.
- --no-auto-refresh effect on background polling.
- --debug stderr mirroring (--debug was enabled but stderr was redirected by the capture script).
- --repo memory backend (--repo-file required; not exercised).
- Caching backend invalidation under external bd mutations (background vc-hash polling behavior).
- Re-test of all findings against caching backend (F2–F6 were observed under --repo beads; they likely affect caching identically since they live above the repository layer, but this was not independently re-verified).

No questions filed (everything observed had a clear-cut answer once isolated).

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text:
## Re-verification + analysis (post-rewrite)

### PTY harness artifact found

While reviewing the filed findings for proper bug structure, I discovered the original Esc-related observations were measurement artifacts. `scripts/capture_bwb_screen.py` (lines 52–66) recognizes the key name `ESC` (0x1b), but I had used `ESCAPE` in several steps. The fallthrough at line 66 (`keymap.get(keys, keys).encode()`) silently sent the literal 6-byte string `ESCAPE` rather than the Escape key.

Re-verified each finding with `send-key:ESC`:

| Finding | Original claim | Re-verification |
|---|---|---|
| `znri.3` | Esc does not return detail → board | **DEBUNKED** — closed as not-reproducible. With real ESC byte, Esc returns to board correctly. |
| `znri.5` Esc-dismiss claim | Esc does not dismiss help modal | **Still real.** With real ESC byte, modal still ignores Esc. |
| `znri.7` general | `q`/`ctrl+q` ignored on fatal screen | **Still real**, and additionally Esc (real ESC byte) is also a no-op on the fatal screen. |

`znri.5`'s visual rendering claim (missing bottom border, no overflow indicator), `znri.2` (caching backend cold-start), `znri.4` (`?` toggle), and `znri.6` (search stale markers) did not depend on Esc and are unaffected by the artifact.

### Bugs rewritten with full project house-style + acceptance criteria

The surviving findings (5 bugs) were updated to match the format used by closed bugs in this repo (e.g. `beads-workbench-ssom`): explicit **Description / Expected vs Actual / Root Cause / Minimal Repro / Impact / Suggested Fix / Acceptance Criteria / Where Discovered / Related** sections, with priorities set explicitly:

| ID | P | Title |
|---|---|---|
| `znri.2` | P0 | Caching repository backend never fires board reads on cold-start project |
| `znri.4` | P2 | '?' does not toggle help closed; only Enter or Space dismiss the modal |
| `znri.5` | P2 | Help modal renders only top portion; bottom border missing, content truncates with no overflow indicator (incl. Esc-doesn't-dismiss) |
| `znri.6` | P2 | Search 'draft'/'partial'/'stale' markers persist after Enter submits the query |
| `znri.7` | P1 | Fatal-error 'no beads project' screen ignores q and ctrl+q; only ctrl+c quits |

### Why automated tests missed these

Each finding sits in a blind spot of the existing test strategy. The tests this repo has are good at what they cover; the bugs all live in the gaps between tiers.

**`znri.2` — Caching backend cold-start**

The repo's caching tests exercise the decorator with already-populated in-memory data or hand-seeded cache files. No existing test models the "first-ever run against a new project" lifecycle: empty session JSONL written, manifest written eagerly with the current `bd_commit_hash`, and the resulting interaction with the board fetch fan-out. The cache "freshness" check passes (manifest matches current hash) so any test asserting "cache reports fresh" is satisfied — but no test asserts that "fresh" implies "non-empty" or that a real read fan-out happened. The integration tier's `RunReadContract` runs against an already-seeded shared fixture cache, so it never exercises the empty-cache branch through the live `bd` path. The full-app verification recipe in `docs/RUNTIME_UI_VERIFICATION.md` only proves the dashboard renders when the operator's cache directory already has data from prior runs against the *real* repo. **Missing test**: a cold-start lifecycle integration test that starts with no cache directory at all and asserts the cache populates from `bd`.

**`znri.4` — '?' does not toggle help closed**

`docs/user-guide/key-bindings.md` documents `?` as a "toggle". `docs/CONFIGURATION.md` § Keybindings, modal context, lists the modal action set as `next/prev/left/right/enter/escape` — `?` is not in the set, so when the modal context owns key routing, `?` is dropped. There is likely a unit test asserting `?` opens help from the shell context; there is unlikely to be a test that drives the second press while the modal is open. **Missing test**: open help, send `?`, assert the modal is hidden. Without this, the "toggle" verb in the docs is unenforced.

**`znri.5` — Help modal rendering + Esc dismissal**

The repo has golden-file conventions (`internal/testing/ui/AssertMatchesGolden*`) but the dashboard UX redesign goldens cover board layout, not the help modal. A golden snapshot of the help modal at 80x24 and 120x34 would catch the missing bottom border immediately. For the Esc-dismiss part, the unit test gap mirrors `znri.4`: a `tea.KeyMsg{Type: tea.KeyEsc}` sent while help is open is not asserted to close the modal. **Missing tests**: a help-modal golden at small + medium widths, and a key-handling test for Esc.

**`znri.6` — Search stale markers persist after Enter**

The repo has `internal/mode/search/parity` (asserts result count/sort match `bd search`) and a `render_regression_test.go` (frame-stacking guard). Neither asserts the *state labels* in the search UI after a submit+response. A unit test of the search model that asserts `draftQuery == appliedQuery` after a successful response would catch this; a golden file of the search panes after submit would also catch it. **Missing test**: post-submit-and-response state assertion in the search model.

**`znri.7` — Fatal-error screen quit affordances**

The fatal-error screen exists for the bd-init failure path. Unit tests likely assert it renders (the message text is present); they probably do not drive its key handler. The component lives ahead of the normal mode lifecycle, so the standard teatest pattern used for board/search/detail does not naturally extend to it. Additionally, the symptom "ctrl+c sometimes needs SIGTERM" hints at alt-screen teardown behavior on error paths that is not modeled by any test. **Missing tests**: a fatal-error model test that drives `q`/`ctrl+q`/`Esc` and asserts `tea.Quit`; ideally a PTY-level recipe in `docs/RUNTIME_UI_VERIFICATION.md` that launches bwb in an empty dir and asserts clean exit on `q`.

### Common pattern across the gaps

Three patterns explain most of the misses:

1. **Lifecycle tests modeled component-by-component, not end-to-end.** The caching decorator works in isolation. The board model works in isolation. The combination of "fresh install + empty cache + first board fan-out" is not exercised. (`znri.2`)
2. **Key routing through overlay/modal context is not tested.** Once a modal is up, the modal context owns keys, and documented global actions (`?` toggle, `Esc` cancel, `ctrl+q` quit) silently stop working. There is no test that asserts the documented global actions still fire from inside a modal or fatal screen. (`znri.4`, `znri.5` Esc-part, `znri.7`)
3. **Async-state-machine post-conditions are not asserted.** The search model can be tested for the typing → submit → loading transition, but the final loaded state (with the stale flag cleared, the applied query updated, the result count complete) is not asserted as a single coherent state. (`znri.6`)

Suggested follow-up at the testing-strategy level (not a bug per finding, but an observation for `docs/TESTING.md`): add a thin "global affordance contract" test that, for each documented global key (`?`, `Esc`, `ctrl+q`, `q` on fatal), asserts the action fires from each major mode AND from each overlay/modal/fatal context. Likely <100 LOC, would have caught three of the five findings here.

title: Add CommandExecutor-level error-injection seam to lean beads.Repository so scenario 10 (PartialDashboardFailure) runs against beads_lean
description:
## Description

E4.1 created the lean `beads.Repository`. Scenario 10 of the parity-contract suite (`PartialDashboardFailure`) is currently **skipped** for the `beads_lean` variant because there is no way to inject a partial error into one of the Dashboard fan-out branches without a `BeadsGateway` wrapper. Before E4.4 deletes the legacy gateway code, the lean Repository must expose an injection seam so the scenario runs (and passes) against it. Otherwise we lose the test coverage that proves Dashboard gracefully degrades when one of its underlying bd calls fails.

## Instructions

1. Add an optional fault-injection hook to `*beads.Repository` (e.g. a `CommandExecutor` override field, an `options.WithExecutor(...)` constructor option, or a similar testable seam). Production wiring keeps using the default real-runner; tests can swap in an executor that errors for specific argvs.
2. Update the lean implementation's bd-call sites to go through this seam so the override actually takes effect.
3. Re-enable scenario 10 (`PartialDashboardFailure`) in `internal/repository/contract/parity_integration_test.go` for `beads_lean`; remove the skip; assert identical behaviour to the legacy variant.
4. Confirm: scenario 10 PASSES for both `beads_legacy`/`beads_lean` (whatever the names) and for `memory`.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `*beads.Repository` exposes a test-only seam for executor / command-result override (one constructor option or one settable field — pick the simplest shape)
- [ ] Scenario 10 (`PartialDashboardFailure`) runs against `beads_lean` (no skip) and passes
- [ ] `mise run test:all` green
- [ ] No production code change is required to use the seam — default behaviour is unchanged

## Files to Modify

- `internal/repository/beads/lean.go` (or wherever the runner-call seam lives)
- `internal/repository/beads/lean_reads.go` / `lean_writes.go` if needed
- `internal/repository/contract/parity_integration_test.go` (remove skip, re-enable)
- Possibly a new `internal/repository/beads/options.go`
close_reason: Closed

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Done. Commit 8d5db0c. Added WithCommandHook option + r.run chokepoint to lean Repository. Scenario 10 (PartialDashboardFailure) now PASSES for beads (memory still skips — no external failure path). Default production behaviour unchanged. All 3 quality gates green. Unblocks k4g4.4.

title: Hydrate from most-recent prior session's cache file (preserves per-session writes)
description:
## Outcome

Warm-cache hydration works in default operation. Each bwb still writes to its own per-session path (preserves Q4), but at startup the loader scans for the most-recent prior session's file (by manifest `synced_at`) for the same project and hydrates from that.

## Files (modify)
- `cmd/bwb/main.go` — change `constructRepository` to compute a separate `hydrateFromPath` (most-recent prior file) vs `writeToPath` (own session path); call `Hydrate(hydrateFromPath)` then `SetCacheFilePath(writeToPath)` (new method below)
- `internal/repository/caching/caching.go` — add `SetCacheFilePath(path string)` to separately set the write path post-Hydrate. Hydrate must NOT change the cache file path; or refactor Hydrate to take a `(loadPath, savePath)` pair. Pick the simpler shape.
- New helper at `cmd/bwb/cache_resolver.go` (or in main.go directly): `findLatestProjectCacheFile(cacheBaseDir, projectHash string) (string, error)`:
  - Glob `<cacheBaseDir>/<projectHash>-*/repo.jsonl.manifest.json`
  - Parse each manifest, pick the one with the latest `synced_at`
  - Skip unreadable/corrupt manifests with a warning
  - Returns the corresponding `repo.jsonl` path (NOT the manifest path)
  - Empty result → return ""

## Simpler shape (recommended): refactor Hydrate to take loadPath separate from writePath

```go
// Hydrate loads from loadPath (read source) and sets writePath for subsequent SaveNow.
// loadPath may be empty (cold start); writePath may be empty (no persistence).
func (c *CachingRepository) Hydrate(loadPath, writePath string) error
```

Update existing callers:
- `cmd/bwb/main.go`: pass (latestPath, ownSessionPath)
- Existing kxci.4 tests pass the same path for both (preserves current semantics for those tests)

Update existing test helpers to use the new 2-arg form.

## Test coverage
- `TestFindLatestProjectCacheFileEmpty`: empty cache dir → ""
- `TestFindLatestProjectCacheFileSingle`: one prior file → returns its path
- `TestFindLatestProjectCacheFileMultiple`: multiple prior files → returns the one with latest synced_at
- `TestFindLatestProjectCacheFileSkipsCorrupt`: one corrupt manifest + one good → returns the good one
- `TestFindLatestProjectCacheFileSkipsDifferentProject`: files with different project-hash prefix are ignored
- `TestHydrateSeparateLoadAndWritePaths`: Hydrate(loadPath, writePath) loads from loadPath, SaveNow writes to writePath
- `TestConstructRepositoryCachingFindsPriorSession`: integration — pre-create a fixture file in `~/.cache/bwb/HASH-prev/`, call constructRepository for session `now`, assert caching repo hydrated from prev's file
- `TestCachingWarmCacheSkipsBackingDashboard`: with matching hash, after Hydrate, Dashboard returns hydrated data with zero backing calls

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `findLatestProjectCacheFile` returns the right file (newest by synced_at) and skips corrupt/wrong-project entries
- [ ] `Hydrate(loadPath, writePath)` separates load and write
- [ ] `constructRepository` wires this so own session writes to its own path, hydrates from latest prior
- [ ] Existing tests still pass (no regression)
- [ ] New tests cover all 7+ scenarios above
- [ ] `mise run quality` green
- [ ] -race clean
close_reason: Closed

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Done. Hydrate(path) → Hydrate(loadPath, writePath) — separate load and write paths preserves Q4 per-session writes while enabling cross-session warm cache. Added filestorage.LoadManifest (efficient manifest-only read; doesn't parse full JSONL). New cmd/bwb/cache_resolver.go: findLatestProjectCacheFile globs ~/.cache/bwb/<projectHash>-*/repo.jsonl.manifest.json, picks newest by SyncedAt, filters wrong schema version + corrupt files. constructRepository wires own-session writePath (defaultRepoFilePath result) and prior-session loadPath (resolver result). Smoke test confirms mechanism: cold session = 10 bd calls (list + per-issue show); warm session = 3 bd calls (hash poll only, dashboard served from memory). 7 resolver tests + LoadManifest tests + TestHydrateSeparateLoadAndWritePaths + TestConstructRepositoryCachingFindsPriorSession. mise run quality green (1200 unit / 1373 integration, -race clean).

title: Persist bd_commit_hash in manifest; skip backing refetch on hydrate when hash unchanged
description:
## Outcome

Sub-second first paint on warm-cache startup, satisfying the kxci.6 AR criterion. Currently `Hydrate` always sets `dashboardDirty=true` for safety, which forces a ~3s backing fan-out on first paint. With persisted+current hash comparison, an unchanged repo skips the fan-out entirely.

## Files (modify)
- `internal/repository/filestorage/filestorage.go` — `Save` accepts an optional `bdCommitHash string` (variadic option or second function); `Load` exposes the persisted hash to callers (either via a new return value, or via a struct return). Pick the least-invasive shape:
  - Option A (recommended): add `SaveWithHash(r *memory.Repository, path string, bdCommitHash string) error` and `LoadWithManifest(path string) (*memory.Repository, Manifest, error)`. Keep existing `Save(r, path)` and `Load(path)` as thin wrappers (Save → empty hash; Load → discard manifest). Tests for both signatures.
  - Option B: change `Save` signature with a variadic option; might break existing callers. AVOID unless A is awkward.
- `internal/repository/caching/caching.go`:
  - `SaveNow()` reads `vcStatusFunc(ctx)` for current hash, falls back to "" on error (still save); calls `filestorage.SaveWithHash` (or equivalent)
  - `Hydrate(path)` reads manifest + current hash; compares:
    - persisted hash == current hash AND non-empty → keep `dashboardDirty=false` (TRUST hydrated state)
    - persisted hash != current hash OR persisted empty (legacy file) OR vcStatusFunc unavailable → set `dashboardDirty=true` (current safe-default behavior)
- `internal/repository/caching/caching_test.go` — new tests

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `SaveWithHash` (or chosen API) writes the hash to manifest; round-trip via `LoadWithManifest` returns same hash
- [ ] `Hydrate` with persisted hash matching current `vcStatusFunc` result → `dashboardDirty` is false; first `Dashboard()` call hits memory only (zero backing.Dashboard calls)
- [ ] `Hydrate` with mismatched hash → `dashboardDirty` is true (current behavior preserved)
- [ ] `Hydrate` with empty persisted hash (e.g. file written by code before kxci.5a) → `dashboardDirty` is true (safe default)
- [ ] `Hydrate` when vcStatusFunc returns error → `dashboardDirty` is true (safe default)
- [ ] Existing `Save`/`Load` signatures preserved (no compile break)
- [ ] `mise run quality` green
- [ ] -race clean
close_reason: Closed

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Done. Option A chosen (non-breaking): SaveWithHash + LoadWithManifest added; Save/Load preserved as thin wrappers. Manifest exported as public type. Caching.SaveNow reads vcStatusFunc (with 2s timeout) and passes hash to SaveWithHash; on vcStatusFunc error, hash stays empty and save still proceeds. Caching.Hydrate: refactored to do vcStatusFunc call OUTSIDE c.mu lock; compares persisted+current hash; equal+non-empty → dashboardDirty=false + seeds lastHash. SPEC GAP CAUGHT (advisor-style review): when dashboardDirty=false, fast-path returns dashboardCache (NOT memory), so without pre-computation Dashboard would return zero struct. Fix: Hydrate pre-computes dashboardCache from loaded.Dashboard() on hash-match (lock-free; memory's own lock). Test TestHydrateMatchingHashSkipsDashboardRefetch asserts both zero backing calls AND non-empty ReadyExplain. Wire-format note: SyncedAt now RFC3339Nano (was bare RFC3339); old files still parse (RFC3339 subset). All ACs pass: round-trip via new APIs; matching hash skips refetch; mismatched/empty/error → safe-default dirty=true; existing Save/Load preserved. mise run quality green (1188 unit / 1361 integration, 0 lint, -race clean).

description:

Compile-time Repository assertion verified. Hash-match warm-path (kxci.7) verified via TestHydrateMatchingHashSkipsDashboardRefetch — precomputed dashboard populated from hydrated memory, backing skipped on hash match. Separate load/write paths (kxci.8) via TestHydrateSeparateLoadAndWritePaths. title: Wire CachingRepository into cmd/bwb: --repo caching default + session-id path
description:
## Outcome

cmd/bwb wires the CachingRepository so that the default startup mode boots from per-session JSONL, serves reads from memory, polls bd for external changes, and saves on shutdown.

## Flag changes
- `--repo beads|memory|caching` (default changes from `beads` to `caching`)
- `--repo-file <path>` — for `caching` mode, becomes the per-session cache file (default derived as `~/.cache/bwb/<project-hash>-<session-id>/repo.jsonl`); for `memory` mode, unchanged; for `beads` mode, unchanged (informational)
- Document in `--help` and `docs/CODING.md`

## Default repo-file path (caching mode)
- Reuse `defaultRepoFilePath(projectRoot)` from kxci.4 / 8pxi.4
- Extend it to take a session ID: `defaultRepoFilePath(projectRoot, sessionID string) string` returning `~/.cache/bwb/<hash>-<sessionID>/repo.jsonl`
- Session ID is the same `session_id` already used by `internal/logging` (find the helper in `internal/logging/` and reuse it; do NOT generate independently)

## Lifecycle wiring (caching mode only)
1. Construct the backing `beads.Repository` (as today)
2. Construct `caching.New(backing, ...)`
3. Call `cache.Hydrate(repoFilePath)` (logs warning + continues on error per kxci.4)
4. Call `cache.Start(ctx)` to launch the refresh + save tick
5. Register a signal handler / defer to call `cache.SaveNow()` + `cache.Stop()` on app exit

For `--repo beads` (legacy): construct beads.Repository directly, no caching
For `--repo memory`: unchanged from 8pxi.4

## Files (modified)
- `cmd/bwb/main.go` — wire the new flag value + lifecycle
- `cmd/bwb/main_test.go` — extend `TestParseCLIRepoFlags` to cover `--repo caching`; add `TestCachingModeStartupWiring` that boots with a tmp dir cache + a stub backing and asserts: (a) initial Dashboard call hits backing; (b) second Dashboard call hits cache (count backing calls); (c) shutdown saves the cache file
- `docs/CODING.md` — update `--repo` documentation

## Migration concern
Default flag flip from `beads` → `caching` is a user-visible behavior change. Document it clearly in the commit message and a docs note. Add a one-line print at startup ("Using caching repository backend; --repo beads disables") so users see what mode they're in.

## Out of scope for this task
- Per-ID Dashboard projection refinement (the current single-bool "dirty" Dashboard is fine for this epic; per-ID is a future optimization)
- Multi-process cache file consolidation (per Q4: each session has its own file; this is intentional)

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `bwb --help` shows `--repo beads|memory|caching` (default caching)
- [ ] `bwb --repo caching` boots and shows the dashboard; cache file appears at expected path
- [ ] Second boot in same project finds the cache file and hydrates from it (verified manually OR via integration test)
- [ ] `bwb --repo beads` still works (legacy path; no caching, no file)
- [ ] `bwb --repo memory --repo-file fixture.jsonl` still works (from 8pxi.4)
- [ ] Test `TestCachingModeStartupWiring` passes
- [ ] Shutdown saves the cache file (visible by file mtime after exit)
- [ ] mise run quality green
- [ ] docs/CODING.md updated with the new --repo values
close_reason: Closed

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Done. Flag default flipped --repo beads → caching (with beads|memory|caching valid). defaultRepoFilePath extended to (projectRoot, sessionID) signature: caching mode uses ~/.cache/bwb/<hash>-<sessionID>/repo.jsonl. constructRepository helper extracted from startInteractive for testability. internal/logging gained SessionID() accessor. caching branch wires: backing → caching.New(WithVCStatusFunc) → Hydrate (best-effort; logs warning on error, continues cold) → Start(ctx). Defer SaveNow + Stop on shutdown. 5 new tests: TestParseCLIRepoFlags +caching cases, TestDefaultRepoFilePathWithSessionID, TestCachingModeStartupWiring, TestCachingModeDefaultRepoFilePath, TestConstructRepositoryCachingWritesCacheFile. docs/CODING.md --repo entries updated. mise run quality green (1175 unit / 1348 integration). Manual smoke: bwb boots; cache file at ~/.cache/bwb/<hash>-<session>/repo.jsonl created with valid manifest. NOTE: discovered AR-blocking gap — Hydrate currently always sets dashboardDirty=true (conservative), causing first Dashboard call to refetch from backing (~3s, violates AR sub-second first paint target). Filing kxci.5a to add bd_commit_hash persistence in manifest + hydrate-time hash comparison.

title: Per-session JSONL hydrate at startup + periodic save
description:
## Outcome

CachingRepository can hydrate its in-memory state from a per-session JSONL file at startup (using `filestorage.Load` from hhho.4), and saves its in-memory state back to that file periodically and at shutdown.

## File path
Per Q4 (settled at kickoff): `~/.cache/bwb/<project-hash>-<session-id>/repo.jsonl` + adjacent `.manifest.json`. Each bwb instance writes to its own path; no cross-process locking.

## Files (modified or new)
- `internal/repository/caching/caching.go` — add `Hydrate(path string) error` and modify the tick to also do periodic save (configurable interval; default 30s); add explicit `SaveNow() error` for shutdown
- `internal/repository/caching/options.go` — `WithSaveInterval(d time.Duration)`
- `internal/repository/caching/caching_test.go` — hydrate/save round-trip tests

## Hydrate behavior
- `Hydrate("")` → no-op; start with empty memory
- `Hydrate("/path")` and file does not exist → no-op, start empty
- `Hydrate("/path")` and schema mismatch (`filestorage.ErrSchemaMismatch`) → log warning, start empty (NOT an error — degrade to cold start)
- `Hydrate("/path")` and other read error → return the error (caller decides)
- Success → backing memory is the loaded memory; mark Dashboard dirty so first read triggers a backing fetch to verify staleness

## Save behavior
- Periodic save: tick body (kxci.3 already runs a goroutine; share it or add a separate timer) calls `filestorage.Save(memory, path)` if path != ""
- Save errors logged as warnings, do not crash or corrupt state
- `SaveNow()` is synchronous; called by app shutdown handler

## Out of scope for this task
- App wiring (path derivation, Start/Stop integration with cmd/bwb shutdown) → kxci.5

## Test coverage
- Hydrate then read: returns hydrated data without backing call (until invalidation)
- Hydrate missing file: starts empty, no error
- Hydrate schema mismatch: starts empty, logs warning
- Save then Hydrate (round-trip): same data
- Save during concurrent mutation: no corruption (test with -race)
- SaveNow returns error on disk full / permission denied (use a tmp dir + chmod to simulate)

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Hydrate handles all 4 cases (empty path, missing file, schema mismatch, success)
- [ ] Periodic save tick works (test with fake clock)
- [ ] SaveNow is synchronous and returns error on failure
- [ ] Round-trip lossless (all hydrated fields preserved after save+reload)
- [ ] -race clean
- [ ] mise run quality green
close_reason: Closed

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Done. Hydrate(path)/SaveNow() + periodic save ticker added. caching.go: cacheFilePath + saveInterval fields, default 30s; tickLoop runs two tickers (refresh + save) in select. options.go: WithSaveInterval(d). 11 new tests, -race clean: TestHydrate{EmptyPath,MissingFile,SchemaMismatch,Success,OtherError}, TestSaveNow{EmptyPath,Success,ConcurrentMutation,Error}, TestPeriodicSaveTickFires, TestRoundTripHydrateThenSave. Locking: SaveNow takes c.mu.RLock briefly to read path+mem pointer, releases, calls filestorage.Save which takes memory.mu.RLock internally — no nested locking, no deadlock path. Limitation for kxci.5: periodic save only fires when tickLoop is running (vcStatusFunc != nil). App wiring must set both options together; documented in tickLoop godoc. SaveNow at shutdown works regardless. mise run quality green (1169 unit / 1342 integration, 0 lint, fmt:check + scripts:check clean).

title: Background refresh tick: bd vc status hash polling
description:
## Outcome

The CachingRepository (from kxci.2) gains a background goroutine that polls `beads.VCStatusHash` on a configurable interval (default 60s). When the hash changes, the Dashboard cache is invalidated (next read refetches from backing).

## Files (modified)
- `internal/repository/caching/caching.go` — add `Start(ctx)` and `Stop()` methods + tick goroutine
- `internal/repository/caching/options.go` — `WithRefreshInterval(d time.Duration)`, `WithVCStatusFunc(func(ctx) (string, error))` (test seam)
- `internal/repository/caching/caching_test.go` — tick tests

## Lifecycle
```go
// Start launches the background refresh goroutine. Idempotent.
// The goroutine exits when ctx is cancelled OR Stop() is called.
func (c *CachingRepository) Start(ctx context.Context)
// Stop signals the background goroutine to exit. Blocks until it does.
func (c *CachingRepository) Stop()
```

## Tick body
```
1. lastHash := current cached hash (under mu lock)
2. h, err := c.vcStatusFunc(ctx)
3. if err != nil: log warning, keep lastHash, return
4. if h != lastHash: lock mu, mark Dashboard dirty, drop ALL cached Issue(id) entries (we don't know which changed), update lastHash, unlock
```

NOTE: We invalidate ALL per-ID Issue cache entries on hash change because we can't tell which specific issues changed without a more expensive `bd query updated>X` poll (option b, deferred). This is a conservative correctness trade.

## Test seam
- `WithVCStatusFunc` lets tests inject a deterministic hash provider
- Tests use a fake clock + manual tick trigger (NOT real timers) where possible. If you need a timer, use a fast interval (~10ms) with explicit `time.Sleep` and accept some flake risk; or expose a `triggerRefresh()` test-only method behind a build tag.

## Test coverage
- Tick with unchanged hash: no invalidation
- Tick with changed hash: Dashboard refetched on next read; per-ID Issue cache cleared
- vcStatusFunc error: tick logs warning, no state change, lastHash preserved
- Start/Stop lifecycle: Start launches goroutine, Stop blocks until exit, second Start is no-op or re-launches cleanly
- Context cancellation: ctx.Done in Start → goroutine exits

## Wiring (deferred to kxci.5)
- kxci.5 will call `Start(ctx)` from `cmd/bwb/main.go` startup and `Stop()` in shutdown.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Start/Stop work; race-clean
- [ ] Hash change invalidates Dashboard + per-ID Issue cache
- [ ] Hash unchanged → no invalidation (verified by counting backing calls)
- [ ] vcStatusFunc errors don't crash or corrupt cache state
- [ ] mise run quality green
close_reason: Closed

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Done. CachingRepository.Start(ctx)/Stop()/RefreshIfChanged(ctx) added; tickLoop polls vcStatusFunc on configurable interval (default 60s). First observation is baseline (no invalidation). Hash change → dashboardDirty=true + memory.Reset() (added to forget.go alongside existing Forget). vcStatusFunc=nil → Start is no-op (lets non-app callers use the cache without polling). WithRefreshInterval(d) + WithVCStatusFunc(fn) options added. Test seam: exported RefreshIfChanged for deterministic tests; real-timer tests use 1-50ms intervals where timer behavior is the assertion target. 8 new tick tests + Stop-during-tick + concurrent-reads-during-tick all pass -race clean. mise run quality green (1158 unit / 1331 integration, 0 lint, fmt:check + scripts:check clean).

title: CachingRepository decorator: read-through, sync writes, catalog TTL
description:
## Outcome

A `CachingRepository` decorator at `internal/repository/caching/caching.go` wraps `memory.Repository` (fast-path) + `repository.Repository` (source of truth, expected to be a `beads.Repository`). Implements the full Repository interface.

## Reads (per-method)
- `Issue(id)`: if the issue is in memory, return it. Otherwise `backing.Issue(id)`, store in memory, return.
- `Dashboard()`: if a non-stale cached DashboardData exists, return it. Otherwise `backing.Dashboard()`, cache it, return. Staleness is set by kxci.3's tick or by mutations; for THIS task, a single in-memory bool "dirty" is enough (cleared on fetch, set on any mutation).
- `Search(q)`: ALWAYS pass through to `backing.Search(q)` (no caching — search is queryable and not easily keyed).
- `Catalogs()`: TTL-cached (default 5min). If cached and `time.Since(fetchedAt) < ttl`, return cached. Otherwise `backing.Catalogs()`, store with new `fetchedAt`, return.
- `HealthCheck()`: pass through to backing.

## Writes (all synchronous: backing first, memory on success)
- `CreateIssue`: `backing.CreateIssue` → on success, mark Dashboard dirty AND seed the new issue into memory (best-effort; if seed fails, leave dirty so next Dashboard refetches)
- `UpdateIssue(id, ...)`: `backing.UpdateIssue` → on success, drop the cached `Issue(id)` AND mark Dashboard dirty
- `CloseIssue(id, ...)`: same as UpdateIssue
- `AddComment(id, ...)`: `backing.AddComment` → on success, drop the cached `Issue(id)` (Dashboard need not be dirty unless comments affect Dashboard projection — they don't)

## Constructor
```go
func New(backing repository.Repository, opts ...Option) *CachingRepository
```
Options: `WithCatalogsTTL(d time.Duration)`, `WithClock(func() time.Time)`. Defaults: TTL=5min, clock=time.Now.

Internal memory uses a per-decorator `*memory.Repository` instance (do NOT reuse the caller's memory instance — keep ownership clean).

## Concurrency
- All methods safe for concurrent use; use `sync.RWMutex` around cache state
- Per the Repository interface godoc: reads may overlap; writes serialize

## Files (new)
- `internal/repository/caching/caching.go`
- `internal/repository/caching/options.go`
- `internal/repository/caching/caching_test.go`

## Test coverage
- Each read method: cache miss → backing called → cache populated → second call hits cache (no second backing call)
- Each write method: backing called, on success cache updated/invalidated; on failure cache untouched
- Catalogs TTL: before TTL → cached; after TTL → refetched
- Race-clean: tests run with `-race`
- Concurrent reads + concurrent writes do not data-race (table-driven goroutine test)

## Out of scope for THIS task
- Background tick / bd vc status polling → kxci.3
- File persistence → kxci.4
- App wiring → kxci.5
- Dirty flag is a single bool; per-ID Dashboard reconstruction is a future optimization

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] CachingRepository implements repository.Repository (compile-time assertion)
- [ ] Per-method tests pass (each read: 2 calls = 1 backing call after first; each write: backing called + cache state asserted)
- [ ] Catalogs TTL test: clock advances, refetch happens
- [ ] -race clean
- [ ] mise run quality green
close_reason: Closed

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Implemented. Files: internal/repository/caching/{caching.go,options.go,caching_test.go} + internal/repository/memory/forget.go (Forget + SeedDetail helpers). Uses dashboardCache+dashboardDirty pattern (simpler); memory.Repository used as per-ID issue cache only. ctx.Err() at entry of cache-hit paths conforms to Repository cancellation contract. SeedDetail added because CreateIssue must seed memory with backing's assigned ID (memory.CreateIssue would overwrite with its own idgen). 18 caching tests pass -race clean. mise run quality green (1149 unit / 1322 integration, 0 lint).

title: Add bd vc status hash accessor on beads runner
description:
## Outcome

`internal/gateway/beads/runner.go` (or a sibling file in the same package) exposes a helper that runs `bd vc status --json` (or parses the human-readable output if no `--json` flag exists) and returns the Dolt commit hash. CachingRepository uses this in its periodic invalidation tick (kxci.3).

## Investigation
- Run `bd vc status --json 2>&1` to see if a JSON form exists. If yes, parse JSON. If no, parse the human-readable `Commit: <hash>` line with a regex.
- Confirm the hash format is stable (alphanumeric, short hex-like).

## Files (new or modified)
- `internal/gateway/beads/vc_status.go` — `VCStatusHash(ctx context.Context, runner *CommandRunner) (string, error)` helper
- `internal/gateway/beads/vc_status_test.go` — unit tests with a recording executor

## Contract
```go
// VCStatusHash returns the current Dolt commit hash from `bd vc status`.
// Returns ("", err) on any failure; the caller treats error as "skip this tick".
// Two consecutive calls with no external bd writes return the same hash.
// Calls that succeed but cannot parse a hash return an error.
func VCStatusHash(ctx context.Context, runner *CommandRunner) (string, error)
```

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Helper compiles and exports as `beads.VCStatusHash`
- [ ] Unit test: success path returns the hash from a stubbed runner output
- [ ] Unit test: error path returns wrapped error (do not panic)
- [ ] Unit test: malformed output (no hash) returns a parse error
- [ ] `mise run quality` green
- [ ] No new dependencies

title: CachingRepository decorator: compose in-memory + bd for fast startup and surgical invalidation
description:
## Outcome (placeholder)

A `CachingRepository` decorator composes `memory.Repository` (fast-path) with `beads.Repository` (source of truth). Reads hit memory first, fall back to bd on miss, populate memory, return. Writes go through bd, update memory on success. On startup the in-memory state is hydrated from the persisted JSONL file (from Epic 1 step 4) so the dashboard renders instantly; background refresh syncs from bd asynchronously.

This is what realises the original stated goal: "serve from in-memory, delegate to bd, persist to disk for fast restart."

## Prerequisite

Depends on Epic [[hhho]] (E1) and Epic [[8pxi]] (E2). Cannot start until both are complete.

## Open design questions (to settle at epic kickoff, not now)

- **Cache invalidation signal (the structural fix for the bd-show false-positive).** Per-ID invalidation in `CachingRepository.UpdateIssue(X)` solves stale-`Issue(X)` -- but `Dashboard()` is a single composite cache entry built from many issues, so bd-show advancing `last-touched` busts Dashboard exactly as today unless we choose a different signal. Options:
  - **(a)** Periodic `bd vc status` Dolt-commit-hash poll on the refresh tick; treat unchanged hash as "no external write"; ignore mtime advances caused by our own bd-show calls
  - **(b)** `bd query "updated>X"` polling -- fetch only changed issues and merge into store; granular but more expensive
  - **(c)** Track our own bd-show invocations and suppress the mtime bump in the cache token check (heuristic; race-prone with concurrent external writes)
  - **(d)** Drop external-write detection entirely; only invalidate on bwb-initiated writes and manual refresh; loses correctness for multi-agent scenarios
- Write semantics: optimistic (mutate memory first, delegate to bd, revert on failure) vs synchronous (bd first, memory on success)
- Background refresh trigger: startup-only, periodic tick, true SWR per-call, or hybrid
- Multi-process safety: per-session cache file vs shared with file-lock
- Catalog refresh strategy if accepted as stale in [[hhho.1]] -- still stale here, or refresh on Catalogs() invalidation?

## Acceptance criteria (high-level, to be refined)

- [ ] `CachingRepository` implements `Repository` and wraps both other implementations
- [ ] Cold-start time on a project with a warm persisted cache is sub-second to first paint
- [ ] External bd writes detected within configurable interval (target: 60s default) using the invalidation signal chosen above
- [ ] Per-ID invalidation: write to issue X does not bust unrelated cached issues
- [ ] `bd show` side-effect on `last-touched` mtime no longer causes cache-wide invalidation (via the chosen mechanism, NOT structural by-default)
close_reason: Closed

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Design questions settled 2026-05-24 (user confirmed all recommended options): Q1 invalidation = (a) periodic 'bd vc status' Dolt-commit-hash poll on the refresh tick; treat unchanged hash as no external write; ignore mtime advances from our own bd-show calls. Q2 write semantics = synchronous (bd first, then memory on success). Q3 refresh trigger = hybrid (startup hydrate from persisted JSONL + periodic tick, 60s default). Q4 multi-process safety = per-session cache file at ~/.cache/bwb/<hash>-<session>/ (no locking). Q5 catalogs freshness = TTL refresh (cache catalogs with ~5min TTL, transparent refresh when stale).
