title: Inventory every distinct bd argv shape bwb emits at runtime
description:
## Description

Audit task. Produce a complete inventory of every distinct argv shape bwb sends to the bd subprocess at runtime. Output: a doc/table that maps each `<consumer>` → `<bd verb + flags>`, with source-code references. This is the input for [[ppja.3]] (per-call argv-cardinality tests).

## Instructions

1. Use grep to find every `runner.Run(ctx, args...)` call site (or equivalent — read `internal/gateway/beads/runner.go` first to know the API surface).
2. For each call site, trace back to the gateway method that owns it, then to the consumer that triggers the gateway method. Build the chain: `<consumer in mode/board, mode/search, app, launcher, etc.>` → `<gateway method>` → `<exact argv>`.
3. Run `mise run bwb` (or its non-interactive equivalent — `mise run smoke --format json` exercises a lot of paths) with a debug logger and capture all argvs from the persistent log (the gateway logs argv per call). Cross-check the static analysis from step 2 against the dynamic observation.
4. Document the inventory in `internal/gateway/beads/doc.go` (or a new `internal/gateway/beads/ARGV_CONTRACT.md`) as a table:
   ```
   | Consumer                         | Method        | argv shape                                            |
   |----------------------------------|---------------|-------------------------------------------------------|
   | board.Init                       | ReadyExplain  | bd ready --explain --json                             |
   | board.Init                       | Query         | bd query status=in_progress --json                    |
   | board.Init                       | Query         | bd query status=closed -a --sort closed --limit 50 ...|
   | board.Init                       | CountIssues   | bd count --by-status --json --status closed           |
   | search.SearchIssues              | Query         | bd query status=open --json (...)                     |
   | ...                              | ...           | ...                                                   |
   ```
5. Flag any argv shape that lacks a corresponding test assertion (the existing `TestBoardInitRealGatewaySubprocessArgvCardinality` covers board.Init; many others are uncovered). This list becomes the work backlog for [[ppja.3]].
6. Apply the "Discovered-during-audit bug discipline" — if you find an argv that's inconsistent with what `interface.go` documents (or with what bd actually accepts), file a bug per the parent epic [[ppja]].

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Inventory doc/table exists covering every distinct argv shape bwb emits
- [ ] Each entry references the consumer (file:line or function name)
- [ ] Static analysis cross-checked against dynamic capture from a real bwb session
- [ ] Argv shapes lacking a pinning test are explicitly listed as the [[ppja.3]] backlog
- [ ] Bugs discovered (if any) filed per discipline and linked back
- [ ] `mise run quality` passes (doc-only changes)

## Files to Modify

- create `internal/gateway/beads/doc.go` extension (or `internal/gateway/beads/ARGV_CONTRACT.md`)
close_reason: Closed

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Done. ARGV_CONTRACT.md created with 16 distinct verb-level argv shapes + 14-shape ppja.3 backlog. No argv inconsistencies vs interface.go found. Discipline yielded no bugs. NOTE: ARGV_CONTRACT.md may have stale routingExecutor refs (lines 102-103, 141) from drafting against pre-ppja.1 state; orchestrator will fix in cleanup.

title: Migrate routingExecutor callers to RecordingExecutor; delete routingExecutor
description:
## Description

Two test-side `exec.Command` swaps exist with overlapping purpose: `routingExecutor` (private, in `internal/gateway/beads/read_gateway_test.go`) and `RecordingExecutor` (public, in `internal/testing/fakes/recording_executor.go`). RecordingExecutor is more capable; consolidate.

## Instructions

1. Read `internal/testing/fakes/recording_executor.go` to confirm it supports route-by-argv (canned response per argv shape). If not, extend it — but I believe it already does (`rec.OnArgs(argv).Return(...)`).
2. Read `internal/gateway/beads/read_gateway_test.go` and identify every callsite of `routingExecutor` (likely the `newTestGateway` helper + the test cases via `routes` maps). Count them so the migration scope is clear up front.
3. Migrate one-by-one:
   - Replace `routingExecutor` construction with `RecordingExecutor`.
   - Replace `routes map[string]routeResponse{...}` with `rec.OnArgs(...).Return(...)` calls.
   - Keep test semantics identical — assertions should not change.
4. After all callers migrated, delete `routingExecutor` and its helper `argsKey` (if unused elsewhere).
5. Run `mise run quality` — must pass with the consolidated executor.
6. Bonus: extend `RecordingExecutor` doc to note it now subsumes the routing-only role.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Every test previously using `routingExecutor` now uses `RecordingExecutor` with no semantic change
- [ ] `routingExecutor` and any private helpers exclusive to it are deleted
- [ ] `mise run quality` passes
- [ ] `grep -rn "routingExecutor" internal/` returns zero matches
- [ ] `RecordingExecutor` doc updated to reflect its broader role

## Files to Modify

- `internal/gateway/beads/read_gateway_test.go` (migration + deletion of routingExecutor)
- possibly `internal/testing/fakes/recording_executor.go` (extension + doc update)
close_reason: Closed

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Done. 54 routingExecutor callsites migrated to a package-internal testRecordingExecutor (fakes.RecordingExecutor cannot be imported from package beads due to cycle). routingExecutor + routeResponse + argsKey deleted. grep returns zero matches. Quality green (757 unit + 851 integration).

title: Extend validatingGateway with post-write invariants (or add validatingWriteGateway)
description:
## Description

Production tripwire for write methods. Mirror the read-side `validatingGateway` decorator pattern from 8qw9.4: track writes and verify subsequent reads are consistent. When a write succeeds but the next read doesn't reflect it (dolt batch-commit lag, bd cache staleness, schema drift), emit `slog.Warn` so the divergence is captured in the persistent log.

## Decision needed during implementation

Two design options — pick the cleaner one after reading the existing validatingGateway:

- **Option A (extend)**: add write-method delegations + invariant checks to the existing `validatingGateway` in `internal/gateway/beads/validating_gateway.go`. Single decorator, all-in-one. Cross-method state (recently-written IDs) lives next to the existing ssom state.
- **Option B (separate decorator)**: new `validatingWriteGateway` in a sibling file. Composable with read decorator. Cleaner separation but requires `NewCLIGateway` to wrap twice.

I lean Option A — keeps related state together and avoids double-wrapping overhead. Validate before committing.

## Instructions

1. Read `internal/gateway/beads/validating_gateway.go` to understand the existing structure + state model.
2. Add new validators to `internal/gateway/beads/contractcheck/`:
   - `ValidateCreateResultShape(result domain.CreateIssueResult) []Violation` — returned ID non-empty, etc.
   - `ValidateWriteObservable(method, writtenID string, observedDetail domain.IssueDetail) []Violation` — fields the write claimed to set actually appear in the observed detail.
3. Cross-method invariant: track the last N (e.g., 16) write operations and their inputs in an ephemeral ring buffer. When a subsequent `ShowIssue` or `ListIssues` runs against an ID in the buffer, run `ValidateWriteObservable` and warn on mismatch.
4. **Critical limitation to document**: dolt batch-commit mode means writes may legitimately not appear in immediate reads. The invariant must either (a) skip when dolt is in batch mode, (b) wait briefly before warning, or (c) document the warning as advisory under batch mode. Pick the approach that minimizes noise.
5. Add unit tests in `validating_gateway_test.go`:
   - Each write invariant has a deliberate-break test (mock inner returns success from Create but next ShowIssue returns nothing → warning fires).
   - Test the dolt-batch-mode exemption if implemented.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Each write method has a corresponding invariant validator in `contractcheck`
- [ ] `validatingGateway` (or new `validatingWriteGateway`) delegates writes and runs validators
- [ ] WriteObservable cross-method invariant exercised by deliberate-break test
- [ ] Dolt batch-mode case handled (no false positives) and documented
- [ ] `mise run quality` passes
- [ ] Decision A vs B documented in the validating_gateway.go header

## Files to Modify

- `internal/gateway/beads/validating_gateway.go` (extend) OR new `validating_write_gateway.go`
- `internal/gateway/beads/validating_gateway_test.go` (extend)
- `internal/gateway/beads/contractcheck/contractcheck.go` (new write validators)
- possibly `internal/gateway/beads/read_gateway.go` (wrapping logic)
close_reason: Closed

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Closed as won't-do per epic-review decision recorded 2026-05-16 (epic 9x70 comment): 'Drop task 9x70.4 (validatingGateway write extension). The task acknowledges dolt batch-commit will produce false positives; all three proposed mitigations gut the value... Revisit only if a real consumer bug surfaces.' Tasker correctly stopped pre-implementation citing the orphaned-decision readiness failure. Orchestrator honoring the drop.

title: Gateway contract violation: SearchIssues multi-status + text filter silently returns empty results
description:
## Discovered during

[[8qw9.1]] (gateway contract audit, 2026-05-16).

## Expected vs actual

SearchIssues routes text queries through `bd search --json --status <csv>`. `bd list` accepts comma-separated status values, but **`bd search --status` accepts ONLY a single token**. `bd search task --status open,closed` returns `[]` without error, silently discarding all results.

## Verification required

Must verify in the running app (`mise run bwb`, Search mode) whether the UI currently exposes a path that sends text + multiple status filters to the bd search route. If no UI path triggers this (the search view may not support multi-status filtering today), the bug is INERT and should be closed as 'not a bug' with rationale. If it does, fix.

Orchestrator preliminary check: read internal/mode/search/model.go and internal/ui/search/search.go to confirm whether multi-status + text is reachable. If not currently reachable, defer or close as not-a-bug.

## Suggested fix layer (if confirmed reachable)

Gateway (SearchIssues in `read_gateway.go`): when routing through `bd search` and multiple statuses are requested, either (a) issue one `bd search` call per status and merge in-memory, or (b) use `--status all` and post-filter in-memory.
close_reason: Closed

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Not a bug — closing per the discipline's 'inert behavior' carve-out. Investigation: grep across internal/mode/search/ and internal/ui/search/ shows zero usage of SearchIssuesQuery.Statuses field. The search UI does not currently expose multi-status filtering, so the silent-empty-result behavior is unreachable from any user-facing path. Additionally, interface.go (post-audit) documents this as CARVE OUT — the gateway already passes --status all and post-filters in-memory for the multi-status case. If multi-status filtering is later added to the search UI, this bug becomes reachable and should be re-opened. Rationale: app verification not required because there is no UI path to verify against.

title: Harden gateway write contract (CreateIssue/UpdateIssue/CloseIssue/AddComment)
description:
## Description

PLACEHOLDER EPIC — captures scope so we don't lose context. Not actively planned yet.

The contract-hardening epic [[8qw9]] covers read methods only. The four write methods on `BeadsGateway` deserve the same treatment:
- `CreateIssue(input)`
- `UpdateIssue(issueID, input)`
- `CloseIssue(issueID, input)`
- `AddComment(issueID, input)`

Write methods have additional contract surface that reads don't:
- **Side effects**: a successful CreateIssue must produce an issue retrievable by ShowIssue with matching fields.
- **Idempotency / collision**: what happens if you Create with a colliding ID? Update a non-existent issue?
- **Confirmation prompts**: bd has interactive confirmations for some destructive operations; bwb passes `BD_NON_INTERACTIVE=1`. Document the assumed bypass.
- **Audit trail**: bd attributes writes to an actor (env var or git user.name); the gateway's `--actor` handling needs explicit contract.
- **Dolt write-ack**: bd's dolt backend has auto-commit policy (off/on/batch); writes may not be visible to subsequent reads in some modes. Contract must document the consistency model.

## Probable scope (when activated)

- Audit bd write behavior hands-on (mirror [[8qw9.1]] approach for the write side).
- Document each write method's contract in `interface.go`.
- Add a `RunWriteContract` suite that runs against fake AND real implementations.
- Extend `validatingGateway` (or add a write-side decorator) for post-write invariants like "the created issue is now visible to ShowIssue."
- Audit `FakeBeadsGateway`'s write methods against the new contract; close fidelity gaps.

## Out of Scope

Read-side contract (covered by [[8qw9]]).
Argv-shape contract (covered by separate placeholder epic — see [[argv-contract-consolidation]]).

## Activation Trigger

Plan and activate after [[8qw9]] closes. Read-side contract patterns inform the write-side approach; deferring avoids reinventing.
close_reason: Closed

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text:
**Epic-review 2026-05-16 (descope)**

Reviewed by orchestrator + independent reviewer before activation. Outcome:

1. **Drop task 9x70.4** (validatingGateway write extension). The task acknowledges dolt batch-commit will produce false positives; all three proposed mitigations (skip-in-batch / wait / advisory) gut the value. Write-then-read across a subprocess + dolt-batch boundary is fundamentally noisy — running this validator risks eroding trust in the slog.Warn signal that 8qw9 just established on the read side. Revisit only if a real consumer bug surfaces that this would have caught.

2. **Re-shape 9x70.3** (fake fidelity) — gate it on 9x70.2 actually surfacing gaps rather than pre-allocating closure work. 8qw9.3 had real fake gaps because reads have richer surface; writes may not.

3. **Tighten 9x70.1 AC** — add evidence floor: "audit log records at least N real bd invocations per write method" so the audit can't pass as desk research.

4. **Priority note**: P1 bugs ule7 and 5q6t (refresh-concurrency, user-facing UI corruption) take precedence over this P2 infrastructure work.

title: Replace 3-issue fixture with realistic scale fixture (~600 issues, edge-case-designed)
description:
## Description

PLACEHOLDER EPIC — captures scope so we don't lose context. Not actively planned yet.

`internal/testing/e2e/embeddedfixture/seed.json` has 3 issues. Every parity test, contract test, and integration test that uses the fixture inherits this scale ceiling. Real DBs have hundreds of issues. The bugs fixed in epic [[vr1d]] (ssom, kh54, o7tk) all needed real-data shapes to manifest — fixture-based tests passed while production failed.

## Probable scope (when activated)

Replace the seed with a designed scale fixture (~500-700 issues) that explicitly exercises:
- ≥ 75 closed issues (engages Done column 50-cap; produces "+" badge)
- ≥ 510 issues in one active group (engages 500 cardinality threshold)
- A block of 10+ closed issues sharing one `updated_at` (kh54 trigger)
- A block of P1 issues sharing one timestamp (sort tie-break)
- One 5-deep parent chain, one 3-wide siblings group
- Edge data: one emoji title, one shell-metacharacter title, one max-length title
- A shared keyword across 20+ issues for search relevance / order testing

Build with a recipe-driven generator (`internal/testing/e2e/embeddedfixture/scale/generator.go`) using a deterministic seed for reproducibility. Pair with a machine-readable invariant manifest (`fixture-invariants.yaml`) so the fixture's edge-case coverage doesn't silently erode as it's edited.

Keep the existing 3-issue seed as the "minimal anchor" fixture for tests that need exact ID assertions; new tests default to the scale fixture.

## Activation Trigger

Activate after [[8qw9]] (contract hardening) — the expanded contract test suite will benefit massively from running against the scale fixture. Doing both at once amplifies the value of each.

## Related

Complements [[8qw9]] (contract hardening). The contract gives us machine-checkable invariants; the realistic fixture gives those invariants real edge cases to exercise.
close_reason: Closed

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text:
**Epic-review 2026-05-16 (descope)**

Reviewed by orchestrator + independent reviewer before activation. Outcome:

1. **Collapse faif.1 + faif.2 + faif.3 into faif.1.** The 3-layer abstraction (recipe.yaml → generator.go → invariants.yaml + verifier) is over-built for what is effectively a stable JSON artifact. A scale fixture *engineered for specific bug-class triggers* should be stable, not regenerated. The recipe-driven generator only earns its keep if the fixture changes frequently — which it shouldn't.

2. **Sharp issue with faif.3 invariants**: examples like `bd count --status closed >= 75` re-derive what the recipe declared — they catch *generator* bugs, not gateway/composer bugs. Invariants should encode **observable bwb behavior** ("Done +badge appears when closed > cap (kh54 regression guard)", "cardinality warning fires at 500 (composer threshold)", "sort tie-breaks stable across equal `updated_at`"), not generator counts.

3. **Faif.1 (new merged scope)**: author `scale-seed.json` directly (committed artifact, ~600 issues handcrafted or via one-shot script — pick whichever is faster to author once) + add a Go smoke test (`scale_fixture_smoke_test.go`) with 8-12 product-behavior invariant assertions. No YAML recipe, no separate generator binary, no YAML invariants manifest, no verifier program. One file you can edit by hand; one test file enforcing what matters.

4. **Keep faif.4 and faif.5 as-is** — dual-fixture API and "run suites against scale fixture, file bugs per discipline" both have real product value.

5. **Priority note**: P1 bugs ule7 and 5q6t (refresh-concurrency, user-facing UI corruption) take precedence over this P2 infrastructure work.

title: Document fake-update discipline and establish drift log
description:
## Description

Process scaffolding that closes the human gap left by contract enforcement. The contract suite catches divergences when they hit a documented invariant; this task ensures the discipline of finding-and-fixing divergences gets practiced consistently, with a permanent log of what was found and when.

## Instructions

1. Create `internal/testing/fakes/doc.go` with package documentation that establishes the fake as a contract-conforming peer of the real implementation. Cover:
   - The fake passes `RunReadContract` against the same fixture as the real gateway.
   - The fake is NOT a casual mock — when adding behavior, ensure it matches real bd's observable surface (consult [[8qw9.1]] interface contract).
   - When a bug's root cause involves fake-vs-real divergence, the fix MUST: (a) update the fake to match real bd, (b) add an assertion to `RunReadContract` (or its invariants) that would have caught the divergence.

2. Add a "Fake drift log" section at the bottom of `doc.go` with a chronological list:
   ```
   ## Fake drift log
   //
   // 2026-MM-DD: <one-line description of divergence and fix>
   //   - Symptom: <bug or test failure that revealed it>
   //   - Cause: <how fake's behavior diverged from real bd>
   //   - Fix: <commit or PR; updated assertion ref>
   ```
   Seed with the divergences discovered during [[8qw9.3]] (the fidelity audit).

3. Update `AGENTS.md` to include a pointer: "When fixing a bug whose root cause involves fake-vs-real divergence, follow the fake-update discipline in `internal/testing/fakes/doc.go`." Add as a one-line bullet in the appropriate section.

4. Optional: extend the existing `bd close` workflow note (if there's a project-specific closure template) to ask "fake-vs-real gap involved? if yes, fake updated?" — but only if a closure template exists. Don't invent one for this.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `internal/testing/fakes/doc.go` exists with discipline text and drift log
- [ ] Drift log seeded with at least the divergences from [[8qw9.3]]
- [ ] AGENTS.md links to the discipline section
- [ ] `go vet ./...` and `mise run lint` pass

## Files to Modify

- create `internal/testing/fakes/doc.go`
- update `AGENTS.md`

## Related

- depends on [[8qw9.3]] (seeds the drift log with findings from the fake audit)
close_reason: Closed

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Done. Created internal/testing/fakes/doc.go with fake-update discipline (3-step protocol: update fake → strengthen invariant → record drift) and drift log seeded with 2 entries from 8qw9.3 (ShowIssue nil-slice, Query expr-ignore). Removed duplicate inline log from beads_gateway.go — single source of truth. AGENTS.md updated with pointer in Testing section. mise run quality green (734 unit + 849 integration, 0 lint).

title: Remove AICM-specific naming; make external dataset parametric
description:
## Description

The parity tests and bwb-smoke shipped with hardcoded references to `ai-config-manager` (env var names, default path, test names, mise task). External DBs should be project-agnostic: one env var for the path, no separate enable flag, no hardcoded default.

## Instructions

1. `internal/testing/datasets/datasets.go`:
   - Rename `AIConfigManager(t)` → `External(t)`.
   - Consolidate env vars: drop `BWB_PARITY_AICM` and `BWB_PARITY_AICM_PATH`; introduce single `BWB_PARITY_EXTERNAL_PATH`. If unset → skip cleanly. If set but `.beads/` missing → skip with reason.
   - Remove `defaultAICMPath` constant.
   - Update Dataset.Name from "ai-config-manager" to "external".
2. Update callers in test files:
   - `internal/dashboard/parity/count_parity_integration_test.go`
   - `internal/dashboard/parity/sort_parity_integration_test.go`
   - `internal/mode/search/parity/search_parity_integration_test.go`
   - `internal/testing/datasets/datasets_test.go` — rename TestAICM* tests to TestExternal*.
3. `.mise.toml`: remove `[tasks."smoke:aicm"]`. Make `[tasks.smoke]` accept passthrough args so user can do `mise run smoke -- --dir /any/path --readonly`.
4. Docs:
   - `docs/RUNTIME_UI_VERIFICATION.md`: replace `BWB_PARITY_AICM=1 BWB_PARITY_AICM_PATH=...` with `BWB_PARITY_EXTERNAL_PATH=...`. Replace `mise run smoke:aicm` with `mise run smoke -- --dir /path/to/external/repo --readonly`.
   - `docs/RELEASING.md`: same replacements.
5. Update the AICM comment in `internal/gateway/beads/runner.go:268` to generic "external databases".

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] grep for `AICM`, `aicm`, `AIConfigManager`, `ai-config-manager` returns 0 hits across the repo (except this ticket).
- [ ] `mise run quality` passes.
- [ ] `BWB_PARITY_EXTERNAL_PATH=/home/hans/dev/github/ai-config-manager mise run test:integration` runs the external parity tests and they pass.
- [ ] `mise run smoke -- --dir /home/hans/dev/github/ai-config-manager --readonly` returns `result: PASS` exit 0.
- [ ] `mise run smoke` (no args) still works against current dir as before.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Done. Renamed datasets.AIConfigManager → datasets.External. Consolidated BWB_PARITY_AICM + BWB_PARITY_AICM_PATH → single BWB_PARITY_EXTERNAL_PATH (no separate enable flag). Removed hardcoded /home/hans/dev/github/ai-config-manager default everywhere. Renamed TestAICM* → TestExternal* across test files. Removed mise smoke:aicm task; smoke now reads BWB_SMOKE_DIR env var (defaults to .). Updated docs. Verified end-to-end: mise run smoke → this-repo Done=461 match; BWB_SMOKE_DIR=/home/hans/dev/github/ai-config-manager mise run smoke → external Done=622 match. External .beads mtimes unchanged. grep for AICM/aicm/AIConfigManager/ai-config-manager returns 0 hits across code/tests/mise/docs.

title: "backend sort assumption broken" warning false-positives on equal UpdatedAt across closed issues
description:
## Description

The composer logs `warn: backend sort assumption broken ... group=Closed count=N threshold=-1` on real datasets even when bd is returning correctly ordered results. The check at `internal/dashboard/composer.go:121-132` uses `UpdatedAt` as a proxy for `closed_at` because `IssueSummary` does not carry `closed_at`. When many closed issues share an `updated_at` (bulk close, migration, scripted updates), the proxy fires a false positive.

## Expected vs Actual

- **Expected**: The "backend sort assumption broken" warning fires only when bd's `closed_at desc` ordering is actually violated.
- **Actual**: It fires whenever the first two returned closed issues have equal or non-descending `updated_at`, even though `closed_at` ordering is correct.

## Root Cause

`IssueSummary` lacks a `ClosedAt` field, so the composer uses `UpdatedAt` as a proxy. This is documented at composer.go:113-120 but the assumption breaks on real data.

## Suggested Fixes (pick one)

1. **Plumb `ClosedAt` through** — add `ClosedAt time.Time` to `IssueSummary`; map it from the gateway; use it in the proxy. Honest fix.
2. **Remove the defensive check** — it has produced more noise than signal; the gateway test already pins the sort flag we send to bd, and the parity test [[izds]] verifies Done order against bd on real data. Cheap fix.
3. **Tighten the proxy** — only warn if `len(in.Closed) >= 10` AND first 10 items are all equal/non-descending. Still proxy-based but quieter.

Recommendation: option 1 if `closed_at` is cheap to surface, otherwise option 2 (the parity test makes the defensive check redundant).

## Minimal Repro

```
cd /home/hans/dev/github/beads-workbench
mise run bwb
# Look at the log: warn: backend sort assumption broken ... group=Closed count=56 threshold=-1
```

## Impact

Low — pure noise in logs. But it conditions operators to ignore composer warnings, masking real issues if the assumption ever DOES break.

## Where Discovered

User-reported during release prep on 2026-05-16. Visible at the bottom of the pasted bwb output.

## Related

- exposed/clarified by parity test [[izds]] sub-test `ClosedAtDescPreservedOnRealData`
- part of epic [[vr1d]]
close_reason: Closed

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Option 2 implemented: removed UpdatedAt-proxy defensive check from composer.go. Empirically supported by izds diagnostic on this-repo (49/50 positions diverge between updated_at and closed_at — proxy was unreliable). 4 nil-warning regression cases added in composer_test. Sort correctness now verified by parity test izds.

title: Document smoke workflow in docs/RUNTIME_UI_VERIFICATION.md and RELEASING.md
description:
## Description

Make the parity test suite and `bwb-smoke` tool part of the documented release workflow so the next maintainer (or AI agent) actually runs them.

## Instructions

1. Update `docs/RUNTIME_UI_VERIFICATION.md`:
   - New section "Pre-release data-consistency checks" listing the three parity tests and the smoke binary.
   - One-liner commands: `mise run test:integration` then `mise run smoke` then optional `mise run smoke:aicm`.
   - Describe what each check covers and which failures block release vs are advisory.
2. Update `docs/RELEASING.md`:
   - Add "Smoke check" step before tagging: run `mise run smoke`; tag only if exit code 0.
3. Update `docs/MONITORING.md` if `bwb-smoke` exposes useful diagnostic surfaces; otherwise note as out-of-scope.
4. Cross-link: `AGENTS.md` mentions `RUNTIME_UI_VERIFICATION.md` already — extend that pointer to call out the new section.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `docs/RUNTIME_UI_VERIFICATION.md` documents the parity suite + smoke tool with copy-pasteable commands
- [ ] `docs/RELEASING.md` includes the smoke check as a release-blocking step
- [ ] An agent or human reading only AGENTS.md → linked docs can run the full pre-release verification without grepping the repo
- [ ] No commands in the docs are aspirational — every command listed actually exists by the time this task closes

## Files to Modify

- `docs/RUNTIME_UI_VERIFICATION.md`
- `docs/RELEASING.md`
- possibly `docs/MONITORING.md`
- possibly `AGENTS.md` (linking only)

## Related

- depends on [[c3vg]] (smoke tool must exist before being documented)
close_reason: Closed

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Docs updated. RUNTIME_UI_VERIFICATION.md has new Pre-release data-consistency checks section with parity package descriptions, copy-pasteable commands, and release-blocking vs advisory table. RELEASING.md has smoke check as a release step before tag. AGENTS.md and MONITORING.md unchanged — existing pointers already cover the new content. All documented commands verified by orchestrator.

title: Cross-OS CI: macOS /private/var symlink + Windows APPDATA/MkdirAll/RWMutex platform edges
description:
Second wave after cqtk (commit 0f5d277). Golden tests fixed. Remaining failures:

**macOS (1 test)**:
- TestRun_CWDAndConfigResolutionAndStartOptions: 'expected config path resolved against start cwd, got /private/var/folders/...'. macOS t.TempDir returns /var/folders/... but path resolution canonicalizes via /private/var symlink. Fix: filepath.EvalSymlinks both sides before compare, OR set expected to the resolved path.

**Windows (multiple tests)**:
- TestLoad_DirectoryAtConfigPathReturnsError: still fails because testUserConfigDir(t) returns real APPDATA on Windows (UserConfigDir ignores HOME on darwin/windows). vkmt fix worked for macOS but Windows needs APPDATA env override too. OR skip this Unix-semantics test on Windows.
- TestLoad_UnreadableConfigReturnsError: 'permission bits differ on windows' — Windows ACLs ≠ Unix permission bits. Skip on Windows.
- TestRunCWDInaccessibleExitsWithCode1: same pattern as TestRunCWDExecuteOnlyExitsWithCode1 (which we already skipped). New name suggests recent rename — re-apply skip.
- TestRWMutexParallelReadOverlap: 'parallel read overlap too slow: 135ms >= 100ms (reads appear serialized)'. Windows scheduler overhead. Either skip on Windows or loosen threshold to 200ms.
- Cascading 'unknown' status tests after TestRunCWDInaccessibleExitsWithCode1 in cmd/bwb: typical of a single test panicking and killing the test process. Likely fixes by skipping the trigger.

Logs: gh run view 25963805190.
close_reason: All 5 cross-OS edges addressed in test-only changes; production unchanged; quality green locally; pending push verification.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: macOS: EvalSymlinks both sides of path compare in TestRun_CWDAndConfigResolutionAndStartOptions (canonicalizes /var → /private/var). Windows: setConfigHome helper now also sets APPDATA so os.UserConfigDir resolves to test temp dir; TestRunCWDInaccessibleExitsWithCode1 skipped (chmod 0o000 no-op on Windows, was causing cascading panic); TestRWMutexParallelReadOverlap threshold loosened 100ms → 200ms (Windows observed 135ms; 200ms still catches serial which would be ~2s). Quality green locally. High confidence per fix.

title: Windows CI: numerous golden tests + 2 platform tests fail; ndtype line endings + AppData path semantics
description:
Discovered after vkmt+x46s pushed (commit 1e796b0). Windows CI shows ~30+ failing tests, grouped:

**Group A — Golden file comparisons (~25 tests)**: TestModelFixtureShapedBoardCaptureGolden, TestBoardModeAllGroupsPopulatedRendersGolden, TestBoardModeDashboardLayoutGoldensAcrossWidths/{w80,w120,w180}, TestRenderBoardColumnsGolden/*, TestRenderBoardResponsiveWideGolden, TestRenderCommentsGolden, TestRenderCompactGolden, TestRenderWideThreeColumnGolden, TestRenderDependencyRichGolden, TestRenderCompactClosedDurationGolden, TestRenderFallbackKeepsInlineRelatedWorkGolden, TestRenderMinimalGolden, TestRenderFullGolden, TestRenderGoldens/*, TestHarnessSupportsTeaTestGoldenVerification, TestModelBoardDetailBoardRoundTripPreservesLayoutAndFocus, TestBoardModeNavigationEmitsSelectionChangedAndActionRequest.

Root cause hypothesis: Windows git checks out .golden files with CRLF by default; tests compare LF-rendered output against CRLF goldens, causing diff. Fix: .gitattributes pinning .golden + .go to LF, OR normalize line endings in golden comparison helpers.

**Group B — Windows-specific platform tests (2 tests)**:
- TestLoad_DirectoryAtConfigPathReturnsError: MkdirAll fails because AppData\Roaming\bwb\ doesn't pre-exist on the runner. Needs MkdirAll for the parent dir before attempting to create the inverted directory-at-file scenario.
- TestRunCWDExecuteOnlyExitsWithCode1: Windows-specific cwd resolution. Needs inspection.

Logs: gh run view 25963410279
close_reason: Line endings + Windows-specific tests addressed; verification pending on push.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Implementation complete. .gitattributes created (LF enforcement for *.golden/.go/.yaml/.yml/.toml/.sh/.md/.json/.jsonl). harness.go NormalizeOutput + AssertMatchesGolden strip CRLF before compare; board_test.go local normalize also patched (defense-in-depth). TestLoad_DirectoryAtConfigPathReturnsError fixed via MkdirAll(filepath.Dir(path)) before MkdirAll(path). TestRunCWDExecuteOnlyExitsWithCode1 + companion skipped on Windows (Unix execute-only chmod is no-op on Windows). Quality green: 624 unit + 652 integration. 90% confidence Windows golden tests resolve after push.

title: macOS CI: 13 internal/config tests fail with config-load assertions
description: Discovered when cross-OS matrix (ug82.8) ran on the first push (commit 9439d6c). 13 tests in internal/config/load_test.go fail on macos-latest. Sample failures: TestLoad_UnknownKeysWarnButDoNotFail (expected warning not in output), TestLoad_KeyBindingOverridesMergeAndValidate (expected toggle search override, got 'ctrl+@'), TestLoad_KeyBindingConflictReturnsError (expected error, got nil), TestLoad_InvalidYAMLReturnsError (expected error, got nil), TestLoad_DirectoryAtConfigPathReturnsError, TestLoad_DuplicateLauncherActionReturnsError, TestLoad_UnreadableConfigReturnsError, TestLauncherOverride_EmptyArgsClearsBuiltinArgs, TestLauncherOverride_AbsentCommandPreservesBuiltinCommand, TestLauncherOverride_NewActionNameAppends (and 3 more in the run). Pattern: load and validation tests not detecting expected error conditions. Probable cause: filesystem case-insensitivity or env-var/HOME resolution differences. Logs: gh run view 25963159939.
close_reason: macOS UserConfigDir path-resolution root cause fixed in test helper; production unchanged; high confidence.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Root cause: os.UserConfigDir() returns $HOME/Library/Application Support on macOS, ignoring $XDG_CONFIG_HOME. Tests set XDG_CONFIG_HOME=configHome and wrote config there, but load.go (via UserConfigDir) looked in the macOS path → no file → silent defaults → assertions about errors/values all failed. Fix: testUserConfigDir(t) helper calls os.UserConfigDir() after t.Setenv(HOME=...) so write and read paths match per platform. No production change. Quality green locally. CI verification on next push.

title: Windows CI: mise tasks fail because PowerShell can't expand bash $(cat ...) syntax
description: Discovered when cross-OS matrix (ug82.8) ran on the first push (commit 9439d6c). All test/lint mise tasks use `go run ...@$(cat .gotestsum-version)` for version pinning. PowerShell on Windows runners cannot expand `$(cat ...)` and fails with 'invalid version: unknown revision $(cat'. Affected tasks: test, test:integration, test:verbose, test:integration:verbose, test:coverage, lint, fmt:check. Fix: migrate from shell expansion to mise [tools] entries (e.g. 'go:gotest.tools/gotestsum = v1.13.0') so binaries are installed by mise and called directly. Removes the .gotestsum-version / .goimports-version / .golangci-version files (replaced by [tools] pins).
close_reason: mise tools migration eliminates PowerShell incompatibility; build gated to non-Windows; quality green locally.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Migrated from 'go run pkg@$(cat ver)' to mise [tools] entries (gotestsum 1.13.0, goimports 0.30.0, golangci-lint 2.1.6). Deleted 3 version files. Build step gated to non-Windows in ci.yml (bash ldflags expansion is bash-only; Windows still runs full test suite). Quality green locally. Awaiting CI verification on next push.

title: Acceptance Review: gateway-boundary test restructure
description:
## Description

Gate task for epic beads-workbench-r1ou. Verifies the restructured test architecture lands cleanly with no coverage loss and hits the stated performance targets.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] **All child implementation tasks closed**
- [ ] **Unit tier perf**: `mise run test` completes in <10s wall time on the baseline hardware (was 18.6s post-i1th)
- [ ] **Integration tier perf**: `mise run test:integration` completes in <30s wall time (was 96s post-i1th, dominated by per-test fixture rebuilds)
- [ ] **Coverage parity**: every observable behavior asserted by the old Bucket A integration tests is still asserted somewhere — either in a unit test (using `FakeBeadsGateway`) or in the contract suite (against real bd). Audit by re-reading the deleted `internal/app/model_integration_test.go` from git history and confirming each `t.Fatalf`/`t.Errorf` site has a counterpart.
- [ ] **Gateway contract coverage**: every read method on `BeadsGateway` (12 methods) is exercised by `RunReadContract`. Verify by grepping for `t.Run` blocks in the contract package and matching against the interface definition in `internal/gateway/beads/interface.go`.
- [ ] **Fake/real parity**: both `TestFakeGatewayReadContract` and `TestRealGatewayReadContract` pass. If any fake adjustments were needed during contract implementation, they were captured as fix commits or tickets — no hidden fake drift remains.
- [ ] **Mutating coverage**: all 4 write methods (CreateIssue, UpdateIssue, CloseIssue, AddComment) are exercised by mutating scenarios. Link/dep mutations are exercised by scenario 2.
- [ ] **No silent test deletions**: total test count (counting subtests where appropriate) does not drop in a way that represents lost coverage. Net count may go down due to consolidation, but the *behavioral surface* covered must not shrink.
- [ ] **Docs in sync**: `docs/TESTING.md` reflects the new tiering, fixture-sharing rules, and contract-test policy.
- [ ] **`mise run quality` green** end-to-end.

## Notes

- A small net-negative test count is expected (we're consolidating 8 app-layer integration tests into ~12 contract subtests + a couple of scenarios + unit-test ports — the actual count depends on how much existing unit coverage already overlapped).
- If the integration-tier target (<30s) is missed by a small margin, document the cause before accepting — likely either a bd subprocess that's slower than expected, or a scenario that grew beyond ~10 steps.
close_reason: All acceptance criteria verified PASS; epic ready to close.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Verifier report: all 10 ACs PASS with direct execution evidence. Unit median 0.188s (target <10s), integration median 28.6s (target <30s, 1.4s margin). Coverage map confirmed: every deleted assertion has a counterpart. All 12 read methods + 4 write methods exercised. 445 test count preserved. Quality green. Advisory only (not blocking): TestSearchModeEmbeddedFixtureInitUsesEmptyQueryFallback dominates integration tier at 13.4s — flag if it grows.

title: Update docs/TESTING.md to describe the gateway-boundary test tiering
description:
## Description

After the restructure, `docs/TESTING.md` should describe the new three-tier model clearly: unit / gateway-integration-read / gateway-integration-mutate, plus the fake/real contract suite that bridges them.

## Why

Future contributors (human and agent) need to know where new tests belong without re-deriving the architecture from the file structure.

## Instructions

1. Read the current `docs/TESTING.md`.
2. Restructure it to cover, in this order:
   - **Tiers**: unit (fast, no external deps), gateway-integration (real `bd`, behind `//go:build integration`)
   - **Where new tests go**:
     - Asserts app behavior given any gateway state? → unit, use `fakes.FakeBeadsGateway`
     - Asserts the bd CLI adapter's contract? → `internal/gateway/beads/contract/` read contract
     - Asserts a multi-step bd write workflow? → add to an existing scenario or create a new one (cap: ~3 scenarios total)
   - **Fixture rules**:
     - Reads → call `embeddedfixture.SharedFixtureRepoPath(t)`. Never mutate the shared snapshot.
     - Mutations → call `embeddedfixture.Seed(t, tb.TempDir())` for a fresh fixture.
   - **The contract test** — why it exists, what it guarantees (fake/real parity), what to do when it catches drift (fix the fake, don't weaken the assertion).
   - **Commands** — already covered, but update if any task names changed.
3. Cross-check `docs/CODING.md` and `AGENTS.md` for any stale references to integration-test patterns; update if needed.
4. Verify links from other docs still resolve.

## Files to Modify

- `docs/TESTING.md`
- (possibly) `docs/CODING.md`, `AGENTS.md` — only if they reference test patterns that changed

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `docs/TESTING.md` documents the three-tier model with a decision tree for "where does my new test go?"
- [ ] Fixture sharing rules are explicit (shared snapshot is read-only)
- [ ] Contract-test rationale is documented (parity guarantee + drift policy)
- [ ] No stale references to the deleted `TestSeedSkipsWhenToolsUnavailable` or the deleted `internal/app/model_integration_test.go`
- [ ] `mise run quality` green (docs don't break any task)
close_reason: TESTING.md describes the new architecture cleanly; stale test references fixed; quality green.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Docs updated. docs/TESTING.md fully restructured around the three-tier model (unit / read contract / mutating scenarios) with decision tree, fixture rules, and fake/real contract section. RUNTIME_UI_VERIFICATION.md updated for renamed test. CODING.md/AGENTS.md clean. test-baseline-2026-05-16.md preserved as historical artifact (explicitly pre-migration). Quality green.

title: Fix tea.Tick(60s) blocking in unit-tier tests (Bucket D, ~30s savings)
description:
## Description

Five fake-based unit tests in `internal/app/model_test.go` and `internal/app/scenario_workflow_test.go` pay 3–12s each because `runBatch` calls each tea.Cmd synchronously, including the 60s `tea.Tick` returned by `scheduleRefreshTickCmd()` inside `Model.Init()`. The block ends early (we see exactly ~3s per call) — likely because something else in the batch returns first via channel select — but the cost still dominates the unit tier.

Affected tests (~30s total wall):
- `TestModelReusableDetailToolScenarioCoversEditorAndLaunchersWithFakes` (12s)
- `TestModelBuiltInLauncherHotkeysUseLauncherService` (9s)
- `TestModelMutationResultMarksBrowseDirtyAndRefreshesOnlyActiveSurface` (3s)
- `TestModelEditIssueActionUsesEditorServiceAndUpdatesDetail` (3s)
- `TestModelEditHotkeyUsesEditorService` (3s)

## Why

These are *pure unit tests*. They should run in milliseconds, not seconds. The auto-refresh tick is irrelevant to what they assert.

## Instructions

1. There is already a test seam: `withRefreshTickScheduler(t, scheduler func() tea.Cmd)` in `internal/app/model_test.go` (around line 2772). It swaps the package-level `scheduleRefreshTickCmd` var for the duration of one test.
2. Audit the 5 affected tests: add `withRefreshTickScheduler(t, func() tea.Cmd { return nil })` at the top of each. This replaces the 60s tick with a no-op for the test's duration.
3. **Better**: investigate whether there's a more general place to install the no-op — e.g. inside `mustNewModel` for tests that don't explicitly want the tick. If yes, prefer that over per-test opt-out, but only if it doesn't break tests that DO care about the refresh tick (search for existing usages of `withRefreshTickScheduler` first to make sure those still get their custom scheduler).
4. Re-run `go test -run 'TestModelEditHotkey|TestModelEditIssueAction|TestModelMutationResultMarks|TestModelBuiltInLauncherHotkeys|TestModelReusableDetailTool' ./internal/app -count=1 -v` and confirm each test now completes in <100ms.
5. Verify no other unit test got faster *or slower* in a way that suggests we broke an intentional dependency on the tick.

## Files to Modify

- `internal/app/model_test.go` — add tick-disable calls (or refactor `mustNewModel`)
- `internal/app/scenario_workflow_test.go` — same

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Each of the 5 named tests completes in <200ms individually
- [ ] Total cold-run unit-tier wall time drops by ≥25s vs baseline (was ~18.6s post-i1th; should drop to ~6–8s after this fix on top of y0be)
- [ ] Tests that explicitly need the refresh tick (search `withRefreshTickScheduler` callers) still receive their own scheduler — no regression
- [ ] `mise run test` green
close_reason: Unit-tier tea.Tick-style blocking eliminated; all 5 named tests <200ms each; quality gate green.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Implementation complete. Root cause was actually toaster.ScheduleDismiss(3s) blocking runBatch synchronously — not scheduleRefreshTickCmd (already neutralized in TestMain). Tasker added a parallel scheduleToastDismissCmd test seam in model.go (same pattern as the existing refresh-tick seam) and neutralized it in TestMain. All 5 named tests now complete in <10ms each. Unit suite: 0.179s wall. mise run quality green.

title: Replace app-layer EmbeddedFixture integration tests with unit-tier fake-based equivalents
description:
## Description

Convert the 8 `TestModelEmbeddedFixture*` tests in `internal/app/model_integration_test.go` to unit tests using `fakes.FakeBeadsGateway`, then delete the integration versions. The gateway-contract tier (already built by the preceding tasks) ensures the fake's behavior matches real bd, so this is a safe move that does not lose coverage.

## Why

These 8 tests collectively cost ~88s of integration time. They're asserting **app behavior** (navigation, modal state, view rendering) — not bd-contract behavior. With the contract suite enforcing fake/real parity, the app behavior can be unit-tested for free.

## Instructions

For each integration test, do an audit-then-port pass:

1. **Audit** what each test actually asserts:
   - `TestModelEmbeddedFixtureBoardToDetailSmokeWorkflow` — board lands on Not Ready lane, enter opens detail with correct content
   - `TestModelEmbeddedFixtureDetailEditHotkeyUsesEditorService` — `e` key in detail invokes editor for selected issue (already uses fake editor!)
   - `TestModelEmbeddedFixtureMutationModalsOpenWithoutCatalogDecodeToast` — `c`/`u`/`a` open modals without a "decode failed" toast
   - `TestModelEmbeddedFixtureFullBoardCaptureGolden` — board view matches golden at width 120
   - `TestModelEmbeddedFixtureStartupLoadsBoardWithoutGatewaySectionErrors` — startup view has no error panels
   - (Bucket B tests handled separately by ko71/i8pt — DELETE the integration versions in this task, do NOT port)
2. **Check coverage**: for each assertion above, search `internal/app/model_test.go` and `internal/app/scenario_workflow_test.go` for an equivalent unit test using `FakeBeadsGateway`. Many already exist.
3. **Port any missing assertions** as new unit tests:
   - Use `FakeBeadsGateway` pre-seeded to match the same data shape (3 issues with bwf-* IDs, similar status mix) so any golden files transfer cleanly.
   - For the golden file (`testdata/model_embedded_board_w120.golden`): re-generate it from the fake-driven unit test if any rendering differs; otherwise the existing file is reused as-is.
4. **Delete** `internal/app/model_integration_test.go` once the audit confirms coverage parity. Keep `runBDInRepo` only if it's still used by the link/dep scenario tests (see i8pt — likely moved to embeddedfixture helper).
5. Document any assertion that genuinely cannot be expressed against the fake (likely: none, but if found, escalate before deleting the corresponding integration test).

## Files to Modify

- `internal/app/model_integration_test.go` — DELETE
- `internal/app/model_test.go` and/or `internal/app/scenario_workflow_test.go` — add ported assertions where missing
- Possibly: `internal/app/testdata/model_embedded_board_w120.golden` — regenerate if golden was bd-specific

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Each of the 6 Bucket A integration tests has a unit-test counterpart asserting equivalent behavior (or the audit explicitly justifies why one isn't needed, with rationale captured in the ticket as a comment)
- [ ] `internal/app/model_integration_test.go` is deleted
- [ ] No regression: every assertion previously made by Bucket A tests is verified in either a unit test or the gateway contract suite
- [ ] `mise run test` runs the ported tests and stays under 10s (combined with the tea.Tick fix from cc7f)
- [ ] `mise run quality` green
close_reason: App-layer integration tests migrated to unit tier with no coverage loss; audit table validated each assertion has a counterpart; quality green.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Migration complete. Audit table: 5 of 7 Bucket A assertions already covered by existing unit tests; 3 new unit tests added (TestModelFixtureShapedBoardCaptureGolden, TestModelStartupBoardLayoutSanityAndNoRuntimeErrors, TestModelMutationModalsOpenWithoutCatalogDecodeToast). Bucket B tests deleted (gateway coverage in i8pt; app-level dep rendering already covered in internal/mode/details). model_integration_test.go deleted along with its hasExecutable/runBDInRepo helpers. Unit suite: 620 tests, 0.607s wall. Integration suite: 649 tests. Quality green.

title: Mutating scenario 2: links and dependencies (folds Bucket B integration tests)
description:
## Description

Add one multi-step end-to-end test covering `bd link` and `bd dep` mutations against real bd. This consolidates the two current Bucket B integration tests (`TestModelEmbeddedFixtureDetailShowsRelatedFromRealBDRelatedLink` and `TestModelEmbeddedFixtureDetailShowsRelatesToDependentOnlyUnderRelated`) into a single gateway-tier scenario.

## Why

Both Bucket B tests today seed the fixture and then call bd directly to add a link/dep before driving the UI. Their value lives at the bd-contract layer (does the gateway expose link/dep results correctly?) — not at the UI layer (the UI rendering can be unit-tested with fakes).

## Instructions

1. Decide handling for link/dep operations:
   - The `BeadsGateway` interface does NOT currently expose link/dep *write* methods (only `bd link` / `bd dep` invoked via subprocess as test setup).
   - **Decision** (do NOT expand the gateway interface in this ticket): treat link/dep creation as **test setup helper** that calls `bd` directly via `runBDInRepo` (already exists in `internal/app/model_integration_test.go` — copy or extract to a shared helper).
   - The *read-side* coverage (i.e. `ShowIssue` includes link/dep info correctly) IS exercised through the gateway and is the load-bearing assertion.
2. Create `internal/gateway/beads/contract/links_scenario_integration_test.go` with `//go:build integration`.
3. `TestRealGatewayLinksAndDepsScenario` should:
   - Build a fresh fixture.
   - **Step 1**: `bd link bwf-2 bwf-3 --type related` (subprocess setup) → call gateway `ShowIssue("bwf-2")` and assert the result includes `bwf-3` under the related-issues field.
   - **Step 2**: On a second fresh fixture (or reset state), `bd dep relate bwf-3 bwf-2` → `ShowIssue("bwf-3")` includes bwf-2 under related, but NOT under blocks. Mirrors the existing `RelatesToDependentOnlyUnderRelated` assertion.
   - **Step 3**: `bd dep add bwf-1 bwf-2` (bwf-1 blocks bwf-2) → call gateway `BlockedIssues({})` and assert bwf-2 appears with bwf-1 as its blocker.
4. If two fresh fixtures per test add noticeable cost, gate Step 2 into a sub-test that builds its own fixture only if needed. Aim for total wall time <15s.
5. Move `runBDInRepo` to a small shared helper in `internal/testing/e2e/embeddedfixture/` if reused across scenario files — keep it package-private to the contract package if only used there.

## Files to Modify

- `internal/gateway/beads/contract/links_scenario_integration_test.go` — new
- (optional) `internal/testing/e2e/embeddedfixture/fixture.go` — extract `RunBD` helper

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Test exercises `bd link`, `bd dep relate`, and `bd dep add` setup paths
- [ ] Each setup mutation verified via the gateway's `ShowIssue` / `BlockedIssues` read
- [ ] Replaces (in behavior coverage) the two current Bucket B tests in `internal/app/model_integration_test.go` — those are deleted in a later task
- [ ] Total scenario wall time <15s
- [ ] `mise run test:integration` green
close_reason: Links/deps scenario green; bd link + bd dep relate + bd dep add all exercised with gateway read verification.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Links/deps scenario landed: BDLinkRelated + BDDepRelate + BDDepAdd as sub-tests. Wall: 6.6s (target <15s). Per-test mutable copies via SharedFixtureRepoPath (each call cp's the cached seed). Tasker corrected ticket step 3 syntax: bd dep add semantics are 'issue depends-on' so 'bwf-1 blocks bwf-2' is 'bd dep add bwf-2 bwf-1' (idempotent against the seed's existing dep). Quality green.

title: Mutating scenario 1: single-issue lifecycle (create→update→comment→close) against real bd
description:
## Description

Add one multi-step end-to-end test that exercises every write method on the gateway against real `bd`, using a dedicated (non-shared) fixture.

## Why

Mutations can't share a fixture safely. But we don't need many tests — one well-chosen lifecycle covers all 4 write methods plus catches ordering bugs (e.g. "update right after create returns stale data") that isolated tests would miss.

## Instructions

1. Create `internal/gateway/beads/contract/lifecycle_scenario_integration_test.go` with `//go:build integration`.
2. `TestRealGatewayIssueLifecycleScenario` should:
   - Build a fresh fixture (use the existing `embeddedfixture.Seed` on a `tb.TempDir()` path — NOT the shared snapshot, since we mutate).
   - Construct the real `BeadsGateway`.
   - Execute these steps in order, asserting after each:
     1. **CreateIssue** with known input → assert returned `CreateIssueResult` has an ID and matches input fields when read back via `ShowIssue`.
     2. **UpdateIssue** on the just-created issue (change title and priority) → `ShowIssue` confirms the change.
     3. **AddComment** on the same issue → `ShowIssue` confirms the comment appears.
     4. **CloseIssue** with a reason → `ShowIssue` confirms status=closed and the reason is stored.
     5. **ListIssues** after close → confirm the closed issue is excluded from the default open-only listing (sanity for cross-method consistency).
3. Assertions should be structured (field comparisons), not string-matched, for the same reason as the read contract.
4. Use `t.Setenv("BEADS_ACTOR", "fixture-user")` for determinism.

## Files to Modify

- `internal/gateway/beads/contract/lifecycle_scenario_integration_test.go` — new

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] All 4 write methods are exercised (CreateIssue, UpdateIssue, AddComment, CloseIssue)
- [ ] Each mutation is verified by a subsequent read (no "write-only" assertions)
- [ ] Total scenario wall time <15s on the baseline hardware (one fixture build + a handful of bd calls)
- [ ] Test fails clearly with a step-labeled error message when any sub-assertion fails (use `t.Logf("step N: <description>")` between steps)
- [ ] `mise run test:integration` green
close_reason: Lifecycle scenario green; all 4 write methods exercised with structured assertions.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Lifecycle scenario landed: Create→Update→AddComment→Close, each verified by ShowIssue + final ListIssues exclusion. Wall: 11.42s (target <15s). All 4 write methods covered. Quality green.

title: Implement read-method contract assertions (all 12 gateway read methods)
description:
## Description

Fill in the per-method assertion bodies inside `RunReadContract` so the suite actually exercises every read method on `BeadsGateway`. Both the fake and real factories must produce identical observable behavior.

## Why

The contract is what makes the fake load-bearing. Until the assertions are concrete, the suite is structure-only.

## Instructions

For each method, write assertions that hold deterministically against the fixture (`seed.json`: 3 issues + 1 dependency, prefix `bwf`). Replace the `t.Skip` stubs in `RunReadContract`.

Per method:

1. **HealthCheck**: returns nil error.
2. **ListIssues** (no filters): returns all 3 fixture issues, sorted by ID. Each has expected Title/Status/Type/Priority/Assignee/Labels.
3. **ListIssues** (status=open): returns only the open ones.
4. **ReadyIssues**: returns the issues with no blockers.
5. **BlockedIssues**: returns the issue with the seeded blocker, with the correct blocker ID populated.
6. **ReadyExplain**: returns a `ReadyExplainResult` whose `Ready` slice matches `ReadyIssues` output.
7. **ShowIssue** (`bwf-1`): returns the full `IssueDetail` with description, status, etc.
8. **ShowIssue** (nonexistent ID): returns an error (both impls must error consistently — assert the typed error code if present).
9. **SearchIssues** (empty query): returns all fixture issues.
10. **SearchIssues** (term from fixture title): returns the matching subset.
11. **Query** (simple expression like `status:open`): returns the open issues.
12. **CountIssues**: returns total=3 (or matches actual seed count).
13. **StatusCatalog / TypeCatalog / LabelCatalog**: each returns a non-empty slice containing at least one expected value (e.g. `open` status; the labels seeded in `seed.json`).

Use a table-driven structure where each method is one `t.Run` with the factory call inside. Assertions should compare structured fields, not string-formatted output — that's where fake/real drift hides.

If a real-vs-fake mismatch is discovered while writing assertions: **file a bug** with `bd create --type=bug` for the fake to be reconciled, then write the assertion against real bd behavior. Do NOT weaken assertions to make the fake pass — the fake is the thing under test.

## Files to Modify

- `internal/gateway/beads/contract/contract.go` — flesh out `RunReadContract`
- (possibly) `internal/testing/fakes/beads_gateway.go` — fix any fake bugs discovered

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] All 12 read methods have concrete (non-skip) assertions
- [ ] Both `TestFakeGatewayReadContract` and `TestRealGatewayReadContract` pass green
- [ ] At least 2 of the methods have "happy path + edge case" coverage (e.g. ShowIssue happy + not-found, SearchIssues empty + term)
- [ ] Any fake/real drift discovered is captured as a bug ticket and either fixed in this task or filed for follow-up
- [ ] `mise run quality` green
close_reason: All 12 gateway read methods covered with structured assertions; both fake and real green; parity contract enforced.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: All 12 read methods + sub-cases (16 subtests) green in both fake and real. One real fake/real drift discovered and fixed: ShowIssue(nonexistent) returns ErrorCodeCommandFailed (real bd exits 1), not ErrorCodeNotFound (fake was wrong). Fake gained ShowIssuesByID + SearchResultsByText opt-in maps and Statuses filtering in ListIssues. Tasker flagged that the ErrorCodeNotFound branch in read_gateway.go:241 is unreachable dead code — filed as separate bug. Quality green.

title: Add gateway contract test scaffolding parameterized over {fake, real}
description:
## Description

Create a `contract` sub-package under `internal/gateway/beads/` that exposes a single function `RunReadContract(t, factory)`. Wire it up twice: once with the fake gateway (runs in the unit tier), once with the real CLI gateway against the shared snapshot (runs under `-tags=integration`).

This is the load-bearing mechanism that lets us trust the fake at the unit tier — if the fake drifts from real bd behavior, this suite catches it.

## Why

Without parity enforcement, fakes silently develop blind spots ("real bd returns null, fake returns empty slice" etc.). One contract function exercised by both runs solves it.

## Instructions

1. Create `internal/gateway/beads/contract/contract.go`:
   - Define `type GatewayFactory func(t *testing.T) beads.BeadsGateway` (the factory returns a gateway *bound to a freshly-prepared fixture*).
   - Define `func RunReadContract(t *testing.T, factory GatewayFactory)` that runs one `t.Run` per read method. The method-specific assertions are stubbed out for now — they'll be filled in by the next ticket.
   - Method list to cover: `HealthCheck`, `ListIssues`, `ReadyIssues`, `BlockedIssues`, `ReadyExplain`, `ShowIssue`, `SearchIssues`, `Query`, `CountIssues`, `StatusCatalog`, `TypeCatalog`, `LabelCatalog`.
2. Create `internal/gateway/beads/contract/fake_contract_test.go` (no build tag):
   - `TestFakeGatewayReadContract` calls `RunReadContract` with a factory that returns a `fakes.FakeBeadsGateway` pre-seeded to match the fixture data.
   - The pre-seeding helper (`primeFakeFromFixtureSpec(t, fake)`) reads `embeddedfixture.ReadSeedSpec(tb)` and populates the fake's response fields accordingly. This guarantees the fake and real both "contain" the same data.
3. Create `internal/gateway/beads/contract/real_contract_integration_test.go` (`//go:build integration`):
   - `TestRealGatewayReadContract` calls `RunReadContract` with a factory that:
     - calls `embeddedfixture.SharedFixtureRepoPath(t)`,
     - wires `beads.NewCommandRunner` + `beads.NewCLIGateway`,
     - returns the real gateway.
4. For this ticket, leave the per-method assertions as `t.Skip("filled in by 163c-followup")`. The next ticket will implement them.

## Files to Modify

- `internal/gateway/beads/contract/contract.go` — new
- `internal/gateway/beads/contract/fake_contract_test.go` — new
- `internal/gateway/beads/contract/real_contract_integration_test.go` — new

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `RunReadContract` exists with one `t.Run` per read method (12 methods)
- [ ] Both `TestFakeGatewayReadContract` and `TestRealGatewayReadContract` compile and run (even if individual subtests `t.Skip`)
- [ ] Fake factory pre-seeds via a helper reading `seed.json` (single source of fixture truth)
- [ ] `mise run quality` green

## Out of Scope

Implementing the actual per-method assertions (covered by the next ticket).
close_reason: Contract scaffolding green; ready for hlml to fill in assertions.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Scaffolding landed. contract.go with RunReadContract + GatewayFactory type. fake_contract_test.go uses external 'contract_test' package and primeFakeFromFixtureSpec to load fake from seed.json. real_contract_integration_test.go uses SharedFixtureRepoPath. Both tests run, all 24 subtests t.Skip as designed. Quality green.

title: Build immutable read-fixture snapshot infrastructure (seed-once, copy-per-test)
description:
## Description

Extend `internal/testing/e2e/embeddedfixture/` to seed the bd fixture **once** per test process and hand each sub-test a fast filesystem copy. This is the foundation that makes the read-contract tier cheap.

## Why

Today every integration test calls `Seed()`, which forks `sh setup.sh` and pays ~10 `bd` subprocess startups (~10–12s). Reads don't mutate the repo, so they can share. Seeding once and copying the seeded `.beads/beads.db` per test brings per-test setup from ~10s to a few ms.

## Instructions

1. Add a new function `SharedFixtureRepoPath(tb testing.TB) string` to `internal/testing/e2e/embeddedfixture/fixture.go`.
   - First call per process: seed a fixture in a process-local cache dir (e.g. `os.TempDir()/bwb-embedded-fixture-cache`), guarded by `sync.Once`.
   - Each call: copy the seeded directory tree to a fresh `tb.TempDir()` sub-path and return that path.
   - The copy must include `.git/` and `.beads/` (SQLite db + any auxiliary files). Use `cp -a` via `os/exec` or `io/fs.WalkDir` + plain Go copy — pick the simplest that preserves perms.
2. Verify the snapshot/restore round-trips correctly by running `bd ready` against the cloned dir as a sanity check inside the helper (and `tb.Fatal` if it fails — defensive).
3. The existing `Seed(tb, repoPath)` function stays in place and remains the source of truth for "what is in the fixture". The shared helper internally calls `Seed` on the cache dir.
4. Add a unit test in `internal/testing/e2e/embeddedfixture/fixture_test.go` (or new file) that:
   - Skips if bd/jq/git missing.
   - Calls `SharedFixtureRepoPath` twice from two sub-tests and asserts:
     - both returned paths are distinct,
     - both contain a `.beads/beads.db`,
     - both successfully respond to a `bd query` call,
     - the second call is at least 10× faster than the first (rough perf guard).
5. The new test file should be gated behind `//go:build integration` (it forks bd).

## Files to Modify

- `internal/testing/e2e/embeddedfixture/fixture.go` — add `SharedFixtureRepoPath`
- `internal/testing/e2e/embeddedfixture/fixture_integration_test.go` — new file with the round-trip test

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `SharedFixtureRepoPath` exists, is documented, and is process-`sync.Once`-guarded
- [ ] Second call returns in <100ms on the baseline hardware
- [ ] Copy preserves `.git/` and `.beads/` so cloned repos are immediately usable by the gateway
- [ ] Round-trip integration test added and passing under `-tags=integration`
- [ ] `mise run test:integration` still green
close_reason: SharedFixtureRepoPath landed; round-trip test verified; quality gate green.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Implementation complete. SharedFixtureRepoPath added with sync.Once-guarded seed cache; second call measured at 2.3ms vs 8s cold (~3500× speedup, well under 100ms target). Integration round-trip test added in fixture_integration_test.go. mise run quality green. Note: this bd version uses .beads/embeddeddolt/ instead of SQLite beads.db — Dolt-backed; cp -a snapshot semantics still apply.

title: Restructure tests around gateway boundary: thin gateway-integration tier + unit tier on fakes
description:
## Description

Today the integration suite (95s wall, 12 tests) runs the full app stack against real `bd` — paying ~10s per test for fixture seeding and bd subprocess startup. Most of those tests assert app behavior that could equally be covered by the fake gateway in <100ms each.

This epic restructures testing around the **gateway boundary**:

1. **Unit tier** — uses `fakes.FakeBeadsGateway`. Covers app behavior. Fast (<10s for the whole tier).
2. **Gateway-integration tier** — the ONLY tests that hit real `bd`. Two subgroups:
   - **2a. Read contract** — single immutable fixture built once per package via `TestMain`, snapshot of `.beads/beads.db` copied per test. Covers every read method on the gateway against well-known data. Fast (1 seed + cheap copies).
   - **2b. Mutating scenarios** — small number (2) of multi-step end-to-end tests on dedicated fixtures: full create→update→comment→close lifecycle, and links/dependencies. Sized to be debuggable (~5–8 steps each).
3. **Fake/real parity is enforced** by a contract test suite parameterized over `{fake, real}` — both implementations run the same read test bodies and must produce identical results. This is what lets us trust the fake at the unit tier.

## Why

- Current integration cost (~95s) is dominated by per-test fixture rebuilds (~10s × 10 tests). Reads can share a fixture; mutations only need a few well-chosen scenarios.
- App-layer integration tests are *double-covered* today — most assertions could be unit tests using the fake; the integration tier should verify only the gateway's bd contract.
- Without a fake/real parity contract test, fakes silently drift from reality. The contract test closes that gap once and forever.

## Success Criteria

- [ ] All child implementation tasks complete
- [ ] Acceptance review task complete
- [ ] `mise run test` (unit tier) finishes in <10s wall time
- [ ] `mise run test:integration` finishes in <30s wall time
- [ ] Total test count does not drop (any deleted test has its coverage replicated in unit or contract tier — no silent coverage loss)
- [ ] Every method on `BeadsGateway` is exercised by at least one contract test against the real bd
- [ ] Mutating scenarios cover all 4 write methods (CreateIssue, UpdateIssue, CloseIssue, AddComment) and the link/dep mutations the app depends on
- [ ] `docs/TESTING.md` describes the new tiering (unit / gateway-integration-read / gateway-integration-mutate)

## Scope decisions (locked during planning)

- **Mutating scenarios = 2**: (a) single-issue lifecycle (create→update→comment→close), (b) links and dependencies (folds the two Bucket B tests). Each ~5–8 steps. Tunable later if a real coverage gap emerges.
- **Read fixture lives at `internal/testing/e2e/embeddedfixture/`** (extend existing package). One seed per `TestMain`, snapshot `.beads/beads.db` SQLite file, `cp` per sub-test.
- **Contract test suite at `internal/gateway/beads/contract/`** — parameterized over fake+real factories.
- **Tea.Tick blocking issue in unit tests** (Bucket D, ~30s) is in-scope as a separate task — it's orthogonal but blocks the <10s unit target.
- **`process_runner_integration_test.go` sleep** (2s, Bucket E) is in-scope.
- **Out-of-scope**: the launcher integration tests stay where they are (real subprocesses, not bd).

## Reference

Cold-run baseline measured today (post-i1th):
- Unit: ~18.6s (target after this epic: <10s)
- Integration: ~96s (target: <30s)
- Top expensive tests are inventoried in the chat thread that produced this epic.
close_reason: Test architecture restructured around gateway boundary. Unit tier 0.188s (53× under target). Integration tier 28.6s (was 95s pre-epic, ~70% reduction). Fake/real parity contract enforced. 445 behavioral tests preserved. Quality green.

title: Acceptance Review: CI/build hardening
description:
## Description

End-to-end manual verification that the full epic (`beads-workbench-ug82`) actually delivers the intended outcomes. Run after all implementation children are closed.

**Blocked by**: ug82.1, ug82.2, ug82.3, ug82.5, ug82.6, ug82.8, ug82.9

## Verification Steps

### 1. Local build observability (covers .1, .3)
```
mise run build
./bwb --version
mise current go
```
- [ ] `bwb --version` shows Version (from `git describe`), Commit (short hash), and Date (UTC ISO8601) — all populated, none `dev`/`unknown`
- [ ] `mise current go` reports `1.26.3`

### 2. Test discipline (covers .2)
```
mise run test
```
- [ ] Output mentions race detector (or test fails with a race report if one exists)
```
mise run test:integration
```
- [ ] Same — race active

### 3. Quality gates (covers .5, .6)
```
mise run fmt:check
mise run test:coverage
```
- [ ] Both succeed on the current tree
- [ ] Deliberately misformat a file → `mise run fmt:check` fails → revert
- [ ] CI on a fresh PR shows: fmt:check step, coverage gate step

### 4. Cross-OS matrix (covers .8)
On any open PR or recent main commit:
- [ ] CI run shows three parallel jobs: ubuntu-latest, macos-latest, windows-latest
- [ ] All three green (or windows failures have linked follow-up issues per the .8 acceptance criteria)

### 5. Release supply chain (covers .9)
Cut a snapshot or test tag:
```
goreleaser release --snapshot --clean
ls dist/
```
- [ ] Sees `*.sbom` (SPDX-JSON) files alongside archives
- [ ] Sees `*.sig` / `*.pem` for the checksum file
- [ ] README has verification snippet documented

## Sign-off

- [ ] All 5 verification sections complete
- [ ] Any follow-up issues filed for known gaps are linked from this task
- [ ] Update epic `beads-workbench-ug82` success-criteria checklist accordingly
close_reason: All locally-verifiable ACs PASS; deferred items are infrastructure-gated (push/install/tag) per ticket allowance.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Verifier report: 22 PASS / 0 FAIL / 3 DEFERRED. All locally-testable ACs pass: bwb --version shows v0.4.0-36-gd9f662c-dirty (commit + date), Go 1.26.3, -race active in all 4 test tasks, fmt:check + coverage gate verified pass/fail, CI matrix correctly configured + mise-action@v4, .goreleaser.yaml validated by goreleaser check, signs/sboms blocks correct, no COSIGN_EXPERIMENTAL, README has verify-blob example. Deferred: (D1) cross-OS CI run needs push, (D2) cosign+syft snapshot needs those tools installed, (D3) signed tag release needs tag push. All deferred items are infrastructure-gated, not implementation gaps.

title: Cross-OS test matrix (ubuntu + macos + windows)
description:
## Description

Expand the CI test job to a matrix across ubuntu, macos, and windows. bwb is a TUI — terminal handling, file paths, and shell behavior differ enough across OSes that a single-OS suite misses real bugs.

**Note**: bwb only **ships** to linux + darwin (per `.goreleaser.yaml`). This task only adds **testing** on windows — it does NOT add windows release builds. Catching windows-specific bugs in CI helps Linux/macOS users too (path separators, line endings, environment quirks).

## Files to Modify

- `.github/workflows/ci.yml` — convert `quality-gates` job to a matrix over `os`

## Instructions

1. Replace the single `runs-on: ubuntu-latest` with a matrix:
   ```yaml
   quality-gates:
     name: Validate scripts, lint, build, vet, and test
     runs-on: ${{ matrix.os }}
     strategy:
       fail-fast: false
       matrix:
         os: [ubuntu-latest, macos-latest, windows-latest]
   ```
2. Some steps are platform-incompatible — guard them:
   - `mise run scripts` runs `bash -n` on a shell script; gate this step with `if: matrix.os != 'windows-latest'`
   - `mise run lint` and `mise run guardrails` should be ubuntu-only (slow, deterministic, no OS-specific value) — gate with `if: matrix.os == 'ubuntu-latest'`
   - Build / vet / unit tests / integration tests run everywhere
   - The coverage gate (from `.5`) is ubuntu-only — gate accordingly
3. Verify `jdx/mise-action@v2` supports windows; if not, swap to `actions/setup-go@v5` with `go-version-file: go.mod` for windows.
4. Expect first windows run to surface ≥1 path-separator or fixture issue. Triage: either fix in this PR if trivial, or file a follow-up and skip the failing test on windows with a TODO comment linking the follow-up issue.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] CI matrix runs on ubuntu-latest, macos-latest, windows-latest
- [ ] `fail-fast: false` is set (one OS failing doesn't kill the others)
- [ ] Build + vet + unit tests + integration tests pass on all three OSes (or windows-specific failures are explicitly skipped with linked follow-up issues)
- [ ] Lint/guardrails/coverage remain ubuntu-only
close_reason: CI matrix configured; mise-action bumped to v4 for Windows compat; local quality green; CI verification on push.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: ci.yml converted to matrix [ubuntu, macos, windows] with fail-fast: false. Gating: scripts/fmt:check skip windows (bash heredocs); lint/guardrails/test:coverage ubuntu-only. jdx/mise-action bumped @v2 → @v4 preemptively (Windows shim fix landed after v2 tagged — verified release dates). Local mise run quality green. Actual cross-OS verification deferred to first push.

title: Add goimports check to CI
description:
## Description

bwb has a `pre-commit` hook in `scripts/git-hooks/pre-commit` that auto-runs `gofmt` on staged files, but **it only protects contributors who ran `mise run hooks:install`**. New contributors (or anyone using a GUI Git client that skips hooks) can land unformatted code. Add a CI check that mirrors what the hook does, plus `goimports` (since the hook only runs `gofmt`).

## Files to Modify

- `.mise.toml` — add a `fmt:check` task (read-only goimports verification)
- `.github/workflows/ci.yml` — add a step that runs `fmt:check`
- `scripts/git-hooks/pre-commit` — extend to run `goimports` on staged files (preserve staged-file scoping)

## Instructions

1. Add to `.mise.toml`:
   ```toml
   [tasks."fmt:check"]
   description = "Check formatting (goimports + go mod tidy)"
   run = """
   go install golang.org/x/tools/cmd/goimports@latest
   OUT=$(goimports -local github.com/hk9890/beads-workbench -l .)
   if [ -n "$OUT" ]; then
     echo "Files not formatted:"; echo "$OUT"
     echo "Run: goimports -local github.com/hk9890/beads-workbench -w ."
     exit 1
   fi
   go mod tidy
   if ! git diff --exit-code go.mod go.sum; then
     echo "go.mod/go.sum not tidy. Run 'go mod tidy' and commit."
     exit 1
   fi
   """
   ```
2. In `.github/workflows/ci.yml`, add a step before "Lint":
   ```yaml
   - name: Format check
     run: mise run fmt:check
   ```
3. Update `scripts/git-hooks/pre-commit` to run `goimports` (in addition to or instead of `gofmt`) for consistency between local and CI. **Important:** preserve the existing staged-file scoping — operate only on files listed by `git diff --cached --name-only -z --diff-filter=ACMR -- '*.go'`, NOT the whole tree. The current hook uses xargs over a staged-files list (`scripts/git-hooks/pre-commit:20-26`); the new step should follow the same pattern. Note also: `goimports` must be installed for the hook to work; either install it at hook-install time or vendor it via `go run golang.org/x/tools/cmd/goimports@latest` (slower per commit, no install required).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `mise run fmt:check` exists and passes on a clean tree
- [ ] CI fails on a PR that introduces unformatted code (verify with a deliberate one-line breakage in a draft PR, then revert)
- [ ] `pre-commit` hook also runs `goimports` on staged files (not the whole tree)
- [ ] `go mod tidy` drift caught by CI
close_reason: goimports + go mod tidy enforced in CI + pre-commit; format violation detection verified; quality green.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: fmt:check task added (uses .goimports-version v0.30.0 pin, matching .golangci-version / .gotestsum-version pattern). CI step 'Format check' added before Lint. Pre-commit hook extended to run goimports on staged files only (preserves existing staged-list scoping). Side effect: 29 Go files had their import groups normalized for -local grouping. Negative test verified: intentional violation triggers FAIL. Quality green.

title: Add coverage gate to CI
description:
## Description

Enforce a coverage floor in CI so we don't silently regress. Initial threshold comes from `beads-workbench-ug82.4` (baseline measurement), set ~5pts below current. Plan is to ratchet up over time, like dtctl does (50 → 55 → 60 → 70).

**Scope:** gates on **unit+integration combined coverage** (per ug82.4 scope decision) — `-tags=integration` is included in the test invocation. This matches reality (integration tests cover the gateway↔bd boundary, a meaningful chunk).

**Blocked by `beads-workbench-ug82.4`** — do not set a numeric threshold until the baseline is measured. Issue is `blocked` + labeled `has:open-questions` until `.4` resolves the threshold value.

## Files to Modify

- `.mise.toml` — add `test:coverage` task (mirrors dtctl's `make test-coverage`, but includes integration)
