- `.github/workflows/ci.yml` — add a coverage step after "Integration tests", uses `COVERAGE_THRESHOLD` env var

## Instructions

1. Read the baseline + recommended threshold from the resolved `.4` task.
2. Add to `.mise.toml`:
   ```toml
   [tasks."test:coverage"]
   description = "Run unit+integration tests and enforce coverage threshold"
   run = """
   go test -race -tags=integration -coverprofile=coverage.out -covermode=atomic ./...
   COVERAGE=$(go tool cover -func=coverage.out | grep total | awk '{print $3}' | sed 's/%//')
   COVERAGE_INT=${COVERAGE%.*}
   echo "Total coverage: ${COVERAGE}% (threshold: ${COVERAGE_THRESHOLD:-XX}%)"
   if [ "$COVERAGE_INT" -lt "${COVERAGE_THRESHOLD:-XX}" ]; then
     echo "FAIL: below threshold"; exit 1
   fi
   """
   ```
   (Replace `XX` with the value from `.4`.)
3. Edit `.github/workflows/ci.yml`:
   - Add `env: COVERAGE_THRESHOLD: XX` at the top of the workflow
   - Add a "Coverage gate" step after "Integration tests" that runs `mise run test:coverage`. (Alternatively, replace the existing unit+integration test steps with `test:coverage` since it supersedes them — your call based on signal clarity in the CI run summary.)
4. Run locally: `COVERAGE_THRESHOLD=XX mise run test:coverage` — must pass.
5. Push to a branch, watch the CI run.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `mise run test:coverage` enforces the threshold locally on unit+integration combined
- [ ] CI fails when combined coverage drops below threshold (verify by temporarily deleting a test, or by setting threshold artificially high in a draft PR — then restore)
- [ ] CI passes with coverage at or above threshold
- [ ] `COVERAGE_THRESHOLD` env var documented inline (comment) for future ratcheting
close_reason: Coverage gate at 71% combined; CI updated; verified pass/fail behavior; quality green.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Coverage gate added at 71% threshold (76.5% combined baseline). New mise task test:coverage runs combined unit+integration tests with -race + coverage profile. CI workflow replaces 'Integration tests' step with 'Tests + coverage gate' (same suite, plus gate, saves ~30s). Documented threshold env var inline for future ratcheting. Verified: pass at 71%, FAIL at 99%. Quality green.

title: Spike: measure coverage baseline
description:
## Description

Timeboxed (~30 min) investigation. Before we set a CI coverage gate, we need to know the current baseline so the gate is calibrated correctly (~5pts below current, so it ratchets up rather than blocks day-1).

**Scope decision:** measure BOTH unit-only and unit+integration coverage. For bwb, integration tests cover the gateway↔bd boundary (a meaningful chunk of behavior). ug82.5 will gate on **unit+integration combined** so the threshold reflects real coverage rather than under-counting.

## Instructions

1. Run unit-only baseline locally:
   ```
   go test -race -coverprofile=coverage-unit.out -covermode=atomic ./...
   go tool cover -func=coverage-unit.out | tail -1
   ```
2. Run combined unit+integration baseline:
   ```
   go test -race -tags=integration -coverprofile=coverage-all.out -covermode=atomic ./...
   go tool cover -func=coverage-all.out | tail -1
   ```
3. Capture both total coverage percentages.
4. Identify the 3-5 packages with the lowest coverage from the combined run:
   ```
   go tool cover -func=coverage-all.out | sort -k3 -n | head
   ```
5. **Update this task's notes** with:
   - Unit-only baseline %
   - Combined unit+integration baseline %
   - Recommended initial gate (combined baseline minus ~5pts, rounded down — this is what ug82.5 will enforce)
   - Top low-coverage packages so we know what's intentionally untested vs. a gap
6. Hand the recommended gate value (and the combined-scope decision) to `beads-workbench-ug82.5`.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Unit-only baseline % recorded in notes
- [ ] Unit+integration combined baseline % recorded in notes
- [ ] Recommended initial gate % (from combined) recorded in notes
- [ ] Top low-coverage packages listed in notes
close_reason: Coverage baseline captured (unit 75.6%, combined 76.5%); recommended gate 71%; doc written; ug82.5 unblocked.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Baseline measured: unit 75.6%, combined unit+integration 76.5%. Recommended gate for ug82.5: 71% (76.5 - 5pts, rounded down) on combined profile. Top low-coverage packages: internal/testing/fakes 28.5% (test infra), internal/ui/modal 43.6% (real gap), internal/testing/ui 52.4% (test helpers), internal/testing/e2e/embeddedfixture 60.0%, internal/ui/styles 61.0%, internal/gateway/beads/contract 62.2%. Full baseline in docs/coverage-baseline-2026-05-16.md.

title: Inject Version/Commit/Date via ldflags for all builds
description:
## Description

Today `bwb --version` only shows a useful version string for tagged releases (goreleaser injects `main.version`). Local builds (`mise run build`, `go build ./cmd/bwb`) show the default. We also lose Commit and Date entirely. Adopt dtctl's pattern: a dedicated `version` package with `Version`, `Commit`, `Date` vars injected via `-ldflags` everywhere — local mise build, CI build, and goreleaser.

## Files to Modify

- `cmd/bwb/main.go` — currently defines `var version = "dev"` at line 21; used at line 92 (`--version` print) AND line 114 (`BuildVersion: version` passed to logger). Both call sites must switch to read from the new package.
- New file: `internal/version/version.go` — package with `Version`, `Commit`, `Date` vars (default `"dev"`/`"unknown"`)
- `.mise.toml` — `build` task: add ldflags injecting `git describe`, `git rev-parse --short HEAD`, and UTC date
- `.goreleaser.yaml` — update `ldflags:` line to set all three vars (currently only `main.version`)
- Any test that asserts on `--version` output (the output format will change — see step 5 below)

## Instructions

1. Create `internal/version/version.go`:
   ```go
   package version

   var (
       Version = "dev"
       Commit  = "unknown"
       Date    = "unknown"
   )
   ```
2. Refactor `cmd/bwb/main.go`:
   - Remove the local `var version = "dev"` at line 21.
   - Replace the `--version` print at line 92 with a read from `internal/version.Version` (or render Version+Commit+Date — see step 5).
   - Replace `BuildVersion: version` at line 114 with `BuildVersion: version.Version`.
   - Import the new `internal/version` package (alias as `bwbversion` if it clashes with anything).
3. Update `.mise.toml` `build` task — model on dtctl's makefile (lines 3-10):
   ```toml
   [tasks.build]
   description = "Build the bwb binary"
   run = """
   VERSION=$(git describe --tags --always --dirty 2>/dev/null || echo dev)
   COMMIT=$(git rev-parse --short HEAD 2>/dev/null || echo unknown)
   DATE=$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)
   go build -ldflags "-s -w \
     -X github.com/hk9890/beads-workbench/internal/version.Version=$VERSION \
     -X github.com/hk9890/beads-workbench/internal/version.Commit=$COMMIT \
     -X github.com/hk9890/beads-workbench/internal/version.Date=$DATE" \
     ./cmd/bwb
   """
   ```
4. Update `.goreleaser.yaml` `ldflags:` block. Replace the existing single-line ldflags with (model on `../dtctl/.goreleaser.yaml:24-28`):
   ```yaml
   ldflags:
     - -s -w
     - -X github.com/hk9890/beads-workbench/internal/version.Version={{ .Version }}
     - -X github.com/hk9890/beads-workbench/internal/version.Commit={{ .ShortCommit }}
     - -X github.com/hk9890/beads-workbench/internal/version.Date={{ .Date }}
   ```
5. Update the `--version` output to show all three values. Current format: `bwb 0.3.1`. New format: `bwb 0.3.1 (commit abc1234, built 2026-05-16T14:23:00Z)`. **This is a public output format change** — search for tests that match on `--version` output (e.g., `grep -rn '"bwb "' cmd/bwb/*_test.go internal/`) and update them.
6. Build locally: `mise run build && ./bwb --version`. Should show all three values populated (not `dev`/`unknown`).
7. (Optional) Verify goreleaser pickup: `goreleaser release --snapshot --clean` — requires goreleaser installed locally. If not installed, defer to ug82.9 verification.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `internal/version` package exists with `Version`, `Commit`, `Date`
- [ ] `cmd/bwb/main.go` no longer holds a local `version` var; both line-92 (print) and line-114 (logger `BuildVersion`) read from `internal/version`
- [ ] `mise run build` produces a binary whose `--version` shows all three values populated (not `dev`/`unknown`)
- [ ] `.goreleaser.yaml` injects all three via template vars
- [ ] Any existing test that asserts on `--version` output is updated
- [ ] A goreleaser snapshot build (`goreleaser release --snapshot --clean`) produces a binary with all three populated (or, if goreleaser is not installed locally, verify via CI on a draft release branch)
close_reason: Version/Commit/Date injected via ldflags across mise + goreleaser; --version shows all three fields for tagged builds; defaults are non-failing.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Implementation complete. New internal/version package with Version/Commit/Date (defaults dev/unknown). bwb --version after mise run build: 'bwb v0.4.0-36-gd9f662c-dirty (commit d9f662c, built 2026-05-16T13:09:45Z)'. Default build shows 'bwb dev (commit unknown, built unknown)' — no failure. .goreleaser.yaml + .mise.toml both inject the three fields. Tests updated to use bwbversion.Version (removed versionMu since the var is now in a package that tests don't mutate). Quality green.

title: Enable -race in test tasks
description:
## Description

Run unit and integration tests with `-race` so the data-race detector catches concurrency bugs the current suite cannot see. bwb has goroutine-driven loading, refresh schedulers, and UI ticks — exactly the surface where race issues hide.

## Files to Modify

- `.mise.toml` — add `-race` to the `test`, `test:integration`, `test:verbose`, and `test:integration:verbose` task `run` commands

## Instructions

1. For each test task in `.mise.toml`, insert `-race` into the `gotestsum -- ...` flags. Example for `test`:
   ```
   run = "go run gotest.tools/gotestsum@$(cat .gotestsum-version) --format pkgname-and-test-fails -- -race ./..."
   ```
2. Run `mise run test` and `mise run test:integration` locally. Confirm both still pass and the runtime increase is acceptable (likely <30s for the current suite).
3. If a real race surfaces, file a follow-up task instead of suppressing it.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] All four `test*` tasks in `.mise.toml` pass `-race` through to `go test`
- [ ] `mise run test` passes locally
- [ ] `mise run test:integration` passes locally
- [ ] CI `ci.yml` "Unit tests" and "Integration tests" steps green
close_reason: -race enabled in all 4 test tasks (with task-level CGO_ENABLED=1); both test tiers green under -race; targets met.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: .mise.toml changes correct (added -race + CGO_ENABLED=1 task overrides to all 4 test tasks). Verification revealed 3 pre-existing races, all already tracked: Race A in cmd/bwb (zgvc #1, global main.version), Race B in internal/app (0ny3, withRefreshTickScheduler global), Race C in internal/ui/shared/markdown (zgvc #3, renderer global). Closing ug82.2 deferred until 0ny3 + zgvc are fixed. Working tree retains the .mise.toml change.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Unblocked by 0ny3 + zgvc fixes. Verified: mise run test cold 2.2s with -race, mise run test:integration cold 29.3s with -race. Both within targets (<10s / <30s). Quality green.

title: Bump Go toolchain 1.24.2 → 1.26.3
description:
## Description

Update the Go toolchain pin from 1.24.2 to 1.26.3 (matches dtctl's pin and current upstream stable). bwb already manages Go via `mise`, so this is a one-line bump plus a verification pass.

## Files to Modify

- `.mise.toml` — change `go = "1.24.2"` to `go = "1.26.3"`
- `go.mod` — bump the `go` directive (and `toolchain` directive if present) to `1.26` (or whatever matches the pinned version's minor); run `go mod tidy`

## Instructions

1. Edit `.mise.toml` line 2.
2. Run `mise install` (or trust that CI's `jdx/mise-action@v2` will install the new version automatically).
3. Edit `go.mod` `go` directive to the matching minor (`go 1.26`). If a `toolchain` line exists, update or remove it.
4. Run `go mod tidy`.
5. Run `mise run quality` (full gate including integration tests). Fix any new vet/staticcheck findings introduced by the newer toolchain.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `.mise.toml` pins `go = "1.26.3"`
- [ ] `go.mod` `go` directive matches
- [ ] `mise run quality` passes locally
- [ ] CI (`ci.yml`) green on the PR
close_reason: Go toolchain bumped to 1.26.3 in .mise.toml + go.mod. Full quality gate green (vet, lint=0 issues, guardrails, 601 unit, 613 integration). Pushed in ad2e372.

title: CI/build hardening — adopt patterns from dtctl
description:
## Description

Adopt the CI, build, and supply-chain patterns from the sister project `dtctl` (../dtctl) that make sense for bwb's stage and trajectory. Today bwb's CI is lean: ubuntu-only matrix, no race detector, no coverage gate, no release signing, no SBOMs, and `bwb --version` only carries the version string at release time (no Commit/Date, nothing for local builds).

This epic ships these as small independent child tasks, ordered by payoff vs. effort. Each child is scoped tightly enough to land in one PR.

## Motivation

1. **Reliability**: race detector catches concurrency bugs the current suite cannot see.
2. **Observability**: `bwb --version` should reveal commit + build date for any binary — local, CI, or release — so bug reports are debuggable.
3. **Quality ratchet**: a coverage floor (set just below the current baseline) prevents regressions without forcing a write-tests-under-duress sprint.
4. **Portability**: macOS users hit bwb today. A matrix prevents "works on my laptop" regressions, especially around TUI/terminal handling.
5. **Supply chain (when distributing more widely)**: cosign keyless signing + syft SBOMs make releases verifiable. Deferred until distribution broadens.

## Approach

Split into 8 implementation children + 1 acceptance review, roughly ordered by payoff and dependency:

1. **`.1` — Go 1.24.2 → 1.26.3** (foundation, tiny diff; blocks .2/.6/.8)
2. **`.2` — `-race` in test tasks** (foundation; depends on .1)
3. **`.3` — ldflags Version/Commit/Date** (build observability)
4. **`.4` — Measure coverage baseline** (spike — informs `.5`)
5. **`.5` — Coverage gate in CI** (blocked by `.4`; gates on unit+integration combined)
6. **`.6` — goimports check in CI** (closes pre-commit-hook gap for contributors without `hooks:install`; depends on .1)
7. **`.8` — Cross-OS test matrix (ubuntu + macos + windows)** (depends on .1)
8. **`.9` — Release signing: cosign keyless + syft SBOMs** (last; meaningful once distribution broadens)
9. **`.10` — Acceptance Review** (manual verification across all changes)

**Removed from scope:** `.7` (govulncheck weekly cron) — descoped at user request; can revisit when contributor base grows.

## Reference

dtctl reference files for each pattern:
- Race + coverage: `../dtctl/.github/workflows/test.yml`, `../dtctl/makefile` (`test`, `test-coverage`)
- Build ldflags: `../dtctl/makefile` lines 3-10, `../dtctl/.goreleaser.yaml` lines 24-28
- goimports CI: `../dtctl/.github/workflows/lint.yml` lines 22-32
- Cross-OS matrix: `../dtctl/.github/workflows/test.yml` lines 18-21
- Cosign + syft: `../dtctl/.github/workflows/release.yml` lines 27-31, `../dtctl/.goreleaser.yaml` lines 49-65

## Out of Scope

Explicitly NOT adopting from dtctl:
- SDK-style dual-module split (bwb is single-module)
- Golden / snapshot tests (no stable formatted-output surface to snapshot)
- markdownlint Docker target (low value at current docs size)
- Homebrew tap publishing (no tap exists yet)
- govulncheck (descoped — was `.7`)

## Success Criteria

- [ ] All 8 implementation children closed (`.1`, `.2`, `.3`, `.4`, `.5`, `.6`, `.8`, `.9`)
- [ ] Acceptance review (`.10`) closed with: locally-built `bwb --version` shows version+commit+date; CI runs race + coverage gate + goimports + tests on ubuntu+macos+windows; tagged release produces signed binaries + SBOMs
close_reason: CI/build hardening epic complete: Go 1.26.3, -race + 4 race fixes, ldflags Version/Commit/Date, coverage gate at 71%, goimports + go mod tidy CI check, cross-OS matrix (mise@v4), cosign signing + syft SBOMs. 22 ACs locally verified; 3 deferred to CI push / release tag.

title: Capture pre-migration test baseline (count + wall time)
description:
## Description

The epic's success criteria reference relative reductions ("<15s", ">80% drop") but no baseline measurement exists. Without a baseline, the acceptance review (i1th.1) and the build-tag ticket (i1th.2) have nothing concrete to compare against.

This ticket captures the baseline numbers BEFORE any other epic work runs. Pre-resolves the empirical questions that would otherwise leak into multiple tickets.

Reviewer finding F29. Blocks i1th.1 (acceptance review) and i1th.2 (build tag).

## Files to Modify

- Create `docs/test-baseline-2026-05-16.md` (or update an existing doc) capturing the numbers — short, just data.

## Instructions

1. **Clean cache:**
   ```bash
   go clean -testcache
   ```

2. **Capture total test count:**
   ```bash
   go test -json ./... 2>/dev/null \
     | jq -r 'select(.Action=="pass" or .Action=="fail") | select(.Test != null and (.Test | contains("/") | not)) | .Package + "::" + .Test' \
     | sort -u | wc -l
   ```
   Record the count.

3. **Capture total wall time** (3 cold runs, take the median):
   ```bash
   for i in 1 2 3; do go clean -testcache; time go test ./... -timeout=180s >/dev/null 2>&1; done
   ```
   Record the 3 wall times and the median.

4. **Capture per-test breakdown for the 9 integration suspects:**
   ```bash
   go clean -testcache
   go test -json ./... -timeout=180s 2>/dev/null \
     | jq -r 'select(.Action=="pass" or .Action=="fail") | select(.Elapsed != null and .Elapsed > 1) | "\(.Elapsed)s  \(.Package)::\(.Test)"' \
     | sort -rn | head -20
   ```
   Record the list.

5. **Write `docs/test-baseline-2026-05-16.md`** with: total count, median wall time, the top-N slowest tests table, hardware fingerprint (`uname -srm`, Go version from `go version`).

6. Commit the baseline doc as part of this ticket.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `docs/test-baseline-2026-05-16.md` exists and contains: total test count, 3 wall-time measurements + median, top-20 slowest tests, hardware/Go fingerprint
- [ ] The integration ticket (i1th.2) can reference a concrete pre-split count for its coverage-parity AC
- [ ] The acceptance review (i1th.1) can compute "80% drop" against a concrete baseline number
close_reason: Baseline doc committed (3a70d5a); 445 tests, 93.4s median.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Baseline captured and committed (3a70d5a). 445 tests, 93.4s median (cold cache, 3 runs: 96.2/92.3/93.4 s). Top-3 slowest: TestModelEmbeddedFixtureBoardToDetailSmokeWorkflow 13.7s, TestSearchModeEmbeddedFixtureInitUsesEmptyQueryFallback 13.4s, TestModelEmbeddedFixtureFullBoardCaptureGolden 13.2s. All slow tests are embedded-fixture (bd subprocess) tests in internal/app and internal/mode/search. i1th.2 '80% drop' target = below ~18.7s.

title: Preserve git-hook install path after Makefile removal
description:
## Description

The current `Makefile` has an `install-hooks` target (referenced from `docs/CHANGE-WORKFLOW.md`) that wires `scripts/git-hooks/` into the repo via `git config core.hooksPath`. When the Makefile is removed in beads-workbench-i1th.5, this functionality must NOT silently disappear.

Reviewer finding F28.

## Files to Modify

- `.mise.toml` (add a new task)
- `docs/CHANGE-WORKFLOW.md` (update the install-hooks reference — coordinate with beads-workbench-i1th.6)

## Instructions

1. Inspect the existing Makefile `install-hooks` target to capture the exact command:
   ```bash
   grep -A5 'install-hooks:' Makefile
   ```
   (Likely `git config core.hooksPath scripts/git-hooks` plus `chmod +x scripts/git-hooks/*`.)

2. Add a mise task in `.mise.toml`:
   ```toml
   [tasks."hooks:install"]
   description = "Install repo git hooks (one-time setup)"
   run = '''
   git config core.hooksPath scripts/git-hooks
   chmod +x scripts/git-hooks/*
   '''
   ```

3. Update `docs/CHANGE-WORKFLOW.md` references from `make install-hooks` → `mise run hooks:install` (coordinate with beads-workbench-i1th.6 to avoid double-edit).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `mise run hooks:install` exists and sets `core.hooksPath` to `scripts/git-hooks`
- [ ] Verified: after running the task, `git config core.hooksPath` returns `scripts/git-hooks`
- [ ] `docs/CHANGE-WORKFLOW.md` references the mise task, not the Makefile target
- [ ] No remaining grep hits for `make install-hooks` in repo
close_reason: hooks:install mise task landed; install path preserved.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: hooks:install mise task added to .mise.toml. Matches Makefile exactly (single 'git config core.hooksPath scripts/git-hooks' command; no chmod needed — hook already executable). Verified: task runs clean and 'git config core.hooksPath' returns 'scripts/git-hooks'. Doc update for CHANGE-WORKFLOW.md intentionally deferred to i1th.6.

title: Update GitHub Actions CI to use mise tasks + separate integration step
description:
## Description

`.github/workflows/ci.yml` currently runs `go test ./...` as a single step, taking ~95s and combining unit + integration. After this epic, CI uses mise as the execution layer and runs unit + integration as separate steps so failures are attributable to category and can be parallelized later. Depends on i1th.4 (mise tasks exist with `[tools] go = <version>`).

## Files to Modify

- `.github/workflows/ci.yml`

## Instructions

1. **Toolchain source-of-truth (locked by i1th.4 decision):** mise installs Go from `.mise.toml [tools]`. **Remove `actions/setup-go@v5`** entirely — do not run both, they will install different Go versions and put both on PATH.

2. **Replace the workflow step list** with the structure below. Note: `checkout` MUST remain the first step (the example below is the complete replacement, not just the new bits):

```yaml
jobs:
  quality-gates:
    name: Validate scripts, lint, build, vet, and test
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Check out repository
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Install mise (handles Go toolchain via .mise.toml)
        uses: jdx/mise-action@v2

      - name: Scripts syntax
        run: mise run scripts

      - name: Lint
        run: mise run lint

      - name: Build
        run: mise run build

      - name: Vet
        run: mise run vet

      - name: Architecture guardrails
        run: mise run guardrails

      - name: Unit tests
        run: mise run test

      - name: Integration tests
        run: mise run test:integration
```

3. **Action pinning convention.** Use `jdx/mise-action@v2` — matches the repo's existing major-pin convention (`actions/checkout@v4`, `actions/setup-go@v5`). Do not pin to a SHA unless the user instructs otherwise.

4. **Verify on a PR branch.** Push a branch with these changes, observe the workflow run, confirm each step succeeds and that "Unit tests" wall time is dramatically shorter than the previous combined step.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `actions/setup-go@v5` is REMOVED from `.github/workflows/ci.yml` (mise handles Go)
- [ ] `actions/checkout@v4` remains as the first step
- [ ] `jdx/mise-action@v2` is the second step
- [ ] Subsequent steps invoke `mise run <task>` for: scripts, lint, build, vet, guardrails, test, test:integration
- [ ] "Unit tests" and "Integration tests" are SEPARATE workflow steps (each appears in the GitHub Actions UI as its own row)
- [ ] No bare `go test ./...` invocation in the workflow
- [ ] On a PR push, all steps pass; the Unit tests step completes in <30s on the GitHub-hosted runner
close_reason: CI workflow uses mise tasks + separate integration step.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Body rewritten (reviewer F24/F25/F26/F27): removed actions/setup-go (mise installs Go via [tools] per i1th.4); included full replacement YAML with checkout as first step; pinned jdx/mise-action@v2 explicitly; dropped 'act' suggestion (not in repo toolset).

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Replaced .github/workflows/ci.yml. Removed actions/setup-go@v5; added jdx/mise-action@v2 as second step; all 7 subsequent steps delegate to mise run <task>. YAML structure matches ticket spec exactly. Final AC item (Unit tests <30s on GitHub runner) requires push to verify — CI will confirm on next PR.

title: Update docs to reflect unit/integration split + mise commands
description:
## Description

Refresh docs to reference the new vocabulary (unit / integration) and mise commands instead of bare `go test` or `make X`. Depends on i1th.4 (mise tasks exist) and i1th.5 (Makefile gone, so docs don't link to a deleted file).

## Files to Modify

- `docs/TESTING.md` — biggest update
- `docs/CHANGE-WORKFLOW.md` — quality-gate command section
- `docs/CODING.md` — Build and Test section
- `AGENTS.md` — quick-reference section (currently calls `bd` commands, may also reference `make`)
- `docs/RUNTIME_UI_VERIFICATION.md` — verify the focused-scenario commands still match (these are unit tests; no change expected but confirm)

## Instructions

1. **`docs/TESTING.md`** — update the "Test Vocabulary" section:
   - Drop the current "End-to-end / smoke tests" tier (no e2e exists)
   - Replace with a clean two-tier vocabulary: **Unit** (default, no tag) + **Integration** (`//go:build integration`, real bd + embedded fixture)
   - Update the "Commands" section to use `mise run test`, `mise run test:integration`, `mise run test:all`, `mise run test:verbose`
   - Document the build-tag pattern for new tests: "If your test forks a real subprocess, replays the embedded fixture, or otherwise costs >1s, put it in a `*_integration_test.go` file with `//go:build integration` at the top."
2. **`docs/CHANGE-WORKFLOW.md`** — replace the quality-gate command sequence with a single `mise run quality` plus a note on the individual tasks
3. **`docs/CODING.md`** — Build and Test section: replace `make build` / `make test` references with `mise run build` / `mise run test`. Note the unit-vs-integration distinction.
4. **`AGENTS.md`** — if it references `make` commands, replace with `mise run` equivalents. Add a one-line note about `mise tasks` showing the full list.
5. **`docs/RUNTIME_UI_VERIFICATION.md`** — the focused-scenario tests there are unit (no real bd via `hasExecutable` gate, they use teatest only). Confirm and leave commands as-is OR wrap in a mise task `test:runtime-ui` if it's worth a shortcut.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Two-tier vocabulary (unit + integration) is the authoritative model in TESTING.md
- [ ] No remaining references to `make <target>` in any doc file (search: `grep -RE 'make [a-z]+' docs/ AGENTS.md`)
- [ ] All "how to run X" instructions point at `mise run <task>`
- [ ] The build-tag pattern for new integration tests is documented
- [ ] No broken internal doc links (deleted Makefile not referenced anywhere)
close_reason: Docs reflect unit/integration split + mise commands; zero stale 'make' references.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: F21 (Major) — 'Files to Modify' lists 5 files but the real scope is a repo-wide sweep. Update instruction 1 to: 'grep -RnE "(make [a-z]+|go test ./\\.\\.\\.)" docs/ AGENTS.md CLAUDE.md and update every hit'. The 5 named files are the minimum expected set, not the exhaustive one.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: F22 (Minor) — Step 5 leaves runtime-ui mise-task decision open. Decision: SKIP the extra task. Runtime-ui tests are package-scoped and the -run flag invocation is what the doc teaches; an extra mise task adds surface without value.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: F23 (Minor) — AC 'no broken internal doc links (Makefile not referenced)' — Makefile isn't a link target. Replace with: 'grep -RnE "\bmake [a-z]+\b" docs/ AGENTS.md CLAUDE.md returns zero hits outside CHANGELOG.md'.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Docs updated across 6 files: TESTING.md (two-tier vocabulary, build-tag rule, mise commands, baseline ref), CHANGE-WORKFLOW.md (mise run quality + quality:fast + hooks:install), CODING.md (full task table + gotestsum/golangci-lint pin paragraph), AGENTS.md (mise tasks note), RELEASING.md (CI clarification), test-baseline.md (header note). RUNTIME_UI_VERIFICATION.md verified clean (focused unit-test invocations remain). Final grep for 'make <target>' in docs/AGENTS/CLAUDE returns zero hits.

title: Remove Makefile; rewire any remaining references to mise
description:
## Description

Per epic scope decision: mise replaces the Makefile entirely. Depends on i1th.4 (mise tasks must exist first).

## Files to Modify

- Delete `Makefile`
- Grep for remaining references and update:
  ```bash
  grep -RE 'make (build|test|vet|lint|quality|guardrails|clean|deps|install|fmt|all|os-info|help)' . \
    --exclude-dir=.git --exclude='*.pyc' --exclude-dir=node_modules
  ```

## Instructions

1. Verify mise tasks from i1th.4 cover every Makefile target. The current Makefile has: build, test, vet, lint, quality (implicit via `all`), clean, deps, install, fmt, all. Map each to a mise equivalent:
   - `make build` → `mise run build`
   - `make test` → `mise run test:all` (mise `test` is unit-only by design — be deliberate about the semantic shift; document it in the doc-update ticket i1th.6)
   - `make vet` → `mise run vet`
   - `make lint` → `mise run lint`
   - `make all` → `mise run quality` (or define a new `all` task that also builds)
   - `make clean` → add a `mise run clean` task if anyone still needs it; otherwise drop
   - `make deps` → drop (modern Go modules don't need this)
   - `make install` → add `mise run install` if needed; otherwise drop
   - `make fmt` → `mise run fmt`
2. Delete the `Makefile`.
3. Verify nothing in the repo still references `make`:
   ```bash
   grep -RE '\bmake [a-z]+\b' . --include='*.md' --include='*.yml' --include='*.yaml' --include='*.go'
   ```
   Update any matches found (point them at the mise equivalent).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `Makefile` removed
- [ ] Every previous Makefile target is reachable via a mise task (or explicitly deprecated)
- [ ] Grep for `make <target>` shows zero matches outside historical/changelog references
- [ ] Quality gate still green
close_reason: Makefile removed; all targets reachable via mise tasks.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: F18 (Major) — Mapping list omits 'validate-scripts' (→ mise run scripts) and 'install-hooks' (→ mise run hooks:install, owned by i1th.8). Add both to the mapping before deleting Makefile to avoid silent regression. Coordinate with i1th.8.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: F19 (Minor) — Step 3 grep is narrowed by --include filters that miss scripts/ and helper directories. Use a broader sweep: 'grep -RnE "\bmake [a-z]+\b" . --exclude-dir=.git --exclude-dir=node_modules' and check every hit (allow only historical refs in CHANGELOG.md).

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: F20 (Minor) — AC 'Quality gate still green' is vague. Replace with 'mise run quality exits 0'.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Makefile deleted. All 6 active targets mapped to existing mise tasks (no new tasks needed): build→build, test→test:all, vet→vet, lint→lint, validate-scripts→scripts, install-hooks→hooks:install, help→mise tasks. README.md 'Developer Convenience Targets' section replaced with 'Developer Tasks' pointing to mise run. docs/ references left for i1th.6. mise run quality exits 0.

title: Add .mise.toml [tasks] section as the execution layer
description:
## Description

Mise becomes the single execution layer for build/test/lint/quality. **Create** `.mise.toml` (file does not yet exist in the repo) with both a `[tools]` section (pinning Go) and a `[tasks]` section covering all developer commands. Depends on i1th.2 (build tag exists), i1th.3 (gotestsum version pinned).

## Files to Modify

- `.mise.toml` (NEW — does not currently exist)

## Instructions

1. **Confirm Go version** from `go.mod` (the `go 1.X.Y` directive) and use that exact version in the new `[tools]` block. This makes mise the single source of truth for the toolchain. Once this lands, beads-workbench-i1th.7 will drop `actions/setup-go@v5` from CI.

2. **Create `.mise.toml`** with this structure:

```toml
[tools]
go = "<version-from-go.mod>"  # exact value; e.g. "1.25.6"

[env]
CGO_ENABLED = "0"

[tasks.build]
description = "Build the bwb binary"
run = "go build ./cmd/bwb"

[tasks.fmt]
description = "Format Go source"
run = "go fmt ./..."

[tasks.vet]
description = "Run go vet"
run = "go vet ./..."

[tasks.lint]
description = "Run golangci-lint at pinned version"
run = "go run github.com/golangci/golangci-lint/v2/cmd/golangci-lint@$(cat .golangci-version) run --timeout=5m"

[tasks.guardrails]
description = "Architecture guardrails (fast)"
run = "go test ./cmd/bwb -run TestArchitectureGuardrails"

[tasks.test]
description = "Unit tests (fast, no external deps)"
run = "go run gotest.tools/gotestsum@$(cat .gotestsum-version) --format pkgname-and-test-fails -- ./..."

[tasks."test:integration"]
description = "Integration tests (real bd binary, embedded fixture)"
run = "go run gotest.tools/gotestsum@$(cat .gotestsum-version) --format pkgname-and-test-fails -- -tags=integration ./..."

[tasks."test:all"]
description = "Run unit + integration tests"
depends = ["test", "test:integration"]

[tasks."test:verbose"]
description = "Unit tests with full output (for debugging a hang or noisy failure)"
run = "go run gotest.tools/gotestsum@$(cat .gotestsum-version) --format standard-verbose -- ./..."

[tasks.scripts]
description = "Validate script syntax (shell + python)"
dir = "{{config_root}}"
run = '''
bash -n internal/testing/e2e/embeddedfixture/setup.sh
find scripts -maxdepth 1 -name '*.py' -print0 | xargs -0 python3 -m py_compile
'''

[tasks."quality:fast"]
description = "Fast pre-commit gate (NO integration tests; ~15s)"
depends = ["vet", "lint", "guardrails", "test"]

[tasks.quality]
description = "Full pre-handoff gate (includes integration tests; ~2min)"
depends = ["vet", "lint", "guardrails", "test", "test:integration"]
```

3. **Note on invocation pattern.** All gotestsum and golangci-lint invocations use `$(cat .X-version)` directly — no shell variable indirection — because the version files contain the leading `v` (see beads-workbench-i1th.3 F10 decision).

4. **Verify** each task:
   ```bash
   mise tasks                       # lists every task with its description
   mise run vet                     # fast smoke
   mise run test                    # unit only — confirm <15s
   mise run quality:fast            # vet+lint+guardrails+test, no integration
   mise run quality                 # full gate
   mise run test:integration        # the 9 real-bd tests
   ```

5. **`quality` vs `quality:fast` semantics** (locked by user decision):
   - `quality:fast` is the in-flight / pre-commit gate — fast, omits the ~85s integration cost.
   - `quality` is the pre-handoff gate — includes integration; required before PR landing.
   - This distinction must be reflected in `docs/CHANGE-WORKFLOW.md` updates (owned by i1th.6).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `.mise.toml` exists with `[tools]`, `[env]`, and `[tasks]` sections
- [ ] `[tools] go` matches the version in `go.mod`
- [ ] `mise tasks` lists all 11 tasks above with descriptions
- [ ] `mise run test` runs unit tests via gotestsum; completes in <15s on this machine; exits 0
- [ ] `mise run test:integration` runs the 9 real-bd tests; exits 0
- [ ] `mise run quality:fast` chains vet+lint+guardrails+test and exits 0
- [ ] `mise run quality` chains the full set including test:integration and exits 0
- [ ] First-failure short-circuit verified: introduce a temporary vet error, run `mise run quality:fast`, confirm subsequent tasks are not executed
close_reason: mise execution layer landed with all 11 tasks verified.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Body rewritten (reviewer F13/F14/F15/F17): changed 'add to' → 'Create' since .mise.toml doesn't exist; added [tools] go = <version-from-go.mod> block (resolves CI Go-install source-of-truth question in i1th.7); added quality:fast variant per user decision; resolved scripts task glob via dir=config_root + find/xargs; aligned all version-pin invocations to no-literal-v convention.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: .mise.toml created with [tools] go=1.24.2 (from go.mod), [env] CGO_ENABLED=0, and 11 tasks (build, fmt, vet, lint, guardrails, test, test:integration, test:all, test:verbose, scripts, quality:fast, quality). All mise run X commands verified: test (601 tests, ~18s), test:integration (613 tests, ~99s), quality:fast, quality. Short-circuit AC confirmed via injected vet error (~145ms fail). Pre-existing flaky TestForcedRotationProducesRotatedOutput in internal/logging noted as separate bug.

title: Adopt gotestsum as test output formatter (no global install)
description:
## Description

Default `go test ./...` output is a wall of text — failures get lost in cached OKs, no summary at the end, no clear grouping. Adopt `gotestsum` as the standard output formatter. Invoke via `go run gotest.tools/gotestsum@$(cat .gotestsum-version)` so contributors do not need a separate global install (mirrors the existing `golangci-lint` pattern with `.golangci-version`).

## Files to Modify

- `.gotestsum-version` (new)

(No doc edits in this ticket — doc paragraph for the gotestsum pin policy is owned by beads-workbench-i1th.6, to avoid mid-epic merge conflicts.)

## Instructions

1. Pick a recent stable gotestsum release (check https://github.com/gotestyourself/gotestsum/releases). Pin its version in a new file `.gotestsum-version`.

2. **Version-pin convention** — match the existing repo convention. Verify the current state:
   ```bash
   cat .golangci-version
   ```
   The repo currently uses **leading-`v` convention** (file contents like `v2.1.6`). The new `.gotestsum-version` MUST follow the same convention: file contents start with `v` (e.g. `v1.12.0`). The invocation therefore reads:
   ```bash
   go run gotest.tools/gotestsum@$(cat .gotestsum-version) --format pkgname-and-test-fails -- ./...
   ```
   Note: NO literal `v` in the invocation — the prefix lives in the file. This matches how `tasks.lint` in beads-workbench-i1th.4 will read `.golangci-version`.

3. Sanity-check a focused run:
   ```bash
   go run gotest.tools/gotestsum@$(cat .gotestsum-version) --format pkgname-and-test-fails -- ./internal/launcher/...
   ```
   Confirm: produces per-package summary AND a final `DONE` line; exits non-zero on failure; lists each failed test with package qualifier.

4. Document the default `--format` for the mise tasks: `pkgname-and-test-fails` for normal runs. A `standard-verbose` variant for debug runs is wired in beads-workbench-i1th.4 as `test:verbose`.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `.gotestsum-version` file exists with a pinned version starting with `v` (matches `.golangci-version` convention)
- [ ] `go run gotest.tools/gotestsum@$(cat .gotestsum-version) --format pkgname-and-test-fails -- ./internal/launcher/...` exits 0 with: per-package PASS/FAIL token, final `DONE` line listing total + failures count, gotestsum process exit code propagates the underlying test exit code
- [ ] Inducing a failure (`-run NoSuchTest` is fine — empty package) produces exit code != 0 and a visible "Failures" listing in the summary
- [ ] No new entries in `go.mod`/`go.sum` (gotestsum is `go run`, not imported)
close_reason: gotestsum v1.13.0 pinned in .gotestsum-version; invocation verified clean.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Body rewritten (reviewer F10/F11/F12): version-pin convention now matches existing .golangci-version (leading v); invocation uses $(cat .gotestsum-version) with no literal v prefix; doc-update paragraph removed (owned by i1th.6); AC bound to concrete signals (exit code, DONE line, Failures section).

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: .gotestsum-version created at v1.13.0 (2025-09-11 stable, leading-v matches .golangci-version convention). Focused run exits 0 with DONE summary; deliberate failure exits 1 with === Failed listing. go.mod/go.sum clean (go run, not imported). File pending bundle-commit.

title: Gate 9 real-bd tests behind //go:build integration
description:
## Description

Add `//go:build integration` to the 9 tests that fork the real `bd` binary against an embedded fixture. These are the slow tests (~10s each, ~85s total) that dominate the suite. After this change, default `go test ./...` should drop from ~95s to ~10s; the integration tests run only via `go test -tags=integration ./...`.

## Files to Modify

The 9 tests are in **5 files**:
- `internal/app/model_test.go` — 7 tests prefixed `TestModelEmbeddedFixture*`
- `internal/mode/search/model_test.go` — `TestSearchModeEmbeddedFixtureInitUsesEmptyQueryFallback`
- `internal/testing/e2e/embeddedfixture/setup_test.go` — `TestSeedSkipsWhenToolsUnavailable` (file contains ONLY this test + a local `hasExecutable` helper; full-file rename suffices, no split needed)
- `internal/launcher/process_runner_contract_test.go` — `TestExecProcessRunnerReapsChildren` and `TestExecProcessRunnerChildSurvivesParentCtxCancel` (these spawn real subprocesses; treat as integration)
- `internal/gateway/beads/runner_test.go` — `TestMissingBDDatabaseDetectionSubstringPin` (runs real bd in empty dir)

## Instructions

1. **Split files where mixed.** Most affected files contain BOTH unit tests AND integration tests. Move the integration tests into a sibling `*_integration_test.go` file in the same package with `//go:build integration` at the top:
   - `internal/app/model_integration_test.go` ← 7 EmbeddedFixture tests + their helpers (see step 2)
   - `internal/mode/search/model_integration_test.go` ← 1 test
   - `internal/testing/e2e/embeddedfixture/setup_integration_test.go` ← rename the existing `setup_test.go` (file already contains only the integration test + local `hasExecutable` helper)
   - `internal/launcher/process_runner_integration_test.go` ← 2 contract tests
   - `internal/gateway/beads/runner_integration_test.go` ← 1 test

2. **Helper movement (pre-resolved, do not re-litigate):**
   - `internal/app/model_test.go` lines 3183/3188 define `hasExecutable` and `runBDInRepo`. Both are used ONLY by the 7 integration tests being moved. **Move both helpers into `model_integration_test.go`.** Do not leave them in the unit file (would be unused → lint fail).
   - The duplicate `hasExecutable` in `internal/testing/e2e/embeddedfixture/setup_test.go` is local to that file; the file rename carries it.

3. **Verify launcher helper safety before moving the contract tests.** Run:
   ```bash
   grep -n "TestExecProcessRunnerReapsChildren\|TestExecProcessRunnerChildSurvivesParentCtxCancel\|hasExecutable" internal/launcher/process_runner_test.go internal/launcher/service_security_test.go internal/launcher/service_test.go
   ```
   If any sibling test file references a helper or constant defined in `process_runner_contract_test.go`, the move would break `-tags=integration` build. If a reference exists, surface it before moving and either duplicate the helper or move it into a tag-free helpers file.

4. **Build tag header**: each new file starts with:
   ```go
   //go:build integration

   package <pkg>
   ```

5. **Verify** both invocations:
   ```bash
   go test ./...                  # must NOT run any of the 9 tests; suite drops to ~10s
   go test -tags=integration ./... # must run the 9 tests; total time ~85-100s
   ```

6. **Coverage parity check.** Use the baseline captured in beads-workbench-i1th.8 (F29 ticket) — total test count across both invocations must equal the pre-split count.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Default `go test ./...` no longer runs any of the 9 real-bd tests
- [ ] Default `go test ./...` wall time drops by **at least 80% vs the baseline** captured in beads-workbench-i1th.8 (relative target, hardware-independent)
- [ ] `go test -tags=integration ./...` runs all 9 integration tests successfully
- [ ] Total test count (unit + integration) equals pre-split count from baseline
- [ ] No helper left behind in a unit file with no remaining caller (verify via `golangci-lint run --enable=unused`)
- [ ] Full quality gate green
close_reason: Build-tag gating landed; unit 18.3s (80% drop), integration 105s, all tests pass.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Body rewritten (reviewer F5/F6/F7/F8/F9): fixed 4→5 files count; pre-resolved setup_test.go and helper-move decisions (hasExecutable + runBDInRepo move to integration file); added launcher helper safety check; AC now uses relative 80% reduction vs baseline ticket i1th.8.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Implemented across 5 packages. 9 real-bd tests + their helpers (hasExecutable, runBDInRepo) moved to sibling *_integration_test.go files with //go:build integration headers. Files: model_integration_test.go (app + search), runner_integration_test.go (gateway), process_runner_integration_test.go (launcher), setup_integration_test.go (embeddedfixture). Orchestrator fix-up: removed dangling hasExecutable from mode/search/model_test.go (left by an earlier tasker run, would fail unit build). Measured: unit=18.3s (80.4% drop from 93.4s baseline, AC met), integration=1:45 (all 9 tests pass). Both modes build clean (go vet, go build, go build -tags=integration).

title: Acceptance Review: test stratification + mise execution layer
description:
## Description

Gate task for epic beads-workbench-i1th. Verifies the unit/integration split and mise task layer landed and produce measurable improvement.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] All child implementation tasks closed
- [ ] **Default fast**: `mise run test` (or equivalent unit-only invocation) completes in <15s wall time on this machine
- [ ] **Integration opt-in**: `mise run test:integration` runs the 9 real-bd tests (with `-tags=integration`); skips them by default
- [ ] **Coverage parity**: `mise run test && mise run test:integration` runs every test that previously ran under bare `go test ./...` — no test silently dropped. Verify by counting tests across both invocations.
- [ ] **Output quality**: gotestsum is used; failures are grouped + highlighted at the bottom of the run (visible without scrolling through cached OK packages)
- [ ] **Single entry point**: `mise run quality` runs the full pre-handoff gate (vet + lint + guardrails + unit + integration)
- [ ] **Makefile gone**: file removed from repo; no remaining references in docs or CI
- [ ] **CI updated**: `.github/workflows/ci.yml` uses `mise run` commands; a separate job step exists for integration so it can be parallelized later
- [ ] **Docs in sync**: `docs/TESTING.md`, `docs/CHANGE-WORKFLOW.md`, `AGENTS.md`, and `docs/CODING.md` reference mise commands (no stale `make X` calls)
- [ ] **Quality gate green** after the migration (full `mise run quality` passes)
close_reason: All AC verified; epic ready to close.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: F2 (Major) — '<15s wall time on this machine' is environment-specific and not binary-verifiable on CI. Use the baseline from i1th.9: assert relative drop (>80% reduction in unit-test wall time vs baseline median). The hard CI threshold lives in i1th.7's '<30s on GitHub-hosted runner' AC bullet.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: F3 (Major) — 'failures grouped + highlighted' is escapable. Replace with: 'gotestsum exits non-zero on failure AND prints a final DONE line including a Failures: N section listing pkg+test for each failure'. This matches the pkgname-and-test-fails format output.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: F4 (Minor) — Coverage-parity AC lacks the baseline number. Once i1th.9 lands, update this AC to: 'sum of test counts across mise run test + mise run test:integration equals the baseline count of N from docs/test-baseline-2026-05-16.md' (with the actual N filled in).

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Baseline (i1th.9) numbers locked: 445 tests pre-split, 93.4s median wall. AC bullets that referenced 'baseline' should compare against these values: (a) unit-test wall time must drop to <18.7s (80% reduction) on the baseline hardware, (b) sum of test counts across 'mise run test' + 'mise run test:integration' must equal 445.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Acceptance review verification complete. All 10 AC bullets PASS with direct execution evidence. Highlights: unit suite 18.635s (within 18.7s threshold = 80.1% drop from 93.4s baseline); integration suite includes all 9 real-bd tests; mise run quality passes end-to-end; Makefile + stale make references gone everywhere (docs grep zero hits); CI uses jdx/mise-action@v2 with separate unit/integration steps. AC2 wall time was very close to threshold (margin ~65ms) but passes on this run.

title: Test strategy: unit/integration stratification + mise execution layer
description:
## Description

Today there is no test stratification — every test runs under `go test ./...` regardless of cost. Result: the default quality gate takes ~95s wall time, dominated by 9 real-bd integration tests that fork the `bd` binary and replay an embedded fixture (~10s each). There is also no consistent execution layer: callers invoke `go test` directly or via the existing Makefile with no shared task vocabulary.

This epic introduces a proper test structure with two categories (unit + integration — no e2e tier since none exist today) and adopts `mise` as the execution layer.

## Why

- **Fast inner loop**: developers should be able to run unit tests in <10s during implementation, opting into the ~85s integration run only when needed.
- **Clear vocabulary**: callers (humans + agents) should not need to memorize 5 different `go test` invocations to run "the right tests".
- **Better output**: default `go test ./...` produces a wall of text; agents and humans both lose signal.
- **Single execution surface**: mise tasks become the one place to look for "how do I run X".

## Reference model

ai-config-manager (`/home/hans/dev/github/ai-config-manager`):
- Build tags: `//go:build integration` (and `e2e` — not needed here)
- Default `go test ./...` runs unit only
- Makefile wraps the tag invocations
- `.mise.toml` for tooling (Go version), task layer to be added in this epic

## Success Criteria

- [ ] All child implementation tasks complete
- [ ] Acceptance review task complete
- [ ] `mise run test` runs unit tests in <15s
- [ ] `mise run test:integration` runs the integration suite (real-bd path)
- [ ] `mise run quality` is the single entry point for the pre-handoff gate
- [ ] CI uses `mise run` commands (single source of truth)
- [ ] Makefile removed; all docs reference mise commands
- [ ] No regressions in test coverage (every test that was running before still runs in one of the categories)

## Scope decisions (locked during planning)

- Migration is the **smallest move**: add `//go:build integration` ONLY to the 9 real-bd tests. Don't reorganize directories. Don't tag unit tests explicitly.
- Mise **replaces** the Makefile entirely (no backwards-compat shim).
- gotestsum is the chosen output formatter, invoked via `go run gotest.tools/gotestsum@latest` (no global install).
- No e2e category — only unit + integration today.
close_reason: Test stratification + mise execution layer landed: 9 implementation tickets closed, AR approved, mise run test=18.6s (80% drop), mise run quality green.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: F1 (Minor) — Success Criteria duplicates child-ticket AC text (Makefile removal, CI mise wiring). Defer to child ACs to avoid drift; keep only cross-cutting outcomes (wall-time reduction, coverage parity, single quality entry, mise as authoritative execution layer).

title: In-process integration test: non-blocking refresh (Board → Search → Board)
description:
## Description

Add an in-process app-level integration test that proves non-blocking refresh works end-to-end without relying solely on PTY captures. PTY is slow and prone to timing flakes; this in-process test is cheap, deterministic, and gives every CI run a regression guard.

The test must drive the app model through a Board → Search → Board cycle and assert:
1. The Board view's output contains the same set of issue id rows BEFORE and AFTER the switch-out (data did not blank).
2. The header spinner glyph appears in the View() output during the in-flight reload.

Depends on `beads-workbench-0x36.1` (SkeletonRow), `beads-workbench-0x36.2` (Board non-blocking), `beads-workbench-0x36.3` (Search non-blocking), `beads-workbench-0x36.6` (Spinner). Blocks `beads-workbench-0x36.5` acceptance.

## Files to Modify

- `internal/app/model_test.go` (or new sibling `model_loading_refresh_test.go`) — new test per the criteria below. Use the existing fake gateway + `withSpinnerTickScheduler` + `withRefreshTickScheduler` seams so the test is deterministic.

## Instructions

1. Use the existing fake gateway and result-injection helpers in `internal/app/model_test.go` (see test setup around lines 27, 391-1070 for patterns).
2. Construct the app model, drive Init() and inject the 3 board gateway results so the board has known issue rows.
3. Capture the View() output and extract the set of issue ids visible on the board.
4. Trigger the Board → Search switch (`maybeAutoRefreshActiveSurfaceCmd` path). Capture View() output BEFORE the next gateway results arrive.
5. Assert: the captured view still contains the same set of issue id rows from step 3 (stale data is preserved).
6. Inject one spinner tick so the spinner frame advances. Assert: View() contains a glyph from the pinned `SpinnerFrames` set.
7. Switch back to Board. Inject new gateway results. Assert: View() now contains the new rows and no longer contains the spinner glyph (idle).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] New test exists in `internal/app/` (model_test.go or sibling) covering the Board → Search → Board flow
- [ ] Test asserts stale board rows remain in `View()` during the Search interlude (no blank)
- [ ] Test asserts spinner glyph from `SpinnerFrames` appears in `View()` during the in-flight reload
- [ ] Test asserts spinner glyph is absent from `View()` once idle
- [ ] Test uses `withSpinnerTickScheduler` and `withRefreshTickScheduler` seams (deterministic, no real time)
- [ ] `go test -count=10 ./internal/app/...` passes
- [ ] `go test ./...` passes
- [ ] `go vet ./...` passes
close_reason: Integration test landed and green at count=10. Stale-data preservation, spinner glyph presence/absence, and Board→Search→Board round-trip all asserted deterministically.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Implemented. New test TestNonBlockingRefreshBoardSearchBoardFlow in internal/app/model_loading_refresh_test.go. Covers all 5 acceptance criteria: cold-start board rows present without spinner; in-flight reload preserves stale rows; spinner glyph from SpinnerFrames appears after TickMsg; spinner absent at idle; Board→Search→Board round-trip preserves the original issue ids. Both withRefreshTickScheduler and withSpinnerTickScheduler installed for determinism. go test -count=10 ./internal/app/... green; full suite clean. No production files modified.

title: Foundation: header spinner + braille ticker (with test seam)
description:
## Description

Ship the animated braille spinner ticker and its header indicator. Split from the original Foundation ticket because the ticker has known interaction risk with the existing flaky tick scheduler (`beads-workbench-0ny3`); shipping it separately keeps that risk off the critical path for `0x36.2/.3/.4`.

After this task:
- Header renders a braille spinner glyph (frame from `⠋⠙⠹⠸⠼⠴⠦⠧⠇⠏`) between "Beads Workbench" and the tab bar whenever `loadingStates()` is non-empty.
- Header is width-invariant across idle/loading — idle renders a 2-cell whitespace placeholder so the tab bar does not shift horizontally.
- New `withSpinnerTickScheduler` test seam exists, parallel to the existing `withRefreshTickScheduler`, so the test suite can disable the spinner ticker.

Does NOT block `0x36.2/.3/.4` (Board/Search/Detail can land their non-blocking-refresh changes without the spinner present — they will simply have no visible refresh signal until this task lands). Blocks `0x36.5` acceptance and `0x36.7` integration test.

## Files to Modify

- `internal/ui/loading/loading.go` — add the braille frame sequence as a pinned constant. Add `NextFrame(prev int) int` and `Glyph(frame int) string` (split API per reviewer — model owns frame state; renderer is pure). Add `tickMsg` and `SpinnerTickCmd(d time.Duration) tea.Cmd`.
- `internal/app/model.go` — hold a spinner frame index on the Model. Wire a `withSpinnerTickScheduler` test seam parallel to the existing `withRefreshTickScheduler` (see `internal/app/model_test.go:3181-3186` for the pattern). Start the ticker from `Init()` via the seam (production = real tick, tests = no-op). Advance frame on `tickMsg`. In `renderHeader()` (lines 776-815), render a fixed 2-cell cell (`Glyph(frame) + " "`) between the title and the first tab when `len(loadingStates()) > 0`; when idle, emit two literal spaces so `lipgloss.Width(headerLeft)` is invariant.
- `internal/app/model_test.go` (or sibling) — tests per the criteria below, including width-invariance assertion and 10x stability run.

## Instructions

1. Pin the spinner glyph sequence as a package-level constant in `internal/ui/loading/loading.go`: `var SpinnerFrames = []rune{'⠋','⠙','⠹','⠸','⠼','⠴','⠦','⠧','⠇','⠏'}`. Expose `NextFrame(prev int) int` (returns `(prev+1) % len(SpinnerFrames)`) and `Glyph(frame int) string` (returns `string(SpinnerFrames[frame % len(SpinnerFrames)])`).
2. Add `tickMsg struct{}` and `SpinnerTickCmd(d time.Duration) tea.Cmd` returning `tea.Tick(d, func(time.Time) tea.Msg { return tickMsg{} })`. Default cadence: 100ms.
3. Add a package-global seam variable in `internal/app` parallel to the existing `scheduleRefreshTickCmd`. Name it `scheduleSpinnerTickCmd`. Default value points at `loading.SpinnerTickCmd`. Tests use `withSpinnerTickScheduler(t, noopTickCmd)` to swap it out — follow the exact pattern in `internal/app/model_test.go:3181-3186`.
4. On `app.Model`, add `spinnerFrame int`. In `Init()`, fire one `scheduleSpinnerTickCmd(100*time.Millisecond)`. On `tickMsg`, advance `spinnerFrame = loading.NextFrame(spinnerFrame)` and re-fire the tick.
5. In `renderHeader()`, between title and first tab: when `len(m.loadingStates()) > 0`, render `loading.Glyph(m.spinnerFrame) + " "` (2 cells). When idle, render `"  "` (2 spaces). The total width of the left segment MUST be the same in both cases. Use a lipgloss style that matches header text styling (e.g. muted/dim).
6. Add a unit test asserting `lipgloss.Width(rendered_header_left(loading=true)) == lipgloss.Width(rendered_header_left(loading=false))`. Add a test asserting one of the 10 pinned spinner glyphs appears in the rendered header when any scope is loading, and that none of them appear when idle.
7. Run `go test -count=10 ./internal/app/...` locally — must be green to confirm the new ticker does not amplify any existing flakes from `beads-workbench-0ny3`.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `SpinnerFrames` constant pinned in `internal/ui/loading` with exactly `⠋⠙⠹⠸⠼⠴⠦⠧⠇⠏`
- [ ] `NextFrame(prev int) int` and `Glyph(frame int) string` exported and pure (no global frame state in the loading package)
- [ ] `scheduleSpinnerTickCmd` seam exists in `internal/app` parallel to `scheduleRefreshTickCmd`; tests can swap it via `withSpinnerTickScheduler(t, noopTickCmd)`
- [ ] Header renders a spinner glyph from the pinned set when any scope in `loadingStates()` is non-empty
- [ ] Header renders 2 whitespace cells when idle; `lipgloss.Width(headerLeft)` is invariant across idle and loading (unit-asserted)
- [ ] `go test -count=10 ./internal/app/...` green (no ticker-induced races)
- [ ] `go test ./...` passes
- [ ] `go vet ./...` passes
- [ ] No behaviour change to board/search/detail beyond the header spinner addition
close_reason: All acceptance criteria met. Spinner ticker live with width-invariant header; go test -count=10 green, no flake regression.

title: Acceptance Review: Non-blocking loading UX
description:
## Description

Verify the epic outcome (`beads-workbench-0x36`) before closing. Manual end-to-end verification against the real built binary is REQUIRED — type-check + unit tests are necessary but NOT sufficient for runtime UI changes.

You MUST test all three touched surfaces (Board, Search, Detail) against a running app before this task can close. Follow `docs/RUNTIME_UI_VERIFICATION.md` section 2 (built binary + embedded fixture) and the agent-visible PTY recipes in section 2 A–C.

PTY proofs are positive-presence (assert what SHOULD be on screen), not negative-existence (assert what should NOT) — negative proofs are racy on the fast embedded fixture.

If any criterion fails: file a bug (`bd create --type=bug ...`). Do NOT close this task until the bug is closed and re-verified. Do NOT silently fix defects inside this acceptance task.

## Pinned glyphs (must match Foundation tasks)

- Skeleton: `░` (U+2591) — pinned in `beads-workbench-0x36.1`
- Spinner frames: `⠋⠙⠹⠸⠼⠴⠦⠧⠇⠏` — pinned in `beads-workbench-0x36.6`

## Verification Procedure

1. `go test ./...` and `go test -count=10 ./internal/app/...` (the count=10 catches ticker-induced races from `0x36.6`)
2. `go vet ./...`
3. Build: `go build -o /tmp/bwb ./cmd/bwb`
4. Set up the embedded fixture and run the manual checklist (`docs/RUNTIME_UI_VERIFICATION.md` section 3).
5. Run PTY captures per the criteria below using `scripts/capture_bwb_screen.py`; attach each capture's output as a comment on this task via `bd comments add`.
6. If any criterion fails: file a bug, leave this task open until the bug is closed and re-verified.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] All implementation tasks closed (`0x36.1`, `0x36.2`, `0x36.3`, `0x36.4`, `0x36.6`, `0x36.7`)
- [ ] `go test ./...` passes
- [ ] `go test -count=10 ./internal/app/...` passes (10 consecutive runs, catches ticker races)
- [ ] `go vet ./...` passes
- [ ] Built binary verified manually against the embedded fixture: board cold start shows chrome + column titles + skeleton glyphs, NOT a full-screen "Loading from gateway…" takeover
- [ ] PTY capture proof for BOARD cold start: capture asserts presence of all 4 column titles (`Not Ready`, `Ready`, `In Progress`, `Done`) AND at least one skeleton glyph `░` AND at least one spinner glyph from the pinned set, at a single checkpoint within ~200ms of startup
- [ ] PTY capture proof for SEARCH cold start: switch to search and submit a low-cardinality query; capture asserts presence of the search input row AND at least one skeleton glyph `░`
- [ ] PTY capture proof for DETAIL cold start: capture asserts presence of detail chrome (issue id row, dependencies section header, etc.) immediately after pressing ENTER on a board row — assert by POSITIVE presence of detail chrome, not absence of the loading text
- [ ] PTY capture proof for BACKGROUND REFRESH: explicit before/mid/after checkpoint flow — `checkpoint:before-switch` → `send-key:<toggle-search>` → `checkpoint:mid-switch` → `send-key:<toggle-back>` → `checkpoint:after-switch`. Each checkpoint's screen must contain the same issue id rows from the first checkpoint (data did not blank between switches)
- [ ] Header spinner glyph from the pinned set appears in PTY captures during loading; idle capture confirms the spinner cell is whitespace (header layout width does not change between idle and loading)
- [ ] No regression of `beads-workbench-db0z.7` (regression test in `0x36.4` passes)
- [ ] All discovered defects filed as separate bug issues and either closed or accepted out-of-scope by the user
close_reason: All acceptance criteria pass — verifier confirmed via PTY positive-presence assertions across board/search/detail cold start, background refresh round-trip, header spinner width invariance, and db0z.7 regression.

title: Detail: keep stale detail visible during refresh
description:
## Description

Apply the non-blocking loading pattern to the Detail surface. The detail pane is a single monolithic gateway call (`Gateway.ShowIssue()`), so per-region loading inside detail isn't meaningful — it's all-or-nothing per issue.

Today `mode/details/model.go:67` triggers a `blockingLoad` whenever `m.Loading && !m.isPreviewingTarget()`, and `details.go:98-104` returns a full-screen loading takeover. Extend the existing preview pattern (`mode/details/model.go:54-57, 362-369, 408-422 loadingPreviewDetail`) to ALL refreshes so that:

- Cold start (no prior detail loaded — `Detail.Summary.ID == ""`): render skeleton/placeholder content.
- Refresh (same issue OR a different but previously-loaded issue): keep stale Summary/Description/Comments visible; header spinner from `beads-workbench-0x36.6` is the only signal.

## CRITICAL constraints (DO NOT VIOLATE)

- **DO NOT remove the `m.detail.Loading = true` assignment at `internal/app/model.go:771`.** `loadingStates()` (`internal/app/model.go:1267-1269`) depends on it to drive the header spinner. Only the View() RENDERING changes here, not the loading-state plumbing.
- **DO NOT regress `beads-workbench-db0z.7`.** When the TARGET ISSUE CHANGES (not refresh of the same issue), scroll offsets MUST reset. Call `ApplyLoadedDetail(newID, ...)` synchronously on selection-change BEFORE issuing the new gateway call so scroll resets immediately — don't wait for the gateway response.

Depends on `beads-workbench-0x36.1` (SkeletonRow helper).

## Files to Modify

- `internal/mode/details/model.go` — change the `blockingLoad` predicate at line 67 so the full-screen takeover only triggers when `Detail.Summary.ID == ""` (no content). Rename/export `loadingPreviewDetail` (lines 408-422) to `placeholderDetail(id, parentRef)` so both cold-start of the main path and preview can share it.
- `internal/ui/details/details.go` — when `state.Loading && state.Detail.Summary.ID == ""`, render skeleton placeholder for description/metadata/dependencies via `loading.SkeletonRow`. When `state.Loading && state.Detail.Summary.ID != ""`, render the existing detail layout unchanged. Replace the current preview-path `Description: loading.View(...)` (around line 411) with skeleton via `loading.SkeletonRow`.
- `internal/app/model.go` — at the selection-change entry point (around line 770, `selectDetailForCurrentMode`), on issue-id change, synchronously call `ApplyLoadedDetail` with a placeholder BEFORE the new `loadDetailCmd`, so scroll offsets reset immediately. Add an inline comment at the `m.detail.Loading = true` line warning future readers not to remove it.
- `internal/mode/details/model_test.go` — cover the three rendering cases (cold-start skeleton, refresh keeps stale, idle) AND add an explicit regression test that scroll offsets reset on issue-switch (no-regression for `beads-workbench-db0z.7`).

## Instructions

1. Change `View()`: full-screen loading takeover ONLY when `Detail.Summary.ID == ""`. For any subsequent refresh — same issue OR different previously-loaded issue — keep the existing detail layout visible.
2. Rename `loadingPreviewDetail` to `placeholderDetail(id, parentRef)`, keep its existing behavior, and call it from both the preview path and the cold-start path so they share one placeholder shape.
3. KEEP the `m.detail.Loading = true` assignment at `internal/app/model.go:771`. Add an inline comment there: `// Required: loadingStates() reads this to drive the header spinner — do not remove.`
4. On issue-id change in `selectDetailForCurrentMode`, immediately call `ApplyLoadedDetail(newID, placeholderDetail(newID, parentRef))` BEFORE the new `loadDetailCmd` so scroll offsets reset synchronously. The gateway response will later replace the placeholder with real content.
5. Add a regression test (in `internal/mode/details/model_test.go`): load issue A, scroll content to offset N>0, switch to issue B (without waiting for gateway), assert all three scroll offsets are 0 immediately (before the new ShowIssue returns).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Detail `View()` only renders the full-screen loading takeover when `Detail.Summary.ID == ""`
- [ ] Refresh of the SAME issue keeps the previous detail content visible; header spinner is the only signal
- [ ] Switching to a DIFFERENT but previously-loaded issue keeps the previous detail visible momentarily then swaps when new data arrives; scroll offsets still reset immediately (no regression of `beads-workbench-db0z.7`)
- [ ] Cold start (no prior detail) renders skeleton content for description/metadata/dependencies via `loading.SkeletonRow`
- [ ] `loadingPreviewDetail` is renamed to `placeholderDetail` and used by both preview and cold-start paths
- [ ] `m.detail.Loading = true` is still set in the app reload path (loadingStates() still reports detail scope to the header)
- [ ] Regression test for `beads-workbench-db0z.7` scroll-reset on issue-switch passes
- [ ] `go test ./internal/mode/details/... ./internal/app/...` passes
- [ ] `go test ./...` passes
- [ ] `go vet ./...` passes
close_reason: Detail non-blocking refresh shipped. Cold start renders skeleton; refresh keeps stale; scroll-reset preserved on selection change (db0z.7 regression test passes); m.detail.Loading wiring intact.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Implementation complete. Detail View() cold-start renders skeleton via skeleton.SkeletonRow; refresh (same or different previously-loaded issue) keeps stale content visible. loadingPreviewDetail renamed to PlaceholderDetail (exported) and used by both preview and cold-start paths. Synchronous m.detail.ApplyLoadedDetail(newID, PlaceholderDetail(...)) added in ensureDetailForCurrentSelectionCmd before m.detail.Loading=true so scroll offsets reset immediately on selection change — preserves beads-workbench-db0z.7. Inline comment added on m.detail.Loading=true warning future readers not to remove. 5 new tests added including TestScrollResetOnIssueSwitchViaApplyLoadedDetail (db0z.7 regression). Full suite green.

title: Search: non-blocking refresh
description:
## Description

Apply the non-blocking loading pattern to the Search surface. The full-screen loading takeover lives in the UI layer (`internal/ui/search/search.go:68-70`), NOT the mode model. The mode model already exposes a `Reloading` field (`internal/mode/search/model.go:46`) that distinguishes refresh from cold-start.

After this task:
- Cold start (no prior results): search input + skeleton result rows (no full-screen takeover).
- Refresh (results present): stale results stay visible; header spinner from `beads-workbench-0x36.6` is the only signal.
- User can type/edit the query at any time (already true today — covered as a regression check, not new work).

Depends on `beads-workbench-0x36.1` (SkeletonRow helper).

## Files to Modify

- `internal/ui/search/search.go` — remove the early return at lines 68-70: `if state.Loading && len(state.Results) == 0 { return loading.View(...) }`. Replace with skeleton-row rendering in the result-list section.
- `internal/mode/search/model.go` — verify (do NOT remove) that `m.loading` continues to be set on reload, since `internal/app/model.go:1258-1270` (`loadingStates()`) depends on it to drive the header spinner.
- `internal/ui/search/search_test.go` or `internal/mode/search/model_test.go` — assertions per the criteria below.

## Instructions

1. Remove the loading short-circuit at `internal/ui/search/search.go:68-70`. The rest of `Render()` should produce the normal search layout (input + result list).
2. In the result-list rendering:
   - `state.Loading && len(state.Results) == 0` → render ~6 skeleton rows via `loading.SkeletonRow`.
   - `state.Loading && len(state.Results) > 0` (or `state.Reloading`) → render `state.Results` unchanged.
   - Idle → unchanged.
3. Verify (regression-style) the user can type/edit while a query is in flight — this should already be true since `handleKey` does not gate on `m.loading`.
4. Confirm `m.loading` is still set in the mode reload path so `loadingStates()` reports search activity to the header spinner.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `internal/ui/search/search.go:68-70` short-circuit is removed
- [ ] Cold start: search input visible + skeleton rows in result area (no full-screen takeover)
- [ ] Refresh: stale result rows remain visible while `Loading` / `Reloading` is true
- [ ] User can type and edit the query while a query is in flight (regression check covered by test)
- [ ] `loadingStates()` still reports `ScopeSearch` while a search is in flight (header spinner stays active)
- [ ] `go test ./internal/mode/search/... ./internal/ui/search/...` passes
- [ ] `go test ./...` passes
- [ ] `go vet ./...` passes
close_reason: Search non-blocking refresh shipped. Cold start shows skeleton, refresh keeps stale results; m.loading wiring intact for header spinner.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Implemented. Removed full-screen loading short-circuit from search Render(); cold start now shows search input + 6 skeleton rows via skeleton.SkeletonRow; refresh path keeps stale rows unchanged; m.loading setters untouched in the reload path (header spinner wiring intact). 5 new tests added: cold start, refresh, idle regression, m.loading-during-reload persistence, typing-while-loading. Scoped + full test suites pass.

title: Board: per-column loading + non-blocking refresh
description:
## Description

Remove the full-screen loading takeover from the Board surface. Replace the single `m.loading` flag with per-column loading state so:

- Cold start: header + tab bar + 4 column frames render immediately, each column shows skeleton rows.
- Refresh: stale rows stay on screen; the only signal is the global header spinner from `beads-workbench-0x36.6`.
- Atomic swap: when the LAST of the 3 parallel gateway calls returns, all 4 columns swap to new data at once (matches current `maybeCompose` semantics — see "Out of scope" below).
- Per-call errors render inline in the affected column(s); other columns keep working.

Depends on `beads-workbench-0x36.1` (SkeletonRow helper). The header spinner from `beads-workbench-0x36.6` provides the in-flight signal but is NOT a hard prerequisite — this task can land before the spinner; the only visible effect is a missing refresh signal until the spinner ships.

## Out of Scope (explicit)

- Per-call partial composition (one of the 3 calls landing → only its columns swap) is NOT in scope. Today `maybeCompose` waits for all 3 calls then writes all 4 columns at once (`internal/mode/board/model.go:343-398`). This task preserves that semantics; "atomic per region" here means the 4-column block.

## Files to Modify

- `internal/mode/board/model.go` — drop the `m.loading` short-circuit in `View()` (lines 226-228). Replace `m.loading` with per-column `loading bool` + `err error` on `columnData`. Update `startReload` (line 305) to mark all 4 columns loading at reload start. Update `maybeCompose` (lines 343-398) to clear all 4 loading flags atomically and copy per-call errors into the affected columns. Replace the current full-board error path (lines 138-167) with per-column error rendering.
- `internal/ui/board/board.go` — render rule per column: skeleton rows when `loading && len(issues)==0`, stale issues when `loading && len(issues)>0`, inline error row when `err != nil`.
- `internal/mode/board/model_test.go` — assertions per the criteria below.

## Instructions

1. Extend `columnData` with `loading bool` and `err error`. DO NOT add `loadedOnce` — `loading && len(issues)==0` is sufficient to distinguish cold-start vs refresh per column.
2. In `Init()` and `startReload()`, set `loading=true` and `err=nil` on all 4 columns. Remove the global `m.loading` flag if no consumer remains (verify with `grep -RIn 'm\.loading' internal/mode/board/`).
3. In `maybeCompose`, when the atomic composition runs: clear all 4 `loading` flags; copy each per-call error into the relevant column(s)' `err` field (Not Ready + Ready ← ReadyExplain error; In Progress ← in_progress query error; Done ← closed query error); replace each column's rows atomically.
4. Remove the `if m.loading { return loading.View(...) }` short-circuit in `View()`.
5. In `internal/ui/board/board.go`, render rule per column:
   - `loading && len(issues)==0` → render ~6 skeleton rows via `loading.SkeletonRow`.
   - `loading && len(issues)>0` → render `issues` unchanged (header spinner from `0x36.6` is the only refresh signal).
   - `err != nil` → render an inline single-line error row (e.g. `"⚠ load failed: <short error>"`) at the top of the column's content area; render the rest as skeleton (cold) or stale issues (refresh) per the same rules.
6. Keyboard input during full cold-start (all 4 columns empty) MUST be a no-op, not a crash. Audit `moveRow` / `currentSelection` to confirm zero-length issue arrays are handled safely.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Board `View()` never returns `loading.View(...)`; it always renders the 4-column layout
- [ ] Cold start: each column with no data and `loading=true` shows skeleton rows built from `░`
- [ ] Refresh: stale rows remain on screen while `loading=true`; no per-column "loading" text appears over them
- [ ] When all 3 gateway calls return, all 4 columns swap atomically (no flicker)
- [ ] If a single gateway call errors, only the affected column(s) show an inline error; the other columns continue rendering their data normally
- [ ] Keyboard navigation during full cold-start (no data in any column) is a no-op, NOT a crash
- [ ] Keyboard navigation works in already-loaded columns while other columns are still loading
- [ ] `go test ./internal/mode/board/...` passes
- [ ] `go test ./...` passes
- [ ] `go vet ./...` passes
close_reason: Per-column loading + non-blocking board refresh shipped. All acceptance criteria met; full suite green.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Implemented per-column loading + non-blocking refresh on board. Removed top-level m.loading/m.loadError; added per-column loading bool and err error to columnData. IsLoading() derives from columns. View() always renders 4-column layout (short-circuit removed). Skeleton rows render via skeleton.SkeletonRow when column is loading with no issues; stale issues remain visible during refresh; per-call errors route only to affected columns (ReadyExplain → Not Ready+Ready, in_progress → In Progress, closed → Done). 5 new tests added covering cold start, atomic swap, partial error, empty-board keyboard no-op, and stale-during-refresh. Scoped tests pass; full suite green.

title: Foundation: SkeletonRow helper
description:
## Description

Ship the shared skeleton-row renderer used by the per-surface non-blocking loading tasks (Board, Search, Detail).

This task is intentionally narrow and additive. The header braille spinner + ticker is split out into `beads-workbench-0x36.6` so the riskier ticker work does not block `.2/.3/.4`.

## Files to Modify

- `internal/ui/loading/loading.go` (or new `internal/ui/skeleton/skeleton.go` if colocation is awkward) — add `SkeletonRow(width int, slots int) string` helper. Use `░` (U+2591) as the skeleton glyph; pin this exactly so `0x36.5` PTY assertions can match.
- `internal/ui/loading/loading_test.go` — unit tests covering: width-respecting output, slot count, deterministic glyph identity.

## Instructions
