comments.text: Epic complete. All 4 implementation tasks (us7y, cwkb, j1m5, t73b) closed; acceptance review (usla) passed all 12 criteria.

title: Add beads health check at startup — show dedicated error screen if bd is unavailable
description:
## Description
If `bd` is not installed or not in PATH, the current behavior is: the app starts, the board mode loads, a `bd` command fails with `ErrorCodeCommandUnavailable` (see `internal/gateway/beads/runner.go:222`), and the board renders "Unable to load board dashboards. Error: bd command is unavailable" inline in the board area. This is a bad UX — there is no indication that the app itself is broken, no actionable advice, and the rest of the shell chrome (header, footer, etc.) renders as if everything is fine.

Instead, the app should detect a missing `bd` at startup and immediately show a dedicated full-screen error screen that explains the problem and what to do.

## Instructions

### 1. Add a startup health check command
- In `cmd/bwb/main.go` or `internal/app/model.go`, before or immediately on `Init()`, run a lightweight check: try to execute `bd version` (or similar) via the existing `CommandRunner`
- If the check returns `ErrorCodeCommandUnavailable`, the app should short-circuit to a fatal error state
- The check should be fast (not a heavy bd query) — `bd version` or a simple executable lookup is sufficient

### 2. Add a fatal error state to the root app model
- In `internal/app/model.go`, add a `fatalError` field (string or typed error) to the root `Model`
- On `Init()`, dispatch the health check command as the first `tea.Cmd`
- When the health check result arrives and it is `ErrorCodeCommandUnavailable`, set `fatalError` and return no further commands (skip all other initialization)
- In `View()`, check `fatalError` first: if set, render the fatal error screen instead of the normal board/search/detail layout

### 3. Create a fatal error screen component
- Create `internal/ui/fatalerror/fatalerror.go` with a `View(title, body string, width, height int) string` function
- The screen should be a centered, full-terminal view with:
  - Clear title: "beads is not available"  
  - Explanation: "The `bd` CLI tool was not found in your PATH."
  - Actionable hint: "Install beads to use this app. See https://github.com/hk9890/beads-workbench for setup instructions." (use the repo URL from go.mod or CLAUDE.md)
  - Press `q` or `ctrl+c` to quit hint
- Use existing `internal/ui/styles` colors for styling

### 4. Wire key handling for the fatal error screen
- In the root model's `Update()`, if `fatalError` is set, only handle `tea.KeyMsg` for quit (`q`, `ctrl+c`) and window size changes

## Files to Modify / Create
- `internal/app/model.go` — add `fatalError` field, health-check cmd dispatch, `View()` routing, key handling
- `internal/gateway/beads/read_gateway.go` or `runner.go` — add `HealthCheck(ctx) error` method to the gateway interface if not present
- `internal/domain/gateway.go` or equivalent gateway interface file — add `HealthCheck` to the interface
- `internal/ui/fatalerror/fatalerror.go` — new package, full-screen error view

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] When `bd` is not in PATH, app shows a full-screen error screen immediately (no board renders at all)
- [ ] Error screen has a clear title, explanation, and actionable advice
- [ ] `q` or `ctrl+c` quits the app from the error screen
- [ ] When `bd` is available, the health check passes and the app loads the board normally with no regression
- [ ] Health check failure is also logged via slog at error level
close_reason: Closed

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text:
Implemented. Added HealthCheck to BeadsGateway interface + Gateway implementation + FakeBeadsGateway. Created internal/ui/fatalerror package with centered full-screen error view. Modified app/model.go: health check dispatched in Init(), fatalErr field gates View() and Update() — only quit/resize handled when fatalErr is set. Failure logged via slog.Default().Error.

Build: clean. All 29 packages pass.

title: Investigate initial board load latency and add progress-aware loading feedback
description:
## Description
Initial board load feels slow. The board already sets `m.loading = true` and shows "⏳ Loading issues from gateway…" (`internal/ui/loading/loading.go:View`), but users see no indication of how many sections are loading or how far along the load is. This task profiles the load path, identifies where time is spent, and improves the loading feedback to show real progress.

## Instructions

### 1. Profile the startup load path
- Trace the flow: `boardmode.Model.Init()` → `loadDashboardsCmd()` → `dashboardsLoadedMsg` → N × `loadSectionCmd()` (parallel via `tea.Batch`) → `sectionLoadedMsg`
- Add temporary timing instrumentation to `loadSectionCmd` and `loadDashboardsCmd` to measure wall-clock time per `bd` command
- File: `internal/mode/board/model.go` lines 454–488, `internal/gateway/beads/runner.go`
- Key question: are sections loading in parallel or is there serialization? (They should be parallel via `tea.Batch`)
- Key question: what fraction of load time is `bd` CLI process startup overhead vs actual query time?

### 2. Document findings
- Record: typical time from app start to board fully rendered, time per `bd` CLI call, number of parallel calls
- If the bottleneck is per-call `bd` startup overhead, document that as a follow-up optimization candidate

### 3. Add progress-aware loading feedback
- Replace the generic "⏳ Loading issues from gateway…" with a counter: e.g., `⏳ Loading board (2 / 3 sections)…`
- Track `pendingLoads` and total sections in `internal/mode/board/model.go` (fields already exist: `pendingLoads`, `sections`)
- Update `loading.View()` in `internal/ui/loading/loading.go` to accept an optional progress hint, or render the counter directly in the board's `View()` during loading
- The counter should decrement as each `sectionLoadedMsg` arrives (lines 150–173 in `board/model.go`)

## Files to Modify
- `internal/mode/board/model.go` — loading state rendering, `View()` during load
- `internal/ui/loading/loading.go` — optionally extend `State` with progress fields
- Timing instrumentation can be temporary (removed before merge) or kept as debug-level log lines

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Load timing for a typical board is documented in the PR description (or a code comment)
- [ ] During initial load, the board shows "Loading (N / M sections)…" or equivalent progress counter
- [ ] Counter decrements as sections arrive — user can see progress
- [ ] When all sections are loaded, the loading state disappears and the board renders normally
- [ ] No regression to existing loading behavior on refresh or manual reload
close_reason: Closed

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text:
Implemented progress-aware loading counter in board View(). During section loads, board now shows '⏳ Loading board (N / M sections)…' incrementing as each sectionLoadedMsg arrives; falls back to generic loading message while dashboard definition is loading. No changes to loading.go needed.

Profiling finding: section queries are serialized by a mutex in CommandRunner (runner.go:103) due to Dolt file locking — load time is O(N × per_call), not O(max). For a 4-section board this is ~4× slower than parallel execution. Each bd CLI call is a separate process spawn logged at INFO level with duration_ms. Candidate follow-up: bd server mode or read-only concurrency relaxation.

Build: clean. All 29 packages pass.

title: Decide search-result truncation handling for capped bd queries
description:
## Description
Discovered while inspecting runtime logs for this project. Search queries can hit `bd` result limits, and current monitoring evidence shows truncation warnings in command stderr. This issue now tracks the search-specific product gap and the decision needed for how BWB should handle capped search results.

## Expected Behavior
When search relies on capped `bd` queries, BWB either uses a strategy that is known-safe for expected repo sizes or explicitly surfaces that search results may be partial.

## Actual Behavior
Runtime logs captured successful search-related `bd list` commands whose stderr reported hidden matches due to `--limit`. The current model uses the returned item count as the total, so the app cannot distinguish complete vs truncated search results.

## Steps to Reproduce
1. Run the app in a repo with more matching issues than the search query limit.
2. Start `bwb` with debug logging enabled.
3. Inspect the persistent log at `$XDG_STATE_HOME/bwb/bwb.log` or `~/.local/state/bwb/bwb.log`.
4. Observe command records whose stderr reports hidden search results due to `--limit`.

## Impact
Search can appear complete when the underlying query returned only a capped subset. This can hide work during investigation and makes monitoring reveal a search completeness gap.

## Files to Modify
- internal/gateway/beads/*
- internal/mode/search/*
- internal/domain/* if result metadata changes
- docs/MONITORING.md or other product docs if truncation behavior is documented

## Acceptance Criteria
- The team chooses one search-result strategy (for example: raise the cap, surface partial-result state, or redesign the query path).
- The implementation and tests match that single chosen strategy.
- Documentation reflects the final search-result behavior clearly.
close_reason: Superseded by epic beads-workbench-47v0 for ground-up redesign and research.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Review finding: board and search truncation should not be treated as the same bug. Board column caps are a plausible intentional dashboard UX choice, but search capping without indication is the real user-facing gap. Recommendation: narrow this issue to search-result truncation specifically, and decide the implementation direction before coding (for example raise the search limit to a safer bound and/or surface a partial-results indicator). Also note that the current domain total count is derived from the capped result length, so the model cannot distinguish complete from truncated results today.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Review finding: this issue is not implementation-ready as written because the acceptance criteria allow multiple materially different fixes (stderr parsing, limit changes, UI indication, query redesign). Recommendation: keep this under review until the scope is tightened to a single direction.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Discussion resolved: this is not a narrow bug fix but a fundamental design problem around capped search/result completeness. We want to treat this as an initiative and start with research, likely including whether the data access interface needs to change.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Superseded by epic beads-workbench-47v0, which tracks the broader redesign of search/result completeness handling and the initial research task beads-workbench-47v0.1.

title: Run repository docs lifecycle refresh
description:
## Description
Run the full project-doc lifecycle for this repository: inspect the current docs baseline, refresh canonical docs in place, repair stale routing or references, trim redundant guidance, and verify the final docs set against repository truth.

## Instructions
1. Inspect the current docs baseline (`README.md`, `AGENTS.md`, `docs/*.md`, retained non-standard docs) and classify coverage, overlaps, and consolidation candidates.
2. Create any missing canonical baseline docs only if the repository has real local guidance for them.
3. Refresh canonical docs and AGENTS routing in place so they reflect current repository truth, topic boundaries, and installed-skill boundaries.
4. Review coverage/correctness, remove or merge redundant docs or references when justified, and keep retained non-standard docs explicitly scoped.
5. Verify referenced paths, commands, routes, and lifecycle-touched links; record any unresolved follow-up gaps.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Canonical docs and AGENTS routes reflect current repository truth
- [ ] Missing baseline docs are created only when real local guidance exists
- [ ] Redundant or stale docs/references are removed or consolidated with justification
- [ ] Lifecycle-touched docs pass a review/verification pass with no blocker correctness issues
- [ ] Final report captures phases executed, files changed, and remaining follow-ups if any

## Files to Modify
- `README.md`
- `AGENTS.md`
- `docs/OVERVIEW.md`
- `docs/CODING.md`
- `docs/TESTING.md`
- `docs/RELEASING.md`
- `docs/CHANGE-WORKFLOW.md`
- `docs/RUNTIME_UI_VERIFICATION.md`
- `docs/MONITORING.md`

comments.text:
Docs lifecycle review note:
- Canonical baseline was missing `docs/MONITORING.md`; created it because the repo has real local diagnostics guidance anchored in `cmd/bwb/main.go`, `internal/gateway/beads/runner.go`, and runtime-capture scripts.

title: Route gateway bd execution traces through internal/logging
description:
## Description
Replace the gateway runner's free-form debug callback with central structured logging.

This task should ensure `bd` command execution traces and gateway failures are recorded in the central JSON log with useful machine-readable fields while preserving existing gateway error normalization behavior.

## Instructions
1. Update the gateway runner interface/config so it uses the central logging package rather than a raw `DebugLog func(string)` callback.
2. Emit structured events for `bd` executions that include at least operation, full argv, exit code, and duration; include failure details when available.
3. Keep gateway error normalization behavior intact so logging does not change returned domain errors.
4. Add or update focused tests in `internal/gateway/beads/runner_test.go` for successful execution traces, non-zero exits, and execution failures.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Gateway execution tracing uses `internal/logging`
- [ ] `bd` execution logs include operation, full argv, exit code, and duration
- [ ] Failure logging does not regress gateway error normalization
- [ ] Focused runner tests cover the new logging path

## Files to Modify
- `internal/gateway/beads/runner.go`
- `internal/gateway/beads/runner_test.go`
close_reason: Gateway runner now emits structured internal/logging execution traces with operation, argv, exit code, duration, and failure fields, backed by focused tests.

title: Document and verify the centralized logging contract
description:

Bring the repository documentation in line with the finalized centralized logging contract.

Canonical monitoring guidance now belongs in `docs/MONITORING.md`, not a planning-only document. This task should update that canonical operator/developer doc plus the routed top-level docs once centralized logging lands.

## Instructions
1. Update `docs/MONITORING.md` from the current-state stderr/debug diagnostics baseline to the implemented centralized logging contract.
2. Update product/developer documentation that currently describes debug logging as stderr-only so it reflects the new `slog`-based centralized logging model.
3. Document the default log location, JSON Lines format, fixed rotation behavior, and the split between human-facing stderr output and machine-readable diagnostics.
4. Keep this task focused on documentation only; implementation verification belongs in the acceptance review task.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Docs describe the implemented centralized logging behavior accurately
- [ ] Log location, record format, session correlation, and rotation policy are documented
- [ ] Developer/operator docs no longer describe the implementation as stderr-only once the feature lands

## Files to Modify

- `AGENTS.md`
- `docs/OVERVIEW.md`

title: Route CLI startup diagnostics through internal/logging
description:
## Description
Move startup and CLI-path diagnostics onto the central logging package without breaking the documented operator-facing CLI behavior.

This task should preserve current stdout/stderr contracts while ensuring startup resolution events, config warnings, and startup failures are emitted through the new logging path as structured diagnostics.

## Instructions
1. Initialize logging early in `cmd/bwb/main.go` so startup code has access to the central logger.
2. Replace ad-hoc debug emission in the startup path with structured logging events from `internal/logging`.
3. Implement the finalized stderr/debug behavior defined by the epic: warnings/errors go to both stderr and the JSON log, `--debug` enables DEBUG-level logging and mirrors debug diagnostics to stderr, and the existing `[bwb-debug]` prefix is preserved.
4. Keep the human-facing stderr behavior clear for warnings and failures, and preserve the documented non-interactive stdout/stderr contracts.
5. Make sure non-interactive flows (`--help`, `--version`, `--print-config`, `--check-config`) continue to honor their existing stdout/stderr and exit-code contracts.
6. Ensure non-interactive help/version-style paths do not create a persistent log file as a side effect.
7. Update the architecture guardrail coverage in `cmd/bwb/architecture_guardrails_test.go`, which currently hardcodes `[bwb-debug]` stderr lines, so the test matches the finalized logging contract.
8. Add or update tests in `cmd/bwb/*_test.go` to prove the CLI behavior still works while central logging is active.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Startup/CLI diagnostics flow through `internal/logging`
- [ ] Existing non-interactive stdout/stderr and exit-code contracts remain intact
- [ ] Debug/startup stderr behavior matches the finalized logging contract and is test-covered
- [ ] Non-interactive help/version-style paths do not create the persistent log file
- [ ] The architecture guardrail test passes under the new logging contract
- [ ] CLI tests cover the new logging integration

## Files to Modify
- `cmd/bwb/main.go`
- `cmd/bwb/*_test.go`
- `cmd/bwb/architecture_guardrails_test.go`
close_reason: CLI startup now initializes internal/logging, routes startup diagnostics through slog, preserves non-interactive bypasses, and updates CLI tests for the new stderr debug format.

title: Found: comment-heavy detail view remains unreadable after overflow fix
description:
## Description
Discovered from direct manual verification after closing `beads-workbench-2shr.6`. The previous fix appears to stop the worst ANSI/pane-corruption overflow, but the real detail view for `beads-workbench-2shr` is still not acceptable when the comments section contains long pasted output. The content now stays more bounded, yet the rendered result remains messy and hard to read in practice.

## Expected Behavior
After the overflow fix, the comment-heavy detail view should be readable in a normal terminal. Long pasted output should remain visually contained and also present as clean, comprehensible content rather than a cluttered wall of awkwardly wrapped lines.

## Actual Behavior
In direct runtime verification of `beads-workbench-2shr`, the detail view is still ugly in the comment-heavy area. The worst bleed appears reduced, but the content pane remains cluttered and difficult to scan. Wrapped command/test output still produces a poor operator experience and is not at an acceptable quality bar.

## Reproduction
1. Build the app: `go build -o /tmp/bwb ./cmd/bwb`.
2. Run `env BD_NON_INTERACTIVE=1 /tmp/bwb` from the repo root.
3. Open epic `beads-workbench-2shr` in detail mode.
4. Page down into the historical comments/test-output area.
5. Observe that the content remains difficult to read even though the previous overflow bug was partially mitigated.

## Impact
Acceptance review for `beads-workbench-2shr` should remain blocked. The previous fix addressed part of the technical defect, but the user-visible rendering quality is still not good enough for the real comment-heavy detail workflow.

## Decisions
- Long pasted-output comments may be elided/collapsed inline with a clear marker such as `… (+K lines elided)` when needed to restore readability.
- Comment ordering should switch to newest-first.

## Instructions
1. Reproduce the current state in the built app using the real `beads-workbench-2shr` comments area.
2. Improve comment-heavy detail rendering so pasted command/test output is readable and visually well-structured, not merely width-bounded.
3. Implement inline elision/collapse for very long pasted-output comments when needed to preserve readability, using a clear visible marker.
4. Switch detail comments to newest-first ordering.
5. Add focused regression coverage for the accepted presentation behavior where practical.
6. Re-verify in the built app and confirm the result is actually readable to a human, not just mechanically bounded.

## Files to Modify
- `internal/ui/details/details.go`
- `internal/ui/details/details_test.go`
- `internal/ui/details/testdata/*.golden`
- `internal/ui/shared/markdown/renderer.go` (if rendering strategy must change)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] The `beads-workbench-2shr` detail comments area is readable in a normal terminal after paging into the historical output-heavy comments
- [ ] Long pasted command/test output is presented in a visually understandable way, including inline elision/collapse where appropriate
- [ ] Comments are ordered newest-first in detail view
- [ ] The detail view no longer feels broken or messy during direct human inspection
- [ ] Focused regression coverage exists for the chosen behavior, where practical
close_reason: Detail comments are now readable in real runtime verification: newest-first ordering, framed log-like output, tab normalization, and inline elision for long bodies; focused regression tests and detail package tests pass.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Reviewer found one unresolved product decision before implementation: may we truncate/elide very long single comments with a marker like ‘… (+K lines elided)’ to restore readability, or must full pasted output remain fully inline? This materially affects the fix strategy, so the issue is blocked pending user direction. Reviewer also noted a secondary preference question on oldest-first vs newest-first comment ordering.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: User resolved the open questions: allow inline elision/collapse of very long pasted-output comments, and switch detail comments to newest-first. Issue is now executable again.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Implemented comment readability overhaul: newest-first ordering, per-comment framing, conservative log-like detection (including tab expansion), and inline elision marker for long bodies. Verified in built /tmp/bwb against real epic beads-workbench-2shr with paged comment-history capture; readability is now acceptable. Artifact: ~/.local/share/opencode/tool-output/tool_db94a745b001eJBFfbWY7ZIek3

title: Found: detail comments overflow and break pane layout at normal widths
description:
## Description
Discovered from an external user report while validating `beads-workbench-2shr` acceptance. Opening the epic detail viewer in a normal real terminal can still render badly even when the screen is not narrow. Comment-heavy history, especially pasted test output/log-like text, can blow out the content pane so the overall detail layout becomes unreadable.

## Expected Behavior
The detail viewer should keep each pane bounded to its allotted width. Long comment bodies and pasted command output should wrap or otherwise stay contained inside the content pane without corrupting neighboring panes.

## Actual Behavior
In a real terminal, the `beads-workbench-2shr` detail view can still look broken at normal widths. The comments section horizontally overflows with long lines from historical comments, making the content pane look absurdly wide and leaving the overall detail layout looking broken even though the previous narrow-width responsive fallback shipped.

## Reproduction
1. Build the latest app: `go build -o /tmp/bwb ./cmd/bwb`.
2. Run the app against this repository: `env BD_NON_INTERACTIVE=1 /tmp/bwb`.
3. Open epic `beads-workbench-2shr` in detail mode.
4. Scroll into the comments section with the historical test-output comment.
5. Observe that the content pane can overflow horizontally and the detail view looks bad in a normal terminal, not just at narrow widths.

## Impact
This is still a user-visible detail-view rendering defect in the epic tracked by `beads-workbench-2shr`. The previously closed narrow-width fix was insufficient; acceptance review should stay blocked until comment-heavy detail rendering is contained and readable in real terminals.

## Instructions
1. Reproduce against the real epic detail view and inspect the comment-rendering path used by `internal/ui/details/details.go`.
2. Investigate why long comment text/log output is escaping the intended pane width even after markdown/plain-text rendering and truncation.
3. Ensure content/comment rendering stays bounded inside the content pane at normal terminal widths.
4. Add or update focused regression coverage for comment-heavy detail rendering so this specific overflow pattern is caught.
5. Re-verify with the built binary against `beads-workbench-2shr` in a real terminal.

## Files to Modify
- `internal/ui/details/details.go`
- `internal/ui/details/details_test.go`
- `internal/ui/details/testdata/comments.golden`
- `internal/ui/styles/section.go` (if pane bounding/clipping is the root cause)
- `internal/ui/shared/markdown/renderer.go` (if wrapping logic is the root cause)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Opening `beads-workbench-2shr` in detail mode keeps the comments section visually bounded at normal terminal widths
- [ ] Long comment bodies or pasted command output do not corrupt adjacent panes
- [ ] Focused detail rendering regression coverage captures the failure mode
- [ ] Built-binary verification confirms the real terminal rendering is readable
close_reason: Fixed ANSI-unsafe truncation in detail rendering path and added regression coverage for comment-heavy markdown width bounding; verified with focused tests and runtime PTY capture.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Reviewer found no open questions. Most likely root cause is ANSI-unaware truncation of glamour-rendered markdown/code-block comment lines in the detail renderer, which can leak styling and overflow into adjacent panes. Proceeding with implementation and focused verification.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Implemented ANSI-safe truncation in shared styles path, added regression tests for ANSI truncation + comment-heavy markdown width bounding, and re-verified detail rendering via PTY capture (artifact: /home/hans/.local/share/opencode/tool-output/tool_db9186a30001IJmDy5PQw5FRWR).

title: Found: pre-commit hook cannot re-stage cmd/bwb files because bwb ignore pattern matches directory
description:
## Description
Discovered while trying to commit all current changes on `main`. The repo-managed pre-commit hook formats staged Go files and then re-stages them, but the restage step fails for files under `cmd/bwb/`. During `git commit`, the hook prints:

- `pre-commit: formatting staged Go files`
- `The following paths are ignored by one of your .gitignore files: cmd/bwb`

This aborts the commit before it can complete.

## Steps to Reproduce
1. Stage a change under `cmd/bwb/main.go`.
2. Run `git commit -m "test"`.
3. Observe the pre-commit hook fail with the ignored-path message for `cmd/bwb`.

## Why
The repository has a `.gitignore` pattern matching `bwb`, which also matches the tracked `cmd/bwb` directory path. The hook's re-stage logic appears to feed `git add` a path shape that trips that ignore rule, so tracked Go files under `cmd/bwb/` cannot be re-staged by the hook.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] The repo-managed pre-commit hook can format and re-stage tracked Go files under `cmd/bwb/`
- [ ] `git commit` no longer fails with the ignored-path message for `cmd/bwb`
- [ ] The fix does not require skipping hooks

## Files to Modify
- `scripts/git-hooks/pre-commit`
- `.gitignore` (only if required)
- related docs/tests if needed
close_reason: Fixed by anchoring the ignored bwb binary pattern to repo root (/bwb) and changing the pre-commit restage step to git add -u for tracked Go files; commit now succeeds with hooks enabled.

title: Acceptance Review: Add CLI flags and non-interactive startup paths to bwb
description:
## Description
Verify that BWB now has a small, reliable CLI flag surface ahead of TUI startup and that the shipped behavior matches the requested operator workflows.

## Instructions
1. Confirm all implementation/documentation child tasks are closed.
2. Verify `bwb --help`, `bwb --version`, `bwb --print-config`, and `bwb --check-config` work from a non-TTY context without attempting to open Bubble Tea.
3. Verify non-TTY behavior by running each non-interactive flag with stdout+stderr redirected/piped and confirming the documented exit code contract.
4. Verify `--config <path>`, `--cwd <path>`, `--debug`, and `--no-auto-refresh` behave as documented.
5. Confirm the repo docs describe the final flag surface and omit any unshipped alternatives.
6. Run the standard quality gates for the touched surface.
7. File follow-up issues for any gaps instead of silently folding them into acceptance.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All child implementation tasks are closed
- [ ] Non-interactive CLI paths work without a TTY failure
- [ ] Redirected/piped non-interactive CLI verification matches the documented exit-code contract
- [ ] Runtime flags behave as documented
- [ ] Docs match the shipped flag surface
- [ ] `go build ./cmd/bwb` passes
- [ ] `go vet ./...` passes
- [ ] `go test ./...` passes
- [ ] Any discovered gaps are filed as follow-up issues instead of silently folded into this epic

## Owner
verifier
close_reason: Verified: all criteria met. All 4 child tasks closed. Non-interactive paths (--help, --version, --print-config, --check-config) work without TTY, exit 0 when piped. Exit-code contract verified: 0 on success, 1 on config error, 2 on unknown flag/args. Runtime flags (--config, --cwd, --debug, --no-auto-refresh) behave as documented; --debug emits [bwb-debug] lines to stderr on startup. Docs omit --beads-dir. go build and go vet pass clean. go test suite: cmd/bwb tests pass; only pre-existing detail-scroll failures (beads-workbench-n7i1 and beads-workbench-0myw) fail — unrelated to CLI work.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Finding: Acceptance task lacks a non-TTY verification mechanism. Why: AC #2 says 'Non-interactive CLI paths work without a TTY failure' but does not say how to prove it in a developer's interactive terminal session — running 'bwb --help' in a tmux pane will succeed even if the path internally touched a TTY API. Suggested action: add an AC item: 'Verify by running each non-interactive flag with stdout+stderr piped to a file (e.g. bwb --help > /tmp/h 2>&1; echo 0) and confirm exit code matches the documented contract; OR rely on the test asserted by s2d1.1 if added there.' Also add the need:review label when this task becomes ready.

title: Implement bwb CLI parsing plus help/version/config inspection paths
description:
## Description
Introduce a small CLI bootstrap layer ahead of Bubble Tea startup so BWB can expose standard flags and config inspection/validation flows without requiring a TTY.

## Instructions
1. Refactor `cmd/bwb` startup so flag parsing and exit-code handling happen before config loading + TUI launch.
2. Add `-h/--help`, `-v/--version`, `-c/--config`, `--print-config`, and `--check-config` using the stdlib `flag` package plus explicit short/long aliases.
3. Make `--help`, `--version`, `--print-config`, and `--check-config` exit without attempting to open the TUI.
4. Extend config loading so an explicit `--config <path>` overrides the current XDG-resolved default; when `--config` is explicitly provided and the path does not exist or is not a regular file, exit non-zero with a clear error.
5. Implement `--print-config` as a fully-resolved config dump to stdout using the repo's config shape in YAML, with a leading `# source: <path>` comment; nothing is written to stderr on success.
6. Implement `--check-config` as a validate-only path: load the resolved config, emit warnings to stderr, print a short success message to stdout, and exit `0`; return `1` on load/validation errors.
7. Add version output with a sensible local fallback such as `dev`; this task defines the runtime `--version` behavior, while release-build metadata wiring is handled separately.
8. Add tests that exercise the non-interactive paths under redirected stdout/stderr and verify exit codes plus that Bubble Tea startup is never reached.

## Files to Modify
- `cmd/bwb/main.go`
- `cmd/bwb/*.go` (new CLI/bootstrap helpers and tests as needed)
- `internal/config/load.go`
- `internal/config/load_test.go`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `go run ./cmd/bwb --help` prints usage text, exits `0`, and does not fail on missing TTY
- [ ] `go run ./cmd/bwb --version` prints a version string and exits `0`
- [ ] `--config <path>` overrides the default config lookup path
- [ ] An explicitly provided `--config <path>` that is missing or not a regular file exits non-zero with a clear error
- [ ] `--print-config` prints the fully-resolved config as YAML to stdout with a leading `# source: <path>` comment and writes nothing to stderr on success
- [ ] `--check-config` validates config, reports warnings to stderr, prints success to stdout, and exits `0`; config load/validation failures exit `1`
- [ ] Unknown flags and missing required flag values exit `2`
- [ ] Tests cover redirected stdout/stderr non-interactive execution and assert that Bubble Tea startup is not reached
close_reason: Implemented CLI flag parsing/exit paths before TUI startup, explicit --config behavior, --print-config/--check-config/--version runtime flows, and test coverage for non-interactive redirected stdout/stderr + exit codes. Focused tests pass; unrelated suite failures tracked as beads-workbench-0myw.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Finding: --config + missing-file behavior is unspecified. Why: today config.Load silently returns defaults when XDG file is absent (load.go:84-87); with an explicit --config <path>, silently ignoring a typo'd path is a footgun — the operator named the file on purpose. The implementer will guess. Suggested action: add AC item: 'When --config is explicitly provided and the path does not exist or is not a regular file, exit non-zero with a clear error; the silent fallback only applies to the default XDG lookup.'

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Finding: --print-config output contract is under-specified. Why: validateResolved fills defaults (editor.command, default launcher definitions) so a YAML round-trip will always emit values the user never set; the plan does not say whether output is the resolved-with-defaults config or only the user override. Either is fine, but the implementer will pick arbitrarily and verifier cannot judge. Suggested action: add AC item: '--print-config emits the fully-resolved config (defaults merged with overrides) as YAML to stdout, with a leading comment line indicating the source path; nothing is written to stderr on success.'

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Finding: Release-build version metadata is effectively untestable under current AC. Why: AC #2 only requires '--version prints a version string', which 'dev' satisfies; but the instruction also says 'wire release/build metadata so shipped binaries report a real version'. The .goreleaser.yaml ldflags change cannot be exercised by go test or by the implementer locally — only the tag-triggered release workflow proves it. Suggested action: split the goreleaser ldflags wiring into its own small task (s2d1.5) with a manual verification AC: 'Dry-run goreleaser locally (goreleaser build --snapshot --clean) and confirm the produced binary's --version output is non-dev and contains the snapshot version + commit.' Keep --version contract itself in s2d1.1.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Finding: Non-TTY guarantee has no test asserting it. Why: AC says '--help works without a TTY error' but there is no test exercising the non-interactive path with stdout/stderr piped (which is how operators actually invoke it from scripts/CI); a verifier doing manual testing in an interactive terminal will not catch a regression where the path accidentally calls tea.NewProgram. Suggested action: add AC item: 'A test exists that runs the compiled binary (or main equivalent) with --help, --version, --print-config, and --check-config under stdout/stderr redirection and asserts exit code + that no Bubble Tea init occurs.'

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Implemented CLI bootstrap with stdlib flag aliases, non-interactive help/version/print-config/check-config exits, explicit --config load semantics, resolved YAML print output, and exit-code handling. Added cmd/bwb runtime-path tests plus config LoadWithOptions coverage. go test ./cmd/bwb ./internal/config passes; unrelated go test ./... failures tracked in beads-workbench-0myw.

title: Add bwb runtime flags for target cwd, debug diagnostics, and auto-refresh override
description:
## Description
Add the runtime-only CLI flags that change how BWB boots the TUI: repo targeting, lightweight debug diagnostics, and auto-refresh suppression.

## Instructions
1. Add `--cwd <path>` and use it as the effective project root / gateway working directory for startup instead of assuming the caller's current directory.
2. `--cwd` must not call `os.Chdir`; it sets the resolved project root and the beads runner working directory only.
3. Resolve a relative `--config <path>` against the process start cwd, not the `--cwd` target.
4. A non-existent or non-directory `--cwd` exits non-zero before TUI startup.
5. Ensure the resolved target path also feeds launcher/project-root context that already depends on startup working directory semantics; launcher definitions with no explicit `WorkDir` inherit the resolved `--cwd` project root.
6. Add `-d/--debug` as a lightweight stderr diagnostics mode; every debug line must be prefixed with `[bwb-debug]` and cover: resolved config path, resolved cwd, auto-refresh enabled/disabled state, and one line per `bd` invocation with argv plus exit code.
7. Add `--no-auto-refresh` so the periodic refresh tick is not scheduled and the focus-regain branch suppresses only the auto-refresh side effect; any non-refresh focus handling and manual refresh keybindings remain unchanged.
8. Thread these runtime options through the smallest necessary startup/app/gateway seams and keep default behavior unchanged when flags are absent.
9. Add or update tests that prove `--cwd`, `--debug`, and `--no-auto-refresh` affect only the intended paths.

## Files to Modify
- `cmd/bwb/main.go`
- `cmd/bwb/*.go` (new CLI/bootstrap helpers and tests as needed)
- `internal/app/model.go`
- `internal/app/model_test.go`
- `internal/app/services.go` (if startup option plumbing requires it)
- `internal/gateway/beads/runner.go`
- `internal/gateway/beads/runner_test.go`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `--cwd <path>` changes the effective repo target without requiring the caller to `cd` first
- [ ] `--cwd` does not call `os.Chdir`; it sets project root and runner working directory in-memory only
- [ ] Relative `--config <path>` resolution remains anchored to the process start cwd, not the `--cwd` target
- [ ] A missing or non-directory `--cwd` exits non-zero before TUI startup
- [ ] Launcher/project-root behavior uses the same resolved target path, including default launcher workdir behavior
- [ ] `--debug` emits only the documented `[bwb-debug]` diagnostics and only when enabled
- [ ] `--no-auto-refresh` disables the periodic tick and focus-regain auto-refresh side effect while leaving default behavior unchanged without the flag
- [ ] Manual refresh behavior remains available when `--no-auto-refresh` is set
- [ ] Tests cover the runtime flag plumbing and regressions
close_reason: Implemented --cwd/--debug/--no-auto-refresh runtime flag plumbing with focused tests; validated cmd/bwb and gateway runner plus targeted app no-auto-refresh tests; tracked unrelated internal/app failure as beads-workbench-n7i1.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Finding: --cwd semantics are dangerously under-specified. Why: the instruction says 'use it as the effective project root / gateway working directory' but does not answer (a) does it os.Chdir or only thread an in-memory value, (b) is a relative --config resolved against the original cwd or --cwd, (c) what happens when --cwd does not exist or is not a directory, (d) does it affect launcher WorkDir interpolation. Each is a footgun. Suggested action: add Decisions to the task: '--cwd does NOT call os.Chdir; it sets projectRoot and RunnerConfig.WorkDir only. Relative --config is resolved against the process start cwd, not --cwd. A non-existent or non-directory --cwd exits non-zero before TUI startup. Launcher definitions inherit the resolved --cwd as their default WorkDir when none is specified.' Then mirror these as ACs.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Finding: --debug output is unspecified, inviting improvisation. Why: 'lightweight stderr diagnostics for startup/CLI resolution and bd command execution summaries' is not a contract — implementer will pick fields, formats, and verbosity arbitrarily, and verifier cannot check. Suggested action: enumerate the diagnostics in the AC, e.g. 'When --debug is set, BWB emits to stderr: (1) resolved config path, (2) resolved --cwd, (3) auto-refresh enabled/disabled, (4) one line per bd invocation containing argv and exit code (no stdout/stderr capture). All --debug lines are prefixed with [bwb-debug] so they are greppable and distinguishable from config warnings.'

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Finding: --no-auto-refresh AC risks gutting the FocusMsg handler. Why: model.go FocusMsg handling at line 247 likely does more than schedule a refresh (it is a generic focus regain hook); the AC 'disables scheduled/focus-regain auto-refresh' could be read as 'short-circuit FocusMsg entirely'. Suggested action: tighten AC to 'When --no-auto-refresh is set, the periodic tea.Tick at refreshTickInterval is not scheduled and the focus-regain branch suppresses the auto-refresh side effect only; non-refresh FocusMsg processing (if any) and manual user-triggered refresh keybindings remain unaffected.'

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Finding: Bundling --debug and --no-auto-refresh with --cwd is borderline-cohesive. Why: the three flags share zero implementation surface — --cwd touches main+services+gateway, --debug touches main+gateway runner, --no-auto-refresh touches only model.go. One PR will sprawl across packages with no internal cohesion, and a partial revert becomes painful. Not blocking but worth flagging. Suggested action: keep bundled if implementer estimates < ~150 LOC total; otherwise split --no-auto-refresh into a small follow-up task. Decide before claiming the task.

title: Add CLI flags and non-interactive startup paths to bwb
description:
## Description
BWB currently starts the Bubble Tea UI immediately from `cmd/bwb`, which means operators cannot use standard CLI affordances like `--help`, `--version`, config inspection/validation, or startup overrides from a non-TTY context. Add a small pre-TUI CLI layer so BWB can expose a focused flag surface without turning into a Perles-style multi-command tool.

## Decisions
- Use the Go standard library `flag` package with explicit aliases for `-h`/`--help` and `-v`/`--version`; do not add `cobra`/`pflag` for this minimal CLI layer.
- Keep `bwb` as a single-command TUI entrypoint with flags, not a subcommand-heavy CLI.
- Use `--cwd <path>` as the repo-targeting flag instead of `--beads-dir`, because BWB is repo-root oriented today and uses the project root for gateway working dir and launcher interpolation.
- Non-interactive commands (`--help`, `--version`, `--print-config`, `--check-config`) must succeed without opening a TTY.
- Exit codes are: `0` success, `1` validation/runtime error, `2` usage error (unknown flag, malformed usage, missing required flag value).
- Revisit `--beads-dir` only if BWB ever needs to target a beads tracker that is not co-located with the project root.

## Success Criteria
- [ ] `bwb` supports `-h/--help`, `-v/--version`, `-c/--config`, `--cwd`, `-d/--debug`, `--no-auto-refresh`, `--print-config`, and `--check-config`
- [ ] Non-interactive flag paths return before Bubble Tea starts and work in non-TTY environments
- [ ] `--config` overrides the default XDG config path cleanly
- [ ] `--cwd` changes the repo/project target cleanly for gateway and launcher startup behavior
- [ ] `--debug` and `--no-auto-refresh` change runtime behavior without affecting default startup
- [ ] CLI help/docs/release metadata reflect the shipped flag surface
- [ ] Documented exit-code behavior matches the shipped CLI behavior
- [ ] `go test ./...`, `go build ./cmd/bwb`, and `go vet ./...` pass
close_reason: all steps complete

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Finding: Flag-parsing library is undecided and will drive implementer improvisation. Why: stdlib flag does not cleanly support both -h and --help, short-flag clustering, or rejecting -version-as-double-dash; choosing pflag/cobra mid-task is a dependency decision that belongs in planning. Suggested action: pick the library now (recommend stdlib flag with manual -h/--help + -v/--version aliasing to avoid a new dep, OR explicitly adopt spf13/pflag) and record the choice in the epic Decisions section before s2d1.1 starts.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Finding: Exit-code contract is unspecified across flags. Why: --help/--version/--check-config/--print-config and unknown-flag handling have GNU/POSIX conventions that will be inconsistently applied without a single contract; verifier in s2d1.4 cannot check codes that were never specified. Suggested action: add a Decisions bullet to the epic stating: success paths exit 0; --check-config validation failure exits 1; unknown flag / bad usage exits 2; missing required flag arg exits 2.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Finding: --cwd rationale is recorded but the revisit trigger is not. Why: future readers will not know when to reconsider --beads-dir; today projectRoot drives both gateway WorkDir and launcher interpolation, but if BWB ever supports a beads tracker decoupled from repo root the decision must flip. Suggested action: append to Decisions: 'Revisit --beads-dir if BWB ever needs to target a beads tracker that is not co-located with the project root.'

title: Detail view stays three-pane and becomes unreadable at normal terminal widths
description:
## Description
When opening `beads-workbench-2shr` in the real `bwb` app, the detail screen keeps the three-pane Dependencies / Content / Metadata layout even at common terminal widths (for example 80x24 and 100x30). The content pane collapses to a tiny sliver, headers truncate, and the main issue description becomes effectively unreadable.

## Expected Behavior
The detail view should adapt at narrower widths so the main issue content remains readable, with a responsive fallback instead of forcing the full three-pane layout.

## Actual Behavior
At 100x30 the content rail is already heavily compressed, and at 80x24 the content header truncates to `Content · 4 co...` while the description collapses into narrow wrapped fragments and ellipsis rows. The layout looks visibly broken even though the app technically renders.

## Reproduction
1. `go build -o /tmp/bwb ./cmd/bwb`
2. Run `env BD_NON_INTERACTIVE=1 /tmp/bwb` from the repo root.
3. Open epic `beads-workbench-2shr` in detail mode.
4. Observe the detail view at widths like 100x30 or 80x24.

## Impact
This is a user-visible runtime UI regression in the main detail workflow. It makes real issue inspection difficult on normal terminal sizes and means the current responsive detail layout does not meet the runtime UI readability bar.

## Instructions
1. Review the detail renderer width logic in `internal/ui/details/details.go`, especially the unconditional three-pane path in `Render` and `splitThreePaneWidths`.
2. Restore a readable responsive fallback for narrower widths instead of always forcing Dependencies + Content + Metadata together.
3. Update focused detail renderer tests and any goldens that currently encode the overly dense layout.
4. Re-verify with built-binary captures at representative widths (at minimum 80 columns and 100 columns).

## Files to Modify
- `internal/ui/details/details.go`
- `internal/ui/details/details_test.go`
- `internal/ui/details/testdata/*.golden`

## Acceptance Criteria
Opening `beads-workbench-2shr` in the built app produces a readable detail screen at representative narrow/medium terminal widths, and focused detail rendering tests reflect the intended responsive fallback behavior.
close_reason: completed

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Implemented the responsive detail fallback in internal/ui/details so widths below the legacy breakpoint render content first with Dependencies and Metadata below. Verified with go test ./internal/ui/details -v and built-binary captures of beads-workbench-2shr at 100x30 and 80x24 showing the readable content-first layout.

title: Found: GoReleaser docs/task command form fails without explicit version suffix
description:
## Description
The release-readiness task used bare `go run github.com/goreleaser/goreleaser/v2 ...` commands that fail on the current Go toolchain because the module path must include an explicit version suffix when invoked this way.

## Instructions
1. Update release tracking guidance to use an explicit module-version form for local GoReleaser validation.
2. Re-run the corrected commands:
   - `go run github.com/goreleaser/goreleaser/v2@latest check`
   - `go run github.com/goreleaser/goreleaser/v2@latest release --snapshot --clean`
3. Record the successful verification in this bug and in the blocked release-readiness task.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Release guidance uses an explicit module version suffix for local GoReleaser commands
- [ ] Corrected GoReleaser validation commands run successfully
- [ ] The blocked release-readiness task is updated with the corrected command form

## Files to Modify
- beads issue `beads-workbench-yixr.1`
- repository docs only if they contain the broken command form
close_reason: Corrected command form and re-verified successfully

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text:
Fixed by updating release-readiness guidance to use explicit module-version GoReleaser commands. Re-verified successfully on current main with:
- go run github.com/goreleaser/goreleaser/v2@latest check
- go run github.com/goreleaser/goreleaser/v2@latest release --snapshot --clean

No repository docs contained the broken form; the correction was needed in beads release-task guidance.

title: Missing test: Up-from-first-result returns to FocusQuery transition not covered
description:
## Description
The acceptance criteria for beads-workbench-2shr.3 require focused tests covering key transitions. The implementation correctly handles Up-on-first-result → FocusQuery (model.go: `if m.selectedRow <= 0 { m.focus = uisearch.FocusQuery }`), but no unit test in model_test.go explicitly asserts this transition.

## Repro
Open internal/mode/search/model_test.go — no test navigates Down into results then presses Up from the first result and checks that focus returns to FocusQuery.

## Expected
TestSearchModeFocusNavigationAndSelection (or a new test) should assert: after entering results via Down, pressing Up when selectedRow==0 returns focus to FocusQuery.

## Actual
No test covers this key transition.

## Acceptance Criteria
- A test in model_test.go explicitly navigates Down from query into results (selectedRow==0) and presses Up, asserting focus is FocusQuery afterward.
- go test ./internal/mode/search passes.
close_reason: Implemented and verified the remaining query-first search behavior/test landing: explicit Enter-to-search flow, vertical query/results navigation, focused Up-from-first-result coverage, updated help text, and passing focused/full test suites.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Finishing the remaining follow-up: add an explicit focused test for Up-from-first-result returning to query, rerun focused and full test suites, then land only the search-navigation files plus tracker state without touching unrelated working tree changes.

title: Redesign search navigation around query-first vertical flow
description:
## Description
The current search UX feels broken because query, results, content, and metadata are all treated as peer focus states, even though query and results behave like a vertical rail. Redesign search interaction so the query becomes the primary entry state, search execution is explicit, and Up/Down vertically connects query with results while keeping result live preview behavior.

## Interaction Spec
### Query mode
- Query is the primary focus state and should visually dominate when active.
- Typing edits the query text only; it does not automatically trigger a search request.
- Enter executes the search using the current query text.
- Down moves focus from query to results when results exist.
- Up in query is a no-op.

### Results mode
- Results remain live-preview driven: moving selection updates Content + Metadata without Enter.
- Down moves to the next result.
- Up moves to the previous result.
- If focus is on the first result and Up is pressed again, focus returns to query instead of staying trapped in results.

### Preview panes
- Left/Right continues to move horizontally between Results, Content, and Metadata.
- Content Left returns to Results; Content Right moves to Metadata; Metadata Left returns to Content.
- Query is reached vertically from Results, not as a left/right pane.

## Instructions
1. Update search mode keyboard handling to make search execution explicit on Enter while query is focused.
2. Stop triggering a search on every typed character, backspace, or clear-key in query mode.
3. Implement vertical navigation between query and results: Down from query enters results, Up from first result returns to query, otherwise Up/Down traverses results normally.
4. Preserve result-selection live preview behavior so Content + Metadata refresh automatically as results move.
5. Keep horizontal navigation between Results, Content, and Metadata coherent with the new vertical query/results model.
6. Update help text, focused mode tests, and any search goldens affected by the new query-first interaction.
7. Verify focused search/app packages and the full repository test suite.

## Files to Modify
- `internal/mode/search/model.go`
- `internal/mode/search/model_test.go`
- `internal/ui/search/search.go`
- `internal/ui/search/search_test.go`
- `internal/app/model.go` (if shell integration needs adjustment)
- affected search goldens/testdata as needed
acceptance_criteria: Query focus uses a query-first interaction model where typing edits the full-screen query, Enter executes the search, Down moves from query into results when results exist, Up on the first result returns to query, results Up/Down continues moving selection and auto-previewing Content + Metadata, Left/Right continues to navigate preview panes, focused tests cover key transitions, and `go test ./internal/mode/search ./internal/ui/search ./internal/app` plus `go test ./...` pass.
close_reason: Implemented query-first search flow: explicit Enter search, vertical query/results navigation, updated help text, tests, and search goldens; focused and full go test suites pass.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Execution approved. Locked interaction model: typing in query edits only; Enter runs the search; Down from query enters results when available; Up on first result returns to query; result movement keeps live preview; Left/Right only traverses Results <-> Content <-> Metadata.

title: Auto-preview detail panes from dependency selection
description:
## Description
In dedicated detail mode, the dependency/browser pane currently behaves like an opener: Up/Down only changes the highlighted row and Enter is required to load that issue. The user wants it to behave like search results instead: while browsing the dependency/browser pane, changing the highlighted row should immediately refresh the content and metadata panes for that highlighted issue.

## Instructions
1. Preserve dedicated detail mode layout and left/right pane focus behavior.
2. When the dependency/browser pane is focused, Up/Down should change the highlighted issue and immediately request/load/render that issue in the content and metadata panes without Enter.
3. Keep focus in the dependency/browser pane while browsing so repeated Up/Down continues traversing issues.
4. Keep traversal usable across dependency and parent-group browser items when the loaded issue context changes.
5. Make loading/stale state obvious while the newly highlighted issue is still loading.
6. Enter in the dependency/browser pane should become a no-op because selection already auto-previews the highlighted issue.
7. Update focused detail-mode tests and any help/golden coverage affected by the new browsing semantics.
8. Verify focused detail packages and the full repository test suite.

## Files to Modify
- `internal/mode/details/model.go`
- `internal/mode/details/model_test.go`
- `internal/app/model.go`
- `internal/app/model_test.go`
- `internal/ui/details/details.go`
- affected detail goldens/testdata as needed
acceptance_criteria: In dedicated detail mode, moving Up/Down in the dependency/browser pane immediately refreshes content and metadata for the highlighted issue without requiring Enter, traversal stays usable while browsing parent/sibling/dependency items, loading state is visible during async refresh, Enter on the dependency/browser pane is a no-op because selection already auto-previews, and `go test ./internal/mode/details ./internal/ui/details ./internal/app` plus `go test ./...` pass.
close_reason: Implemented and verified: detail dependency browsing now auto-previews content/metadata on selection change, with Enter as no-op and stable anchored selection.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Requested during search planning so dedicated detail dependency browsing matches search-style live preview semantics.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: User approved the dedicated detail dependency-browser live-preview direction and requested reviewer feedback before implementation.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Verdict on approved direction: SOUND but architecturally heavier than the issue currently suggests. What: 'auto-update Content+Metadata on Up/Down in dependency pane' is a one-line behavior change in mode/details/model.go (lines 183-189 already special-case ScrollUp/Down to moveRelatedSelection); the hard parts are everything around it. Why: the issue text reads as a small UX tweak but it actually requires (a) a new shell pathway because detail mode does NOT emit mode.SelectionChangedMsg today (app/model.go:388 hard-filters non-Board/non-Search), (b) a decision about what 'currentSelection' means when the user is browsing a different issue inside detail, (c) repurposing Enter, (d) the same stale-detail rule aqps needs. Suggested action: keep 2shr.2 as-is but mark blocked-by aqps so it can reuse aqps's exported render helpers and stale-loading rule rather than reinventing them.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Architectural finding: detail dependency-browse needs a NEW load path that does not corrupt shell selection. What: today, detail mode's HandleKey on dep-pane Up/Down only updates BrowserSelectedIndex (mode/details/model.go:183-189). To trigger Content+Metadata refresh, something must call loadDetailCmd for the highlighted dep-issue. But if you do this by setting m.detail.SelectionID/TargetID and calling loadDetailCmd, you're overwriting the shell's idea of 'which issue is selected' (app/model.go currentSelection() comes from selectedByMode[Board|Search], NOT m.detail.SelectionID — but ensureDetailForCurrentSelectionCmd will fight the dep-browse on the next mode switch). Why: Esc back to board could now leave the user on a different highlighted row than expected; o (open detail) from board could re-load the wrong issue. Suggested action: introduce an explicit DetailBrowseTargetID separate from m.detail.SelectionID. The browser pane drives DetailBrowseTargetID + loadDetailCmd; m.detail.SelectionID (the shell-anchored selection) only changes on explicit Enter or on Esc-then-reopen. Document this in AC.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Enter-key repurpose decision missing. What: instructions say 'remove or repurpose dependency-pane Enter behavior' but do not pick. Today Enter emits OpenRelatedIssueIntent which the shell handles by replacing m.detail.SelectionID/TargetID and reloading (app/model.go:454-467) — i.e., Enter 'commits' the dep-browsed issue as the new anchored detail. Why: that semantic is actually still useful: live-preview lets you peek; Enter promotes the peeked issue to the new permanent detail anchor (changes header context, makes its dependencies the new browser, makes it the issue Esc-back returns to). Suggested action (recommended): KEEP Enter as 'commit dep-browsed issue as new detail anchor' and add this as an AC bullet. Update mode/details tests to assert that Up/Down loads-but-does-not-anchor while Enter swaps the anchor (and resets the BrowserItems to the newly anchored issue's dependencies).

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: UX regression risk: mid-browse browser-list rewrite. What: when dep-browse loads a new detail, mode/details.ApplyLoadedDetail calls syncBrowserPanel which calls browserItemsFromDependencies on the NEW detail and calls selectBrowserIssue(issueID) — meaning the browser list under the cursor gets replaced mid-browse if the user lingers on a row long enough for ShowIssue to resolve. Why: this will jerk the cursor to a different position (or to -1 if the new issue's dep set doesn't contain the highlighted one), breaking 'keep traversal usable across dependency items' from the AC. Suggested action: when in live-preview mode (Up/Down without Enter), do NOT call ApplyLoadedDetail's syncBrowserPanel — only update Content+Metadata data. Browser list should only be rebuilt on Enter (anchor commit). Add this as an explicit AC bullet and add a test that Up/Down preserves BrowserItems and BrowserSelectedIndex across detailLoadedMsg.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Tests essential before merge. What: (1) mode/details test that Up/Down in dep pane (a) advances BrowserSelectedIndex, (b) emits a loadDetail intent for the new ID, (c) does NOT change m.detail.SelectionID; (2) test that Enter in dep pane DOES change SelectionID and rebuilds browser list; (3) app/model integration test that dep-browse loadDetail does not overwrite selectedByMode[Board]; (4) app/model test that Esc from detail returns user to original board selection (NOT the dep-browsed issue); (5) loading affordance test mirroring aqps's stale-preview test; (6) golden update for any detail-mode wide rendering that shows 'browsing: bw-X (preview)' header context vs anchored. Suggested action: add this list to AC.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Acceptance criteria gaps after user clarification: (1) browse-vs-anchor distinction (DetailBrowseTargetID vs SelectionID), (2) Enter semantic ('commit anchor'), (3) browser-list immutability during live preview (only rebuild on Enter), (4) header context indication that user is previewing vs anchored, (5) Esc behavior (return to original anchored detail or to board?), (6) reuse of aqps's stale-loading affordance. Suggested action: add each as an AC bullet before implementation starts.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: User clarified final interaction rule: Enter in the dependency/browser pane is now a no-op because Up/Down already auto-select and auto-preview issues. Also added dependency on aqps so shared pane extraction/loading rules land first.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Implementation decisions locked for execution: aqps lands first; detail dependency live preview reuses aqps shared pane helpers and stale/loading rule; preview browsing must keep the dependency/browser list stable while moving through rows; Up/Down previews immediately, Enter is a no-op, and board/search anchored selections must not be corrupted by detail browse preview state.

title: Acceptance Review: Improve developer workflow and validation tooling
description:
## Description
Verify the developer-workflow/tooling epic before closure.

## Instructions
1. Confirm the Makefile, hook workflow, and validation changes are all implemented and documented.
2. Run the relevant Makefile targets and direct commands to ensure the wrappers match the documented workflow.
3. Confirm the hook install flow is documented and the validation scope remains intentionally minimal for this repo.
4. Ensure any follow-up gaps are tracked separately rather than silently folded into this epic.

## Files to Inspect
- `Makefile`
- `.github/workflows/ci.yml`
- `.golangci.yml`
- `.golangci-version`
- `scripts/git-hooks/pre-commit`
- `README.md`
- `docs/CODING.md`
- `docs/TESTING.md`
- `docs/RUNTIME_UI_VERIFICATION.md`
- `docs/CHANGE-WORKFLOW.md`
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Makefile targets work and remain thin wrappers around documented commands
- [ ] The repo-managed pre-commit hook installs and formats staged Go files correctly
- [ ] CI runs the intended script validation and conservative lint checks
- [ ] Documentation is aligned across README, docs, and workflow guidance
- [ ] Any follow-up issues are tracked separately
close_reason: Verified: all criteria met. Makefile targets are thin wrappers (build/test/vet/lint/validate-scripts/install-hooks all pass). CI pipeline runs script validation, pinned golangci-lint (0 issues), build, vet, and test. Pre-commit hook installs correctly via make install-hooks and formats staged Go files. Docs aligned in CHANGE-WORKFLOW.md. Failing tests (internal/mode/search, internal/ui/search, internal/testing/ui) are pre-existing, unrelated to this epic's scope, and tracked in open bugs beads-workbench-yumi and beads-workbench-72vc.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Implementation tasks completed. Follow-up bugs beads-workbench-yumi and beads-workbench-72vc were filed for unrelated failing test suites discovered during task verification. Acceptance review must determine whether those follow-ups block epic closure or can remain tracked separately.

title: Improve developer workflow and validation tooling
description:
## Description
Improve the local developer workflow for beads-workbench with a thin Makefile, a lightweight repository-managed git hook, and conservative validation checks inspired by ai-config-manager but adapted to this repo.

## Success Criteria

- [ ] A repo-managed pre-commit hook can format staged Go files consistently
- [ ] CI and local validation cover the repo's actual helper script surface (`.sh` and `.py`) and a conservative Go lint profile
- [ ] Docs explain the workflow without creating a second source of truth
- [ ] Acceptance review confirms the workflow is usable and aligned across docs, Makefile, and CI

title: Polish ad-hoc verification toolkit usability and debug ergonomics
description:
## Description
Track the focused usability improvements made to the ad-hoc UI verification toolkit after real verifier feedback.

## Scope
This polish work keeps the toolkit small and script-oriented while improving day-to-day usability:
- field-specific state assertions in the wrapper
- one stronger transient-message wait primitive in the PTY helper
- better failure/debug evidence on capture failures
- explicit guardrail to keep the wrapper thin and non-framework-like

## Outcome
The work should make the helper scripts faster to trust in ad-hoc UI investigation without expanding them into a generalized test framework.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Wrapper supports field-targeted verification for representative mutation flows.
- [ ] PTY helper supports a transient-message wait primitive.
- [ ] Failure output/debug evidence is more useful than before.
- [ ] Real-app verification confirms the scripts remain practical and small.
close_reason: Usability/debug polish completed via beads-workbench-7t7u and verified

title: Search preview metadata goldens are stale after gutter implementation in 3lhw
description:
## Summary

After task beads-workbench-3lhw added a permanent 2-char selection gutter to the metadata pane, the search preview pane renders the metadata with the new gutter (e.g. `  Core`, `  Type    : task`), but the golden files for the search package still expect the old format without the gutter.

## Failing tests

```
go test ./internal/ui/search/...
--- FAIL: TestRenderGoldens/results_with_preview_w120
--- FAIL: TestRenderGoldens/results_narrow_w80
--- FAIL: TestRenderGoldens/default_all_results_w120
```

## Actual vs Expected

Actual output (correct new behavior):
- `  Core` (2-char idle gutter before group title)
- `  Type    : task` (2-char idle gutter before field)
- `  -----...` (2-char idle gutter before divider)

Golden files still expect:
- `Core` (no gutter)
- `Type    : task` (no gutter)
- `-------...` (divider at col 0)

## Repro

```bash
go test ./internal/ui/search/... -run TestRenderGoldens -v
```

## Fix

Update the three failing golden files in `internal/ui/search/testdata/` to match the new 2-char gutter format:
- `search_results_preview_w120.golden`
- `search_results_narrow_w80.golden`
- `search_default_all_results_w120.golden`

Or regenerate them with `UPDATE_GOLDEN=1 go test ./internal/ui/search/...`.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `go test ./internal/ui/search/...` passes with no failures
- [ ] The updated goldens show the 2-char gutter (`  `) on all metadata lines in the search preview panel
close_reason: Updated stale search preview goldens after metadata gutter implementation and verified focused search tests.
