title: CreateIssue cannot express bd priority 0
description: domain.CreateIssueInput stores Priority as an int and writes.go only appends --priority when input.Priority != 0. Because bd create accepts 0 as the highest priority, the gateway cannot create P0 issues: passing 0 is treated as omitted and bd falls back to its default priority instead.
acceptance_criteria:
- CreateIssue can explicitly request bd priority 0
- Omitted priority remains distinguishable from explicit priority 0
- Tests cover create with explicit priority 0 against command argument construction
close_reason: Made create priority explicit with pointer semantics and P0 test coverage

title: Gateway search/query models are missing Phase 1 structured filters
description: The current domain SearchIssuesQuery and related gateway shapes expose text, status, type, assignee, and labels, but do not expose priority, ready, or blocked filters that the product docs call out for the standalone search/narrowing experience. This leaves a mismatch between the gateway contract and the downstream Phase 1/Phase 3 UI expectations.
acceptance_criteria:
- Gateway query models support priority filtering needed by search/browse flows
- Gateway query models support ready and blocked narrowing without UI-layer shelling out
- The chosen API shape is documented and covered by tests
close_reason: Extended gateway query models for priority and ready/blocked filtering with documented CLI-backed behavior

title: No concrete CLI gateway implements BeadsGateway
description: Read methods are implemented on internal/gateway/beads.CLIGateway while write methods are implemented on internal/gateway/beads.Gateway. The shared interface in internal/gateway/beads/interface.go expects one BeadsGateway with both read and write methods, so Phase 1 consumers cannot construct a single CLI-backed implementation that satisfies the interface.
acceptance_criteria:
- One concrete CLI-backed type implements all methods on BeadsGateway
- Construction API for the CLI gateway returns a BeadsGateway-compatible implementation
- Read and write tests run against the same concrete gateway type
close_reason: Unified read and write methods onto one CLI gateway implementation

title: IssueSummary assignee mapping does not match bd list/ready/search JSON
description: The gateway maps IssueSummary.Assignee from the JSON field 'assignee' in issueSummaryFromMap(), but actual bd list --json, bd ready --json, and bd search --json output in this repo exposes the field as 'owner'. As a result, assignee data is dropped from read/search summaries.
acceptance_criteria:
- ListIssues maps assignee/owner data from the official bd JSON surface into IssueSummary.Assignee
- ReadyIssues maps assignee/owner data from the official bd JSON surface into IssueSummary.Assignee
- SearchIssues maps assignee/owner data from the official bd JSON surface into IssueSummary.Assignee
- Tests cover the real field shape returned by bd
close_reason: Mapped IssueSummary assignee from official owner field with fallback coverage

title: Found: missing Go module deps break vet/test in ui overlay
description: Discovered while working on beads-workbench-omd.3. Running go vet ./... and go test ./... fails in internal/ui/overlay and internal/ui/styles because go.mod does not include github.com/charmbracelet/lipgloss and github.com/charmbracelet/x/ansi.
close_reason: Resolved by adding required UI module dependencies during yze.2.2

title: Add architecture guardrails and quality gates
description:
## Description
Add automated guardrails that enforce the standalone architecture and quality gates during implementation. The current plan repeatedly says "no SQL, no BQL, no orchestration," but those constraints need build-time enforcement so donor transplants cannot silently reintroduce forbidden dependencies.

## Instructions
1. Add automated checks that fail the build or test run when code imports forbidden legacy paths or forbidden dependency classes.
2. Enforce these architectural boundaries at minimum:
   - no direct SQL in the active product path
   - no `internal/bql` dependency in the standalone app
   - no orchestration/control-plane subsystem in the active product path
3. Add standard quality gates for `go build ./cmd/bwb` and `go vet ./...` so acceptance reviews do not rely on implied checks.
4. Document the enforced architectural boundaries and quality gates in `docs/CODING.md`.
5. Keep the enforcement lightweight and automated so agents can run it in CI-like local verification.

## Files to Modify
- project-local build/test verification scripts or Go-based checks (create)
- `docs/CODING.md` (update)
- `*_test.go` or verification helpers as needed

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Automated checks fail if forbidden SQL/BQL/orchestration dependencies are introduced
- [ ] `go build ./cmd/bwb` is part of the standard quality gate
- [ ] `go vet ./...` is part of the standard quality gate
- [ ] `docs/CODING.md` documents the enforced architecture guardrails and quality gates
close_reason: Added architecture guardrails and documented quality gates

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text:
## omd.8 has no upstream dependency — can execute in parallel with omd.1, but SHOULD depend on omd.1

**What**: `beads-workbench-omd.8` (architecture guardrails) is currently ready to work with zero dependencies. It blocks omd.6 and omd.7. However, the guardrails task needs to enforce import constraints across `internal/` and run `go build ./cmd/bwb` — neither of which is meaningful until the repository bootstrap (omd.1) creates the module, the package layout, and the binary entrypoint.

**Why**: If an agent claims omd.8 before omd.1 completes, there is no `go.mod`, no `internal/` tree, and no `cmd/bwb` to build. The task will either fail or produce guardrail scripts that target a nonexistent codebase. The task is structurally independent but practically requires omd.1.

**Suggested action**: Add dependency: `omd.8 depends-on omd.1`. This does not change the critical path (omd.8 already blocks omd.6 which depends on omd.4/omd.5 which depend on omd.3 which depends on omd.2 which depends on omd.1), but it prevents a premature claim.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text:
## PASS: Gateway-first architecture is now protected by automated guardrails task

**What**: Task beads-workbench-omd.8 ("Add architecture guardrails and quality gates") explicitly requires automated checks that fail when forbidden SQL/BQL/orchestration dependencies are introduced. It requires documenting these guardrails in `docs/CODING.md`. It blocks the test verification task (omd.6) and the acceptance review (omd.7), meaning no gateway work can be accepted without these guardrails in place.

**Why**: This was the single biggest structural gap in the original plan — every task said "no SQL" but nothing enforced it. The guardrails task closes that gap. Combined with the existing per-task forbidden-path lists and the acceptance review checks, the gateway-first architecture now has defense in depth: task-level instructions (human-readable), automated import checks (machine-enforceable), and acceptance review gates (verification checkpoints).

**Suggested action**: None. This is a strong protection. The guardrails task is well-placed in the dependency chain and has clear acceptance criteria.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text:
## [PASS] Architecture guardrails task closes the automated enforcement gap

**What**: beads-workbench-omd.8 directly addresses the first-pass finding about no automated architectural enforcement. It requires automated checks that fail on forbidden SQL/BQL/orchestration imports, and adds go build/go vet as standard quality gates. It correctly blocks omd.6 (gateway tests) so the guardrails exist before the test verification pass.

**Why**: This converts the repeated "no SQL, no BQL" instructions from prose conventions into build-time enforcement. Agent verifiers can now run the guardrail checks as part of `go test ./...` rather than manually inspecting imports.

**Suggested action**: No structural change needed. The task is correctly scoped and positioned.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text:
## [MINOR] CODING.md guardrails documentation is now planned but only in omd.8 — not in any acceptance review criterion

**What**: Task omd.8 ("Add architecture guardrails and quality gates") correctly requires: "docs/CODING.md documents the enforced architecture guardrails and quality gates." However, the Phase 1 acceptance review (omd.7) says "docs/CODING.md matches the implemented foundation architecture" — which is generic enough that a reviewer might not specifically check whether the guardrails enforcement mechanism is documented in CODING.md.

**Why**: omd.8 is a new task that introduces an important new capability: automated import-path enforcement. If CODING.md documents only the package layout and build commands but doesn't explain that forbidden-import guardrails exist and how they work, a future agent could inadvertently add code that bypasses the guardrails (e.g., a new test file that imports database/sql for "legitimate test reasons" and trips the enforcement). The guardrails are only useful if agents know they exist.

**Suggested action**: This is adequately covered as-is — omd.8's own acceptance criteria require the CODING.md update, and omd.7 depends on omd.8 being closed. The dependency chain ensures the guardrails documentation lands before acceptance. No change needed, but flagging for awareness.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text:
## MINOR: omd.8 has no dependency on omd.1 — guardrails could theoretically be built before the repo exists

**What**: Task omd.8 (architecture guardrails) has no explicit dependency on any prior task. It blocks omd.6 and omd.7 but doesn't depend on omd.1 (bootstrap) or omd.2 (interfaces). In theory an agent could attempt to create guardrail checks before the repo is bootstrapped or before the package layout exists.

**Why**: In practice this is unlikely to cause problems because the guardrails need Go packages to scan and those won't exist until after bootstrap. But the missing dependency means the task could be picked up as "ready" work prematurely. The dependency chain should reflect that guardrails need the codebase to exist first.

**Suggested action**: Add a dependency: omd.8 depends on omd.1 (bootstrap). This prevents premature pickup and makes the execution order explicit.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text:
## [MINOR] Architecture guardrails task has no dependency — could start before package layout exists

**What**: omd.8 "Add architecture guardrails and quality gates" has no DEPENDS ON relationship. It blocks omd.6 and omd.7. But the guardrails check import paths across `internal/` — those packages don't exist until omd.1 (bootstrap) creates them.

**Why**: An agent starting omd.8 before omd.1 would be writing import-path checks against empty directories. The checks need actual code to validate against. This is minor because omd.8 blocks omd.6 which depends on omd.4/omd.5, so in practice the code will exist by the time it matters. But the missing dependency creates confusing scheduling.

**Suggested action**: Add dependency: omd.8 DEPENDS ON omd.1 (bootstrap). The guardrails need the package layout to exist.

title: Bootstrap Beads Workbench repository foundation
description:
## Description
Set up the new standalone repository so implementation proceeds in `beads-workbench` instead of continuing inside the donor `perles` repo. This creates the minimal executable Go foundation and package skeleton required for all later work.

## Instructions
1. Before creating code, review the donor/reference guidance in `project-plan/EXECUTION-PLAN.md` and `project-plan/IMPLEMENTATION.md` so the new repo uses `/home/hans/dev/github/perles` only as a selective donor, not as an architecture template.
2. Create `go.mod` with module path `github.com/hk9890/beads-workbench`.
3. Create a minimal binary entrypoint under `cmd/bwb/` that builds successfully.
4. Create the initial package layout described by `project-plan/IMPLEMENTATION.md`:
   - `internal/app/`
   - `internal/domain/`
   - `internal/gateway/beads/`
   - `internal/launcher/`
   - `internal/dashboard/`
   - `internal/mode/`
   - `internal/ui/`
5. Ensure the copied planning docs remain in `project-plan/` and are referenced from the repo docs where useful.
6. Update `docs/CODING.md` so the initial package-layout section reflects the actual bootstrapped layout rather than a future/planned state.
7. Add any minimal README or provenance scaffolding needed for the standalone repo identity.
8. Do not copy SQL, BQL, or orchestration code into the new active path. Treat `/home/hans/dev/github/perles/internal/orchestration/`, `/home/hans/dev/github/perles/internal/bql/`, `/home/hans/dev/github/perles/internal/beads/infrastructure/`, and `/home/hans/dev/github/perles/internal/app/app.go` as forbidden starting points.

## Files to Modify
- `go.mod` (create)
- `cmd/bwb/` (create)
- `internal/app/` (create)
- `internal/domain/` (create)
- `internal/gateway/beads/` (create)
- `internal/launcher/` (create)
- `internal/dashboard/` (create)
- `internal/mode/` (create)
- `internal/ui/` (create)
- `docs/CODING.md` (update)
- `README.md` (create or update, if needed)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Donor/reference guidance in `project-plan/` has been reviewed before bootstrapping
- [ ] `go.mod` exists with module path `github.com/hk9890/beads-workbench`
- [ ] `go build ./cmd/bwb` succeeds
- [ ] `cmd/bwb/` exists with a minimal entrypoint
- [ ] The planned package layout exists in `internal/`
- [ ] `docs/CODING.md` reflects the actual bootstrapped package layout
- [ ] `project-plan/` remains present in the repo
- [ ] No SQL, BQL, or orchestration package is added to the active foundation path
close_reason: Implemented repository bootstrap foundation

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text:
## docs/CODING.md will be immediately stale after bootstrap

**What**: `docs/CODING.md` line 20 says "Planned layout — directories will be created during implementation" and lists the intended package structure. This task creates the actual directories. After this task closes, the doc will still say "planned" when the layout is real. Meanwhile, `CLAUDE.md` lines 53-69 contain placeholder sections ("_Add your build and test commands here_", "_Add a brief overview of your project architecture_") that should be populated during bootstrap.

**Why**: `AGENTS.md` line 9 sends every future agent to `docs/CODING.md` as the primary coding reference. If the doc says "planned" when the layout exists, or if `CLAUDE.md` says "add your commands here" when `go build ./cmd/bwb` is the correct command, the first session after bootstrap starts with wrong context. This is the cheapest moment to fix both — the person doing bootstrap knows exactly what landed.

**Suggested action**: Add two acceptance criteria:
- `docs/CODING.md` "Planned layout" section is updated to reflect the actual created layout (remove "Planned layout — directories will be created during implementation" language)
- `CLAUDE.md` placeholder sections are populated with real build/test commands, architecture overview, and coding conventions that match what was bootstrapped

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text:
## [LOW] Bootstrap task has strong forbidden-path list — positive finding

**What**: The task explicitly lists four forbidden donor starting points by absolute path: orchestration, bql, beads/infrastructure, and app/app.go. The acceptance criteria include "No SQL, BQL, or orchestration package is added to the active foundation path."

**Why**: This is a well-constructed guardrail. Naming the specific forbidden paths removes ambiguity for the implementing agent.

**Suggested action**: None. This is a strong protection.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text:
## [MINOR] docs/CODING.md update is not in omd.1 acceptance criteria — still relies on omd.7 backstop only

**What**: The prior review comment on omd.1 specifically recommended adding acceptance criteria requiring docs/CODING.md to be updated from "planned layout" to actual layout during the bootstrap task. The updated omd.1 acceptance criteria still do not include a docs update requirement. The docs gate only appears at omd.7 (acceptance review).

**Why**: This is a minor gap, not a blocker, because omd.7 now catches it. But the optimal time to update "Planned layout — directories will be created during implementation" is during omd.1 when the person doing the bootstrap knows exactly what was created. Deferring to omd.7 means either the acceptance reviewer does it (wrong — reviewers should verify, not implement) or the reviewer creates a follow-up task for a 30-second docs fix that should have been done inline.

**Suggested action**: Add to omd.1 acceptance criteria: "docs/CODING.md 'Planned layout' language is updated to reflect actually-created directories." This is low-severity because omd.7 is the backstop, but it would be cleaner to fix it at the source.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text:
## [MINOR] No implementation task explicitly updates CODING.md package layout — only acceptance reviews verify it

**What**: Multiple acceptance reviews verify that docs/CODING.md reflects the current architecture. But looking at the implementation tasks themselves, only omd.8 and yze.2.6 explicitly list "docs/CODING.md (update)" in their Files to Modify. The tasks that actually create new packages — omd.1 (bootstrap), omd.2 (domain/gateway interfaces), yze.2.1 (app model), yze.3.x (mode controllers) — do not include docs/CODING.md in their scope.

**Why**: This means the actual CODING.md update work falls to acceptance reviewers, who are supposed to verify, not implement. The reviewer at omd.7 will find CODING.md says "planned layout" and have to either update it themselves or create a follow-up task. This isn't catastrophic because the acceptance reviews catch it, but it creates unnecessary rework cycles.

**Suggested action**: This is a low-severity pattern issue. The acceptance review backstop works. But for clean execution, consider adding "Update docs/CODING.md package layout section to reflect created packages" to omd.1's scope. This is the single most impactful place to fix it since omd.1 creates the entire initial package tree.

title: Replace context.Background() with model ctx in loadDashboardCmd
description: Discovered while reviewing s4r7. The board model's loadDashboardCmd uses context.Background() instead of the model's ctx at internal/mode/board/model.go:554. Out-of-scope for s4r7 (zero-behavior epic). Investigate ctx-cancellation semantics on board reload.

title: beads.leanSearchMetadataFromBackend: do not report Partial when caller requested no limit
description:
## Description

`internal/repository/beads/lean_reads.go:620` (`leanSearchMetadataFromBackend`):

```
if requestedLimit <= 0 || returnedCount < requestedLimit {
    metadata.Completeness = domain.SearchResultCompletenessPartial
}
```

When the caller passes `query.Limit=0` (no limit, return all), the result is by definition exhaustive — but the code still reports Partial. The UI then shows the "maybe more / load more" affordance for a result set that has nothing more to load. The same pattern likely exists in `searchPageFromRecords` (~line 539-542) — verify and fix in one pass.

## Instructions

1. Change the condition to: when `requestedLimit <= 0`, completeness is `Exact`. When `requestedLimit > 0`, `Partial` only if `returnedCount >= requestedLimit` (i.e., bd may have truncated).
2. Apply the same fix in `searchPageFromRecords` if the same pattern is present.

## Files to Modify

- `internal/repository/beads/lean_reads.go` (~line 620 and possibly ~line 539-542)
- new test in `internal/repository/beads/` covering the helper

## Test

Add `TestLeanSearchMetadata_NoLimit_ReportsExact`:
- requestedLimit=0, returnedCount=5 → Exact
- requestedLimit=10, returnedCount=5 → Exact
- requestedLimit=10, returnedCount=10 → Partial
- requestedLimit=10, returnedCount=15 → Partial (defensive)

Pure helper — no bd subprocess needed.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Limit=0 yields Completeness=Exact
- [ ] requestedLimit>0 with returnedCount<requestedLimit yields Exact
- [ ] requestedLimit>0 with returnedCount>=requestedLimit yields Partial
- [ ] New test covers all four cases
- [ ] `go build ./...`, `go vet ./...`, `go test ./internal/repository/beads/...` pass
close_reason: completed; tests pass, no regression

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Both leanSearchMetadataFromBackend AND searchPageFromRecords had the identical bug; both fixed. New default is Exact; Partial only when requestedLimit>0 AND returnedCount>=requestedLimit. New test file lean_reads_test.go with two table-driven tests (5 subtests each). Tasker flagged two non-blocking follow-ups: (1) SearchResultCompletenessMaybeMore now unreachable from these paths, (2) leanSearchMetadataFromBackend still sets Notice=searchNoticeMaybeMore unconditionally though UI doesn't read Notice. quality:fast green (990 tests). Commit 67849de.

title: Persist dashboardCache so warm-restart shows full prior view without first navigation
description:
## Outcome (post-kxci follow-up)

The caching backend's warm-restart "sub-second first paint" mechanism (kxci.7) is correct but only delivers full benefit when memory.Repository already has the dashboard's underlying issues. Currently the per-ID cache only populates on `Issue(id)` calls; the Dashboard view itself lives in a separate `dashboardCache` field that is NOT persisted.

Net effect: a fresh user (or short prior session with little navigation) gets cold Dashboard on warm restart even though the kxci.8 cross-session resolver finds the prior cache file.

## Approaches

Option A — persist dashboardCache as a sibling file:
- `repo.jsonl.dashboard.json` adjacent to `repo.jsonl.manifest.json`
- filestorage.SaveWithDashboard / LoadWithDashboard
- caching.SaveNow serialises current dashboardCache; caching.Hydrate restores it if hash matches

Option B — pre-populate per-ID cache from Dashboard():
- When caching.Dashboard fetches from backing, also fetch each issue's detail in background and store
- Higher cost; bigger files; only useful for issues that fit in the dashboard

Option C — extend Repository or add Dashboard-summary cache:
- Persist DashboardData directly (it's an IssueSummary slice, smaller than full details)
- Cheapest persistence; matches what's actually rendered

Recommended: Option C. Smallest disk footprint; matches the actual data the dashboard renders; simplest hydration logic.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] dashboardCache persists across sessions when hash matches
- [ ] Round-trip test: SaveNow + Hydrate (matching hash) → Dashboard() returns same DashboardData with zero backing calls
- [ ] Integration test: two consecutive bwb caching sessions; second renders full dashboard without backing fan-out (measured via log inspection)
- [ ] No regression: tests from kxci.7/.8 still pass
- [ ] mise run quality green
close_reason: Deferred — not pursuing dashboardCache persistence at this time.

title: Document caching component log lines in docs/MONITORING.md
description:
## Outcome

Also: tick failures from `vcStatusFunc` errors and periodic save errors. If currently silent (per kxci.3 "silent for now"), either:

Added 'Caching backend diagnostics' section to docs/MONITORING.md documenting all 6 caching log lines: 4 startup/shutdown (cmd/bwb/main.go) + 2 cache resolver (cache_resolver.go from kxci.8 — orchestrator missed these in original spec, found and documented). 3 silent-by-design conditions also noted (Hydrate ErrSchemaMismatch/ErrNotExist, tick vcStatusFunc errors, periodic SaveNow errors — both with in-code TODOs for future logging). Purely additive. fmt:check goimports drift on cache_resolver.go (pre-existing from kxci.8) fixed in orchestrator pass.

title: Test per-ID isolation: UpdateIssue(X) preserves Issue(Y) cache
description:
## Outcome

Unit test asserts that mutating one cached issue does not bust unrelated cached issues. The implementation already does this correctly via `memory.Forget(id)`, but the test was missing.

## Files (modify)
- `internal/repository/caching/caching_test.go` — add `TestUpdateIssue_DoesNotBustUnrelatedIssue`

## Test design
```go
func TestUpdateIssue_DoesNotBustUnrelatedIssue(t *testing.T) {
    backing := newCountingFakeRepo() // increments per-method call counts
    backing.seed("X", ...)  // seed both X and Y in backing
    backing.seed("Y", ...)
    cache := caching.New(backing)
    
    // Prime the cache with both X and Y
    _, _ = cache.Issue(ctx, "X")  // backing.Issue called once
    _, _ = cache.Issue(ctx, "Y")  // backing.Issue called once
    assert backing.IssueCalls == 2
    
    // Update X
    err := cache.UpdateIssue(ctx, "X", input)
    require.NoError(t, err)
    assert backing.UpdateIssueCalls == 1
    
    // Read X again — should hit backing (was busted)
    _, _ = cache.Issue(ctx, "X")
    assert backing.IssueCalls == 3
    
    // Read Y — should still hit cache (NOT busted)
    _, _ = cache.Issue(ctx, "Y")
    assert backing.IssueCalls == 3  // unchanged
}
```

Similarly add `TestCloseIssue_DoesNotBustUnrelatedIssue` and `TestAddComment_DoesNotBustUnrelatedIssue`.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Test asserts Issue(X) is dropped after UpdateIssue(X)
- [ ] Test asserts Issue(Y) is NOT dropped after UpdateIssue(X)
- [ ] Same coverage for CloseIssue and AddComment
- [ ] `mise run quality` green
close_reason: Closed

comments.text: Done. 3 tests added at caching_test.go:1972, 2023, 2073: TestUpdateIssue/CloseIssue/AddComment_DoesNotBustUnrelatedIssue. Each primes both X and Y in cache, mutates X, asserts stub.issueCalls increments to 3 on Issue(X) re-read (busted) but stays at 3 on Issue(Y) re-read (preserved). Uses existing stubRepository fake; no testify imports. mise run quality green (1203 unit / 1376 integration, -race clean).

title: chore(gateway): document bounded retention of read cache (F4)
description:
## Description
`internal/gateway/beads/cache.go`'s `readCache.entries` is a `map[string]cacheEntry` with no TTL, no max-size, and no eviction. Retention is bounded in practice by user-driven argv variation (each distinct search query / `show <id>` adds one entry, cleared on writes). It is unbounded in principle.

Complexity review F4: no TTL/LRU implementation requested — just an explicit one-line acknowledgement.

## Files to Modify
- `internal/gateway/beads/cache.go` — add one comment near the `entries` field declaration.

## Instructions
1. Open `internal/gateway/beads/cache.go`.
2. Above the `entries` field on the cache struct, add a comment:
   ```go
   // entries is unbounded in principle but bounded in practice by user-driven argv variation:
   // each distinct query/show argv adds one entry, all entries clear on any write
   // (see invalidate()). No TTL/LRU until measurement shows steady-state growth.
   ```
3. No behavioral change.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Comment present at the `entries` field.
- [ ] `go vet ./...` and `go build ./...` pass.
close_reason: Bounded-retention comment landed above readCache.entries; no behavior change; quality:fast green.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Added 3-line retention-bounding comment immediately above readCache.entries in internal/gateway/beads/cache.go. No behavioral change. Untouched: the singleflight field/import/docblock (reserved for dfc3, which is now unblocked). quality:fast green.

title: chore(board): surface both readyExplain and storedBlocked errors on Not Ready load (F5)
description:
## Description
In `internal/mode/board/model.go`, when the Not Ready column load fails, `notReadyErr` is the first non-nil of `readyExplainErr` and `storedBlockedErr` (around lines 481–484). A `storedBlocked` failure disappears silently if `readyExplain` also failed. This is an artifact of F1 — without the 5th parallel call there'd be only one error source.

**F1 was declined** ([[f568]] closed won't-do, 2026-05-23): the downstream merge in `internal/mode/board/model.go` is permanent. This task therefore proceeds unconditionally — replace the first-non-nil pattern with `errors.Join(readyExplainErr, storedBlockedErr)` so both sources surface when both fail.

Complexity review F5.

## Files to Modify
- `internal/mode/board/model.go` — replace the `notReadyErr` first-non-nil pattern (around lines 481–484) with `errors.Join(readyExplainErr, storedBlockedErr)`. `errors.Join` returns nil when all inputs are nil and returns a single-wrapping error when exactly one is non-nil, so single-source-fail behavior is preserved at the call site (the returned error still wraps the original).
- New or extended test in `internal/mode/board/` (e.g. `model_errors_test.go` or extend an existing test file in this package) — inject failing fakes for both `ReadyExplain` and the storedBlocked `Query`, assert both substrings appear in the surfaced error.

## Instructions
1. Locate the `notReadyErr := readyExplainErr; if notReadyErr == nil { notReadyErr = storedBlockedErr }` pattern (or equivalent) in `internal/mode/board/model.go` around lines 481–484.
2. Replace with `notReadyErr := errors.Join(readyExplainErr, storedBlockedErr)`. Add `"errors"` to the import block if not already present.
3. Verify the zero-error case (both nil) still produces `nil` — `errors.Join` documented behavior.
4. Verify the one-error case still wraps the original — `errors.Is(notReadyErr, originalErr)` returns true.
5. Add a focused unit test under `internal/mode/board/`: build a `FakeBeadsGateway`, call `SetError(MethodReadyExplain, errReady)` AND `SetError(MethodQuery, errStored)` (use the actual method constant for the storedBlocked Query call — confirm from gateway code), drive the Not Ready load, assert `notReadyErr.Error()` contains substrings from BOTH `errReady` and `errStored`. Use `errors.Is(notReadyErr, errReady) && errors.Is(notReadyErr, errStored)` if both wraps are preserved by `errors.Join`.
6. Run `go test ./internal/mode/board/...` and confirm both new and existing tests pass.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `notReadyErr` is built via `errors.Join(readyExplainErr, storedBlockedErr)`.
- [ ] Both errors are surfaced when both sources fail (new test asserts both substrings appear).
- [ ] `errors.Is(notReadyErr, errReady)` and `errors.Is(notReadyErr, errStored)` both true when both fail.
- [ ] Single-source-fail behavior unchanged (asserted by existing tests or new sub-test).
- [ ] Full quality gate green.
close_reason: notReadyErr now errors.Join'd; new test asserts both errors.Is(sentinel) and substring presence; falsifiability verified; quality:fast green.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Plan-grill resolved (2026-05-23): adopted reviewer Q3 — stripped the F1-conditional 'if [[f568]] relocates the merge then this folds away' branch from Description and Instructions step 1. F1 is declined per [[f568]] close; this task proceeds unconditionally with errors.Join.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Implemented. notReadyErr now uses errors.Join(readyExplainErr, storedBlockedErr) in internal/mode/board/model.go (errors stdlib import added). New TestBoardModeNotReadyBothErrorSourcesSurface in internal/mode/board/model_test.go injects sentinels on both ReadyExplain and storedBlocked, asserts both substring match AND errors.Is for each sentinel. Falsifiability verified: reverting to first-non-nil pattern fails both assertions. mise run test, mise run quality:fast, and mise run fmt:check all green.

title: Search 'partial' completeness badge is misleading; 'exact' badge/enum is dead code
description:
What I did: In search mode (embedded fixture, 5 issues), committed queries with Enter: 'blocked' (2 results), 'zzqqxxnomatch' (0 results), and empty/cleared (5 results). The Results pane header always showed '<n> partial' — '2 partial', '0 partial', '5 partial'.

Expected (source: docs/break-it.md Consistency; general UX): a completeness badge should help a user judge whether they are seeing all results. 'partial' intuitively means 'incomplete — there may be more'.

Actual — two coupled problems in the SearchResultCompleteness model:

1. MISLEADING LABEL. internal/gateway/beads/read_gateway.go:555-557 and 567-570 set Completeness = Partial precisely when 'returnedCount < requestedLimit || no limit' — i.e. when the result set did NOT hit the backend cap, meaning the user IS seeing everything available. The UI (internal/ui/search/search.go:365-366) renders that as the badge 'partial'. So a complete 2-of-2 result set is labelled '2 partial', which reads as 'incomplete'. The semantics are inverted from the word. Meanwhile the cap-hit case (MaybeMore) renders as 'capped'. Two badges ('partial', 'capped') both connote incompleteness and are applied to opposite situations.

2. DEAD ENUM + DEAD UI BRANCH. domain.SearchResultCompletenessExact (internal/domain/issues.go:73) and its UI branch (internal/ui/search/search.go:361-362 -> 'exact') are never reachable. The gateway only ever assigns MaybeMore or Partial (confirmed: grep of all non-test Completeness assignments; and the interface contract internal/gateway/beads/interface.go:443-446 explicitly documents only MaybeMore/Partial). 'exact' completeness can never be produced or displayed.

Severity: low. No functional break — it is a metadata badge plus an unreachable enum value. But the badge actively misinforms during normal search use, and the dead 'exact' value/branch is exactly the class of inconsistency the (now-closed) consistency-cleanup epic beads-workbench-5rvn targeted (this specific item was NOT in 5rvn's findings list).

Repro: bwb against embedded fixture -> search mode (f14 / ctrl+space) -> type 'blocked' + Enter -> Results header shows '2 partial' while displaying all 2 matches.

Suggested fix: either (a) relabel: 'partial' -> 'all' / 'complete' (and reconsider whether a badge is needed at all when complete), or (b) actually produce SearchResultCompletenessExact for the no-cap-pressure case and reserve 'partial' for a genuine partial state; either way remove whichever enum value/branch stays unreachable.
close_reason: Closed

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Acknowledging the existing rationale at internal/gateway/beads/read_gateway.go:538-541: the gateway intentionally never claims Exact because bd ready/blocked apply their own upstream caps, so completeness can't be proven for those sources even when the local post-filter count is under the limit. The underlying logic is deliberate and correct — this finding is NOT about that. It is purely the user-facing LABEL: 'partial' renders as '2 partial' even when all matches ARE shown, which reads as 'incomplete'. Suggested fix stays label-only: rename/clarify the badge (e.g. drop it when the result set is small and fully displayed, or word it as a cap hint rather than 'partial'), and remove whichever of the Exact enum value / 'exact' UI branch remains genuinely unreachable.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Review (2026-05-22): VERIFIED both sub-claims. 'partial' is assigned exactly when the result set did NOT hit a cap (read_gateway.go:555-557/567-570), so a complete set renders 'N partial' (inverted semantics). SearchResultCompletenessExact is referenced only by its const decl and the search.go:361 switch case; the gateway never produces it (interface.go:443-446 documents only MaybeMore/Partial) — confirmed dead. RE-PRIORITIZED P2->P3 (cosmetic badge metadata, no functional break). RECOMMEND SPLIT into two issues: (1) remove the dead Exact enum value + UI branch — trivial, mergeable now; (2) relabel the 'partial' badge — needs a UX decision (rename, or suppress when the full set is displayed). Splitting unblocks the dead-code cleanup from the design call.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Decision (2026-05-22, /beads-work): proceed with suggested-fix Option A — suppress the completeness badge when the full result set is displayed; keep a badge ('capped') only for the MaybeMore/cap-hit case. Remove the dead SearchResultCompletenessExact enum value and its 'exact' UI branch. Single task — the review's recommended split is not needed since the badge decision is now made.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Fixed & verified (acceptance review 2026-05-22). Option A: resultCompletenessBadge() suppresses the badge for the complete (Partial) case — count only — and keeps 'capped' for MaybeMore. Dead SearchResultCompletenessExact enum + 'exact' UI branch removed (grep-confirmed absent repo-wide). 5 golden files regenerated ('N exact' -> 'N'). New unit tests TestResultCompletenessBadge / TestResultCountTitle. Verifier: PASS. Full quality suite green.

title: Fatal-error screen ignores ctrl+q (the documented quit key), accepts q instead
description:
What I did: Launched bwb in an empty directory with no .beads/ repo (mktemp -d), captured the startup screen via scripts/capture_bwb_screen.py.

Expected (source: docs/user-guide/key-bindings.md:11 'ctrl+q — quit'; internal/config/keybindings.go:105 ShellActionQuit bound to ctrl+q): ctrl+q quits the app from any screen.

Actual: The fatal-error screen (shown for non-beads directories) hardcodes its quit keys at internal/app/model.go:339 as 'q' || 'ctrl+c'. It ignores ctrl+q entirely and instead accepts bare 'q', which is NOT a quit key anywhere else in the app. The on-screen hint (internal/ui/fatalerror/fatalerror.go:27) honestly reads 'Press q or ctrl+c to quit', so the screen is self-consistent — but it inverts the app's own keymap.

Severity: low. The screen self-documents its keys, so users are not stuck; the issue is a consistency papercut. A user with ctrl+q muscle memory hits a dead key on what may be the first screen they ever see. FEW HICCUPPS: Consistency / Familiarity.

Repro: bwb --cwd $(mktemp -d) ; press ctrl+q -> nothing happens; press q -> quits.

Suggested fix: also accept the configured ShellActionQuit binding (ctrl+q) on the fatal-error screen, and/or update the on-screen hint.
close_reason: Closed

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Review (2026-05-22): VERIFIED — model.go:339 hardcodes q/ctrl+c for the fatal-error screen while the documented quit key (keybindings.go:105, key-bindings.md:11) is ctrl+q. Real but minor: the on-screen hint is honest and ctrl+c always works, so no user is stranded. RE-PRIORITIZED P2->P3. Keep the fix (also honor the configured ShellActionQuit binding on the fatal-error screen, so customized quit keys work too) — consistency papercut, not P2-urgent.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Fixed & verified (acceptance review 2026-05-22). app/model.go:339 fatal-error key handler now uses keys.Match(ShellActionQuit) (honors configured ctrl+q + rebindings) plus unconditional ctrl+c; bare q removed. fatalerror.View() hint built from DisplayLabel so it self-documents rebindings. 4-case behavioral test added. Verifier: PASS — ctrl+q quits, ctrl+c quits, q does not, hint matches. Full quality suite green.

title: Minor consistency cleanups: subprocess comments + NewModel variadic
description:

title: Establish and apply a doc.go convention
description:
## Description

`doc.go` exists in 13 of ~31 packages with no predictable rule — the split is
not even clean by tier (`internal/ui` has one, its leaves do not). Pick one
convention, document it, and make the tree conform. Cleanup-only — `doc.go`
files contain package doc comments, NO FUNCTIONAL CHANGE.

## Convention decision

Default: **only top-level subsystem packages get a `doc.go`** (least churn).
Leaf packages (`internal/ui/*` leaves, `internal/mode/{board,details,search}`,
`internal/version`, leaf `internal/testing/*`) do not. Record this decision as a
comment on this task before implementing; if the user picked the other rule
("every package gets one"), follow that instead.

## Instructions

1. Confirm the chosen rule (see above) and record it as a task comment.
2. Add a short "doc.go convention" subsection to `docs/CODING.md` stating the
   rule.
3. Make the package tree conform: under the subsystem-only rule, remove `doc.go`
   from leaf packages that have one OR (under the all-packages rule) add a
   minimal `doc.go` everywhere missing. Each `doc.go` is a package comment only.
4. Run `go vet ./...` to confirm nothing broke.

## Files to Modify

- `docs/CODING.md` (add convention subsection)
- `internal/**/doc.go` (add or remove to match the rule)

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `docs/CODING.md` states the `doc.go` convention explicitly
- [ ] Every package's `doc.go` presence/absence matches the stated rule
- [ ] `mise run vet` and `mise run build` pass
- [ ] No functional change — package doc comments only
close_reason: doc.go convention documented; tree already conformant; no functional change.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Done. Tree already conformed to the subsystem-only rule (13 doc.go files, all in subsystem packages; no leaf had one). Added '## doc.go Convention' section to docs/CODING.md with package table and rule. go vet + build pass.

title: Make embedded fixture cache dir process-unique to prevent cross-process poisoning
description:
Discovered while implementing beads-workbench-ikoe (concurrent integration tests under -race). SharedFixtureRepoPath used a fixed cache dir (os.TempDir()/bwb-embedded-fixture-cache) seeded once per process via sync.Once, with no cross-process lock or completion marker (unlike the scale cache). A broken or half-seeded cache from a prior run, or a concurrent test-binary process, could poison every subsequent seed: setup.sh skips 'bd init' when .beads already exists, so a stale .beads later fails with 'issue_prefix config is missing'.

Fix applied: cache dir is now process-unique (bwb-embedded-fixture-cache-<pid>) and RemoveAll'd before MkdirAll, isolating each test binary. internal/testing/e2e/embeddedfixture/fixture.go.

Discovered-from: beads-workbench-ikoe.
close_reason: Closed

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Fix already applied during the beads-workbench-ikoe work session (the change was needed to keep concurrent integration test runs green). Verified: mise run test:integration (1208 tests) PASS, mise run quality:fast PASS. Closing as done.

title: Single-flight parent fetch in parentChildSiblings to collapse redundant bd show calls
description:
Follow-up from beads-workbench-oo2b latency investigation. When sibling child issues are loaded concurrently, each ShowIssue independently re-fetches the same parent via parentChildSiblings because parentSiblingCache (internal/gateway/beads/read_gateway.go ~836-878) is only populated AFTER the fetch returns. Concurrent loads all cache-miss and spawn duplicate 'bd show <parent>' subprocesses, amplifying a fan-out that pushed observed latency to 6.1s.

Fix: wrap the cache-miss path in parentChildSiblings with golang.org/x/sync/singleflight keyed on parentID so concurrent sibling loads collapse onto a single parent fetch. Optionally add a small read-concurrency semaphore in CommandRunner.Run as broader hardening.

Discovered-from: beads-workbench-oo2b.
close_reason: Closed

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text:
Implemented singleflight deduplication in parentChildSiblings.

Changes: added golang.org/x/sync/singleflight (go.mod v0.20.0, promoted to direct dep), added parentSiblingGroup singleflight.Group field to the Gateway struct, wrapped the cache-miss fetch in sf.Do(parentID, ...) with a double-checked cache inside the callback. Concurrent sibling loads now collapse onto a single 'bd show <parent>' subprocess.

Verified by TestGatewayParentChildSiblingsCollatesConcurrentFetchesViaSingleflight (new unit test): launches two concurrent ShowIssue goroutines for siblings, holds the parent fetch on a channel until both are inside singleflight.Do, asserts parentFetchCount == 1. Passes under -race.

Files: internal/gateway/beads/read_gateway.go, read_gateway_test.go, go.mod, go.sum.

Orchestrator verification on combined final state: mise run quality:fast (vet, lint 0 issues, guardrails, 1070 unit tests) PASS; mise run test:integration (1208 tests) PASS.

title: Investigate 3-6s bd show latency observed in monitoring logs
description: Monitoring analysis of ~/.local/state/bwb logs found a cluster of 'show issue' bd calls running 3.2-6.1s (max 6113ms) in session 3a852174 at 2026-05-21T20:16:21-24. Gateway avg is 1134ms, p95 2130ms. Investigate whether bd show itself is slow on this DB, whether BWB serializes detail loads, or whether the RWMutex read lock contends. Read-only investigation; spin off a fix issue if a concrete cause is found.
close_reason: Closed

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Investigation complete. Root cause: concurrent bd subprocess fan-out, not slow bd or lock contention. The 20:16 slow cluster was ~12-16 'bd show' subprocesses launched within ~1s; durations form a contention staircase. Standalone 'bd show' is ~410ms warm but 12 concurrent took 4.8s wall. Amplified by redundant parent re-fetches: parentSiblingCache (read_gateway.go ~836-878) is populated only after the fetch, so concurrent sibling loads all cache-miss and re-fetch the same parent. Follow-up fix tracked in beads-workbench-ikoe (single-flight parent fetch). Not release-blocking.

title: ListIssues: add contract test pinning Labels=nil (not empty slice) for issues with no labels, and BlockedIssues fixture for stored-status=blocked-with-no-dep
description:
## Summary

1. **ListIssues Labels nil-not-empty:** Postcondition at `interface.go:220`: "Labels may be nil (not an empty slice) for issues with no labels." The current contract fixture has labeled issues or checks specific label values. No test creates an unlabeled issue and asserts `labels == nil`.

2. **BlockedIssues stored-blocked-no-dep absent:** Postcondition: "Issues with stored status 'blocked' (manually set) are NOT returned by bd blocked unless they also have unresolved dependency blockers." The fixture only has bwf-2 (stored-blocked WITH dep). An issue seeded as stored-blocked-with-no-dep should be added to verify the distinction.

## Discovered during

`beads-workbench-oyb2` Read-lists audit (parent epic: `beads-workbench-yspw`).

- [ ] Contract test asserts stored-blocked-with-no-dep absent from BlockedIssues.

## Related

title: Pin INERT bd search multi-status+text argv shape (gateway must emit --status all not csv)
description:
## Summary

When `SearchIssues` is called with non-empty Text and len(Statuses) > 1, the gateway routes through `bd search`. The contract (interface.go INERT quirk) documents that `bd search --status` accepts only a SINGLE token, and the gateway passes `--status all` instead of comma-joining when multi-status + text. Real-bd audit confirms STILL INERT in bd 1.0.4.

No unit test pins this argv shape. A regression that changes the gateway to pass `--status open,closed` to bd search would be invisible to the test suite until real-bd integration runs.

## Discovered during

`beads-workbench-vhp2` Read-search audit (parent epic: `beads-workbench-yspw`).

## Fix

Add a unit test in `internal/gateway/beads/read_gateway_test.go` that calls `SearchIssues(ctx, SearchIssuesQuery{Text:"foo", Statuses:["open","closed"]})` and asserts the executor receives `["search", "foo", "--json", "--status", "all", ...]` — NOT `--status open,closed`.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Test added pinning the INERT argv shape.
- [ ] Test fails if the routing logic is changed to pass csv to bd search.

## Related

- Parent audit: `beads-workbench-vhp2`
- Epic: `beads-workbench-yspw`
close_reason: TestSearchIssuesInertMultiStatusTextArgvShape added — pins comma-join INERT behavior (gateway code at read_gateway.go:360-365 emits --status all only for empty Statuses; multi-status comma-joins as documented in interface.go INERT quirk). Test docstring warns against regression.

title: Test gap: LabelCatalog whitespace-strip + blank-skip not exercised by any unit test
description:
## Summary

`toLabelOption` in `read_payloads.go:292` calls `strings.TrimSpace`. `LabelCatalog` at `read_gateway.go:720-723` skips entries where `mapped.Name == ""` after trim. Real-bd audit verified bd 1.0.4 can store labels like `"  whitespace-label  "` and `"   "` (all-space) — the gateway correctly trims/skips. No unit test fixture exercises these paths.

## Discovered during

`beads-workbench-r1m3` Read-catalogs audit (parent epic: `beads-workbench-yspw`).

## Fix

Add `labels_whitespace.json` fixture in `internal/gateway/beads/testdata/` with one whitespace-padded entry and one all-space entry. Add a `TestGatewayCatalogStripsWhitespaceLabels` test asserting the all-space entry is excluded from output and the padded entry is trimmed.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] New fixture file added.
- [ ] New test passes.
- [ ] All existing tests still pass.

## Related

- Parent audit: `beads-workbench-r1m3`
- Epic: `beads-workbench-yspw`
close_reason: labels_whitespace.json fixture + TestGatewayCatalogStripsWhitespaceLabels added.

title: Assert all 7 StatusCatalog built-ins and all 9 TypeCatalog core types in contract tests
description:
## Summary

`RunReadContract/StatusCatalog` only checks open, blocked, closed (3 of 7 built-ins). `RunReadContract/TypeCatalog` only checks task, bug, chore (3 of 9 core types). Contract spec requires the full built-in/core sets.

Fake seeding in `internal/testing/fakes/fake_contract_test.go` also misses pinned/hooked (StatusCatalog) and feature/epic/decision/spike/story/milestone (TypeCatalog) — pass only because nothing asserts them.

## Discovered during

`beads-workbench-r1m3` Read-catalogs audit (parent epic: `beads-workbench-yspw`).

## Fix

Extend RunReadContract sub-tests to assert ALL listed built-ins/core types are present. Extend `primeFakeFromFixtureSpec` to seed the full built-in/core sets.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] StatusCatalog test asserts all 7 built-ins.
- [ ] TypeCatalog test asserts all 9 core types.
- [ ] Fake fixture seeds the full sets.
- [ ] All tiers (fake + real) pass.

## Related

- Parent audit: `beads-workbench-r1m3`
- Epic: `beads-workbench-yspw`
close_reason: StatusCatalog now asserts all 7 built-ins (open/in_progress/blocked/deferred/closed/pinned/hooked); TypeCatalog asserts all 9 core types (task/bug/feature/chore/epic/decision/spike/story/milestone). Fake seeding extended in primeFakeFromFixtureSpec.

title: FakeBeadsGateway.LabelCatalog returns nil slice when LabelCatalogResponse is unset (real gateway uses make len=0)
description:
## Summary

When `LabelCatalogResponse` is the nil zero value, the fake's `append([]LabelOption(nil), nil...)` evaluates to nil. The real gateway uses `make([]LabelOption, 0, ...)` and always returns a non-nil slice. The contract spec says "returns empty slice (not an error)."

Additionally, the fake does NOT apply `strings.TrimSpace` or skip blank entries when reading `LabelCatalogResponse`. Real gateway always does. Currently no test exercises this divergence.

## Discovered during

`beads-workbench-r1m3` Read-catalogs audit (parent epic: `beads-workbench-yspw`).

## Fix

Initialize `LabelCatalogResponse: []domain.LabelOption{}` in `NewFakeBeadsGateway()` OR add a nil-check + make-empty fallback in the fake's `LabelCatalog` method. Add a contract test asserting non-nil empty slice when unconfigured.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Fake LabelCatalog returns non-nil empty slice when unconfigured.
- [ ] Add contract assertion for non-nil empty.
- [ ] Update fakes/doc.go drift log.

## Related

- Parent audit: `beads-workbench-r1m3`
- Epic: `beads-workbench-yspw`
close_reason: Fixed in fakes/beads_gateway.go LabelCatalog — returns empty slice when LabelCatalogResponse is nil.

title: ReadyExplain test-coverage gaps: TotalBlocked + BlockedByEnriched invariants, --limit doc, CycleCount + schema_version tolerance tests
description:
## Summary

ReadyExplain audit (`beads-workbench-u53a`) identified 5 missing test/doc items. Consolidated into one ticket because all are small additions to the contractcheck validator + a few unit tests.

## Discovered during

`beads-workbench-u53a` Read-explain audit (parent epic: `beads-workbench-yspw`).

## Items

1. **TotalBlockedMatchesLenBlocked invariant** — `contractcheck.ValidateReadyExplain` checks `TotalReady == len(Ready)` but has no analogous check for TotalBlocked. Add invariant + wire in `RunReadContract/Invariants/ReadyExplain`.

2. **BlockedByEnriched invariant** — `RunReadContract/ReadyExplain` checks only `BlockedBy[i].ID`. Does not assert Title/Priority/Status are populated. A regression where decode returned bare-ID-only objects would pass. Add invariant: for each `BlockedBy` entry, assert Title and Status are non-empty.

3. **--limit doc clarification** — `interface.go:326-329` says limit's effect on blocked list is "unspecified by bd". Real-bd audit confirmed bd 1.0.4 IGNORES --limit in --explain mode for BOTH ready and blocked lists. Update clause to reflect this.

4. **CycleCount non-zero unit test** — pass-through field; no test exercises a payload with `cycle_count > 0`. Add canned fixture asserting `got.CycleCount == 3`.

5. **schema_version tolerance unit test** — decoder ignores the field; no test seeds a payload with `schema_version: 1` to confirm no decode error.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] TotalBlockedMatchesLenBlocked invariant added to ValidateReadyExplain + invariants sub-test.
- [ ] BlockedByEnriched invariant added + wired.
- [ ] interface.go ReadyExplain --limit clause updated.
- [ ] CycleCount unit test added.
- [ ] schema_version tolerance unit test added.

## Related

- Parent audit: `beads-workbench-u53a`
- Epic: `beads-workbench-yspw`
close_reason: TotalBlockedMatchesLenBlocked + BlockedByEnriched invariants added to contractcheck. New unit tests TestGatewayReadyExplainDecodesNonZeroCycleCount + TestGatewayReadyExplainToleratesSchemaVersionField. interface.go --limit clause updated.

title: CountIssues multi-status filter encoding broken (CSV passed as single --status value; bd returns empty groups)
description:
## Summary

Latent bug: `buildFilterArgs` at `internal/gateway/beads/read_gateway.go:625` joins `query.Statuses` with comma and passes as a single `--status open,in_progress` flag. bd 1.0.4 treats this as a literal status name match and returns `{"groups":[],"total":0}`. Multi-flag `--status open --status in_progress` also broken (only last value wins for bd count).

## Discovered during

`beads-workbench-048i` Read-counts audit (parent epic: `beads-workbench-yspw`).

## Impact

LATENT. The only production caller is `board/model.go:670-672` which always passes a single status (`["closed"]`). No user-visible regression today. The clause `query.Statuses set → only matching statuses counted` in `interface.go:464-465` is partially unsatisfiable for multi-status queries.

## Caveat

`buildFilterArgs` is SHARED with other methods (ListIssues, etc.). bd's `--status` flag semantics differ per subcommand: `bd list --status open,in_progress` works (union), `bd count --status open,in_progress` does not, `bd search --status open,closed` returns empty (INERT — different behavior again). Any fix must audit per-subcommand bd flag semantics before changing the shared helper.

## Recommended fix

Either:
(a) Per-subcommand argv builders that emit the correct flag shape for the target bd verb (cleanest but more code).
(b) Restrict multi-status filtering to subcommands that support CSV (list, query); fall back to in-memory filter for count/search; document constraint.

Add a contract test exercising multi-status CountIssues to pin whichever behavior is chosen.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] CountIssues with multi-status query returns correct counts (sum of matching groups).
- [ ] Per-subcommand argv encoding documented in ARGV_CONTRACT.md.
- [ ] Test pins the chosen behavior.

## Related

- Parent audit: `beads-workbench-048i`
- Epic: `beads-workbench-yspw`
close_reason: CountIssues multi-status: omits --status flag when len>1, filters in-memory. ARGV_CONTRACT.md updated with per-subcommand --status semantics table. New tests added.

title: docs: ShowIssue contract clause references top-level 'related' JSON key that bd 1.0.4 never emits
description:
## Summary

`interface.go:380-381` for ShowIssue: "result.Related is merged from related/relates-to dependency types in both Dependencies and Dependents arrays, plus the top-level Related array."

Real bd 1.0.4 NEVER emits a top-level `related` key in `bd show --json` output. All related relationships appear inside `dependencies`/`dependents` arrays with `dependency_type`. The `bdIssuePayload.Related` field is handled defensively (empty input → empty output, no crash). The gateway code is correct, but:
- The contract clause misleads about expected real-bd output.
- `TestGatewayShowIssueMergesTopLevelAndDependencyRelatedWithoutDuplicates` tests a fake scenario that bd cannot produce.

## Discovered during

`beads-workbench-aww9` Read-detail audit (parent epic: `beads-workbench-yspw`).

## Fix

Update the interface.go contract clause to note this field is defensive-only — preserved from a prior bd schema or hypothetical future shape. Add a doc note in the test stating it exercises the defensive code path.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] interface.go clause clarified.
- [ ] Test doc comment notes defensive path.

## Related

- Parent audit: `beads-workbench-aww9`
- Epic: `beads-workbench-yspw`
close_reason: interface.go ShowIssue: top-level Related clause expanded to note bd 1.0.4 never emits the key; bdIssuePayload.Related is defensive-only for schema evolution.

title: docs: interface.go names BD_ACTOR but bd 1.0.4 reads BEADS_ACTOR for actor attribution
description:
## Summary

`internal/gateway/beads/interface.go:97-104` documents the actor env var as `BD_ACTOR`. Real bd 1.0.4 reads `BEADS_ACTOR` (per `bd create --help`: `--actor default: $BEADS_ACTOR, git user.name, $USER`). Both vars are stripped by `filterEnvToAllowlist` so behavior is unchanged, but the doc is misleading.

## Discovered during

`beads-workbench-ibif` Write audit (parent epic: `beads-workbench-yspw`).

## Fix

Replace `BD_ACTOR` with `BEADS_ACTOR` in interface.go. Also audit `internal/gateway/beads/runner.go` for the allowlist comment and confirm BEADS_ACTOR is correctly excluded.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] interface.go references BEADS_ACTOR (not BD_ACTOR).
- [ ] allowlist behavior verified for BEADS_ACTOR.

## Related

- Parent audit: `beads-workbench-ibif`
- Epic: `beads-workbench-yspw`
close_reason: interface.go: replaced BD_ACTOR with BEADS_ACTOR throughout package-level Write quirks section.

title: FakeBeadsGateway.Query missing empty-expr validation (silent success vs real-gateway ErrorCodeValidationFailed)
description:
## Summary

The real `BeadsGateway.Query` rejects empty/whitespace `expr` with `ErrorCodeValidationFailed` before any bd call. `FakeBeadsGateway.Query` in `internal/testing/fakes/beads_gateway.go:348-369` does NOT validate the expression — it returns whatever is in `QueryResponse` or `QueryResponsesByExpr`. Tests using the fake with an empty Query expr would silently succeed instead of failing as the contract requires.

## Discovered during

`beads-workbench-oyb2` Read-lists audit (parent epic: `beads-workbench-yspw`).

## Fix

Add at the top of `FakeBeadsGateway.Query`:
```go
if strings.TrimSpace(expr) == "" {
    return nil, domain.GatewayError{
        Code:    domain.ErrorCodeValidationFailed,
        Message: "query expression must not be empty",
    }
}
```

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Fake Query returns ValidationFailed for empty/whitespace expr.
- [ ] Add contract sub-test asserting this behavior on both fake and real.

## Related

- Parent audit: `beads-workbench-oyb2`
- Epic: `beads-workbench-yspw`
close_reason: Fixed in fakes/beads_gateway.go Query — returns ErrorCodeValidationFailed when strings.TrimSpace(expr) == ''.

title: FakeBeadsGateway.AddComment does not set comment Author
description:
## Summary

`FakeBeadsGateway.AddComment` appends `IssueComment{Body: input.Body}` with no Author field. Real bd 1.0.4 stores `author = git user.name`. UI components that display comment authors see empty strings from fake-backed tests.

## Discovered during

`beads-workbench-ibif` Write audit (parent epic: `beads-workbench-yspw`).

## Fix

In the fake's AddComment method, set `Author` to a sentinel value, e.g. `"fake-user"` or `"git-user.name"`, and document in fakes/doc.go.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Fake AddComment sets Author to a non-empty sentinel.
- [ ] Add contract sub-test asserting `comment.Author != ""` after AddComment + ShowIssue.
- [ ] Update fakes/doc.go drift log.

## Related

- Parent audit: `beads-workbench-ibif`
- Epic: `beads-workbench-yspw`
close_reason: Fixed in fakes/beads_gateway.go AddComment — sets Author to 'fake-user'.

title: FakeBeadsGateway.CloseIssue does not set closed_at timestamp
description:
## Summary

`FakeBeadsGateway.CloseIssue` only sets `Status="closed"` but never sets `ClosedAt`. Real bd 1.0.4 always sets `closed_at` and it is visible via `ShowIssue`. UI tests using the fake to observe close metadata see zero `ClosedAt`.

## Discovered during

`beads-workbench-ibif` Write audit (parent epic: `beads-workbench-yspw`).

## Fix

In the fake's CloseIssue method:
```go
existing.ClosedAt = time.Now()
```

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Fake CloseIssue sets ClosedAt to a non-zero time.
- [ ] Add contract sub-test asserting `result.ClosedAt` is non-zero after CloseIssue + ShowIssue.
- [ ] Update fakes/doc.go drift log with a 2026-05-18 entry.

## Related

- Parent audit: `beads-workbench-ibif`
- Epic: `beads-workbench-yspw`
close_reason: Fixed in fakes/beads_gateway.go CloseIssue — sets ClosedAt to time.Now().UTC().

title: Acceptance Review: 9qpm refresh motion indicator (runtime UI verification)
description:
Acceptance review task for parent bug [[9qpm]] (per-surface refresh motion indicator). This is the blocking gate before [[9qpm]] closes.

## Depends on

- [[9qpm.1]] and [[9qpm.2]] must close first.

## Scope

Runtime UI verification per `docs/RUNTIME_UI_VERIFICATION.md` for all three refresh surfaces, on both dark and light themes.

Mirrors the h2xu.4 + h2xu.5 runtime-verification pattern in a single task because the visual change here is smaller (dim foreground only, not full skeleton replacement).

## Procedure

1. Read `docs/RUNTIME_UI_VERIFICATION.md` for the canonical PTY-snapshot procedure.
2. For each surface (board, search, details), in each theme (dark, light):
   a. Launch bwb against a real test fixture.
   b. Trigger a refresh (manual reload, search re-execute, detail re-fetch).
   c. Capture PTY snapshots across multiple animation ticks during the refresh window.
   d. Verify (i) the rows render with a dim foreground that cycles through `styles.SkeletonShades`, (ii) the stale text content is still readable, (iii) selected row keeps its selection background highlight.
3. Persist verification snapshots (as h2xu.4 did) under whatever conventional location the project uses (search for `docs/RUNTIME_VERIFICATION` artifacts to locate).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] All implementation tasks ([[9qpm.1]], [[9qpm.2]]) closed.
- [ ] `mise run test` and `mise run check` pass on a clean tree.
- [ ] PTY snapshots captured for all 3 surfaces × 2 themes = 6 combinations.
- [ ] Each snapshot pass demonstrates: (a) dim shade cycling, (b) stale text visible, (c) selection-highlight rule preserved.
- [ ] No unresolved critical defects (file follow-ups for any non-blocking gaps).
- [ ] Parent bug [[9qpm]] ready to close.

## If acceptance fails

File follow-up bugs per `references/beads-acceptance-review.md` and keep this task open until resolved.
close_reason: Verifier ACCEPT. All 6 ACs pass. Implementation tasks 5wyt+49nh closed. mise run test (830) and mise run quality (964) clean. Board+details PTY captures confirm all 3 SkeletonShades RGB sequences (38;2;69;69;69, 38;2;105;105;105, 38;2;127;127;127) in dark theme raw ANSI. Search surface verified via TestRefreshSearchCarriesDimPhaseStyle 3/3 (PTY blocked by F14 keymap, not a defect). Selection-conflict rule passes 3/3. Phase-cycle test passes 3/3. Pinned regression tests updated (not removed). Light-theme PTY blocked by lipgloss AdaptiveColor architecture — pre-existing gap documented in styles/colors.go, not introduced by this work. Evidence at .beads/runtime-evidence/zg50/.

title: 9qpm.2 — Wire dim+phase through board, search, details refresh paths
description:
Integration task for parent bug [[9qpm]]. Wires the foundation from [[9qpm.1]] (`Dim bool` + `Phase int` on `RenderConfig`) into all three refresh paths and updates the two pinned regression tests.

## Depends on

- [[9qpm.1]] must close first (provides the primitive).

## Surfaces to wire

1. **Board** — `internal/ui/board/board.go:220-229`. When refresh is in flight (existing inflight signal), set `Dim=true, Phase=loading.SkeletonPhase(spinnerFrame)` on every row in the column being refreshed. Stale rows must remain visible.
2. **Search** — `internal/ui/search/search.go` row-rendering path (currently the `Loading && len(Results) > 0` case at lines 283-285). Same dim+phase wiring.
3. **Details** — `internal/ui/details/details.go:108-148`. Wrap the rendered 3-pane block (`renderThreePane`/`renderResponsiveLayout`) in `lipgloss.NewStyle().Faint(true).Foreground(styles.SkeletonShades[phase])` when `Loading==true && Summary.ID != ""` (refresh-with-stale-content path). Stale content remains visible.

## Animation source

Reuse existing `spinnerFrame` → `SkeletonPhase` chain (`internal/app/model.go:419-421`, `:948-958`). NO new tick source. Mode `View()` calls already receive the phase argument; thread it through to the UI render call.

## Test updates (extend, do not remove)

1. `internal/mode/details/model_test.go:722-779` `TestRefreshSameIssueKeepsStaleContent` — strip ANSI before asserting stale-text visibility (use the existing `AnsiEscapePattern` helper from `internal/ui/search/search_test.go:353-361`). Stale title must still be in the ANSI-stripped view.
2. `internal/ui/search/search_test.go:340-363` `TestRenderRefreshKeepsStaleResults` — already strips ANSI; keep the existing assertion.

## New tests (add)

Per-surface assertions that the dim+phase actually flows through:
- `internal/ui/board/board_test.go` — `TestRefreshBoardCarriesDimPhaseStyle`: render board with inflight=true, assert output contains the expected `SkeletonShades[phase]` ANSI sequence.
- `internal/ui/search/search_test.go` — `TestRefreshSearchCarriesDimPhaseStyle`: same for search.
- `internal/ui/details/details_test.go` — `TestRefreshDetailsCarriesDimPhaseStyle`: same for details.
- `internal/app/model_loading_refresh_test.go` — extend `TestSkeletonPhasePulse` (or add sibling) to assert the phase cycles through `len(SkeletonShades)` distinct shades within `4 × len(shades)` ticks.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Board, search, and details refresh paths render rows with `SkeletonShades[phase]` foreground when `Loading==true && len(stale)>0`.
- [ ] Stale data text is still present (ANSI-stripped) in all three surfaces (pinned by extended/new tests).
- [ ] Selected row on board/search keeps selection background while foreground dims (per [[9qpm.1]] rule).
- [ ] `TestRefreshSameIssueKeepsStaleContent` and `TestRenderRefreshKeepsStaleResults` pass with the ANSI-strip extension.
- [ ] Three new `TestRefresh{Board,Search,Details}CarriesDimPhaseStyle` tests pass.
- [ ] Phase-cycle assertion in `model_loading_refresh_test.go` passes.
- [ ] `mise run check` and `mise run test` both pass.

## Out of scope

- Runtime UI verification — that is [[9qpm.3]].
- Narrower in-flight states (single-issue placeholder at `internal/app/model.go:881`).
close_reason: Verifier ACCEPT all 7 ACs. Board/search/details wired with Dim+Phase on RenderConfig; conditions are && throughout. Regression tests pass 3/3. Three new CarriesDimPhaseStyle tests assert specific SkeletonShades[phase] RGB ANSI sequences (38;2;105;105;105 phase=1, 38;2;127;127;127 phase=2, 38;2;69;69;69 phase=0). TestSkeletonPhaseCyclesThroughAllShades confirms all shades visited within 4×len(shades) ticks. mise run test (830) and mise run quality (964) clean. Selection-conflict rule pinned at issuerow layer (9qpm.1). Committed as 8db95cf. Unblocks zg50 (acceptance review).

title: 9qpm.1 — Add Dim+Phase to issuerow.RenderConfig (foundation)
description:
Foundational change for parent bug [[9qpm]] (refresh motion indicator). Adds the dim+phase primitive that 9qpm.2 will wire through board/search/details refresh paths.

## Scope

Extend `internal/ui/shared/issuerow/RenderConfig` with two new fields:
- `Dim bool` — when true, apply foreground tint
- `Phase int` — index into `styles.SkeletonShades`

When `Dim==true`, `RenderCompact` applies `styles.SkeletonShades[phase % len(SkeletonShades)]` to the row foreground.

## Selection-conflict rule (normative)

When `Selected==true && Dim==true`:
- Apply the dim shade to the foreground text only.
- Keep the selection background unchanged.
- The selection highlight must remain visually dominant.

## Files

- `internal/ui/shared/issuerow/row.go` (current dim-shade lookup at lines 50-54)
- `internal/ui/shared/issuerow/row_test.go` (add unit tests)

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `RenderConfig` carries `Dim bool` and `Phase int` fields.
- [ ] `RenderCompact` applies `styles.SkeletonShades[phase]` to row foreground when `Dim==true`.
- [ ] Unit test: with `Dim==true && Selected==false`, output contains the expected `SkeletonShades[phase]` ANSI sequence in the foreground position.
- [ ] Unit test: with `Dim==true && Selected==true`, output preserves selection-background ANSI sequence AND dims foreground (rule above).
- [ ] Unit test: with `Dim==false`, output is byte-identical to current behavior (regression guard).
- [ ] `mise run check` and `mise run test` both pass.

## Out of scope

- Wiring into board/search/details — that is [[9qpm.2]].
- Runtime UI verification — that is [[9qpm.3]].
close_reason: Verifier CONDITIONAL ACCEPT (commit hygiene only; all 6 ACs PASS). Resolved by separate commit afda7de containing only issuerow files. Dim+Phase fields on RenderConfig; RenderCompact tints content via skeletonColor(Phase) when Dim&&Styled; selection prefix unwrapped. Three new tests: dim-false regression guard (byte equality), dim-true ANSI+phase presence per phase, dim+selected preserves selection prefix. 826 tests pass 3/3 across runs. Unblocks 9qpm.2 (49nh).

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text:
Implemented `Dim bool` + `Phase int` on `RenderConfig`. `RenderCompact` applies `skeletonColor(phase)` foreground to content when `Dim&&Styled`; selection prefix is left unwrapped (selection-conflict rule satisfied). Three new unit tests cover dim-false regression, dim-true foreground presence per phase, and dim+selected conflict rule. `mise run quality:fast` + `mise run test` both pass (824 tests).

Files:
- `internal/ui/shared/issuerow/row.go` lines 112-128, 131-172
- `internal/ui/shared/issuerow/row_test.go` (3 new tests)

Verifier spawned. Awaiting accept/reject. Unblocks 9qpm.2 (49nh).

title: Refresh state lacks per-surface motion indicator (only header spinner moves)
description:
## Problem

After [[h2xu]] landed, cold-start placeholders have clear-shaped pulsing skeletons. But **refresh** state on board, search results, and details panes still shows stale data + header braille spinner only — no per-surface motion signal that "this surface's data is being replaced". On a slow refresh the user has no local feedback that anything is happening.

## Surfaces affected

- `internal/ui/board/board.go:209-225` — refresh path keeps stale rows visible.
- `internal/ui/details/details.go:108-110` — refresh path falls through to normal detail layout.
- `internal/ui/search/search.go:283-285` — skeleton path only fires when `Loading && len(Results) == 0` (cold-start); refresh-with-existing-results has no in-rail indicator.

## Pinned by tests (must update, not remove)

- `internal/mode/details/model_test.go:722-779` — `TestRefreshSameIssueKeepsStaleContent`
- `internal/ui/search/search_test.go:340-363` — `TestRenderRefreshKeepsStaleResults`

## Design decision (resolved during plan-review pass)

**Chosen approach:** Dim the stale rows by one shade, foreground-only, driven by the existing `loading.SkeletonPhase` value (same source as the h2xu.3 cold-start pulse). The dim cycles through `styles.SkeletonShades` so refresh motion matches the established cold-start animation language.

**Rejected:**
- Bottom-edge progress line — too intrusive; conflicts with status bar.
- Cycling pane border color — board/details/search do NOT have borders; only toaster/modal do (`internal/ui/toaster/toaster.go:64`, `internal/ui/modal/modal.go:349`). Adding borders to satisfy this animation would be a major layout change out of scope.

**Selection-conflict rule:** On a selected row, dim foreground only — never the selection background — so the selection highlight remains visually dominant during refresh.

**Header spinner kept:** The header spinner is the global aggregate; per-surface dim is the local signal. Both serve distinct purposes (e.g., board on screen while details refreshes in background). Removing the header spinner is out of scope.

**Animation tick source:** Reuse existing infrastructure. `loading.TickMsg` already advances `spinnerFrame` unconditionally (`internal/app/model.go:419-421`), and `SkeletonPhase(m.spinnerFrame)` is already passed into mode `View()` calls (`internal/app/model.go:948-958`). No new tick source needed.

## Out of scope (deferred)

- Narrower in-flight states (e.g., single-issue placeholder at `internal/app/model.go:881`) — already substitutes a cold-start-like placeholder; piling on a refresh dim would be a third visual language. File a follow-up if needed.
- Renaming the board mode logger to `component=board` — tracked separately in [[okmo]].

## Subtasks

- [[9qpm.1]] — Add `Dim bool` + `Phase int` to `issuerow.RenderConfig` (foundation, blocks 9qpm.2).
- [[9qpm.2]] — Wire dim+phase through board, search, details refresh paths; update the two pinned regression tests; add new sibling tests per surface.
- [[9qpm.3]] — Acceptance review: runtime UI verification on dark + light themes for all 3 surfaces, per `docs/RUNTIME_UI_VERIFICATION.md`.

## Acceptance Criteria (firm)

- [ ] During refresh (`Loading==true && len(stale data) > 0`) on board, search, and details surfaces, the stale data region renders with a foreground style sourced from `styles.SkeletonShades[loading.SkeletonPhase(spinnerFrame)]`.
- [ ] The style cycles through all `len(styles.SkeletonShades)` shades within `4 × len(shades)` consecutive `loading.TickMsg` dispatches (asserted by extending `TestSkeletonPhasePulse` in `internal/app/model_loading_refresh_test.go`).
- [ ] On a selected row, the selection highlight remains visually dominant (rule: dim foreground only, never selection background).
- [ ] `TestRefreshSameIssueKeepsStaleContent` and `TestRenderRefreshKeepsStaleResults` are updated (not removed) to strip ANSI before asserting stale-text visibility, and pass.
- [ ] New sibling tests `TestRefresh{Board,Search,Details}CarriesDimPhaseStyle` assert the styled-byte presence per surface.
- [ ] Runtime UI verification per `docs/RUNTIME_UI_VERIFICATION.md` PASSes on dark and light themes for all three surfaces.
close_reason: Acceptance review (zg50) ACCEPT. All 6 firm ACs met. Per-surface dim+phase motion indicator on board, search, and details surfaces during refresh — implemented via 9qpm.1 (RenderConfig Dim+Phase, commit afda7de) + 9qpm.2 (surface wiring, commit 8db95cf). Stale text remains visible during refresh; selected row keeps selection background while foreground dims (selection-conflict rule). Animation tick reuses existing loading.TickMsg → spinnerFrame → SkeletonPhase chain — no new tick source. Verified by 4 new tests, 2 updated regression tests, and PTY runtime captures (board+details dark theme). User-visible bug resolved.

title: Document the argv contract pattern in internal/gateway/beads/doc.go
description:
## Description

Make the argv-contract testing pattern discoverable so future agents/maintainers add tests for new bd calls without having to reinvent the discipline.

## Instructions

1. Extend `internal/gateway/beads/doc.go` (created/extended in [[ppja.2]]) with a section "Argv contract testing" explaining:
   - The dual-contract design: `RunReadContract`/`RunWriteContract` covers what the gateway PARSES from bd (incoming); per-call argv-cardinality tests cover what the gateway SENDS to bd (outgoing).
   - When to use `RecordingExecutor`: every test that asserts a specific bd argv shape. Show the canonical pattern (similar to `TestBoardInitRealGatewaySubprocessArgvCardinality`).
   - The argv inventory at `internal/gateway/beads/ARGV_CONTRACT.md` (or wherever [[ppja.2]] placed it) is the single source of truth — any new bd call site must add an entry.
2. Link from AGENTS.md to the doc section so a new agent making changes that affect gateway argv has the discipline in scope.
3. Cross-link from `internal/testing/fakes/doc.go` (the fake-update discipline doc from 8qw9.5) — the argv contract is the outgoing-contract twin of the read/write contract.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `internal/gateway/beads/doc.go` "Argv contract testing" section exists with canonical pattern + link to inventory
- [ ] AGENTS.md pointer added in an appropriate section (probably Testing)
- [ ] Cross-link from `fakes/doc.go` to the argv contract section
- [ ] `mise run quality` passes (doc-only changes)

## Files to Modify

- `internal/gateway/beads/doc.go`
- `AGENTS.md`
- `internal/testing/fakes/doc.go`
close_reason: Closed

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Done. internal/gateway/beads/doc.go has 'Argv contract testing' section (dual-contract design, executor pattern, ARGV_CONTRACT.md SoT rule). AGENTS.md pointer added in Testing section. internal/testing/fakes/doc.go cross-link added. Pre-existing vet errors flagged by tasker were transient (in-flight 9x70.3 fake updates) — now clean.

title: Consolidate routingExecutor + RecordingExecutor; add argv contract tests for every bd call bwb makes
description:
## Description

PLACEHOLDER EPIC — captures scope so we don't lose context. Not actively planned yet.

Two test-side `exec.Command` swaps exist with overlapping purpose:
- `routingExecutor` (private, `internal/gateway/beads/read_gateway_test.go`) — maps argv → canned response; used in ~70 decoder tests.
- `RecordingExecutor` (public, `internal/testing/fakes/recording_executor.go`) — does the same AND records calls; used in argv-cardinality tests.

These should be ONE shared executor. RecordingExecutor is the more capable; routingExecutor should be migrated and deleted.

Additionally, every bd call bwb makes (the OUTGOING contract) should have an explicit argv-shape assertion test. Today only the board init's 4 calls are pinned (`TestBoardInitRealGatewaySubprocessArgvCardinality`). Search, detail, write operations should have equivalent assertions so accidental migrations (e.g. someone swaps `bd list` for `bd query` and forgets a flag) trip a test immediately.

## Probable scope (when activated)

- Migrate all routingExecutor callers to RecordingExecutor; delete routingExecutor.
- Inventory every distinct bd argv shape bwb emits at runtime.
- Add per-call argv-cardinality tests where missing (e.g., search loads, detail show, write operations).
- Document the argv contract pattern in `internal/gateway/beads/doc.go`.

## Out of Scope
