Read-side semantic contract (covered by epic [[8qw9]]).
Write-side semantic contract (covered by separate placeholder epic — see [[harden-gateway-write-contract]]).

## Activation Trigger

Plan and activate after [[8qw9]] closes. Before activation, audit whether bd's argv has changed (recent bd releases may have renamed flags) so the test pinning is current rather than retroactive.
close_reason: Closed

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text:
**Epic-review 2026-05-16 (tightening)**

Reviewed by orchestrator + independent reviewer before activation. Most defensible of the 3 epics queued this session — solid cleanup (ppja.1 removes 68 routingExecutor refs) + extends a proven pattern (the existing TestBoardInitRealGatewaySubprocessArgvCardinality). GO as planned with one tightening:

**ppja.3 (dynamic-flag clause)** — the original wording "parametrize the test or pin the common cases" punts on the hardest case. Dynamic flags (e.g. `--limit` driven by terminal height) are exactly where argv regressions hide. Tightened to: "every dynamic flag pinned at default + max + min + 1 boundary value." See updated description on ppja.3.

**Priority note**: P1 bugs ule7 and 5q6t take precedence over this P3 work — defer activation.

title: Dashboard warn log emits duplicated 'component' JSON key
description:
## Symptom

Records in `~/.local/state/bwb/bwb.log` from the dashboard component contain
the `component` key twice:

```
{"...","component":"dashboard","component":"dashboard","group":"Closed",...}
```

263 occurrences observed (all "backend sort assumption broken" warnings).

## Root Cause

The logger handed to the board mode already carries `component=dashboard`
(set in `cmd/bwb/main.go:46`: `services.Logger = opts.logManager.Component("dashboard")`).

`internal/mode/board/model.go:397` then redundantly adds it again:

```go
logger := m.logger.With("component", "dashboard")
```

slog's default JSON handler emits both keys.

## Fix

Drop the redundant `.With("component", "dashboard")` in
`internal/mode/board/model.go:397`, OR rename it to a sub-component label
(e.g. `"subcomponent", "compose"`) if the intent was scoping.

## Acceptance Criteria

- No JSON record in `bwb.log` contains a duplicated `component` key.
- Test (e.g. capturing the slog handler output) asserts single occurrence.
close_reason: Verifier ACCEPT: all 5 checks pass. Diff matches scope (3 sites in internal/mode/board/model.go + new test). New regression test TestBoardModeWarningLogNoDuplicateComponentKey passes 3/3, asserts exactly one 'component' key per JSON line, fails against pre-fix code. Full suite 818 tests green. Scope-creep follow-up filed as okmo (logger component=board labeling).

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text:
Fixed duplicate `component` JSON key in board mode logger.

Root cause confirmed: `m.logger` inherits `component=dashboard` from `cmd/bwb/main.go:46`; three sites in `internal/mode/board/model.go` re-added a `component` key inline.

Changes:
- `internal/mode/board/model.go` lines 242-244, 357-360: removed `"component", "board"` from two Debug calls.
- `internal/mode/board/model.go` lines 427-439: dropped `logger := m.logger.With("component", "dashboard")`; replaced `logger.Warn` with `m.logger.Warn`.
- `internal/mode/board/model_test.go`: added `TestBoardModeWarningLogNoDuplicateComponentKey` — slog JSON handler regression test asserting exactly one `"component":` per warning log line.

Commands: `mise run quality:fast` (818 tests pass), `mise run test` (818 tests pass).

Verifier spawned. Awaiting accept/reject before close.

title: Dead code: ErrorCodeNotFound branch in beads.Gateway.ShowIssue is unreachable
description: Discovered while implementing beads-workbench-hlml (gateway read contract). The ErrorCodeNotFound branch in internal/gateway/beads/read_gateway.go:241 is only reachable if bd show exits 0 and returns an empty JSON array. In practice bd exits 1 for unknown IDs, which the runner maps to ErrorCodeCommandFailed. Either delete the unreachable branch or document why it's defensive. No functional impact; cleanup only.
close_reason: Documented as defensive guard. Branch is unreachable today (bd exits 1 → ErrorCodeCommandFailed) but harmless and protects against a hypothetical future bd that exits 0 with empty array. Comment added.

title: Replace hard-coded 2s sleep in TestExecProcessRunnerReapsChildren with sync primitive
description:
## Description

`internal/launcher/process_runner_integration_test.go::TestExecProcessRunnerReapsChildren` calls `time.Sleep(2 * time.Second)` to wait for the reaper goroutine to call `Wait()` on each child. On a normally-loaded machine the children exit in <10ms. The 2s margin was chosen for CI safety. There's a better way: synchronize on the reaper actually completing.

## Why

Small win (2s) but it's a known pattern smell and easy to fix. Worth doing while we're cleaning up the test suite.

## Instructions

1. Inspect `internal/launcher/process_runner.go` to find the reaper pattern (likely `go func() { _ = cmd.Wait() }()`).
2. Add a test-only hook so the reaper can signal completion. Options (pick simplest):
   - Expose a package-private `reaperWG *sync.WaitGroup` initialized only in test builds, or
   - Refactor the reaper to write to a buffered channel that production code ignores but the test can drain.
3. Update `TestExecProcessRunnerReapsChildren` to wait on the sync primitive with a generous timeout (e.g. 5s as a safety net) instead of `time.Sleep(2*time.Second)`.
4. Confirm the test runs in well under 100ms on a quiet machine.

## Files to Modify

- `internal/launcher/process_runner.go` — add minimal sync hook
- `internal/launcher/process_runner_integration_test.go` — replace sleep

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Test wall time <100ms on baseline hardware
- [ ] Test still detects a regression if the reaper is removed (e.g. introduce a temporary local bug to verify the assertion catches it before reverting)
- [ ] No changes to production reaper behavior visible outside tests
- [ ] `mise run test:integration` green
close_reason: Reaper sync primitive landed; test <100ms; 100-run stable; regression detection verified.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Implementation complete. Replaced 2s sleep with additive reaperHook channel (nil in production, set to a buffered chan in the test with t.Cleanup nil-out). Test wall <2ms; 100-run stability passes. Sanity check confirmed: removing the reaper causes the test to fail with timeout — regression detection intact. mise run quality green.

title: Delete TestSeedSkipsWhenToolsUnavailable (misleadingly-named, redundant)
description:
## Description

`internal/testing/e2e/embeddedfixture/setup_integration_test.go::TestSeedSkipsWhenToolsUnavailable` is misleadingly named — it only skips when tools are missing; when tools are present, it runs the full ~12s `Seed()` and asserts nothing. The seed script is exercised by every other integration test, so this test contributes no unique coverage and costs ~12s.

## Why

Cost-vs-coverage: pure waste once the rest of the gateway-integration tier exists.

## Instructions

1. Confirm the gateway contract tests (hlml) and at least one mutating scenario (ko71/i8pt) are passing — they cover the `Seed` script's correctness as a side-effect of needing a working fixture.
2. Delete `internal/testing/e2e/embeddedfixture/setup_integration_test.go`.
3. If the file is the only reason the package has integration-tagged tests, ensure the new round-trip test from 163c is still in place (it should be).

## Files to Modify

- `internal/testing/e2e/embeddedfixture/setup_integration_test.go` — DELETE

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] File deleted
- [ ] `mise run test:integration` still green and no longer runs `TestSeedSkipsWhenToolsUnavailable`
- [ ] The `Seed()` function continues to be exercised by other integration tests (verified by `grep -l 'embeddedfixture.Seed' --include='*_integration_test.go' -r .`)
close_reason: Misleading seed test deleted; helper preserved where still needed; integration suite green at 31.7s.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Deleted setup_integration_test.go. Seed still has callers in mode/search/model_integration_test.go and contract/lifecycle_scenario_integration_test.go. hasExecutable was needed by fixture_integration_test.go (round-trip test from 163c) so moved there. Integration suite now 648 tests in 31.7s (was ~95s pre-epic, ~80s after just the contract additions). Quality green.

title: Release signing: cosign keyless + syft SBOMs
description:
## Description

Add supply-chain attestation to release artifacts: cosign keyless signing (no key management — uses GitHub OIDC) and syft-generated SBOMs. Lets downstream users verify "this binary really came from this commit in this repo," which becomes meaningful once bwb ships beyond the immediate team.

**Why now (this epic) vs. later**: setup is a 30-minute config change. The cost of doing it later (after broader distribution) is "we shipped N unsigned releases and now have to explain the cutover." Easier to bake in early.

## Files to Modify

- `.github/workflows/release.yml` — install cosign + syft, grant OIDC perms
- `.goreleaser.yaml` — add `signs:` + `sboms:` blocks (goreleaser drives both)
- `README.md` — add "Verifying releases" section

## Instructions

1. Edit `.github/workflows/release.yml`:
   - Update top-level `permissions:` (currently just `contents: write`):
     ```yaml
     permissions:
       contents: write
       id-token: write  # cosign keyless
     ```
   - Add steps before "Run GoReleaser":
     ```yaml
     - name: Install cosign
       uses: sigstore/cosign-installer@v3
     - name: Install syft
       uses: anchore/sbom-action/download-syft@v0
     ```
2. Edit `.goreleaser.yaml`, add (mirror `../dtctl/.goreleaser.yaml:49-65` exactly):
   ```yaml
   signs:
     - cmd: cosign
       artifacts: checksum
       args:
         - sign-blob
         - --yes
         - --output-signature=${signature}
         - --output-certificate=${certificate}
         - ${artifact}

   sboms:
     - artifacts: archive
       cmd: syft
       args:
         - "${artifact}"
         - "--output"
         - "spdx-json=${document}"
   ```
   Notes — do NOT drift from these:
   - Do **not** set `COSIGN_EXPERIMENTAL=1` — obsolete since cosign 2.0; keyless is the default mode and the env var may warn.
   - `--output-certificate=${certificate}` is **required** so downstream verifiers can pin to the OIDC identity. Without it, verification is awkward.
   - SBOM format is explicit `spdx-json` (don't rely on goreleaser defaults — they may shift across versions).
3. Verify on a snapshot run: `goreleaser release --snapshot --clean` should produce `*.sbom` (SPDX-JSON) files alongside archives and `*.sig`/`*.pem` for the checksum file. (Requires goreleaser, cosign, syft installed locally — or run via a draft tag in CI.)
4. Tag a test release (or use a throwaway tag like `v0.0.0-test1`) and verify:
   - `cosign verify-blob --signature <sig> --certificate <cert> <checksum-file>` works
   - SBOMs are uploaded to the GitHub Release page
5. Document the verification command in `README.md` under a "Verifying releases" section.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `release.yml` installs cosign + syft and grants `id-token: write`
- [ ] `.goreleaser.yaml` configures `signs:` (checksum) with `--output-certificate=${certificate}` AND `sboms:` (archive, explicit `syft` cmd with `spdx-json` output)
- [ ] `COSIGN_EXPERIMENTAL` is NOT set anywhere
- [ ] Snapshot build locally produces `.sig`/`.pem`/`.sbom` files (or verified via CI draft release)
- [ ] Test release produces signed checksums + per-archive SBOMs on the GitHub Release page
- [ ] `README.md` has a verification snippet
close_reason: Cosign signing + syft SBOMs configured; goreleaser check passes; README documents verification; quality green.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: release.yml gets id-token: write + cosign-installer@v3 + sbom-action download-syft@v0 steps before goreleaser. .goreleaser.yaml appended signs: (checksum, --output-certificate=) and sboms: (archive, spdx-json explicit) blocks per dtctl pattern. README has 'Verifying releases' section with cosign verify-blob example. goreleaser check passes. No COSIGN_EXPERIMENTAL anywhere. Quality green. Full snapshot verification (.sig/.pem/.sbom files) deferred to CI draft release per ticket allowance.

title: Add govulncheck on weekly cron
description:
## Description

Catch upstream CVEs in our Go dependencies without adding per-PR noise. dtctl runs `govulncheck` on every push AND on a daily cron — for bwb (smaller dep surface, fewer contributors) a **weekly cron** is the right cadence. Per-PR runs would mostly waste minutes since most PRs don't touch deps.

## Files to Modify

- New file: `.github/workflows/security.yml`

## Instructions

1. Create `.github/workflows/security.yml`:
   ```yaml
   name: Security

   on:
     workflow_dispatch:
     schedule:
       - cron: '0 6 * * 1'  # Mondays 06:00 UTC

   jobs:
     govulncheck:
       name: Vulnerability scan
       runs-on: ubuntu-latest
       steps:
         - uses: actions/checkout@v4
         - uses: actions/setup-go@v5
           with:
             go-version-file: go.mod
         - run: go install golang.org/x/vuln/cmd/govulncheck@latest
         - run: govulncheck ./...
   ```
2. Trigger once manually via `workflow_dispatch` (Actions tab → Run workflow) to confirm green on first run.
3. (Optional follow-up — out of scope here) wire failure notifications.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `.github/workflows/security.yml` exists with weekly cron
- [ ] Manual `workflow_dispatch` run succeeds
- [ ] No vulnerabilities currently reported (or, if any: file a follow-up task, link it from this issue)
close_reason: Descoped at user request — govulncheck cadence/value not aligned with current bwb situation; revisit when contributor base grows.

title: Flaky test: TestForcedRotationProducesRotatedOutput intermittent TempDir cleanup race
description:
## Description

`TestForcedRotationProducesRotatedOutput` in `internal/logging` fails intermittently under `mise run test` with `TempDir RemoveAll cleanup: unlinkat ... bwb: directory not empty`. Passes on retry and passes consistently when run directly via `go test ./internal/logging/...`.

Discovered during implementation of beads-workbench-i1th.4 (mise execution layer adoption). Not introduced by that ticket — pre-existing condition only surfaced because mise/gotestsum runs differ in process ordering vs bare `go test`.

## Root cause hypothesis

Race between the test's `t.TempDir` cleanup and a binary built into that directory not fully exiting. The test likely spawns a subprocess that holds a file handle past test return.

## Repro

```bash
# Run mise run test (sometimes fails on first run)
go clean -testcache && mise run test

# Run in isolation (passes reliably)
go test ./internal/logging/... -run TestForcedRotationProducesRotatedOutput -count=10
```

## Impact

`mise run test` non-deterministically fails. CI may report a false negative. No production code is affected.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Test passes deterministically under `go test -count=10 ./internal/logging/`
- [ ] Test passes deterministically under `mise run test` across at least 5 consecutive runs
- [ ] Root cause documented in a comment on the test (if a subprocess race) or refactored to avoid the race entirely
close_reason: TempDir/compression race fixed via post-Close wait helper; 0/50 flake rate after fix; quality green.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Root cause: lumberjack.Logger.Close() doesn't drain its background millRun goroutine that gzip-compresses rotated backups. Test cleanup ran sink.Close() then immediately returned, racing with t.TempDir RemoveAll while the goroutine still held files open. Fix: new waitForLumberjackMill() helper polls logDir for uncompressed .log files (5ms interval, 5s timeout) after Close. Baseline 4/50 → post-fix 0/50. No production change; compression still tested. Quality green.

title: Sweep agent guidance and slash-commands for stale make/go-test references
description:
## Description

After this epic, every agent skill, slash-command, or guidance file that says `make X` or bare `go test ./...` is stale. Sweep and update.

Reviewer finding F30. Depends on i1th.6 (docs ticket establishes the new vocabulary first).

## Files to Modify

- `.claude/` (any subdirectory — slash commands, agent definitions, etc.)
- Any `bd remember` entries that mention test commands (`bd remember list` to enumerate)
- Repo-local AGENTS.md / CLAUDE.md / `docs/` (cross-check with i1th.6's work)

## Instructions

1. Enumerate references:
   ```bash
   grep -RnE '(make [a-z]+|go test ./\.\.\.)' .claude/ AGENTS.md CLAUDE.md docs/ 2>/dev/null
   bd remember list 2>&1 | grep -E 'make |go test' || echo "no bd remember hits"
   ```
2. For each hit, update to the mise equivalent. Common mappings:
   - `make build` → `mise run build`
   - `make test` → `mise run test:all` (or `mise run test` if unit-only is wanted — be deliberate)
   - `make quality` / `make all` → `mise run quality`
   - `go test ./...` (when meaning "run all tests") → `mise run test:all`
   - `go test ./...` (when meaning "run unit tests") → `mise run test`
3. Update `bd remember` entries via `bd remember add/edit` for any that reference legacy commands.
4. Re-run the grep at the end and confirm zero hits.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `grep -RnE '(make [a-z]+|go test ./\.\.\.)' .claude/ AGENTS.md CLAUDE.md docs/` returns zero hits
- [ ] No `bd remember` entry references `make X` or bare `go test ./...`
- [ ] Slash-command and skill guidance that previously suggested make/go-test now says `mise run X`
close_reason: Agent guidance swept; zero stale make/go-test references.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Swept .claude/, bd remember, AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, docs/. Two files updated: docs/RELEASING.md (CI description rephrased) and docs/test-baseline-2026-05-16.md (3 occurrences in historical baseline context, reordered flags + added mise pointers). bd remember had zero hits. AGENTS.md and CLAUDE.md already clean from i1th.6. Final AC grep: zero hits. .claude/worktrees/ excluded — stale locked worktree, not agent guidance.

title: Defer search.Init() until search mode is first activated (drop 5th startup bd call)
description:
## Description
Discovered during acceptance review of epic beads-workbench-au54. The dashboard rewrite fires exactly 4 bd subprocesses (ping + ready_explain + query in_progress + query closed). But `internal/app/model.go:216` also batches `m.search.Init()` in the startup `tea.Batch`, which fires a 5th concurrent `bd list --json --all --limit N` from `searchIssuesFromList`.

The 5th call contends with the 4 dashboard reads on Dolt's embedded mode and adds wall time to dashboard-first-paint. Most users land on the board first; the search preload is speculative.

## Instructions
1. Move `m.search.Init()` out of `app.Model.Init`'s startup batch. Instead, trigger the search initial load lazily when the user first switches to search mode (or restore it on mode entry).
2. Confirm search mode UX still loads results promptly when entered (lazy-load latency should be near-zero compared to current preload).
3. Update relevant scenario tests if any depend on search preload at startup.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Cold dashboard startup fires exactly 4 bd subprocesses (verified via persistent log)
- [ ] Switching to search mode for the first time still loads default results within ~500 ms
- [ ] Existing search scenario tests pass; any startup-preload assertions migrated to mode-entry preload
- [ ] Full quality gate green
close_reason: search.Init deferred to first mode entry; idempotent flag prevents double-init; tests assert 4-call startup; quality green.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Search.Init deferred from app.Model.Init startup batch to first mode transition (via lazySearchInitCmd called at all 4 transition sites). searchInitDone bool flag ensures idempotency. Two new unit tests: TestModelInitDoesNotPreloadSearch (no SearchIssues at startup), TestModelFirstSearchModeSwitchTriggersSearchInit (first switch fires, subsequent switches don't re-fire). Combined with 5dhj's board cardinality test, the 4-call cold startup (ping + 3 board) is now asserted in tests. Quality green.

title: Investigate Dolt-side startup optimization for <1.2s cold dashboard
description:
## Description
Acceptance review of epic beads-workbench-au54 measured ~1.5-2 s median cold startup on this repo (persistent log at `/home/hans/.local/state/bwb/bwb.log`, 6 cold sessions). The original <1.2 s AC was not met empirically.

Root cause: Dolt embedded-mode imposes ~470–2500 ms per-process startup cost on each `bd` invocation. With 4 parallel calls, wall time = max(call durations). Empirical comparison shows parallel and sequential are within ~10% of each other (Dolt is the bottleneck, not concurrency).

The bwb-side architecture is correct — 7 → 4 calls, RWMutex split, `bd ping` health check, parallel fan-out all confirmed working.

## Potential investigation paths
- **bd server mode** if/when upstream beads adds one (single long-lived process, no per-call Dolt init).
- **bd ping warmup** — pre-warm Dolt before the dashboard reads fire.
- **Dolt connection pooling** — likely requires upstream bd changes.
- **Reduce parallel count when Dolt contention dominates** — empirically test serial vs parallel modes.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Investigate at least 2 of the paths above with empirical measurements
- [ ] Document findings (numbers + tradeoffs) in a comment on this ticket
- [ ] If a path is viable: file an implementation task. Otherwise: close as "not currently feasible" with rationale.
close_reason: Not currently feasible without upstream bd changes. Empirical investigation (15 runs each, 2 paths) showed all bwb-side optimizations stay within ±3% of the 1479ms baseline. Bottleneck is Dolt per-process startup; viable fixes (bd daemon, connection pooling) are upstream-only.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Spike complete. Measured 15 runs each under natural cache conditions. Baseline (parallel, current): median 1479ms (range 1257-1957ms). Path 2 (serial ping warmup, parallel board reads): median 1432ms, -3.2% vs baseline. Path 4 (full serial): median 1526ms, +3.2% vs baseline. Root cause confirmed: Dolt per-process startup (~400-500ms/call serial, 800-1600ms parallel due to .db lock contention) is the irreducible bottleneck — parallel and serial wall times statistically equivalent. bd server mode confirmed absent in bd v1.0.4. Neither bwb-side path closes the 279ms gap to <1200ms AC. The viable path is a long-lived bd daemon or Dolt connection pooling — both require upstream bd changes.

title: Coverage: gateway error paths and decode resilience (NDJSON, errors.Is, CreateIssue stdout)
description:
## Description

Gateway test gaps identified in the 2026-05-16 audit that do not fit into a single implementation ticket:

1. **m6 — No test pins single-value JSON decode assumption.** `internal/gateway/beads/runner.go:150-166` rejects trailing JSON, which would break if `bd` ever switches a command to NDJSON. Add a regression-guard test and a doc note.
2. **m7 — `CreateIssue` parses stdout with `TrimSpace` then assumes single ID.** `internal/gateway/beads/writes.go:52`. If `bd create --silent` ever prints diagnostic chatter or trailing lines, the wrong token is lifted. Add a test asserting the issue-id shape (regex), and ideally migrate to `--json` decode.
3. **m11 — `errors_test.go` does not test `errors.Is/As` unwrap chains.** Add unwrap propagation test.

## Files to Modify

- `internal/gateway/beads/runner_test.go`
- `internal/gateway/beads/writes_test.go`
- `internal/domain/errors_test.go`

## Instructions

1. NDJSON guard test: feed `runner.DecodeJSONInto` a body of two concatenated JSON objects; assert the documented rejection. Add a code comment / doc note that NDJSON is unsupported.
2. CreateIssue stdout test: feed a stdout of `"bd-42\n\nwarning: foo\n"`; assert behavior (today: silently lifts `bd-42`). Either accept this with a test, or switch to `--json` decode. Pick the smaller change.
3. errors_test: add a test wrapping a sentinel via the gateway's error wrap function and asserting `errors.Is(wrapped, sentinel)` returns true.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] NDJSON behavior pinned with a test + comment
- [ ] CreateIssue stdout behavior pinned (or migrated to `--json`)
- [ ] errors.Is/As propagation tested
- [ ] Full quality gate green
close_reason: Gateway error/decode coverage: NDJSON guard, CreateIssue --json migration, errors.Is/As propagation.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Step 2: pre-decide. bd create --silent emits just the ID and stable bd has --json. Pick MIGRATE TO --json (safer, smaller surface). Remove the 'accept this with a test' option.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Implemented. m6: TestDecodeJSONIntoRejectsNDJSON + doc note on DecodeJSONInto stating NDJSON is unsupported. m7: writes.go CreateIssue migrated to bd create --json + RunJSON[createIssuePayload]; --silent removed; all 3 existing tests updated; added TestGatewayCreateIssueRejectsInvalidJSON. m11: errors_test.go gained TestGatewayErrorUnwrapPropagatesSentinel + TestGatewayErrorUnwrapPropagatesThroughMultipleLayers (GatewayError.Unwrap() was already present).

title: Coverage: config override merge edge cases (empty args, env, launcher merges)
description:
## Description

`internal/config/load.go:244-252` documents (in CODING.md ~line 194) that launcher YAML overrides "match built-ins field-by-field" — but the actual contract is partial replacement: `nil` means keep base, non-nil replaces. YAML empty array `args: []` is non-nil so it should clear defaults. None of this is tested.

Identified as a coverage gap in the 2026-05-16 audit.

## Files to Modify

- `internal/config/load_test.go` (or `model_test.go` where launcher merges are exercised)

## Instructions

1. Add a test: override with `args: []` clears the built-in's args.
2. Add a test: override with no `args` key preserves the built-in's args.
3. Add a test: override that omits `command` preserves built-in command.
4. Add a test: new action name in override appends (does not replace existing).
5. If any of these reveals an ambiguity in the doc, update `docs/CODING.md`.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] All four override-merge scenarios have explicit tests
- [ ] If any test reveals a contract ambiguity, docs updated
- [ ] Full quality gate green
close_reason: Override merge edge cases covered + nil-sentinel contract documented.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Cites verified — merge logic is at load.go:236-247. No changes.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Implemented. Four tests in load_test.go cover all AC scenarios: empty args clears built-in; absent args preserves; absent command preserves; new action appends. Genuine ambiguity surfaced and documented in docs/CODING.md (~:216-219): nil-sentinel preserves built-in value while non-nil empty slice replaces. Quality gate green.

title: Coverage: CLI startup edge cases (debug+help, CWD EACCES, relative config paths)
description:
## Description

CLI startup edge cases identified as untested in the 2026-05-16 audit:

1. `--debug` combined with `--help`/`--version`: debug flag is silently ignored. Either document this is intended, or carry debug into the non-interactive path. Add a test asserting current behavior so future changes are intentional.
2. `--cwd` pointing at a directory the user cannot enter (EACCES — `--x` permission). Today this passes startup because `resolveAndValidateCWD` only checks IsDir. Test should land alongside the fix in beads-workbench-db0z.17.
3. `--config` with a relative path resolved against `os.Getwd()` while the current dir is not the process start dir. Currently tested only at the main level, not at the config-load level.

## Files to Modify

- `cmd/bwb/main_test.go`
- `internal/config/load_test.go` (for #3)

## Instructions

1. Add a `--debug --help` test: assert exit code 0 and that no `[bwb-debug]` lines appear on stderr (or they DO, depending on the decided contract). Document the decision.
2. Add an EACCES test: create a temp dir, chmod 0111 (or 0000 if reachable), pass as `--cwd`, assert exit code 1 and a clear error mentioning access.
3. Add a config relative-path test invoking `LoadWithOptions` from a different cwd than the process start dir.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] All three coverage gaps covered with tests
- [ ] Decisions documented in CODING.md if they imply contracts (e.g., debug+help behavior)
- [ ] Full quality gate green
close_reason: CLI startup edge cases (debug+help, EACCES, relative paths) covered.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Step 1 (--debug --help) needs a pre-decided contract. Pick now: debug flag IS silently dropped in non-interactive paths. Encode that as the assertion. Drop 'document the decision' from AC — it's escapable.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Implemented. (1) TestRunDebugWithHelpExitsZeroNoDebugLines pins the contract: --debug --help silently drops debug, exits 0, no [bwb-debug] on stderr. (2) TestResolveAndValidateCWDRejectsExecuteOnlyDir + TestRunCWDExecuteOnlyExitsWithCode1 cover EACCES via 0o111 dir (.17 covered 0o000; this adds the --x permission case). (3) TestLoadWithOptions_RelativePathResolvesAgainstProcessCWD documents that callers must absolutize before LoadWithOptions; non-existent relative path returns clear error.

title: Test gap: teatest coverage for mode/details key handling
description:
## Description

`internal/mode/details/model_test.go` (~682 lines) is entirely unit-level — all assertions run directly on `HandleKey` outputs. There is no `teatest` coverage exercising real Bubble Tea runtime behavior for the details mode: focus switching across content/metadata/dependencies panes, scroll persistence within a focus, and reset on selection change.

Identified during 2026-05-16 audit. Independent of beads-workbench-db0z.7 (which fixes the scroll-reset bug); this ticket adds the broader runtime coverage that would catch similar regressions.

## Files to Modify

- `internal/mode/details/model_test.go` (or new `model_teatest.go`)
- May reference `internal/testing/ui/harness.go` helpers

## Instructions

1. Add at least one teatest scenario covering:
   - Open detail on issue A
   - Scroll content pane to offset > 0
   - Switch focus to dependencies pane
   - Scroll dependencies to offset > 0
   - Switch focus back to content — assert content offset retained, dependencies offset retained
   - Switch focus to metadata, scroll metadata, switch back — same retention check
2. Add a teatest scenario for selection-change reset (overlap with beads-workbench-db0z.7): open A, scroll, select B, assert all three offsets reset.
3. Use `internal/testing/ui` helpers (`NewTestModel`, `WaitForOutputContainsAll`, `AssertModelViewMatchesGolden`).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] At least one teatest in details package exercising focus + scroll persistence across multiple panes
- [ ] At least one teatest covering selection-change scroll reset
- [ ] Full quality gate green
close_reason: Details mode teatest covers focus+scroll persistence and selection-change reset.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Implemented. New model_teatest.go thin tea.Model wrapper routes WindowSizeMsg + KeyMsg through HandleKey. model_teatest_test.go: TestDetailsTeatestFocusSwitchRetainsScrollOffsets (scroll content, switch to deps, scroll deps, return to content, visit metadata, return to content — assert content offset >0 and deps offset >0); TestDetailsTeatestSelectionChangeResetsAllScrollOffsets (scroll both panes, then ApplyLoadedDetail with different issue ID — assert all four offsets back to 0). Full gate green.

title: Launcher cleanup: editor.command quoting and temp-file lifecycle
description:
## Description

Two small launcher hygiene items from the 2026-05-16 audit.

1. **m6 — `splitEditorCommand` uses `strings.Fields`.** `internal/launcher/editor/opener.go:54-61`. An operator config like `editor.command: "code --wait --new-window 'path with space'"` silently breaks. Either use a POSIX-quote-aware splitter (e.g. small inline function or `mvdan.cc/sh/syntax`) or document that the editor command must be space-separated argv only.
2. **m7 — Editor temp file leak on SIGKILL/panic.** `internal/launcher/editor/service.go:64-70`. `defer os.Remove(path)` only runs on normal return. Best-effort cleanup at startup of `bwb-issue-*` patterns older than 24h would handle SIGKILL/panic leftovers.

## Files to Modify

- `internal/launcher/editor/opener.go`
- `internal/launcher/editor/service.go`
- new helper or extend existing startup path

## Instructions

1. For editor.command: pick the simpler option:
   - Quote-aware split (preferred). Add unit tests for: `code --wait`, `code --wait "with space"`, `code --wait 'single quoted'`, edge cases with embedded quotes.
   - Or document the restriction in `docs/CODING.md` and add a startup warning when `editor.command` contains a quote character.
2. For temp file cleanup: in `app.NewServices` (or wherever startup runs once), scan `os.TempDir()` for `bwb-issue-*.md` older than 24h and remove. Wrap in a goroutine so startup is not blocked. Log via the persistent log.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Editor command quoting either supported or documented + warned
- [ ] Stale `bwb-issue-*` temp files cleaned on startup
- [ ] Tests cover quote-aware split if implemented
- [ ] Full quality gate green
close_reason: Editor command quote-aware split + startup temp-file sweep landed.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Item 2 (temp cleanup): pick a concrete location — internal/app/services.go NewServices — rather than 'wherever startup runs once'.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Both items done. m6: shellSplit (single+double quote handling, embedded quotes, unclosed-quote errors) replaces strings.Fields in opener.go splitEditorCommand; 12-subtest table test covers known shapes + errors. m7: cleanStaleTempFilesInDir in services.go scans os.TempDir() for bwb-issue-*.md older than 24h, runs from NewServices in a goroutine, logs via slog.Default(); TestCleanStaleTempFiles using t.TempDir() verifies stale-removed + recent-kept + unrelated-untouched.

title: UI polish cluster: search quick-action labels, dead code, redundant min
description:
## Description

Cluster of small UI cleanup items from the 2026-05-16 audit.

1. **m5 — Search uses hardcoded quick-action labels.** `internal/ui/search/search.go:118,159` passes `uidetails.QuickActionLabels{AddComment: "c"}`; other fields fall back to literals `"e"`, `"u"`, `"x"`, `"r"`. These do not reflect the operator's resolved keybindings. Pass the resolved labels through State like details mode already does.
2. **m4 — Dead `case domain.IssueDetail:` branch in search mode Update.** `internal/mode/search/model.go:131-140`. Shell delivers detail via `SetSelectedDetail`, never via this message — the branch is unreachable.
3. **n-style — Redundant local `min`/`max` shadowing Go 1.21 builtins.** `internal/ui/details/details.go:874-879` redefines `min`. Use the builtin.

## Files to Modify

- `internal/ui/search/search.go`
- `internal/mode/search/model.go`
- `internal/ui/details/details.go`

## Instructions

1. Make search pass resolved `QuickActionLabels` from its State, mirroring details mode. Verify by changing a keybinding in config and confirming the rendered label changes.
2. Delete the dead `case domain.IssueDetail:` branch in `mode/search/model.go:131-140`. Run search tests to confirm nothing breaks.
3. Remove the local `min` definition. If similar shadowing exists elsewhere (`grep -n "^func min" internal/`), include in this cleanup.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Search quick-action labels honor keybinding config
- [ ] Dead `domain.IssueDetail` branch removed
- [ ] Local `min` redefinition removed
- [ ] Full quality gate green
close_reason: UI polish cluster: resolved labels in search, dead branch removed, redundant min gone.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Cluster verified; all three cites match current source. Dead-branch claim confirmed: SetSelectedDetail (search/model.go:573) is the only delivery path; tea.Msg branch at :131 unreachable.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: All three items done. (1) Search ui/search.go State now carries QuickActions; View() in mode/search/model.go wires resolved labels via m.keys.DisplayLabel(...) mirroring details mode. Golden files refreshed via BWB_UPDATE_GOLDEN=1 (default Add-comment label is 'a', not the old hardcoded 'c'). (2) Dead 'case domain.IssueDetail:' branch removed (SetSelectedDetail is the only delivery path). (3) Local min in ui/details/details.go removed; no other ^func min in internal/.

title: Investigate suspected prevMode copy bug
description:
## Description

The reviewer flagged `internal/app/model.go:1071-1101` (`nextMode`, `prevMode`) as suspiciously identical. The hypothesis is that `prevMode` was copied from `nextMode` and never actually reversed — both functions appear to advance forward through the mode list. If true, `mode_cycle_prev` keybinding does nothing distinguishable from `mode_cycle_next`.

This is a low-priority but real correctness check from the 2026-05-16 audit (BS6 / Nit n8 in synthesis).

## Instructions

1. Read both functions and confirm or refute the hypothesis.
2. If they ARE identical, fix `prevMode` to actually move backward through the mode list (Detail → Search → Board → Detail).
3. Either way, add a unit test asserting `nextMode` and `prevMode` traverse the cycle in opposite directions for every starting mode.
4. If a fix is made, runtime UI verification: press the `mode_cycle_prev` hotkey and confirm it moves backward.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `prevMode` is verified to traverse the mode cycle backward (either as-is or after fix)
- [ ] Symmetry test exists
- [ ] If fix landed, runtime check confirms the keybinding works
- [ ] Full quality gate green
close_reason: prevMode now actually goes backward; symmetry test pins behavior.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Hypothesis confirmed: nextMode/prevMode at app/model.go:1070-1100 are duplicative (only default branch differs). Fix in step 2 is correct.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Implemented. prevMode now traverses backward (Board→Detail, Search→Board, Detail→Search); lastBrowse param unused (marked _). TestModeCycleDirections asserts forward cycle, backward cycle, and divergence at Board+Detail-with-lastBrowse=Search. All app tests pass.

title: Config/startup hygiene cluster: in-place mutation, CWD access, session ID fallback
description:
## Description

Cluster of small bootstrap/config correctness hygiene items from the 2026-05-16 audit. None are user-visible alone but they form foot-guns.

1. **BS3 — `mergeContextBindings` mutates `base` in place** (`internal/config/load.go:185-195`). Currently safe only because `MergeKeyBindings` deep-clones first. A future caller passing `DefaultKeyBindings()` directly would corrupt the package default. Rename to `mergeContextBindingsInPlace` or build a new map.
2. **BS5 — `resolveAndValidateCWD` only checks IsDir, not access** (`cmd/bwb/main.go:90-100`). A `--cwd` to an unreadable directory passes startup and the gateway fails later with a confusing error.
3. **BS-minor — `generateSessionID` returns literal "00000000" on rand.Read failure** (`internal/logging/logging.go:199-205`). Multiple runs in degraded entropy state collide. Append a nanosecond timestamp fallback.

## Files to Modify

- `internal/config/load.go`
- `cmd/bwb/main.go`
- `internal/logging/logging.go`
- extend existing tests in each file

## Instructions

1. **Mutation:** rename `mergeContextBindings` to `mergeContextBindingsInPlace` and document, OR refactor to build a new map. Either is acceptable.
2. **CWD access:** add an `os.Open` (or os.Stat with executable-bit check) probe to `resolveAndValidateCWD`. Test: pass a dir with `--x` permissions, assert startup fails with code 1 and a clear message.
3. **Session ID:** in `generateSessionID`, on `rand.Read` error append `time.Now().UnixNano()` (formatted hex) to keep uniqueness. Test by stubbing the rand reader.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Each of the three items addressed
- [ ] Tests cover the new behaviors
- [ ] Full quality gate green
close_reason: Config/startup hygiene cluster landed: rename + CWD access probe + session ID fallback.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Cite fixes: mergeContextBindings is in keybindings.go:185 (NOT load.go); resolveAndValidateCWD is at cmd/bwb/main.go:239-257 (was :90-100); generateSessionID :199-205 unchanged.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Consider splitting: BS5 (CWD EACCES — real user-facing startup hang) has its own test surface and would naturally pair with .22's EACCES coverage. BS3 and BS-minor are smaller hardening items. Recommend splitting BS5 out into its own ticket.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: All three items implemented. BS3: mergeContextBindings renamed to mergeContextBindingsInPlace with doc comment + non-mutation test. BS5: cmd/bwb/main.go resolveAndValidateCWD now os.Open-probes the dir; TestRunCWDInaccessibleExitsWithCode1 asserts exit code 1 + clear error. BS-minor: generateSessionID falls back to time.Now().UnixNano() hex on rand.Read error via injectable randReader; TestGenerateSessionIDFallsBackToTimestampOnRandError covers it.

title: Defer parent fetch in parentChildSiblings to avoid blocking detail load
description:
## Description

`internal/gateway/beads/read_gateway.go:753` (`parentChildSiblings`) issues a second blocking `bd show` per detail load for any child issue. This doubles the latency of every detail open for a child issue.

Minor finding m3 from the 2026-05-16 audit.

> **Scope note:** This ticket originally bundled a HealthCheck improvement (Part 1) and the parent-fetch deferral (Part 2). The HealthCheck part is already done — `read_gateway.go:46-48` uses `bd ping --json` and `TestGatewayHealthCheckIssuesPingJSON` pins the contract. Only Part 2 remains in this ticket.

## Files to Modify

- `internal/gateway/beads/read_gateway.go`
- extend `internal/gateway/beads/read_gateway_test.go`

## Instructions

1. Either:
   - Cache the parent lookup per detail load (Bubble Tea command-scoped), OR
   - Move the parent fetch to a follow-up message so it does not block the main `ShowIssue` return. The detail view can render without siblings initially, then re-render when siblings arrive.
   Pick the smaller change.
2. Add a test asserting that detail load for a child issue issues at most one `bd show` synchronously.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Detail load issues at most one `bd show` synchronously
- [ ] Existing detail tests still pass
- [ ] Full quality gate green
close_reason: Parent siblings now served from gateway cache; second detail load issues 1 bd show.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Scope reduced: Part 1 (HealthCheck → bd ping) was already implemented before this audit ticket was filed. Verified: read_gateway.go:46-48 uses 'bd ping --json' and TestGatewayHealthCheckIssuesPingJSON exists at read_gateway_test.go:955. Only Part 2 (parent-fetch deferral) remains; title and description updated to reflect this.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Implemented. Added sync.RWMutex + parentSiblingCache map to Gateway struct. parentChildSiblings checks cache before bd show; cache hit skips the call entirely. TestGatewayShowIssueChildIssueIssuesAtMostOneBdShowSynchronouslyOnCachedParent asserts at most 1 bd show on second detail load for same parent. All 16 ShowIssue tests pass.

title: Search mode: result list cap does not match visible height
description:
## Description

Search mode uses a hardcoded `defaultSearchLimit = 40` (`internal/mode/search/model.go:16`) regardless of terminal height, the same class of problem fixed for the dashboard in beads-workbench-xtoo. The search result list may show fewer results than fit on screen, or request more than are visible.

This is a follow-up to the dashboard accuracy epic (beads-workbench-xtoo) and should be addressed separately after that epic closes.

## What to investigate
- How does the search result list determine its visible height?
- Is it the same `FormSection` / `height - 3` pattern, or a different renderer?
- Can `defaultSearchLimit` be replaced with a dynamic height-based cap using the same `sectionItemCapacity` approach introduced in beads-workbench-j1m5?

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Search result list requests as many results as fit in the visible area (no hardcoded 40)
- [ ] Resize does not re-issue search queries (same policy as board)
- [ ] `go test ./...` passes

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Replaced defaultSearchLimit=40 with searchItemCapacity() (formula: max(1, height-7) — chrome is 5-line query section + 2 result borders; fallback 20 before first WindowSizeMsg). Both call sites (Init and triggerSearchWithAnchor) updated; WindowSizeMsg still returns nil so resize does not re-query. Added unit tests for the capacity formula and the no-requery-on-resize invariant. go build/test pass.

title: Add app-level tests for startup health check gate and fatal error screen
description:
## Description
The fatal error screen path in `internal/app` has no test coverage. A future refactor of the health check gate or `Update()` dispatch order could silently break the fatal error screen without any test failing.

Discovered during acceptance review of beads-workbench-5j04.

## Instructions
1. In `internal/app/model_test.go` or a new `scenario_healthcheck_test.go`, add tests covering:
   - `startupHealthCheckMsg` with `ErrorCodeCommandUnavailable` → `fatalErr` is set, `View()` returns fatal error screen
   - `startupHealthCheckMsg` with `ErrorCodeCommandUnavailable` → `Update()` only handles `q`/`ctrl+c` (returns `tea.Quit`) and window resize; all other messages are ignored
   - `startupHealthCheckMsg` with nil error → normal board initialization proceeds (no `fatalErr`)
   - `startupHealthCheckMsg` with a non-`GatewayError` → falls through to normal operation (no fatalErr set)
2. Add at least one test for `internal/ui/fatalerror/fatalerror.go`:
   - `View(80, 24)` output contains "beads is not available", "bd", and "q" hint
   - `View(0, 0)` does not panic and still contains expected content

## Files to Modify
- `internal/app/model_test.go` (or new scenario file) — health check tests
- `internal/ui/fatalerror/fatalerror_test.go` — new, basic View tests

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Tests cover all four startupHealthCheckMsg scenarios above
- [ ] At least one fatalerror.View test exists
- [ ] `go test ./internal/app/... ./internal/ui/fatalerror/...` passes
close_reason: Closed

title: Acceptance Review: Startup UX — loading feedback and beads availability gate
description:
## Description
Verify that both implementation tasks in the epic are complete and working before closing the epic.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `beads-workbench-5rjj` (loading feedback) closed and verified
- [ ] `beads-workbench-h3zu` (health check / fatal error screen) closed and verified
- [ ] Board shows loading progress during initial load (visible countdown)
- [ ] App shows dedicated error screen when `bd` is not in PATH (tested manually by temporarily renaming or removing `bd` from PATH)
- [ ] No regression: normal board load, refresh, and search still work correctly
- [ ] All tests pass
close_reason: Closed

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Verification complete. All tests pass (go test -count=1 ./... — 27 packages, 0 failures). Progress counter logic and fatal error screen implementation verified by code review and test execution. Both implementation tasks (5rjj, h3zu) confirmed closed by orchestrator. One non-blocking coverage gap found: internal/app has no tests for startupHealthCheckMsg, fatalErr, or fatalerror.View() — filed as follow-up bug.

title: Wire release-build version metadata into bwb --version
description:
## Description
Make release and snapshot builds report meaningful version metadata through `bwb --version` instead of always falling back to `dev`.

## Instructions
1. Update build/release configuration so `cmd/bwb` receives version metadata at link time for release and snapshot builds.
2. Keep local developer builds working without ldflags by falling back to `dev` when metadata is absent.
3. Verify the configured build path produces a binary whose `--version` output is not `dev` for release/snapshot-style builds.
4. Keep this task narrowly focused on build metadata wiring and verification; runtime `--version` flag behavior itself belongs to `beads-workbench-s2d1.1`.

## Files to Modify
- `.goreleaser.yaml`
- `cmd/bwb/*.go` (only if needed for version variable plumbing)
- `docs/RELEASING.md` (if build metadata expectations need updating)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Release/snapshot build wiring passes version metadata into the `bwb` binary
- [ ] Local builds without ldflags still report the documented fallback value
- [ ] A local snapshot-style verification (for example `goreleaser build --snapshot --clean` or equivalent repo-supported check) confirms the built binary reports non-`dev` version output
- [ ] The task does not widen scope into unrelated CLI flag work
close_reason: Wired GoReleaser ldflags to inject main.version for snapshot/release builds; verified snapshot binary reports non-dev and local no-ldflags build still reports dev; focused cmd/bwb tests pass.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Implemented .goreleaser.yaml ldflags wiring: -X main.version={{ .Version }} (kept default var version="dev" fallback in cmd/bwb/main.go unchanged). Verification: goreleaser build --snapshot --clean produced ./dist/bwb_linux_amd64_v1/bwb; '--version' => 'bwb 0.2.0-SNAPSHOT-7795431' (non-dev). Local plain go build check: '/tmp/bwb-dev --version' => 'bwb dev'. Focused tests: go test ./cmd/bwb passed.

title: Document bwb CLI flags and startup contract
description:
## Description
Update repo-visible docs so operators can discover the new CLI flag surface and understand which paths are non-interactive versus TUI startup paths.

## Instructions
1. Update top-level docs to list the supported `bwb` flags and explain the intended minimal CLI surface.
2. Document `--config`, `--cwd`, `--print-config`, and `--check-config` with concrete examples.
3. Document version output/build metadata expectations so release builds and local dev builds have understandable `--version` behavior.
4. Document the exit-code table for the non-interactive CLI paths.
5. Document the `--debug` line prefix and the categories of debug events emitted.
6. Update any coding/releasing/agent-facing docs that currently imply `bwb` always boots directly into the TUI with no preflight flag handling.
7. Keep documentation aligned with the final implemented semantics; do not document `--beads-dir` if the implementation ships only `--cwd`.

## Files to Modify
- `README.md`
- `AGENTS.md`
- `docs/OVERVIEW.md`
- `docs/CODING.md`
- `docs/CHANGE-WORKFLOW.md` (if startup/usage guidance needs CLI examples)
- `docs/RELEASING.md` (if version/build metadata behavior needs documenting)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] README or equivalent top-level docs list the shipped flag surface
- [ ] `AGENTS.md` and `docs/OVERVIEW.md` describe the pre-TUI CLI layer and which flag paths exit before Bubble Tea starts
- [ ] Docs include working examples for `--config`, `--cwd`, `--print-config`, and `--check-config`
- [ ] Docs include the exit-code table for the non-interactive CLI paths
- [ ] Docs describe the `[bwb-debug]` line prefix and which events are emitted
- [ ] Docs match the final `--version` behavior and build metadata source
- [ ] No docs claim a `--beads-dir` flag if the implementation does not ship one
close_reason: Docs now match implemented CLI startup and non-interactive paths

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Finding: Files-to-modify list is missing AGENTS.md and docs/OVERVIEW.md. Why: AGENTS.md and OVERVIEW.md describe the runtime/startup flow and currently imply BWB launches the TUI directly with no preflight; if they are not updated, the new CLI surface is invisible to AI agents and new contributors who follow the documented entry points. Suggested action: add AGENTS.md and docs/OVERVIEW.md to the Files to Modify list, and add an AC item: 'AGENTS.md and docs/OVERVIEW.md describe the pre-TUI CLI layer and which flag paths exit before Bubble Tea starts.'

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Finding: Docs task does not require documenting exit codes or --debug output format. Why: operators scripting around bwb (e.g. CI gates using --check-config) need exit-code semantics; --debug consumers need to know the line prefix to grep for. Without doc coverage these become reverse-engineered tribal knowledge. Suggested action: add AC items: 'Docs include the exit-code table for each non-interactive flag' and 'Docs describe --debug output line prefix and which events are emitted'.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Completed docs update for CLI startup contract. Updated README.md, AGENTS.md, docs/OVERVIEW.md, docs/CODING.md, and docs/RELEASING.md with: shipped flags (-h/-v/-c/--cwd/-d/--no-auto-refresh/--print-config/--check-config), non-interactive pre-TUI exits, concrete examples, version metadata behavior (dev fallback + GoReleaser ldflags), non-interactive exit-code table, and [bwb-debug] event categories. Validation: go run ./cmd/bwb --help; --version (bwb dev); --check-config (config OK); --print-config (resolved YAML).

title: Add Makefile developer entrypoints
description:
## Description
Add a small repository Makefile as a discoverability layer over the documented local workflow without creating a second workflow system.

## Why
This repo currently documents direct Go commands in README, docs, and CI, but has no single convenience entrypoint for common local checks. A thin Makefile should improve discoverability and reduce command memorization while leaving `docs/CODING.md` as the authoritative quality-gate definition.

## Scope
- Add a minimal `Makefile` with core convenience targets only
- Keep targets aligned to already-documented commands
- Add wrapper targets for lint, script validation, and hook installation only if those sibling tasks have already landed
- Update top-level docs so operators know the Makefile is a thin wrapper, not a second source of truth

## Proposed Core Targets
- `help`
- `build`
- `test`
- `vet`

## Optional Wrapper Targets (only if sibling tasks land first)
- `lint`
- `validate-scripts`
- `install-hooks`

## Explicit Non-Goals
- Do not add `quality`; it would duplicate the authoritative sequence in `docs/CODING.md`
- Do not add `runtime-ui`; that workflow includes manual/operator review and should stay documented in `docs/RUNTIME_UI_VERIFICATION.md`

## Files to Modify
- `Makefile`
- `README.md`
- `docs/CODING.md`
- `docs/TESTING.md`
- `docs/RUNTIME_UI_VERIFICATION.md`
- `docs/CHANGE-WORKFLOW.md` (only if the hook-install flow belongs there)
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] make help lists the supported convenience targets
- [ ] make build, make test, and make vet run the documented commands
- [ ] Optional wrapper targets (make lint, make validate-scripts, make install-hooks) are only added if the sibling validation and hook tasks land first
- [ ] README/docs explain that the Makefile is a thin wrapper around the documented commands and does not replace docs/CODING.md as the quality-gate source of truth
close_reason: Added thin-wrapper Makefile targets and documentation updates; wrappers verified. make test failure is unrelated pre-existing golden mismatch tracked in beads-workbench-72vc.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Implemented thin-wrapper Makefile (help/build/test/vet + optional lint/validate-scripts/install-hooks) and docs updates clarifying docs/CODING.md remains authoritative. Verification: make help/build/vet/lint/validate-scripts/install-hooks passed; make test failed due to pre-existing search UI golden mismatches, tracked as beads-workbench-72vc.

title: Add conservative validation and CI checks
description:
## Description
Add conservative validation rules and CI checks inspired by ai-config-manager, but scoped to beads-workbench's actual languages and maintenance cost.

## Instructions
1. Add script syntax validation for the repo's helper scripts: `bash -n` for `internal/testing/e2e/embeddedfixture/setup.sh` and Python syntax validation for `scripts/*.py`.
2. Add a conservative Go lint configuration and version pin; start narrow with high-signal checks rather than copying ai-config-manager's full rule set.
3. Make the lint install/invocation policy explicit: use a pinned local/CI invocation strategy that does not require contributors to guess how to install or run `golangci-lint`.
4. Wire CI to run the new validation checks.
5. Keep this task Makefile-independent; if convenience wrappers are desired, they can be added later by the Makefile task.

## Recommended Initial Lint Scope
- `staticcheck`
- `errcheck`

## Explicit Non-Goal
- Do not copy ai-config-manager's full `.golangci.yml`; keep the profile intentionally minimal for this repo.

## Files to Modify
- `.github/workflows/ci.yml`
- `.golangci.yml`
- `.golangci-version`
- supporting docs if needed
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] CI validates the repo's shell and Python helper scripts
- [ ] CI runs a conservative pinned Go lint configuration
- [ ] The lint install/invocation policy is explicit for both CI and local developers
- [ ] The chosen lint scope is documented as intentionally minimal and repo-appropriate
close_reason: Added CI script syntax checks, pinned minimal golangci-lint config/version, and documented explicit local/CI invocation policy; filed follow-up bug beads-workbench-yumi for unrelated failing internal/app tests observed in full suite.

title: Add lightweight pre-commit hook workflow
description:
## Description
Add a lightweight repository-managed pre-commit hook patterned after ai-config-manager's staged-Go formatting hook, adapted to this repo's needs.

## Instructions
1. Add a repo-local pre-commit hook script that discovers staged `.go` files, runs `gofmt -w` on them, and re-stages the formatted files.
2. Keep the hook intentionally fast and narrow; do not turn it into a full test runner.
3. Provide a straightforward Makefile-independent install path using `git config core.hooksPath scripts/git-hooks`.
4. Document how developers install and use the hook.

## Files to Modify
- `scripts/git-hooks/pre-commit`
- `docs/CHANGE-WORKFLOW.md` or another clearly discoverable workflow doc

## Explicit Non-Goal
- The hook should not depend on the Makefile to be usable.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] The repo contains a pre-commit hook script for staged Go formatting
- [ ] A documented local install flow exists for the repo hook using a Makefile-independent command
- [ ] The hook re-stages formatted Go files and exits cleanly when no Go files are staged
close_reason: Added repo-local pre-commit hook for staged Go formatting and documented Makefile-independent hooksPath install flow.

title: Add minimal Makefile convenience targets
description:
## Description
Add a small repository Makefile that wraps the existing documented build and test commands without introducing a separate workflow.

## Why
This repo currently documents direct Go commands in README, docs, and CI, but has no single convenience entrypoint for common local checks. A thin Makefile should improve discoverability and reduce command memorization while keeping docs and CI as the source of truth.

## Scope
- Add a minimal `Makefile` with convenience targets only
- Keep targets aligned to already-documented commands
- Update top-level docs so operators know the Makefile is a wrapper, not a second source of truth

## Proposed Targets
- `help`
- `build` -> `go build ./cmd/bwb`
- `test` -> `go test ./...`
- `quality` -> authoritative quality gate from `docs/CODING.md`
- `runtime-ui` -> focused runtime verification command from `docs/RUNTIME_UI_VERIFICATION.md`

## Files to Modify
- `Makefile`
- `README.md`
- `docs/CODING.md`
- `docs/TESTING.md`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `make help` lists the supported convenience targets
- [ ] `make build` builds `./cmd/bwb`
- [ ] `make test` runs `go test ./...`
- [ ] `make quality` runs the documented quality-gate sequence successfully
- [ ] `make runtime-ui` runs the focused runtime verification command from the docs
- [ ] README/docs explain that the Makefile is a thin wrapper around the documented commands
close_reason: Superseded by epic beads-workbench-rvy5 child task beads-workbench-rvy5.1 for the broader developer workflow initiative.

title: verify_bwb_state_flow help text shows unsupported --app-command syntax
description: The wrapper help text says '--app-command -- env BD_NON_INTERACTIVE=1 /tmp/bwb', but invoking the script exactly that way fails with argparse: unrecognized arguments: -- env BD_NON_INTERACTIVE=1 /tmp/bwb. The accepted form is '--app-command env BD_NON_INTERACTIVE=1 /tmp/bwb'. Expected: the help example matches actual parser behavior, or the parser accepts the documented delimiter form.
acceptance_criteria: The documented --app-command example is executable as written, or the parser is updated to support the currently documented syntax.
close_reason: Aligned --app-command help text with accepted parser syntax; verified via --help output.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Review: Straightforward doc/parser mismatch. Acceptance criteria are fine — fix either the help text or the parser, not both. Verdict: EPIC BLOCKER but trivial — a 1-line fix. Should take <5 minutes. Fix it alongside ypab/lsbv since you'll be in the same files.

title: capture_bwb_screen times out on open status modal
description:
## Description
Make `scripts/capture_bwb_screen.py` capture modal states deterministically without timing out when the app continues emitting blink updates.

## Instructions
1. Replace the post-input idle-wait termination with a deterministic drain or equivalent time-boxed completion rule that works for blinking text inputs.
2. Preserve child-process cleanup on both success and timeout.
3. Verify the existing status-modal repro and at least one non-modal capture path still complete successfully.
4. Update supporting docs or wrapper scripts only if behavior or flags change.

## Files to Modify
- `scripts/capture_bwb_screen.py`
- `scripts/verify_bwb_state_flow.py` (if needed)
- `docs/RUNTIME_UI_VERIFICATION.md` (if behavior or flags change)
acceptance_criteria:
- Reproducing the status-modal capture path no longer times out solely because the modal stays open
- The capture script still terminates the child process cleanly on success and timeout
- The expected modal screen is capturable in a deterministic verification run
close_reason: Current capture helper now captures the open status modal deterministically; fixed/superseded by later verification-toolkit script work

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Root cause confirmed: idle-based settle detection is incompatible with cursor blink. What: The capture loop (capture_bwb_screen.py lines 85-95) breaks only when select() returns no data for settle_wait duration. The status modal returns textinput.Blink (modal.go:162), which produces periodic cursor escape sequences on the PTY. The stream never goes idle, so the loop runs until timeout. Why: This is a fundamental design conflict — any Bubble Tea component with Blink will trigger the same timeout. Suggested action: Replace idle-based termination with a step-count-based approach: after all steps are sent, read for a fixed post-step settle duration (e.g. drain for N seconds then break), rather than waiting for silence.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Fix direction: time-boxed drain, not silence detection. What: After all steps are fed, switch to a fixed drain window (e.g. 0.5s) that reads all available output but exits on elapsed time, not on silence. The current 'continue on data, break on no-data' pattern (lines 89-95) must be inverted to 'drain until time, then break'. Why: This is the minimal change — it keeps the existing PTY/pyte architecture, still captures the final screen state, and works regardless of whether the target component produces periodic output. Do NOT add app-side signaling (e.g. a 'capture ready' marker) — that couples the capture tool to app internals and is disproportionate to the problem.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Risk: settle_wait semantics change affects all existing callers. What: Changing the post-step drain from 'wait for silence' to 'wait for duration' changes behavior for ALL capture invocations, not just modal ones. Currently non-modal captures exit early when output stops; after the fix they'll always wait the full drain window. Why: This could slow down non-modal captures unnecessarily. Suggested action: Keep settle_wait short (0.25-0.5s) which is acceptable for both cases, or add a --drain-time flag separate from --settle-wait so existing non-modal behavior is preserved. Prefer the simpler option (just use settle_wait as drain time) unless test suite timing becomes a problem.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Verification plan: Three checks needed. (1) Repro the exact steps from the bug (3,RIGHT,ENTER for status modal) — must complete without timeout. (2) Run an existing non-modal capture path (e.g. just navigate to a pane) — must still produce correct output and not regress in timing. (3) Confirm the captured screen JSON actually contains the modal content (text input field visible), not a pre-modal or blank screen. All three can be scripted as shell assertions in a test harness.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Verdict: straightforward. The bug is a clear design mismatch (idle detection vs periodic output), the fix is a ~10-line change to the drain loop in one file (capture_bwb_screen.py), and verification is mechanical. No architectural changes needed.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Execution started. Reviewer analysis suggests the timeout is caused by capture script silence detection interacting with modal cursor blink. Plan: fix scripts/capture_bwb_screen.py to use deterministic post-step draining rather than waiting for full PTY idleness, then verify modal and non-modal capture paths.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Execution note: before editing, I checked for overlap and found scripts/capture_bwb_screen.py already has substantial in-progress worktree changes. I reran the exact modal repro against the current worktree and it PASSED: the status modal was captured successfully, no 10s timeout occurred, checkpoint status-modal-open was recorded, and child cleanup completed cleanly (ctrl+c + sigterm). To avoid clobbering parallel script work, I did not add overlapping edits. Current reality: nh66 appears fixed in the existing in-progress capture-script changes, but it is not yet landed in git.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Reproduced the original status-modal capture path with the current helper and it no longer times out. The underlying modal-capture problem appears to have been fixed by the later capture-script work from the verification-toolkit effort, so this bug is now effectively resolved by that work.

title: Enrich related issue references for left rail
description:
## Description
Expose the metadata needed for left-rail rows by enriching related issue references from `bd show --json`.

## Instructions
1. Extend `domain.IssueReference` to carry issue type, priority, and status in addition to id and title.
2. Update gateway parsing in `internal/gateway/beads/read_gateway.go` so references parsed from `dependencies`, `dependents`, and `related` preserve `issue_type`, `priority`, and `status` when present.
3. Keep compatibility with existing detail rendering callers that only read id and title.
4. Add or update tests covering the richer reference mapping from `bd show --json`.
5. Do not distinguish Children in this task; parent-child relationship separation remains out of scope for v1.

## Files to Modify
- internal/domain/issues.go
- internal/gateway/beads/read_gateway.go
- internal/gateway/beads/read_gateway_test.go

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `IssueReference` includes type, priority, and status fields needed by the rail row renderer
- [ ] `bd show` dependency and related parsing preserves those fields when present
- [ ] Existing detail rendering behavior continues to work with richer references
- [ ] Children remain out of scope for this task
- [ ] Tests pass: go test ./internal/gateway/beads
close_reason: Implemented and verified

title: Add detail-mode rail navigation and related issue open behavior
description:
## Description
Add dedicated detail-mode keyboard navigation for the left rail and allow opening related issues directly from it.

## Instructions
1. Update the shell-owned detail presentation state and app integration so dedicated detail mode tracks pane focus across the left rail, content pane, and metadata rail.
2. Reuse existing detail scroll bindings as focus-conditional behavior: when the content pane is focused, up and down keep scrolling exactly as today; when the left rail is focused, up and down move the selected related issue row. Metadata remains non-interactive in v1.
3. Use left and right to move pane focus between left rail, content, and metadata.
4. When Enter is pressed on a focused left-rail item, emit a mode-level open-related-issue intent from `internal/mode/details/model.go`. Handle that intent in `internal/app/model.go` by loading the selected related issue and keeping dedicated detail mode active.
5. Preserve current content scrolling behavior and scroll restoration when moving between issues.
6. Keep search preview and compact detail rendering non-interactive.
7. Add or update tests for focus movement, related-issue open behavior, and existing detail-mode round-trip expectations.

## Files to Modify
- internal/mode/details/model.go
- internal/mode/details/model_test.go
- internal/app/model.go
- internal/app/model_test.go

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Left, right, up, down, and Enter support the approved detail-mode rail interaction model
- [ ] Up and down remain content scroll controls when the content pane is focused and become row-selection controls when the left rail is focused
- [ ] Enter on a focused left-rail item loads the selected related issue through a mode-level intent and keeps dedicated detail mode active
- [ ] Content-pane scrolling and scroll restoration continue to work as before
- [ ] Search preview and compact detail remain non-interactive summaries
- [ ] Tests pass: go test ./internal/mode/details ./internal/app
close_reason: Implemented detail-mode pane focus navigation, left-rail related issue open intent handling, and coverage for focus/open round-trips.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Keybinding conflict not addressed. What: detail context currently binds j/down to scroll_down and k/up to scroll_up (config/keybindings.go:144-145). Task says up/down should select rail rows when rail is focused, but up/down already mean scroll. The task says 'preserve current content scrolling behavior when the content pane is focused' but does not specify how the same keys dispatch differently per focused pane. Why: a tasker must design the focus-aware key dispatch from scratch since the current HandleKey in mode/details/model.go has no concept of pane focus. This is a design decision disguised as an implementation task. Suggested action: add explicit instructions for how pane focus changes key dispatch — either (a) new detail actions like rail_move_up/rail_move_down that share the same default bindings but dispatch based on focus state, or (b) reuse existing scroll_up/scroll_down with focus-conditional behavior documented in the task.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Enter key conflict with shell-level issue actions. What: task says Enter opens a selected related issue from the rail. But the app model (internal/app/model.go) handles key dispatch at the shell level before mode-specific handling. If Enter is already consumed by the shell for other actions in detail mode, the rail's Enter will never fire. Why: the task's files-to-modify list includes app/model.go which suggests the author is aware of shell-level dispatch, but the instructions don't describe how Enter dispatch precedence works when the rail is focused vs when it is not. Suggested action: add an instruction that rail Enter is dispatched as a mode-level intent (a tea.Cmd or custom message) from mode/details back to the app, not as a direct key match in app/model.go.

title: Add narrow related issue row renderer for left rail
description:
## Description
Add a shared one-line related-issue row renderer that stays readable inside a 22-28 column left rail.

## Instructions
1. Build on the enriched `domain.IssueReference` metadata from the prerequisite reference-mapping task; do not change gateway or domain mapping in this task unless a tiny adapter is required at the renderer boundary.
2. Update shared compact issue row rendering so the detail left rail can show type, priority, compact status, compact issue id, and a truncated title on one dense line.
3. Keep the renderer list-oriented and compact; do not introduce tree glyphs, nesting, or multi-line row layouts.
4. Ensure selected and unselected rows remain visually distinct for detail-mode focus.
5. Add or update tests covering narrow widths in the 22-28 column range.

## Files to Modify
- internal/ui/shared/issuerow/row.go
- internal/ui/shared/issuerow/row_test.go
- internal/ui/shared/renderhelpers/helpers.go
- internal/ui/shared/renderhelpers/helpers_test.go

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Related-issue rows remain one line wide and readable at 22, 24, and 28 columns
- [ ] Rendered rows include type, priority, status, compact issue id, and truncated title
- [ ] Selected rows remain visibly distinct from unselected rows
- [ ] No tree glyphs, nesting markers, or multi-line layouts are introduced
- [ ] Tests pass: go test ./internal/ui/shared/...
close_reason: Implemented shared compact related-issue row renderer for narrow 22-28 column rails with metadata tokens, selection contrast, and focused tests.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Data model gap blocks row rendering. What: acceptance criteria say rows must include type, priority, status, compact issue id, and truncated title. But domain.IssueReference only has ID and Title. The gateway's referencesFromMapArray discards status, priority, and issue_type from bd show --json output even though bd already provides them. The shared RenderCompact in issuerow takes domain.IssueSummary not IssueReference. Why: a tasker picking up syf.3 will discover they cannot render the specified fields because the data does not exist in the domain model. This is a hidden prerequisite. Suggested action: either (a) add a prerequisite task to enrich IssueReference with Status/Type/Priority and update the gateway parser, or (b) explicitly reduce the row spec to ID + truncated title only and adjust acceptance criteria.

title: Implement wide-screen detail layout with left related issues rail
description:
## Description
Implement the wide-screen detail renderer for the approved left related-issues rail.

## Instructions
1. Add a dedicated 3-column detail layout that activates only on wide terminals at about 140 columns or wider.
2. Keep the left rail compact at roughly 20 percent of available width, clamped to 22-28 columns, and keep the existing metadata rail at 34 columns.
3. Render the left rail as always-expanded grouped lists in this order: Blocked by, Blocks, Related. Render only non-empty groups.
4. Reuse the shared rail row renderer rather than duplicating row formatting logic inside the detail view.
5. Focus-pane styling in the renderer must be input-driven only. Default visual focus to the content pane until the navigation task wires active pane movement.
6. When the left rail is active in wide layout, suppress the duplicate inline Related Work section from the main content column. Continue showing inline related-work sections in fallback layouts below the wide breakpoint.
7. Preserve current two-column and single-column behavior below the wide-screen breakpoint.
8. Keep v1 scope explicit: no tree presentation, no nesting, no children group.
9. Add or update golden tests for wide-screen left-rail rendering and below-breakpoint fallback behavior.

## Files to Modify
- internal/ui/details/details.go
- internal/ui/details/details_test.go
- internal/ui/details/metadata.go

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Detail mode switches to a 3-column layout at the wide-screen breakpoint and keeps existing fallback layouts below it
- [ ] Left rail width stays within the approved compact range and the content column remains dominant
- [ ] Left rail renders grouped Blocked by, Blocks, and Related lists using the shared row renderer
- [ ] Wide layout avoids duplicating the same related-work lists in the content body
- [ ] Renderer remains tree-free: no nesting, connectors, or collapsible groups
- [ ] Golden coverage captures the left-rail layout and fallback behavior
- [ ] Tests pass: go test ./internal/ui/details
close_reason: Implemented wide 3-column detail layout with compact related-issues left rail, preserved fallback layouts, and added/updated goldens and tests.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Left/right focus bindings not in scope but needed. What: this task adds the 3-column layout and says 'extend the detail renderer input state only as needed to show focused-pane and selected-row styling.' But left/right arrow keys are not currently bound in the detail context (they are only in board and modal). The task does not mention adding new detail keybinding actions. syf.5 handles keyboard navigation but depends on syf.4, meaning syf.4 will ship a layout with focus styling but no way to actually change focus. Why: the acceptance criteria for syf.4 say 'Golden coverage captures the left-rail layout' but those goldens will need to show focus states that cannot be triggered by keyboard in syf.4 alone. This is fine if syf.4 is understood as purely rendering, but the 'focused-pane styling' instruction creates ambiguity. Suggested action: clarify in syf.4 that focused-pane styling is driven by input state props (not by keyboard handling) and that focus is hardcoded to content pane until syf.5 wires navigation.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Related Work duplication. What: the current detail renderer already shows related work inline in the content sections (renderContentSections calls renderReferences for Blocked by, Blocks, Related at lines 156-158 of details.go). When the wide-screen left rail is active, the same references will appear twice — once in the left rail and once inline in the content body. Why: users will see duplicate information, wasting screen real estate and creating confusion. Suggested action: add an instruction to suppress or collapse the inline Related Work section in renderContentSections when the wide-screen left rail is active.

title: Research left related issues rail UX and interaction model
description:
## Description
